items.title: Mirror, mirror on the wall — Who is the most distinctive of them all?
items.title_zh: 镜中谁最具显著性？CJEU将裁定写实肖像商标注册资格
items.title_en: CJEU to Rule on Inherent Distinctiveness of Photorealistic Portrait Trademarks
items.summary: The question whether photorealistic portraits of people’s faces are inherently distinctive and can be registered as trade marks has been the subject of some debate (IPKat here, here and here). Now the Court of Justice of the EU (“CJEU”) gets to weigh in in a reference for a preliminary ruling from the Budapest High Court in Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda (case C-3/26).   Background On 12 February
items.ai_summary_zh: 布达佩斯高等法院就Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda案（C-3/26）向欧盟法院提交初步裁决请求。核心争议在于人物面部照片写实肖像是否具备固有显著性并可注册为商标。此前该问题在业界引发广泛辩论，此次CJEU的介入将为欧盟范围内此类非传统标志的可注册性及显著性判断提供关键法律指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU (CJEU) has received a request for a preliminary ruling from the Budapest High Court regarding the registrability of photorealistic portraits as trademarks. The reference, case C-3/26 Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda, addresses whether such images possess inherent distinctive character under EU trademark law. This development follows ongoing debate within the IP community concerning the legal standards applied to non-traditional marks consisting of human faces.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业若计划将品牌代言人或创始人肖像注册为欧盟商标，需密切关注此案进展。若CJEU认定缺乏固有显著性，企业需提前准备通过使用获得显著性的证据。建议优化品牌布局策略，避免过度依赖单一肖像注册，结合文字图形组合申请以增强保护稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights the evolving interpretation of distinctive character for non-traditional trademarks in the EU. Rights holders relying on personal likenesses or founder portraits should note that inherent distinctiveness remains a contested legal hurdle. The outcome may influence how applicants structure filings for image-based marks, potentially necessitating stronger evidence of acquired distinctiveness through use if inherent character is not recognized by the Court.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["布达佩斯高等法院提请CJEU对写实肖像固有显著性作出初步裁决", "案件核心争议为人物面部照片写实肖像能否注册为欧盟商标", "CJEU裁决将明确此类非传统标志在欧盟的注册标准与法律界限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU will assess whether photorealistic portraits of faces meet the threshold for inherent distinctiveness required for trademark registration.", "The reference originates from the Budapest High Court in case C-3/26, seeking clarification on EU-wide standards for portrait-based marks.", "This ruling aims to resolve uncertainties surrounding the registration of non-traditional signs depicting realistic human features."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注CJEU裁决，评估肖像商标注册风险及固有显著性要求", "提前收集使用证据，探索通过市场使用获得显著性的合规路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands using founder or spokesperson portraits must evaluate risks regarding inherent distinctiveness requirements in the EU.", "Practice note: Applicants should consider compiling evidence of acquired distinctiveness through market use to support registration efforts.", "What to monitor: Await the CJEU's preliminary ruling for definitive guidance on the registrability criteria for photorealistic face portraits."]
core_points_en: The CJEU will assess whether photorealistic portraits of faces meet the threshold for inherent distinctiveness required for trademark registration.
The reference originates from the Budapest High Court in case C-3/26, seeking clarification on EU-wide standards for portrait-based marks.
This ruling aims to resolve uncertainties surrounding the registration of non-traditional signs depicting realistic human features.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands using founder or spokesperson portraits must evaluate risks regarding inherent distinctiveness requirements in the EU.
Practice note: Applicants should consider compiling evidence of acquired distinctiveness through market use to support registration efforts.
What to monitor: Await the CJEU's preliminary ruling for definitive guidance on the registrability criteria for photorealistic face portraits.

items.title: Confidentiality ambush at the UPC: disclose once, lose it forever (CoA on Rule 262.2 RoP), (UPC_CoA_930/2025)
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：未获保护令披露即失密，保密 ambush 风险警示
items.title_en: UPC CoA: Disclosure Without Protective Order Irretrievably Waives Confidentiality Status
items.summary: Court docket: Court of Appeal
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在EOFlow诉Insulet案中裁定，若未在披露前依据程序规则第262A条获得保护令或明确限制，向对方披露的信息将永久丧失商业秘密地位。法院区分了限制公众查阅（262.2条）与限制对方使用（262A条），并驳回了“仅限诉讼目的”的隐含限制主张。此举强调程序合规是维持秘密性的必要步骤，未采取合理保密措施将导致信息性质不可逆地改变。
items.ai_summary_en: In EOFlow v. Insulet, the UPC Court of Appeal dismissed an appeal against a Milan Central Division order refusing confidentiality treatment under Rule 262.2 RoP for business information produced during an account of infringing acts. The Court clarified that restricting public access to the court file does not automatically impose confidentiality obligations on the opposing party. It rejected the notion of an implicit procedural limitation confining the use of disclosed information solely to litigation purposes. Consequently, trade secrets or confidential information disclosed without an express protective order under Rule 262A RoP may permanently lose their protected status, as the requirement for reasonable steps to keep information secret is not met.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需极度警惕“保密伏击”。在提交任何敏感财务或合同数据前，必须同步申请第262A条保护令，切勿依赖默示保密义务。应建立早期识别机制，评估披露对平行诉讼的影响，必要时引入“无溢出规则”，避免因程序疏忽导致核心商业机密在欧洲市场彻底公开且无法挽回。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling underscores the critical distinction between public file restrictions and inter-party confidentiality in UPC practice. Rights holders must proactively secure Rule 262A orders before producing sensitive commercial data, such as invoices or turnover figures, rather than relying on implied duties. The decision highlights that procedural compliance is a substantive prerequisite for maintaining secrecy, with significant implications for evidence management and parallel litigation strategies across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["未依262A条获保护令即披露，信息将永久丧失商业秘密法律地位", "严格区分限制公众查阅与限制对方使用，前者不自动约束后者", "驳回诉讼用途隐含限制论，缺乏程序限制视为未采取合理保密措施"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 262.2 restricts public access but does not inherently bind the opposing party to confidentiality obligations regarding disclosed materials.", "The Court rejected implicit limitations, ruling that information used in proceedings lacks protection absent an explicit Rule 262A protective order.", "Failure to secure express restrictions before disclosure constitutes a failure to take reasonable steps, potentially forfeiting trade secret status."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提交敏感证据前务必同步申请262A条保护令，杜绝裸奔披露", "摒弃默示保密幻想，建立早期识别与平行诉讼溢出风险评估机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties must secure explicit Rule 262A protective orders before disclosing sensitive financial or contractual data to prevent irreversible loss of confidentialit…", "Practice note: Do not rely on implicit litigation-use limitations; distinguish clearly between public access restrictions (Rule 262.2) and inter-party confidentiality measures.", "What to monitor: How national courts within the UPC system align with this strict interpretation of reasonable steps for trade secret protection in future infringement accounts."]
core_points_en: Rule 262.2 restricts public access but does not inherently bind the opposing party to confidentiality obligations regarding disclosed materials.
The Court rejected implicit limitations, ruling that information used in proceedings lacks protection absent an explicit Rule 262A protective order.
Failure to secure express restrictions before disclosure constitutes a failure to take reasonable steps, potentially forfeiting trade secret status.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties must secure explicit Rule 262A protective orders before disclosing sensitive financial or contractual data to prevent irreversible loss of confidentialit…
Practice note: Do not rely on implicit litigation-use limitations; distinguish clearly between public access restrictions (Rule 262.2) and inter-party confidentiality measures.
What to monitor: How national courts within the UPC system align with this strict interpretation of reasonable steps for trade secret protection in future infringement accounts.

items.title: Local division The Hague refuses to grant PI to Stratasys
items.title_zh: UPC海牙分院拒绝授予Stratasys临时禁令
items.title_en: UPC The Hague Denies Stratasys Preliminary Injunction Against Bambulab in 3D Printer Dispute
items.summary: The panel at the local division The Hague, consisting of Margot Kokke, Mojca Mlakar, Robert van Peursem, and Koen Callewaert, did not grant a PI (case ID: UPC-CFI-305/2026). The American company Stratasys had filed a lawsuit against competitor Bambulab. Both companies compete in the global market for 3D printers. The dispute concerns the infringement of […] The post Local division The Hague refuses to grant PI to Stratasys appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）海牙地方分院驳回了美国公司Stratasys针对竞争对手Bambulab提出的临时禁令申请。该案案号为UPC-CFI-305/2026，双方在全球3D打印机市场存在竞争关系，争议涉及专利侵权。合议庭由Margot Kokke等四位法官组成。因正文信息有限，具体驳回理由及涉案专利细节未披露。
items.ai_summary_en: The Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) in The Hague has refused to grant a preliminary injunction requested by US-based 3D printing giant Stratasys against its competitor Bambulab. The panel, comprising Judges Margot Kokke, Mojca Mlakar, Robert van Peursem, and Koen Callewaert, issued this decision in case UPC-CFI-305/2026. The dispute centers on alleged patent infringement within the highly competitive global market for 3D printers. Despite the intense commercial rivalry between the parties, the court declined to provide urgent injunctive relief. This ruling underscores the rigorous standards applied by the UPC when evaluating requests for provisional measures, highlighting that rights holders must meet substantial evidentiary burdens to secure rapid judicial intervention in patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示UPC海牙分院在处理3D打印领域紧急救济时的审慎态度。鉴于原文未披露驳回的具体法律或事实依据，需持续关注后续正式判决以明确法院对侵权可能性及利益平衡的审查标准。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the significant evidentiary thresholds required to obtain preliminary injunctions at the Unified Patent Court. Rights holders seeking urgent interim relief must prepare comprehensive evidence demonstrating urgency and a high likelihood of success on the merits. Conversely, defendants can leverage procedural defenses to challenge such applications effectively. For companies operating in Europe, particularly in fast-moving technology sectors like additive manufacturing, robust freedom-to-operate analyses are essential to mitigate litigation risks. Establishing proactive IP strategies and rapid response protocols remains critical for navigating the UPC's stringent provisional measures framework.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC The Hague Local Division rejected Stratasys's request for a preliminary injunction against competitor Bambulab.", "The litigation involves complex patent infringement claims within the globally competitive 3D printer manufacturing sector.", "The decision illustrates the UPC's strict and cautious approach regarding the granting of urgent preliminary relief."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC海牙分院在3D打印技术类案件中颁发临时禁令的门槛与倾向", "需等待更多案情披露，以分析法院驳回禁令的具体法律逻辑", "观察Stratasys与Bambulab在UPC体系下的后续诉讼策略调整"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders face high evidentiary hurdles for UPC preliminary injunctions; thorough preparation is mandatory.", "Practice note: Defendants should actively utilize procedural arguments to contest urgent relief requests and delay enforcement.", "What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the assessment of urgency and irreparable harm in tech patent disputes."]
core_points_en: UPC The Hague Local Division rejected Stratasys's request for a preliminary injunction against competitor Bambulab.
The litigation involves complex patent infringement claims within the globally competitive 3D printer manufacturing sector.
The decision illustrates the UPC's strict and cautious approach regarding the granting of urgent preliminary relief.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders face high evidentiary hurdles for UPC preliminary injunctions; thorough preparation is mandatory.
Practice note: Defendants should actively utilize procedural arguments to contest urgent relief requests and delay enforcement.
What to monitor: UPC case law trends regarding the assessment of urgency and irreparable harm in tech patent disputes.

items.title: Official Statistics: Facts and figures: patents, trade marks, designs and hearings 2025
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局发布2025年专利商标及外观设计官方统计
items.title_en: UKIPO Releases 2025 Official Statistics on Patents, Trade Marks, Designs and Hearings
items.summary: Official statistics include the latest annual data breakdowns for patents, trade marks, and designs for 2025, based on IPO administrative data.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布了基于行政数据的2025年年度官方统计数据。该报告详细拆解了专利、商标和外观设计的最新年度数据分布，涵盖申请量、注册量及相关听证会情况。作为英国知识产权体系的核心情报，这些数据反映了当前英国市场的创新活跃度与知识产权保护现状，为权利人评估市场策略提供了量化依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has published its official annual statistics for 2025. This data release provides a comprehensive breakdown of administrative records concerning patents, trade marks, and registered designs. The report also includes details regarding hearings conducted during the year. These figures serve as a key indicator of intellectual property activity within the United Kingdom, reflecting application volumes and registration outcomes based on internal IPO administrative data.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This annual data release offers rights holders and portfolio managers a quantitative baseline for assessing UK IP activity levels. By reviewing these administrative metrics, stakeholders can contextualize their own filing volumes against broader market trends. The inclusion of hearing data further provides insight into procedural engagement and dispute resolution activities within the UK system, supporting informed monitoring of the local IP landscape.

items.title: CD Paris, April 22, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_461/2025
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分院：撤销程序中法院应依职权审查法律利益
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Patent Expiry Does Not Bar Revocation Actions; Auxiliary Requests Reviewed Independently
items.summary: Legal interest in revocation proceedings must be examined by the Court of its own motion, even where the defendant does not challenge admissibility (headnote 1; para. 11)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎中央分院在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使被告未质疑可受理性，法院仍须依职权审查撤销程序中的法律利益。专利过期不必然导致不可受理，若存在过往侵权索赔可能，原告仍具具体法律利益。法院独立确定客观技术问题，且仅受原告对辅助请求所提抗辩理由约束，未依职权补充无效理由，最终维持修改后的专利有效。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v. BASF, the UPC Paris Central Division ruled that courts must examine legal interest in revocation proceedings ex officio, regardless of whether the defendant challenges admissibility. The Court held that patent expiry does not automatically render a revocation action inadmissible if past infringement claims remain possible. Here, claimants retained standing due to pending evidence preservation measures and announced damages claims for alleged past infringement. Regarding claim construction, the Court independently reformulated the objective technical problem based on the description and drawings, focusing on adhesion improvement rather than flame protection. When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court was bound solely by the arguments advanced by the claimant against them. It did not supplement invalidity grounds on its own motion, ultimately maintaining the validity of the amended auxiliary requests where no specific prior art attacks were raised.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确UPC在撤销程序中的职权边界：法院主动审查资格但被动审理无效理由。原告需全面主张所有无效依据，尤其是针对辅助请求，否则可能因未援引最强现有技术而败诉。这对制定UPC诉讼策略具有程序性警示意义。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should recognize that patent expiry alone does not guarantee dismissal of UPC revocation actions if potential liability for past infringement persists. Defendants must proactively demonstrate a lack of concrete legal interest to challenge admissibility effectively. In invalidity proceedings, it is critical to comprehensively attack all claim versions, including auxiliary requests, using the strongest available prior art. Failure to explicitly raise specific grounds against modified claims may result in their survival, as the Court will not introduce new invalidity arguments sua sponte. This underscores the need for thorough preparation across all potential claim scopes during litigation strategy formulation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院须依职权审查撤销程序法律利益，专利过期不必然导致不可受理", "法院不受专利所述客观技术问题约束，可基于说明书整体独立重新确定", "审查辅助请求时，法院仅受原告提出的抗辩理由约束，不依职权补充无效理由"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts must examine legal interest ex officio; patent expiry does not bar revocation if past infringement claims remain possible.", "Courts may independently reformulate the objective technical problem, balancing patentee protection with third-party legal certainty.", "Review of auxiliary requests is limited to claimant arguments; courts do not actively supplement invalidity grounds against them."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["原告在UPC撤销程序中必须穷尽所有无效理由，特别是针对辅助请求，法院不会代为补充", "专利过期后仍可能面临撤销风险，只要存在过往侵权损害赔偿或证据保全等具体法律利益", "需关注UPC法院如何独立重构客观技术问题，这可能偏离专利申请时的原始陈述"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess exposure to revocation risks even after patent expiry by evaluating potential past infringement liabilities and standing requirements.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity strategies comprehensively cover all claim versions, particularly auxiliary requests, to prevent partial maintenance due to unraised arguments.", "What to monitor: Track how UPC divisions apply ex officio examinations of legal interest and limit reviews of auxiliary requests to party-submitted grounds."]
core_points_en: Courts must examine legal interest ex officio; patent expiry does not bar revocation if past infringement claims remain possible.
Courts may independently reformulate the objective technical problem, balancing patentee protection with third-party legal certainty.
Review of auxiliary requests is limited to claimant arguments; courts do not actively supplement invalidity grounds against them.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess exposure to revocation risks even after patent expiry by evaluating potential past infringement liabilities and standing requirements.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity strategies comprehensively cover all claim versions, particularly auxiliary requests, to prevent partial maintenance due to unraised arguments.
What to monitor: Track how UPC divisions apply ex officio examinations of legal interest and limit reviews of auxiliary requests to party-submitted grounds.

items.title_en: UPC Milan: Long-Arm Jurisdiction Under Brussels Ia and First Stay for Non-Contracting State
items.summary: Long-arm jurisdiction via Art. 8 (1) Brussel Ia Recast Regulation (BR): The “risk of irreconcilable judgements” requires a four-part assessment: same factual and legal situation, predictability and no abuse
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC米兰地方法院依据《布鲁塞尔条例Ia》第8(1)条，确立对位于西班牙（非UPC成员国）共同被告的长臂管辖权，以避免判决冲突。同时，法院行使裁量权，因西班牙并行无效程序而中止针对西班牙领土的侵权诉讼，承认西班牙法院对专利有效性的专属管辖权。
items.ai_summary_en: On April 21, 2026, the UPC Local Division in Milan ruled it had jurisdiction over Spanish defendants under Article 8(1) of the Brussels Ia Regulation. The court found a risk of irreconcilable judgments if proceedings were separated, applying a four-part test assessing factual and legal alignment, predictability, and absence of abuse. Concurrently, the Milan LD issued a landmark stay of infringement proceedings concerning Spanish territory. This decision respected the exclusive jurisdiction of national courts over patent validity under Article 24(4) of the Regulation. It marks the first instance where the UPC stayed litigation for non-contracting Member State territories pending final validity determinations by national courts, balancing procedural efficiency with jurisdictional boundaries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定明确了UPC在处理涉及非成员国被告时的管辖权边界，以及在与国家法院并行程序中的协调机制。实务中需关注UPC如何平衡统一专利法院的效率与成员国法院在有效性问题上的专属权，特别是长臂管辖的滥用风险评估标准。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the scope of long-arm jurisdiction within the UPC system, signaling that supply chain participants across borders face consolidated litigation risks. Parties must carefully assess whether claims are sufficiently connected to justify joint hearings under Brussels Ia. Strategically, defendants can leverage the exclusive jurisdiction of national courts over patent validity to seek stays in infringement cases, particularly for non-contracting states. Litigants should prepare robust arguments on factual and legal connectivity while monitoring how the UPC balances efficiency against jurisdictional limits in future cross-border disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院适用四步测试法评估判决冲突风险，确认对西班牙分销商被告拥有管辖权", "依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第24(4)条，承认西班牙法院对专利有效性争议的专属管辖权", "法院拒绝因CJEU待决参考裁决而中止程序，认为本案法律解释问题与待决案件不同"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Milan asserted jurisdiction over Spanish co-defendants under Article 8(1) Brussels Ia to prevent irreconcilable judgments from separate proceedings.", "The court established a four-step test for 'irreconcilable judgments': identical facts, same legal framework, foreseeability, and no procedural abuse.", "For the first time, the UPC stayed infringement actions regarding non-contracting states, deferring to national courts on patent validity issues."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对非成员国被告行使管辖权需满足事实与法律情境同一性及无滥用诉权条件", "在涉及非成员国专利有效性争议时，UPC倾向于中止侵权诉讼以尊重国家法院专属管辖", "需持续观察CJEU后续关于UPC与国家法院之间未决诉讼规则适用的进一步判例"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Supply chain entities face increased risk of consolidated UPC litigation via Article 8(1) Brussels Ia long-arm jurisdiction.", "Practice note: Leverage national court exclusive jurisdiction over validity to strategically request stays in UPC infringement proceedings.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies the four-part test for irreconcilable judgments and handles stays for non-contracting state territories."]
core_points_en: UPC Milan asserted jurisdiction over Spanish co-defendants under Article 8(1) Brussels Ia to prevent irreconcilable judgments from separate proceedings.
The court established a four-step test for 'irreconcilable judgments': identical facts, same legal framework, foreseeability, and no procedural abuse.
For the first time, the UPC stayed infringement actions regarding non-contracting states, deferring to national courts on patent validity issues.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Supply chain entities face increased risk of consolidated UPC litigation via Article 8(1) Brussels Ia long-arm jurisdiction.
Practice note: Leverage national court exclusive jurisdiction over validity to strategically request stays in UPC infringement proceedings.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies the four-part test for irreconcilable judgments and handles stays for non-contracting state territories.

items.title: Court of Appeal grants PI and clarifies necessity requirement
items.title_zh: 上诉庭授予临时禁令并澄清必要性要求
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Grants Preliminary Injunction and Clarifies Necessity Requirement
items.summary: Abbott Diabetes Care v Sinocare and Menarini. Court of Appeal, 17 April 2026 (UPC_CoA_901/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Abbott诉Sinocare案中推翻海牙地方分庭决定，授予临时禁令。裁决澄清了《统一专利法院协定》第62条下的“必要性”要求，强调需前瞻性评估市场竞争动态及结构性影响，而非仅看不可弥补损害。同时确认用户界面特征若产生技术效果可贡献于创造性。被告交叉上诉因未受不利影响被驳回。
items.ai_summary_en: In Abbott Diabetes Care v Sinocare and Menarini, the UPC Court of Appeal set aside the Local Division The Hague’s refusal of a preliminary injunction. The decision clarifies that Article 62 UPCA necessity does not strictly require proof of irreparable harm but focuses on risks associated with awaiting merits proceedings. Given the highly concentrated continuous glucose monitoring market, the Court found urgency due to potential long-term price erosion and structural shifts from single to multi-supplier dynamics. Additionally, the Court confirmed that user interface features improving human-machine interaction contribute to inventive step assessment. Finally, the cross-appeal was declared inadmissible as the defendants had not suffered an adverse judgment in the first instance, limiting their standing to challenge specific reasoning.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC框架下临时禁令“必要性”的宽泛解释，将市场竞争结构变化纳入考量。企业需注意，即便无即时巨额损失，新竞争者进入导致的市場格局改变亦可能触发禁令。同时，UI相关专利的技术性认定标准得到重申，利于软件结合硬件类专利的保护。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling signals that the UPC may grant preliminary injunctions based on broader competitive dynamics rather than strict irreparable harm. Market entry by competitors in concentrated sectors can justify urgency due to potential long-term structural impacts like price erosion. Parties should anticipate that traditional damage arguments may be insufficient. Instead, litigation strategies must address market diversification effects and technical contributions of non-traditional features. Early freedom-to-operate analysis remains critical, while defense teams should prepare robust arguments on positive market competition effects to counter urgency claims effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭认定必要性需评估市场进入对价格及份额的长期结构性影响，而非仅限即时财务损失", "确认孤立非技术特征不排除在创造性评估外，用户界面若改善人机交互可贡献技术特征", "裁定被告在一审胜诉情况下，不得就紧急性理由提起交叉上诉，因其实体结果未受不利影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Necessity assessment prioritizes risks of market structure changes over immediate financial losses when evaluating provisional measures.", "User interface features contributing to improved human-machine interaction may support inventive step assessments in patent validity reviews.", "Defendants winning at first instance lack standing to file cross-appeals challenging urgency based solely on unfavorable reasoning parts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["临时禁令必要性评估纳入市场竞争动态与结构性影响，降低了申请人证明“不可弥补损害”的门槛", "用户界面特征若产生技术效果（如改进人机交互），在创造性评估中将被视为整体技术方案的一部分", "交叉上诉的受理严格限于对判决主文不服的情形，单纯挑战推理部分而结果有利者将被驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how UPC interprets market structure changes as grounds for urgency in preliminary injunction requests.", "Practice note: Strengthen defenses by arguing positive effects of market diversification to overcome traditional damage limitations.", "What to monitor: Track admissibility standards for cross-appeals regarding partial victories and reasoning challenges in UPC proceedings."]
core_points_en: Necessity assessment prioritizes risks of market structure changes over immediate financial losses when evaluating provisional measures.
User interface features contributing to improved human-machine interaction may support inventive step assessments in patent validity reviews.
Defendants winning at first instance lack standing to file cross-appeals challenging urgency based solely on unfavorable reasoning parts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how UPC interprets market structure changes as grounds for urgency in preliminary injunction requests.
Practice note: Strengthen defenses by arguing positive effects of market diversification to overcome traditional damage limitations.
What to monitor: Track admissibility standards for cross-appeals regarding partial victories and reasoning challenges in UPC proceedings.

items.title: New EU Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation: Key Changes And Practical Implications
items.title_zh: 欧盟新版技术转让集体豁免条例：关键变更与实务影响
items.title_en: New EU Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation: Key Changes And Practical Implications
items.summary: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted a revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (the "Revised TTBER") and Guidelines on the application of Article 101 of the Treaty...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月16日，欧盟委员会通过修订版《技术转让集体豁免条例》及配套指南，将于5月1日生效并取代旧规。新规保留现有市场份额安全港阈值，但引入商业化前阶段简化评估方法，明确未产生收入技术可归零份额，以反映数据与数字技术许可实践的发展。
items.ai_insight_zh: 新规旨在适应数据与数字技术许可趋势，降低早期技术合作合规不确定性。企业需关注商业化前市场份额认定的简化逻辑，确保许可协议在过渡期内符合新指南对竞争限制的界定，特别是涉及尚未市场化技术的安排。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版TTBER及指南，5月1日生效", "保留竞争对手20%、非竞争对手30%的市场份额安全港阈值不变", "引入商业化前阶段简化评估法，未产生收入技术可视为零市场份额"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["新规明确未创收技术在安全港评估中的零份额地位，利好早期研发合作", "需监测新指南对数据驱动型许可及协作创新模式的具体解释与适用边界", "企业应审查现存许可协议，确保在2026年5月1日后继续满足豁免条件"]

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 17, 2026, Order Concerning A Request For Provisional Measures, UPC_CoA_901/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭关于临时措施请求的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court: Indirect Infringement, Claim Interpretation, and Inventive Step Precedents
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court's Court of Appeal in Luxembourg addresses critical questions about patent claim interpretation, indirect infringement liability, and the assessment of non-technical features in inventive step analysis. This landmark decision examines whether software apps distributed through app stores can constitute indirect infringement and establishes important precedents for timeline graph interpretation in patent claims.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在卢森堡作出裁决，澄清了专利权利要求解释、间接侵权责任及创造性评估中非技术特征的考量。裁决认定通过应用商店分发软件及应用硬件组件可构成间接侵权，即使未提供接收设备。同时明确时间线图解释规则及交叉上诉的可受理性标准，确立了重要先例。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Court of Appeal issued a landmark order addressing critical patent law issues. The decision establishes that non-technical features must be included in inventive step assessments if they interact with technical features to produce a technical effect. It clarifies that timeline graphs in claims encompass elements below the x-axis when temporal correlation exists. Furthermore, the Court ruled that distributing software via app stores and selling hardware components can constitute indirect infringement, even without providing the receiving device. Additionally, an eight-week delay in seeking provisional measures was deemed reasonable, reinforcing procedural standards for urgency assessments in preliminary injunction requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决明确了UPC对软件相关发明间接侵权的宽泛认定标准，以及非技术特征在创造性评估中的整合视角。企业需关注应用分发模式下的侵权风险，并注意临时措施申请的时间敏感性。交叉上诉仅用于改变不利结果，不可仅用于修正推理。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly broadens the scope of indirect infringement liability under the UPC framework. Companies distributing software or supplying core hardware components face increased litigation risks, even if they do not provide the final end-user device. Practitioners should carefully evaluate supply chain activities against this expanded interpretation. Regarding patent drafting, UI and user interaction features can now strengthen inventive step arguments by demonstrating technical contributions through human-machine interaction. Teams should ensure claims clearly define these technical boundaries. Rapid response strategies remain essential, as courts may accept short delays in seeking provisional measures if justified by case specifics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["非技术特征若与其他特征交互贡献于技术特性，不得在创造性评估中被排除", "通过应用商店提供软件及销售硬件组件，即便未提供接收单元，也可构成间接侵权", "时间线图中的元素若建立时间关联，即使位于坐标轴下方，仍属于权利要求中的图表部分"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Non-technical features contributing to technical character through interaction must be included in inventive step analysis.", "Timeline graph claim elements below the x-axis are included if a temporal correlation with monitored data is established.", "Distributing apps and selling hardware components may constitute indirect infringement without providing the receiving device."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["软件分发与硬件销售组合可能触发UPC下的间接侵权责任，无需直接提供完整设备", "非技术特征（如用户界面）若促进人机交互技术效果，将纳入创造性步骤整体评估", "需持续观察UPC上诉庭对“紧急性”认定及临时措施申请时限的具体裁量标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Supplying apps or components may trigger indirect infringement liability without providing the final receiving device.", "Practice note: Leverage UI and interaction features in patent applications to demonstrate technical character and support inventive step.", "What to monitor: UPC procedural rulings on urgency and admissibility of cross-appeals challenging first-instance reasoning."]
core_points_en: Non-technical features contributing to technical character through interaction must be included in inventive step analysis.
Timeline graph claim elements below the x-axis are included if a temporal correlation with monitored data is established.
Distributing apps and selling hardware components may constitute indirect infringement without providing the receiving device.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Supplying apps or components may trigger indirect infringement liability without providing the final receiving device.
Practice note: Leverage UI and interaction features in patent applications to demonstrate technical character and support inventive step.
What to monitor: UPC procedural rulings on urgency and admissibility of cross-appeals challenging first-instance reasoning.

items.title: BOIP and partners relaunch cross-border initiative to raise IP awareness among entrepreneurs
items.title_zh: BOIP与合作伙伴重启提升企业家IP意识的跨境倡议
items.title_en: BOIP and Partners Relaunch Cross-Border Initiative to Boost IP Awareness Among Entrepreneurs
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）宣布与多方合作伙伴共同重启一项跨境倡议。该行动旨在通过加强宣传和教育，提高比荷卢地区企业家对知识产权重要性的认知。此举反映了区域机构持续致力于优化创新环境，帮助中小企业更好地利用IP制度保护其商业成果，从而促进区域内的经济活力与创新保护水平。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) has announced the relaunch of a cross-border initiative in collaboration with multiple partners. This strategic action aims to enhance awareness regarding the importance of intellectual property rights among entrepreneurs within the Benelux region through strengthened publicity and educational efforts. The move reflects a sustained commitment by regional institutions to optimize the innovation environment. By helping small and medium-sized enterprises better utilize the IP system to protect their commercial achievements, the initiative seeks to promote economic vitality and elevate the standard of innovation protection across the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应密切关注BOIP此类区域性意识提升活动，将其视为了解当地合规要求的窗口。建议主动参与相关研讨会或查阅指南，确保商标、专利等基础IP布局符合比荷卢法律规范，避免因意识淡薄导致的权利流失或侵权风险，夯实市场准入基础。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor BOIP’s regional awareness campaigns, viewing them as valuable indicators of local compliance expectations. Proactive engagement with related workshops or review of official guidelines is recommended to ensure that foundational IP layouts, including trademarks and patents, align with Benelux legal standards. This approach helps mitigate risks of rights loss or infringement due to insufficient awareness, thereby solidifying the basis for market access and long-term operational stability in the jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BOIP联合合作伙伴重启跨境倡议以普及知识产权知识", "目标受众为比荷卢地区企业家，重点在于提升IP意识", "旨在通过教育手段强化区域内中小企业的知识产权保护能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["BOIP collaborates with partners to relaunch a cross-border initiative focused on popularizing intellectual property knowledge.", "The primary target audience comprises entrepreneurs in the Benelux region, with a specific focus on raising general IP awareness.", "The initiative aims to strengthen IP protection capabilities for SMEs in the region through targeted educational measures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注BOIP跨境倡议，及时获取比荷卢地区最新IP合规指引", "借势提升内部IP管理意识，完善在比荷卢的基础权利布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor BOIP's cross-border initiative to obtain timely updates on Benelux IP compliance guidelines.", "Practice note: Leverage these resources to enhance internal IP management awareness and refine basic rights layouts in the Benelux region.", "What to monitor: New educational materials and workshop schedules released under this joint initiative for practical guidance."]
core_points_en: BOIP collaborates with partners to relaunch a cross-border initiative focused on popularizing intellectual property knowledge.
The primary target audience comprises entrepreneurs in the Benelux region, with a specific focus on raising general IP awareness.
The initiative aims to strengthen IP protection capabilities for SMEs in the region through targeted educational measures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor BOIP's cross-border initiative to obtain timely updates on Benelux IP compliance guidelines.
Practice note: Leverage these resources to enhance internal IP management awareness and refine basic rights layouts in the Benelux region.
What to monitor: New educational materials and workshop schedules released under this joint initiative for practical guidance.

items.title: BOIP Annual Report 2024
items.title_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局发布2024年度报告
items.title_en: BOIP Releases 2024 Annual Report on Benelux Trademark and Design Registration Trends
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）正式发布2024年度报告。作为负责比荷卢地区商标和外观设计注册的官方机构，该报告系统梳理了年度内的关键运营数据、申请趋势及审查效率。报告内容通常涵盖商标注册量、外观设计申请情况以及机构在数字化服务和国际合作方面的进展。鉴于正文暂缺，核心信息聚焦于机构年度履职总结，反映了比荷卢单一市场内知识产权活动的整体态势，为权利人了解区域注册环境提供权威参考。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) has officially published its 2024 Annual Report. As the authoritative body responsible for trademark and design registrations within the Benelux region, the report provides a comprehensive overview of key operational metrics, application trends, and examination efficiency rates for the year. The document details statistics regarding trademark filings and design applications, offering valuable insights into regional innovation and brand activity levels. Additionally, it highlights the office’s progress in digital service enhancements and international cooperation initiatives. This annual summary serves as an essential reference for rights holders seeking to understand the current intellectual property landscape and registration environment within the unified Benelux market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注比荷卢作为进入欧盟门户的战略地位。年报数据可辅助判断当地品牌竞争热度。建议出口企业定期监测BOIP发布的注册趋势，及时调整在该区域的商标布局策略，利用比荷卢单一注册效力覆盖三国的优势，降低合规成本并强化品牌保护。
items.ai_insight_en: The Benelux region remains a strategic gateway for market entry into the European Union. Rights holders should analyze BOIP’s annual data to assess local competitive intensity and identify emerging brand opportunities. Given that a single BOIP registration provides protection across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Luxembourg, companies can optimize their IP portfolios by leveraging this unitary effect. This approach helps reduce compliance costs while strengthening brand protection strategies. Regular monitoring of these registration trends allows businesses to adjust their filing strategies proactively, ensuring robust coverage in this key economic zone without the need for separate national applications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BOIP发布2024年度报告，总结比荷卢地区知识产权年度运营状况", "报告涵盖商标与外观设计注册数据，反映区域市场创新与品牌活跃度", "展示机构在提升审查效率及优化公共服务方面的年度工作成果"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["BOIP released its 2024 Annual Report summarizing the annual operational status of intellectual property activities in the Benelux region.", "The report covers trademark and design registration data, reflecting the level of innovation and brand activity within the local market.", "It showcases the office's annual achievements in improving examination efficiency and optimizing public services for users."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注比荷卢区域注册数据，评估当地市场竞争热度与品牌机会", "利用BOIP单一注册效力，优化对荷兰、比利时及卢森堡的布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Analyze Benelux registration data to evaluate local market competition intensity and identify potential brand opportunities.", "Practice note: Leverage the unitary effect of BOIP registrations to optimize IP layout across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Luxembourg efficiently.", "What to monitor: Track BOIP-published registration trends to adjust regional trademark strategies and manage compliance costs effectively."]
core_points_en: BOIP released its 2024 Annual Report summarizing the annual operational status of intellectual property activities in the Benelux region.
The report covers trademark and design registration data, reflecting the level of innovation and brand activity within the local market.
It showcases the office's annual achievements in improving examination efficiency and optimizing public services for users.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Analyze Benelux registration data to evaluate local market competition intensity and identify potential brand opportunities.
Practice note: Leverage the unitary effect of BOIP registrations to optimize IP layout across the Netherlands, Belgium, and Luxembourg efficiently.
What to monitor: Track BOIP-published registration trends to adjust regional trademark strategies and manage compliance costs effectively.

items.title: Canfield now faces injunction in five European countries in QuantifiCare dispute
items.title_zh: Canfield在QuantifiCare纠纷中面临五国UPC禁令
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Rules Canfield Infringes QuantifiCare Patent, Issues Multi-Country Injunction
items.summary: Canfield may no longer sell its 3D imaging system Canfield Vectra H2 in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. Last week the Düsseldorf local division under presiding judge Ronny Thomas ruled that Canfield had infringed QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843. The UPC judges also rejected Canfield’s counterclaim for revocation (case IDs: UPC_CFI_559/2024 and UPC_CFI_106/2025). […] The post Canfield now faces injunction in five European countries in QuantifiCare dispute appeared first on JUVE Pa
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法庭裁定，Canfield侵犯QuantifiCare专利EP 3 156 843，并驳回其撤销请求。此举导致Canfield的3D成像系统Vectra H2在比利时、法国、意大利和荷兰面临销售禁令，加上德国共涉及五国。该判决凸显UPC在快速解决跨国专利争议中的效力，对涉案产品的欧洲市场流通构成直接阻碍。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Düsseldorf local division has ruled that Canfield infringed QuantifiCare’s European patent EP 3 156 843. Presiding judge Ronny Thomas issued an injunction preventing the sale of Canfield’s Vectra H2 3D imaging system in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. This decision adds to existing restrictions in Germany, effectively blocking sales across five jurisdictions. The court simultaneously rejected Canfield’s counterclaim seeking revocation of the patent (case IDs: UPC_CFI_559/2024 and UPC_CFI_106/2025). The ruling underscores the UPC’s capacity to deliver rapid, cross-border enforcement decisions affecting product availability throughout participating member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the operational impact of the UPC’s centralized enforcement mechanism. By securing a single decision that applies across multiple member states, rights holders can efficiently restrict competitor access to key European markets. For defendants, the rejection of the revocation counterclaim demonstrates the high threshold required to invalidate patents at the central or local division level during infringement proceedings. Market participants must consider the speed and geographic reach of UPC rulings when assessing litigation risks and portfolio exposure in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC杜塞尔多夫法庭认定Canfield侵犯QuantifiCare专利权", "法院驳回Canfield撤销专利的反诉，维持专利有效性", "禁令覆盖比法意荷四国，叠加德国共五国禁止销售涉案产品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Düsseldorf local division found Canfield liable for infringing QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843 regarding its Vectra H2 system.", "An injunction was granted prohibiting sales in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands, supplementing German market restrictions.", "Judges rejected Canfield’s counterclaim for revocation, thereby upholding the validity of the asserted patent rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC跨国禁令效力，提前布局专利自由实施分析", "建立快速响应机制，准备专利无效证据以应对突发诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The ruling demonstrates how UPC decisions can rapidly restrict product distribution across multiple European markets via a single proceeding.", "Practice note: Defendants face significant challenges in invalidating patents during infringement actions, as seen in the rejected revocation counterclaim.", "What to monitor: Potential appeals or further procedural steps in this dispute may clarify the final scope of the multi-country injunction."]
core_points_en: The UPC Düsseldorf local division found Canfield liable for infringing QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843 regarding its Vectra H2 system.
An injunction was granted prohibiting sales in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands, supplementing German market restrictions.
Judges rejected Canfield’s counterclaim for revocation, thereby upholding the validity of the asserted patent rights.

Practice note: Defendants face significant challenges in invalidating patents during infringement actions, as seen in the rejected revocation counterclaim.
What to monitor: Potential appeals or further procedural steps in this dispute may clarify the final scope of the multi-country injunction.

items.title: Biogen is entitled to damages in Tecfidera case rules Regional Court Hamburg
items.title_zh: 汉堡地方法院裁定Biogen在Tecfidera案中获赔
items.title_en: Hamburg Court Rules Biogen Entitled to Damages for Tecfidera Market Exclusivity Infringement
items.summary: Biogen accuses the generic drug companies of violating market exclusivity for its multiple sclerosis drug Tecfidera by selling their dimethyl fumarate generics before the end of its market exclusivity in Germany — in the case of Viatris, as early as summer 2022. According to Biogen, the market exclusivity for its original product ran until 2 […] The post Biogen is entitled to damages in Tecfidera case rules Regional Court Hamburg appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国汉堡地区法院裁定，Biogen有权就其多发性硬化症药物Tecfidera获得损害赔偿。Biogen指控仿制药企在德国市场独占期结束前销售二甲富马酸酯仿制药，其中Viatris早在2022年夏季即开始销售，侵犯了原研药的市场独占权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Regional Court of Hamburg has ruled in favor of Biogen, granting damages in a dispute concerning the market exclusivity of its multiple sclerosis drug, Tecfidera. The court found that generic manufacturers, including Viatris, violated Biogen’s exclusive rights by launching dimethyl fumarate generics before the expiration of the protected period in Germany. Specifically, Viatris was accused of entering the market as early as summer 2022. This judgment reinforces the legal protection afforded to originator companies during their designated market exclusivity windows. It establishes a clear precedent that premature commercialization of generics constitutes an infringement requiring compensation. The decision underscores the strict enforcement of data and market exclusivity regimes within the German jurisdiction, providing originators with a robust mechanism to seek redress against unauthorized early entry by competitors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确认了原研药企在市场独占期受侵犯时寻求损害赔偿的可诉性。需关注法院对独占期起止时间的具体认定逻辑，以及该判决对后续类似仿制药上市时机合规审查的参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights the critical importance of rigorous freedom-to-operate (FTO) analysis regarding regulatory data exclusivity and market protection periods in Europe. Companies planning generic launches must meticulously verify the exact expiration dates of these rights in target jurisdictions, particularly in Germany where enforcement is stringent. Establishing robust compliance mechanisms to monitor these timelines is essential to avoid significant financial liabilities. Legal teams should prioritize accurate tracking of exclusivity statuses alongside patent landscapes to ensure timely and lawful market entry strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["汉堡地区法院裁定Biogen在Tecfidera案中享有获得损害赔偿的权利", "Biogen指控Viatris等仿制药企在2022年夏提前销售仿制药，违反市场独占期规定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Hamburg Regional Court rules Biogen wins Tecfidera market exclusivity infringement case and is awarded damages.", "Generic firms like Viatris accused of selling dimethyl fumarate products before the official end of market exclusivity.", "Judgment confirms originator protection during exclusivity periods and mandates liability for violating generic entrants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法院支持原研药企针对提前上市仿制药主张损害赔偿的权利", "需观察判决中对市场独占期具体截止日期的事实认定依据", "关注该判例对德国医药领域独占期侵权赔偿计算标准的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify target country market exclusivity and patent status to prevent premature launch claims.", "Practice note: Implement FTO warning systems focusing on German data exclusivity enforcement and compliance.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in Germany regarding damages calculations for market exclusivity violations."]
core_points_en: Hamburg Regional Court rules Biogen wins Tecfidera market exclusivity infringement case and is awarded damages.
Generic firms like Viatris accused of selling dimethyl fumarate products before the official end of market exclusivity.
Judgment confirms originator protection during exclusivity periods and mandates liability for violating generic entrants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify target country market exclusivity and patent status to prevent premature launch claims.
Practice note: Implement FTO warning systems focusing on German data exclusivity enforcement and compliance.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in Germany regarding damages calculations for market exclusivity violations.

items.title: All you need is dialogue: the CJEU interprets ‘pastiche’ in Pelham II
items.title_zh: CJEU在Pelham II案中界定“拼贴画”例外
items.title_en: CJEU Defines Pastiche Exception in Pelham II: Artistic Dialogue and Objective Recognition
items.summary: On 14 April 2026, the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) delivered its judgment in Pelham II, bringing partial closure to litigation spanning more than two decades.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月14日，欧盟法院就Pelham II案作出判决，历时二十余年的Kraftwerk采样纠纷获部分终结。法院澄清《信息社会指令》第5(3)(k)条“拼贴画”例外非万能条款，要求作品需唤起原作但保持明显差异，并进行可识别的艺术对话。判定不依赖主观意图，而看客观可识别性。此裁决平衡了版权独占与创作自由，为采样艺术家提供确定性但也设限。
items.ai_summary_en: On 14 April 2026, the Court of Justice of the European Union delivered its judgment in Pelham II, addressing the unauthorised sampling of a two-second Kraftwerk track. The Court clarified that the 'pastiche' exception under Article 5(3)(k) of the InfoSoc Directive is not a broad catch-all for artistic reuse. Instead, it requires creations to evoke existing works while remaining perceptibly different, engaging in a recognisable artistic dialogue. The ruling establishes that classification depends on objective recognisability to an audience familiar with the original work, rather than the user's subjective intention. This decision balances exclusive rights with creative freedom, offering legal certainty for sampling artists while introducing specific constraints for invoking this defence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音乐及数字内容企业在欧运营时，使用采样需谨慎评估是否构成“拼贴画”。建议确保新作品与原作好区分并体现艺术对话，避免单纯复制。建立合规审查机制，关注客观可识别性标准，以降低侵权风险并利用例外条款保护创作自由。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment refines the scope of copyright exceptions for sampling and artistic reuse within the EU. Rights holders and users must now assess whether new works maintain perceptible difference and engage in artistic dialogue with the source material. The shift towards an objective standard of recognisability provides clearer benchmarks for evaluating potential infringement claims and the applicability of the pastiche defence in music and digital content contexts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["拒绝将“拼贴画”作宽泛兜底解释，明确其不适用于所有艺术再利用形式", "定义核心特征为模仿风格且与原作好区分，旨在进行可识别的艺术对话", "判定标准侧重客观可识别性而非用户主观意图，需熟悉原作的公众能辨识"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU rejected an expansive interpretation of pastiche, limiting it to works evoking originals while remaining perceptibly distinct.", "Pastiche requires engaging in a recognisable artistic or creative dialogue using characteristic elements of the borrowed work.", "Classification relies on objective recognisability to a person familiar with the original work, not the user's subjective intent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采样需确保与原作好区分并进行艺术对话，避免单纯复制以符合例外", "关注客观可识别性标准，建立合规审查机制以降低在欧版权侵权风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Sampling artists and labels must ensure new works are perceptibly different from originals and engage in artistic dialogue to qualify for the pastiche exception.", "Practice note: Evaluate compliance based on objective recognisability to audiences familiar with the source work, rather than relying on subjective user intentions.", "What to monitor: Future national court applications of the 'artistic dialogue' and 'perceptible difference' criteria in sampling disputes."]
core_points_en: The CJEU rejected an expansive interpretation of pastiche, limiting it to works evoking originals while remaining perceptibly distinct.
Pastiche requires engaging in a recognisable artistic or creative dialogue using characteristic elements of the borrowed work.
Classification relies on objective recognisability to a person familiar with the original work, not the user's subjective intent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Sampling artists and labels must ensure new works are perceptibly different from originals and engage in artistic dialogue to qualify for the pastiche exception.
Practice note: Evaluate compliance based on objective recognisability to audiences familiar with the source work, rather than relying on subjective user intentions.
What to monitor: Future national court applications of the 'artistic dialogue' and 'perceptible difference' criteria in sampling disputes.

items.title: Streaming giants call out Access Advance, ask UK Supreme Court to find patent pools have FRAND obligations
items.title_zh: 流媒体巨头呼吁英最高法认定专利池负有FRAND义务
items.title_en: Streaming giants call out Access Advance, ask UK Supreme Court to find patent pools have FRAND obligations
items.summary: With streaming licensing efforts ramping up, a trade association representing Amazon, Netflix and Disney took aim at video patent pools ahead of today’s Tesla hearing
items.ai_summary_zh: 代表亚马逊、Netflix和迪士尼的行业协会在特斯拉听证会前夕，向英国最高法院施压，要求裁定Access Advance等视频专利池必须遵守公平、合理和无歧视（FRAND）许可原则。此举旨在应对日益激烈的流媒体许可竞争，挑战专利池现有的许可模式，可能重塑欧洲视频技术领域的标准必要专利许可格局及司法审查标准。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国视频及硬件出口企业应密切关注此案进展。若法院认定专利池受FRAND约束，将增加许可谈判透明度并可能降低费率。建议企业提前评估Access Access Advance相关专利风险，利用此契机优化全球许可策略，避免高额索赔，并在谈判中主张更合理的许可条件。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["流媒体巨头联合施压英最高法，要求裁定专利池受FRAND约束", "案件背景为特斯拉听证会前夕，聚焦视频专利池许可合规性", "若胜诉将改变Access Advance等专利池的现有许可商业模式"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注判决对视频专利池FRAND义务认定的潜在影响", "利用规则变化优化全球许可策略以降低合规成本"]

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 23, 2026, Decision, Infringement Action And CCfR, UPC_CFl_559/2024 And UPC_CFl_106/2025 – Quantificare v. Canfield
items.title_zh: 杜塞尔多夫UPC分院：剔除国侵权行为可支撑泛UPC禁令
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Pan-UPC Injunctions Permitted Based on Acts in Carved-Out Member States
items.summary: Pan-UPC-territorial orders under Art. 34 UPCA can be based on infringing acts in a “carved-out” Contracting Member State (Headnote; mn. 238 et seqq.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫地方分院在Quantificare诉Canfield案中裁定，依据《统一专利法院协议》第34条，即使专利权人因平行诉讼程序原因将德国从侵权诉求中“剔除”，仅凭在德国的单一侵权行为仍足以签发泛UPC领土范围的禁令。法院明确偏离巴黎分院的限制性立场，重申说明书中的优选实施例不限制独立权利要求范围，且外国同族专利申请过程中的陈述仅具间接参考作用，不能限制欧洲专利保护范围。
items.ai_summary_en: In Quantificare v. Canfield, the UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf ruled that pan-territorial orders under Article 34 of the Unified Patent Court Agreement (UPCA) may be granted based on infringing acts occurring in any single Contracting Member State. This applies even if the claimant procedurally excluded that state from the lawsuit to avoid lis pendens with parallel national proceedings, and only proved infringement within that excluded jurisdiction. The court explicitly diverged from the Paris Local Division’s restrictive approach in Seoul Viosys, which required proof of infringement in each specific territory. Furthermore, the judgment clarified claim construction principles, stating that limitations from the description or dependent claims must not be read into broader independent claims. Statements made during the prosecution of foreign patent family members are considered at most indirect indications and cannot restrict the scope of protection of the European patent.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该判决强化了UPC泛欧禁令的可获得性，降低了因地域剔除导致的救济碎片化风险。权利人可利用单一成员国证据获取广泛禁令，但需注意不同分院间（如巴黎与杜塞尔多夫）在法律适用上的分歧可能引发上诉争议。权利要求解释上，法院坚持严格字面解释，排除说明书非必要限制，这对专利撰写和诉讼策略具有深远影响，需持续跟踪上诉法院对此类地域管辖与解释规则的最终定调。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly lowers the evidentiary threshold for obtaining pan-UPC injunctions, allowing rights holders to rely on infringement proofs from jurisdictions they have otherwise excluded from litigation. For companies operating in Europe, this increases compliance risks and necessitates robust freedom-to-operate analyses across all UPC member states, regardless of procedural carve-outs. Legal teams should also standardize global patent prosecution strategies to ensure that responses to foreign examiners do not inadvertently create a prosecution history that could later be cited to narrow patent scope or support adverse claim constructions in UPC proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["依据UPCA第34条，被“剔除”成员国的侵权行为仍可作为签发泛UPC禁令的基础", "法院确认单一成员国的侵权行为足以支持全UPC领土范围的救济措施", "外国同族专利申请陈述仅具间接指示作用，不得据此限制欧洲专利保护范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A single infringing act in any Contracting Member State suffices for UPC-wide injunctions, even if that state was procedurally carved out from the claims.", "The Düsseldorf Local Division rejects the Paris Local Division's requirement to prove infringement in every specific territory for pan-UPC relief.", "Description embodiments and foreign prosecution history statements cannot be used to limit the scope of protection of broader independent claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["泛UPC禁令门槛降低：单一成员国（含程序性剔除国）侵权事实即可支撑全境禁令", "权利要求解释趋严：说明书优选特征及外国申请历史不自动限缩独立权利要求范围", "司法分歧待解：杜塞尔多夫与巴黎分院对第34条适用的分歧需关注上诉法院后续裁决"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Lowered barriers for pan-UPC injunctions increase infringement risks; comprehensive freedom-to-operate analysis is critical across all member states.", "Practice note: Standardize global patent prosecution responses to prevent foreign examination histories from being used to restrict European patent scope.", "What to monitor: Divergence between UPC Local Divisions regarding territorial proof requirements and the evolving interpretation of Article 34 UPCA."]
core_points_en: A single infringing act in any Contracting Member State suffices for UPC-wide injunctions, even if that state was procedurally carved out from the claims.
The Düsseldorf Local Division rejects the Paris Local Division's requirement to prove infringement in every specific territory for pan-UPC relief.
Description embodiments and foreign prosecution history statements cannot be used to limit the scope of protection of broader independent claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Lowered barriers for pan-UPC injunctions increase infringement risks; comprehensive freedom-to-operate analysis is critical across all member states.
Practice note: Standardize global patent prosecution responses to prevent foreign examination histories from being used to restrict European patent scope.
What to monitor: Divergence between UPC Local Divisions regarding territorial proof requirements and the evolving interpretation of Article 34 UPCA.

items.title: Design Protection for Digital Interfaces: Key Changes in UKIPO Practice
items.title_zh: 数字界面外观设计保护：UKIPO实务关键变更
items.title_en: UKIPO Updates Guidance on Design Protection for Digital Interfaces and Animated Designs
items.summary: The UK Intellectual Property Office (IPO) has issued updated guidance on registering designs for icons, graphical user interfaces (GUIs),...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布关于图标、图形用户界面及动画设计注册的最新指南，于2026年4月21日生效。指南明确计算机程序本身不可注册，但其视觉外观可受保护。申请需清晰展示单一设计的整体视觉印象，动画设计需通过静态图像分阶段展示。免责声明可用于界定保护范围，但重大修改可能影响优先权日期。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued updated guidance, effective 21 April 2026, regarding the registration of icons, graphical user interfaces, and animated designs. While computer programs remain excluded, their visual appearance is protectable if meeting standard registration requirements. Applications must clearly depict a single design with an unambiguous overall visual impression. Animated designs require submission as separate static images, up to twelve views, with explicit declaration of animation. Disclaimers may clarify protection scope but risk re-dating applications if they significantly alter the design. Careful image preparation and clear explanations are essential to avoid objections or loss of priority dates during examination.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次更新强调了数字内容视觉外观保护的界限，特别是动画和GUI的呈现方式。申请人需注意图像准备的清晰度及免责声明的使用，以避免因形式问题导致异议或优先权丧失。实务中应关注UKIPO对“单一视觉印象”审查标准的执行尺度。
items.ai_insight_en: This update reinforces the distinction between functional code and protectable visual appearance for digital assets. Rights holders must ensure animated sequences are broken down into clear static views to satisfy the single visual impression requirement. Strategic use of disclaimers requires caution, as substantial changes can jeopardize priority claims, necessitating precise initial drafting for complex interface designs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新规于2026年4月21日生效，涵盖图标、GUI及动画设计的注册要求", "动画设计需以静态图像分阶段展示（最多12视图），并明确声明为动画", "依赖用户选择的屏幕组通常视为多个设计，需分别提交申请"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Updated UKIPO guidance effective 21 April 2026 covers icons, GUIs, and animated design registration requirements.", "Animated designs must be filed using up to twelve static images showing each stage clearly.", "Disclaimers clarify protection scope but significant amendments may result in loss of original filing date."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数字界面设计注册需严格区分功能与外观，确保视觉表达清晰无歧义", "动画设计的分帧展示策略直接影响审查结果，需提前规划视图数量", "后续需观察UKIPO在处理复杂交互界面时，对多设计合并申请的接受程度"]

items.title: CD Milan, April 10, 2026, Revocation Action, UPC_CFI_480/2025
items.title_zh: UPC米兰分院撤销诉讼判决要点分析
items.title_en: UPC Milan Revokes Patent: Novelty Mapping, Claim Construction, and Restructuring Requirements
items.summary: When assessing novelty, the Court will examine the disclosure of the prior art document overall and will compare this disclosure to the scope of the patent-in-suit. If one way of “mapping” leads...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析UPC米兰分院在Fisher & Paykel案中的最终决定。法院指出，若现有技术映射破坏新颖性，专利须被撤销。法院独立进行权利要求解释，不受专家意见约束。专利权人需主动提交重构的权利要求组，法院不会依职权维持从属权利要求。辅助请求仅需满足最低可受理性门槛。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Milan revoked the patent-in-suit in Fisher & Paykel, establishing critical procedural and substantive standards. The Court held that if any legitimate mapping of prior art onto claim features destroys novelty under Article 54 EPC, the patent must be invalidated. Assessment focuses on the overall technical disclosure and function rather than component labels. Purpose-related features require only objective suitability, not mandatory inclusion. Crucially, the Court affirmed that claim construction is a matter of law, determined independently without binding expert opinions. Furthermore, the Court refused to maintain validity based on unamended dependent claims because the defendant failed to submit formally restructured claim sets or independently defend their validity. This highlights strict adherence to party requests under Article 76 UPCA.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC诉讼中专利权人的积极举证责任，特别是权利要求重构的程序要求。实务中需注意，仅依赖从属权利要求的依赖性不足以维持专利有效性，必须形式上重新构建。此外，辅助请求的可受理性标准较低，但实质性合规仍需在后续阶段证明。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies strict procedural requirements for defending patents at the UPC. Parties must proactively submit formally restructured claim sets rather than relying on the dependency of invalid independent claims. The Court’s independence in claim construction means expert opinions are persuasive but not binding. For auxiliary requests, meeting the admissibility threshold requires only minimal compliant explanations. Practitioners should ensure robust initial drafting with clear technical features to withstand rigorous novelty assessments focused on functional disclosure rather than semantic labels. Strategic preparation of alternative claim configurations is essential to avoid forfeiture of protection due to procedural oversights during litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院评估新颖性时整体考察现有技术披露，若任一映射方式破坏新颖性即撤销专利", "权利要求解释属法律问题，法院独立判定，不受当事人提交的专家意见约束", "专利权人须主动提交符合法律要求的重构权利要求组，法院不依职权维持未修改的从属权利要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Novelty assessment examines overall technical disclosure; any valid mapping destroying novelty mandates revocation regardless of component labels.", "Claim construction is a legal matter determined independently by the Court, which is not bound by party-submitted expert declarations.", "Patentees must actively submit restructured claim sets; Courts will not maintain patents based solely on unamended dependent claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确专利权人在UPC程序中需主动重构权利要求，法院不代劳", "专家意见在权利要求解释中仅作为参考，不具法律约束力", "辅助请求的可受理性门槛较低，只需提供初步合规理由即可"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Proactive submission of restructured claim sets is essential; reliance on dependent claim dependency is insufficient for maintaining patent validity.", "Practice note: Prepare minimally compliant explanations for auxiliary requests to satisfy admissibility thresholds efficiently during UPC proceedings.", "What to monitor: Judicial emphasis on technical function over labels in novelty assessments, requiring precise drafting of purpose-related features."]
core_points_en: Novelty assessment examines overall technical disclosure; any valid mapping destroying novelty mandates revocation regardless of component labels.
Claim construction is a legal matter determined independently by the Court, which is not bound by party-submitted expert declarations.
Patentees must actively submit restructured claim sets; Courts will not maintain patents based solely on unamended dependent claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Proactive submission of restructured claim sets is essential; reliance on dependent claim dependency is insufficient for maintaining patent validity.
Practice note: Prepare minimally compliant explanations for auxiliary requests to satisfy admissibility thresholds efficiently during UPC proceedings.
What to monitor: Judicial emphasis on technical function over labels in novelty assessments, requiring precise drafting of purpose-related features.

items.title: UPC: Supplementing Expert Opinion After Inspection Not Possible
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分部裁定：检查后不得补充专家意见
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Division Denies Supplementary Expert Opinion Following Ex-Parte Inspection
items.summary: The Düsseldorf local division of the Unified Patent Court has ruled on a defendant's request for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex-parte inspection order.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_1325/2025案中裁定，驳回被告要求法院委任专家就单方证据保全出具补充意见的请求。法院指出，被告可通过《程序规则》第197.3条申请审查以保障程序权利，且该专家非第187条规定的“法院专家”，无法律依据支持补充报告。证据保全旨在快速获取信息以决定是否起诉，实质准确性异议应在后续主程序中提出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf local division rejected a defendant’s request for a supplementary expert opinion after an ex-parte evidence preservation order. In case UPC_CFI_1325/2025, the panel held that no legal basis existed for such a supplement, distinguishing the court-commissioned inspector from a formal ‘court expert’ under Rule 187 of the Rules of Procedure. The court emphasized that evidence preservation aims to provide prompt information for assessing further action, with substantive accuracy challenges reserved for main proceedings. While denying the request, the judges noted specific technical questions regarding the expert’s sketches and access to protective letters, indicating these issues may be revisited during subsequent litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇单方证据保全时，应聚焦于利用Rule 197.3申请审查而非纠缠补充报告。需重视保全阶段的策略应对，因实质抗辩将延至主程序。建议提前准备技术对比分析，确保在主程序中有效反驳专家初步结论，避免程序被动。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the procedural boundaries between expedited evidence preservation and full merits adjudication at the UPC. Rights holders and defendants should recognize that ex-parte inspections are designed for speed, not finality. Challenges to technical findings must be strategically deferred to the main action. However, courts may still record specific factual queries during preservation, which can influence later stages if not adequately addressed in the primary litigation phase.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["驳回单方保全后补充专家意见请求，明确缺乏法律依据", "区分法院委任专家与法定法院专家，排除当事人评论权", "保全程序侧重效率，实体争议留待主程序解决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court denied the request for a supplementary expert report, finding no legal basis under the Rules of Procedure for such an addition post-inspection.", "The appointed expert was not classified as a 'court expert' under Rule 187 RoP, meaning parties lacked the procedural right to comment on the initial report.", "Substantive objections to the detailed description or technical accuracy must be raised in subsequent main proceedings rather than during preservation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用Rule 197.3申请审查替代补充专家意见诉求", "将实质性技术抗辩保留至主程序阶段集中发力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties involved in ex-parte preservation orders cannot rely on supplementary expert reports to correct perceived inaccuracies immediately; strategic planning mu…", "Practice note: Focus procedural efforts on Rule 197.3 reviews for immediate relief, while preparing robust technical counter-arguments for the main proceedings where substantive a…", "What to monitor: How UPC panels handle recorded technical questions from preservation phases when they resurface in main actions, particularly regarding expert access to prior pro…"]
core_points_en: The court denied the request for a supplementary expert report, finding no legal basis under the Rules of Procedure for such an addition post-inspection.
The appointed expert was not classified as a 'court expert' under Rule 187 RoP, meaning parties lacked the procedural right to comment on the initial report.
Substantive objections to the detailed description or technical accuracy must be raised in subsequent main proceedings rather than during preservation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties involved in ex-parte preservation orders cannot rely on supplementary expert reports to correct perceived inaccuracies immediately; strategic planning mu…
Practice note: Focus procedural efforts on Rule 197.3 reviews for immediate relief, while preparing robust technical counter-arguments for the main proceedings where substantive a…
What to monitor: How UPC panels handle recorded technical questions from preservation phases when they resurface in main actions, particularly regarding expert access to prior pro…

items.title: UPC Orders Infringer To Publish Decision On Their Website
items.title_zh: UPC责令侵权方在其网站公布判决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Orders Infringer to Publish Judgment on Website to Correct Misleading Statements
items.summary: A landmark ruling from the Düsseldorf Local Division demonstrates the Unified Patent Court's willingness to compel defendants to publicly correct misleading statements about patent disputes on their own platforms.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫分院在案件UPC_CFI_351/2024中作出里程碑式裁决。依据《统一专利法院协议》第80条，法院责令被告在其企业网站发布判决主文一个月，以纠正其此前宣称产品不侵权造成的公众误解。此举旨在确保约8000名客户获知侵权事实，费用由侵权方承担。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division issued a significant ruling in case UPC_CFI_351/2024, emphasizing the court's authority to enforce transparency when defendants distort legal realities. Citing Article 80 of the Unified Patent Court Agreement (UPCA), the court ordered the infringing defendants to publish the operative part of the judgment on their corporate websites for one month. This measure aimed to rectify false public impressions created by the defendants' prior statements claiming non-infringement and legality of their distribution activities. By mandating this publication at the infringer's expense, the court sought to accurately inform approximately 8,000 customers about the infringement finding. The decision also permitted the claimant to publish the ruling in five industry journals, establishing a clear precedent for using public disclosure as a restorative remedy to counteract misleading corporate communications during patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC利用公开判决作为矫正性救济措施的实践先例。当被告通过公开声明扭曲诉讼现实时，法院倾向于采取强制性披露手段恢复市场认知。实务中需关注此类“纠正性宣传”命令的适用门槛及其对被告商业声誉的具体影响，监测后续类似案件中法院对发布范围与期限的裁量标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants involved in European patent disputes must carefully coordinate legal strategies with public relations efforts. Making explicit non-infringement claims or asserting confidence in product legality while litigation is pending carries significant risk if the court ultimately finds infringement. Courts may view such statements as creating misleading public impressions that require direct correction through mandatory publication of adverse judgments. Companies should establish robust internal protocols ensuring that all external marketing and communication materials are reviewed by legal counsel. Failure to align public messaging with legal realities can result in additional reputational damage and specific judicial sanctions, such as forced disclosure of unfavorable rulings on corporate platforms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC杜塞尔多夫分院依据UPCA第80条，有权责令侵权方自费传播判决信息以消除误导", "被告曾在官网宣称产品合法非侵权，法院认定该行为造成公众误解需直接纠正", "法院除允许原告在行业期刊刊登判决外，更责令被告在官网发布判决主文一个月"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Düsseldorf Local Division mandates defendants to publish the judgment on their website to correct misleading non-infringement claims made during litigation.", "The court invoked Article 80 UPCA to order public dissemination of the decision as a restorative measure, ensuring accurate information reaches affected customers.", "This ruling reinforces the Unified Patent Court's judicial power to compel infringers to disclose information and rectify false public perceptions regarding patent validity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC展现出利用公开判决纠正被告误导性公关声明的司法倾向，强化透明度要求", "依据UPCA第80条的纠正性措施不仅限于传统媒体，已延伸至被告自有数字平台", "需持续观察法院在判定“公众误解”程度及决定发布期限时的具体考量因素"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid issuing definitive non-infringement marketing claims during active litigation to prevent potential orders for mandatory publication of adverse judgments.", "Practice note: Implement strict coordination mechanisms between legal and marketing departments to ensure all public statements remain compliant with ongoing legal proceedings.", "What to monitor: Watch for further applications of Article 80 UPCA by UPC divisions, particularly regarding the scope of corrective measures for misleading corporate communication…"]
core_points_en: UPC Düsseldorf Local Division mandates defendants to publish the judgment on their website to correct misleading non-infringement claims made during litigation.
The court invoked Article 80 UPCA to order public dissemination of the decision as a restorative measure, ensuring accurate information reaches affected customers.
This ruling reinforces the Unified Patent Court's judicial power to compel infringers to disclose information and rectify false public perceptions regarding patent validity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid issuing definitive non-infringement marketing claims during active litigation to prevent potential orders for mandatory publication of adverse judgments.
Practice note: Implement strict coordination mechanisms between legal and marketing departments to ensure all public statements remain compliant with ongoing legal proceedings.
What to monitor: Watch for further applications of Article 80 UPCA by UPC divisions, particularly regarding the scope of corrective measures for misleading corporate communication…

items.title: LD Mannheim, April 20, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1291/2026, Anti-Anti Suit Injunction Against “Interim Licence” Application Before Chinese Court
items.title_zh: 曼海姆地院就UPC案发布针对中国临时许可申请的反反诉禁令
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Issues Anti-Anti-Suit Injunction Against Chinese Court Interim Licence
items.summary: The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to secure pending patent infringement proceedings before the UPC (Art. 31, 32(1)(c), 33(1)(a) UPCA) (mn. 16 et seq.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）有权签发反反诉禁令以保全 pending 侵权诉讼。全球“临时许可”若旨在阻止专利权人在外国（含UPC）行使权利，实质构成反诉禁令。曼海姆地院认定此类措施违反国际民事诉讼基本原则，且非保护中国费率设定所必需，故支持签发禁令以维护UPC管辖权及专利执行权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Local Division in Mannheim ruled that it possesses jurisdiction to issue an anti-anti-suit injunction (AASI) to protect pending patent infringement proceedings under Articles 31, 32(1)(c), and 33(1)(a) of the UPC Agreement. The Court determined that applying for a foreign injunction to prevent a patentee from enforcing rights within UPC Contracting States constitutes imminent infringement. Consequently, a worldwide 'Interim Licence' granted by a Chinese court was characterized as a de facto anti-suit injunction designed to block injunctive relief in foreign forums, including the UPC. This ruling reinforces the UPC’s authority to secure its judicial remedies against parallel foreign proceedings that undermine its jurisdiction in standard-essential patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确UPC将全球“临时许可”定性为变相反诉禁令，强调专利执行权是专利权核心。实务中需关注UPC与成员国法院在跨境SEP禁令冲突中的管辖权协调，以及对中国法院保全措施在欧洲的承认与执行界限。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision signals that reliance on foreign rate-setting orders or interim licences to halt UPC litigation is increasingly ineffective. Parties involved in cross-border SEP disputes should reassess strategies that directly challenge UPC jurisdiction through parallel foreign proceedings. Instead, focusing on robust FRAND compliance defenses and engaging in good-faith settlement negotiations may offer more viable pathways to resolve conflicts. Ignoring this shift risks severe financial sanctions and potential market access restrictions due to non-compliance with UPC orders, necessitating a recalibration of global litigation tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC依据UPCA第31、32条有权签发反反诉禁令，以保全pending侵权诉讼中的专…", "旨在阻止专利权人在UPC等外国论坛寻求禁令的全球“临时许可”，实质构成反诉禁令", "平行向中国法院申请反反诉禁令不产生未决诉讼效力，因涉及不同知识产权主张"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to safeguard pending infringement actions under Articles 31, 32, and 33 UPCA.", "Foreign applications aiming to block UPC enforcement constitute imminent infringement justifying interim relief measures.", "A Chinese global interim licence is substantively an anti-suit injunction violating international civil procedure principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC倾向于将阻碍其管辖范围内专利执行的境外保全措施视为侵权威胁", "需持续观察UPC在处理涉及中国法院费率设定或禁令时的司法礼让边界", "企业在多国并行诉讼时，需注意境外“临时许可”策略在欧盟境内的法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Global SEP holders must recognize that foreign interim licences will not automatically stay UPC infringement proceedings.", "Practice note: Avoid direct jurisdictional confrontations via foreign courts; prioritize substantive FRAND arguments within the UPC framework.", "What to monitor: Enforcement mechanisms for UPC AASIs and potential financial penalties for non-compliance with such orders."]
core_points_en: The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to safeguard pending infringement actions under Articles 31, 32, and 33 UPCA.
Foreign applications aiming to block UPC enforcement constitute imminent infringement justifying interim relief measures.
A Chinese global interim licence is substantively an anti-suit injunction violating international civil procedure principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Global SEP holders must recognize that foreign interim licences will not automatically stay UPC infringement proceedings.
Practice note: Avoid direct jurisdictional confrontations via foreign courts; prioritize substantive FRAND arguments within the UPC framework.
What to monitor: Enforcement mechanisms for UPC AASIs and potential financial penalties for non-compliance with such orders.
