items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 27, 2026, Decision, UPC_CoA_409/2025, UPC_CoA_410/2025, UPC_CoA_420/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院裁定登录系统不构成应诉管辖
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: CMS Login Does Not Constitute Entering an Appearance Under Brussels Ia
items.summary: Logging into the UPC's Case Management System does not constitute an "entering of an appearance" to establish jurisdiction under the Brussels Ia Regulation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在涉及 NUC Electronics 与 Hurom 的案件中裁定，被告仅登录案件管理系统查看文件，不构成《布鲁塞尔条例 Ia》意义上的“出庭应诉”。法院指出，确立管辖权需被告采取实质性步骤，如提交初步异议或答辩状，单纯访问不足以视为对法院管辖权的自愿接受或争议确认。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal ruled that merely logging into the Case Management System to access case files does not amount to 'entering an appearance' under Article 26(1) of the Brussels Ia Regulation. The decision clarifies that passive file access lacks the deliberate choice required to establish jurisdiction. Jurisdiction is only confirmed when a defendant takes active procedural steps, such as filing a Preliminary Objection regarding jurisdiction or submitting a Statement of Defence on the merits. This distinction prevents accidental submission to UPC jurisdiction through routine administrative access.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，切勿因登录查看案情而误判为已应诉。应严格把控首次提交文件的时机与内容，避免因程序性操作不当导致丧失管辖权异议权利，需由专业律师评估后采取行动。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity for rights holders and defendants managing cross-border patent disputes within the UPC framework. It confirms that preliminary case assessment activities, such as reviewing documents in the digital system, do not inadvertently waive jurisdictional objections. Legal teams can safely monitor proceedings without fear of accidental submission, provided they refrain from filing substantive responses before deciding on their jurisdictional strategy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅登录 UPC 系统查看文件不视为法律意义上的出庭应诉行为", "确立管辖权需提交初步异议或实体答辩等实质性诉讼步骤", "明确区分被动查阅与主动抗辩，防止管辖权被意外确立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Passive access to UPC case files via the Case Management System does not constitute entering an appearance under Brussels Ia Regulation.", "Establishing jurisdiction requires active procedural steps, specifically filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence.", "The ruling distinguishes between mere file inspection and the deliberate legal choice to submit to the court's international jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["登录系统查阅卷宗不会触发管辖权，企业可安全进行初步评估", "首次提交异议或答辩状是确立管辖的关键节点，须谨慎决策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can review UPC case materials without risking accidental acceptance of the court's jurisdiction under EU regulations.", "Practice note: Ensure no substantive filings are made until a formal decision on jurisdictional challenges or defense strategy is finalized.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard pleadings that might trigger jurisdictional consent."]
core_points_en: Passive access to UPC case files via the Case Management System does not constitute entering an appearance under Brussels Ia Regulation.
Establishing jurisdiction requires active procedural steps, specifically filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence.
The ruling distinguishes between mere file inspection and the deliberate legal choice to submit to the court's international jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can review UPC case materials without risking accidental acceptance of the court's jurisdiction under EU regulations.
Practice note: Ensure no substantive filings are made until a formal decision on jurisdictional challenges or defense strategy is finalized.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard pleadings that might trigger jurisdictional consent.

items.title: InterDigital, Dolby, Nokia fuelling spike in AV1 litigation
items.title_zh: InterDigital、杜比和诺基亚推高AV1专利诉讼激增
items.title_en: InterDigital, Dolby, Nokia fuelling spike in AV1 litigation
items.summary: US district court lawsuits over the so-called royalty-free video codec specification reached an all-time high in 2025 and these cases come with intriguing legal arguments including lack of FRAND, higher damages and potential injunctions
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年针对所谓免版税视频编码标准AV1的美国地区法院诉讼达到历史新高。InterDigital、杜比和诺基亚等权利人发起诉讼，主张缺乏FRAND许可、要求更高损害赔偿并寻求潜在禁令。尽管AV1标榜免版税，但权利人的法律行动揭示了标准实施中的知识产权冲突，凸显了即使在被视为开放的标准中，SEP持有者仍积极通过司法途径维护其商业利益。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音视频企业需警惕“免版税”标准的法律陷阱。AV1诉讼激增表明SEP持有人正利用FRAND争议扩大索赔范围。建议出口企业重新评估AV1合规策略，提前进行FTO分析，并在许可谈判中做好应对禁令和高额赔偿的准备，避免被动卷入跨国IP纠纷。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年AV1相关美国诉讼创历史新高，打破免版税预期", "InterDigital等巨头主张缺乏FRAND许可及高额赔偿", "案件涉及潜在禁令，凸显SEP在开放标准中的博弈"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕AV1“免版税”背后的SEP诉讼风险", "加强FRAND合规审查以应对高额赔偿诉求"]

items.title: Licensing negotiation groups under scrutiny: cartel risk or analytical drift?
items.title_zh: 许可谈判组受审视：卡特尔风险还是分析偏差？
items.title_en: Scrutinizing SEP Licensing Groups: Cartel Risk or Analytical Drift in Competition Law?
items.summary: Saturday opinion: Treating implementer coordination in SEP licensing as cartel conduct risks “analytical drift”, where antitrust becomes a proxy for trade or industrial policy rather than a disciplined competition analysis
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评论指出，将实施者在标准必要专利（SEP）许可中的协调行为直接视为卡特尔行为，可能导致“分析偏差”。这种偏差使得反垄断执法沦为贸易或产业政策的代理工具，而非基于严谨的竞争分析。文章强调应区分合法的集体谈判与非法的共谋，避免过度干预市场机制，确保反垄断法在SEP领域的适用保持专业性和纪律性，以平衡专利权人与实施者的利益。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary argues that classifying implementer coordination in standard-essential patent licensing as cartel conduct risks significant analytical drift. Such an approach may transform antitrust enforcement into a proxy for trade or industrial policy, rather than maintaining a disciplined competition analysis. The article emphasizes the critical need to distinguish between legitimate collective bargaining and illegal collusion. By avoiding excessive intervention in market mechanisms, regulators can ensure that antitrust laws remain professional and rigorous within the SEP sector. This balanced perspective is essential for protecting the interests of both patent holders and implementers, ensuring that competition law serves its intended purpose without being distorted by broader policy objectives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临SEP许可时，可探索联合谈判以增强议价能力，但需严格合规以避免反垄断调查。建议建立内部合规审查机制，确保谈判策略符合欧盟竞争法，避免被认定为价格固定或市场分割。同时，密切关注欧盟相关立法动态，灵活调整应对策略，维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in joint negotiations for SEP licenses must navigate strict compliance requirements to avoid triggering EU antitrust investigations. Establishing robust internal compliance review mechanisms is essential to ensure that negotiation strategies align with European competition law standards. Parties should carefully structure their interactions to avoid any appearance of price-fixing or market allocation. Furthermore, continuous monitoring of evolving EU legislative dynamics regarding SEP regulation is necessary. This allows stakeholders to adapt their strategies proactively, safeguarding their legal rights while maintaining operational flexibility in complex licensing environments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["警惕将SEP实施者协调简单定性为卡特尔，防止反垄断分析出现偏差", "反对利用反垄断法作为贸易或产业政策的替代工具，主张回归竞争本质", "强调需进行严谨的竞争分析，以区分合法集体谈判与非法垄断协议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Avoid simplistically characterizing implementer coordination in SEP licensing as cartel behavior to prevent analytical drift.", "Reject using antitrust law as a substitute for trade or industrial policy, advocating instead for a return to core competition principles.", "Conduct rigorous competition analysis to clearly differentiate between lawful collective negotiations and illegal monopolistic agreements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["联合谈判需谨慎合规，严防触犯欧盟反垄断红线", "建立内部审查机制，确保SEP许可策略符合竞争法要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Joint SEP negotiations require careful compliance to strictly avoid crossing EU antitrust red lines.", "Practice note: Implement internal review mechanisms to ensure SEP licensing strategies fully comply with competition law requirements.", "What to monitor: Track developments in EU legislation and enforcement trends regarding collective bargaining in SEP contexts."]

items.title: Accord PPH avec le Canada
items.title_zh: 法国工业产权局与加拿大签署专利审查高速路协议
items.title_en: France and Canada Launch Patent Prosecution Highway Pilot to Accelerate Cross-Border Examination
items.summary: Accord PPH avec le Canada llegrand@inpi.fr 03 avril 2026 Les conditions pour utiliser le PPH Les déposants peuvent demander l'accélération du traitement de leur demande auprès de l'un des deux offices à partir du moment où elle aura été examinée par l'autre office, indépendamment de l'ordre chronologique des dépôts et des revendications de priorité. De manière classique, après dépôt d'une requête PPH, le traitement d'une demande de brevet déposée à l'INPI sous priorité d'une demande déposée à l'
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局（INPI）宣布与加拿大知识产权局（OPIC）达成专利审查高速路（PPH）协议。申请人可基于一方对权利要求的可专利性肯定评价，请求另一方加速处理对应申请。该机制不依赖申请或优先权的 chronological 顺序，旨在缩短审查周期，提升跨国专利申请效率。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) has announced the establishment of a Patent Prosecution Highway (PPH) pilot program with the Canadian Intellectual Property Office (CIPO). This agreement allows applicants to request accelerated examination in either jurisdiction once the corresponding application has been examined by the other office, regardless of the chronological order of filings or priority claims. To qualify, the claims in the second filing must correspond to those deemed patentable in the first office, meaning they must be of similar or narrower scope. The initiative aims to enhance the efficiency of cross-border patent grants. CIPO data indicates that utilizing the PPH mechanism can reduce the total grant procedure timeline by an average of twenty-three months. This bilateral cooperation provides a structured pathway for faster patent issuance in both European and North American markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该协议为在法加两国布局专利的企业提供了程序性便利。通过利用一方审查结果加速另一方进程，申请人可更灵活地管理全球专利组合的审查节奏。需关注INPI后续发布的具体操作指南及案件对应性认定标准，以评估实际加速效果。
items.ai_insight_en: This PPH agreement offers strategic advantages for entities managing patent portfolios across France and Canada. By leveraging mutual recognition of examination results, applicants can significantly reduce waiting times and associated costs in both jurisdictions. Practitioners should consider filing strategies that prioritize offices with faster examination timelines to secure early allowance, subsequently triggering PPH acceleration in the counterpart office. This approach optimizes the speed of global patent acquisition. Stakeholders should monitor specific procedural requirements and claim mapping standards to ensure eligibility. Effective use of this mechanism requires careful coordination between international filings to maximize the benefits of accelerated examination pathways.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INPI与OPIC建立PPH合作，允许基于对方可专利性认定请求加速审查", "加速请求不受申请提交顺序或优先权主张顺序的限制，双向均可适用", "OPIC数据显示，PPH请求可使加拿大专利授权程序平均缩短约23个月"]

Claim consistency is required; accelerated treatment depends on claims being identical or narrower than those already accepted as patentable by the examining office.
Significant time savings are projected, with the PPH mechanism expected to shorten the overall examination and grant cycle by approximately twenty-three months.

items.title: Ericsson, InterDigital, Nokia and Qualcomm provide insights into 6G – and what it could mean for patent licensing
items.title_zh: 爱立信等解析6G专利许可前景
items.title_en: Ericsson, Nokia, InterDigital, Qualcomm Discuss 6G Impact on SEP Licensing at IPBC Europe
items.summary: New services, devices and verticals – 6G is set to stir up SEP value creation said panellists at IPBC Europe 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 在IPBC Europe 2026会议上，爱立信、InterDigital、诺基亚和高通专家探讨6G技术对标准必要专利（SEP）价值创造的影响。与会者指出，随着新服务、设备及垂直行业的涌现，6G将显著改变SEP许可格局。会议聚焦于技术演进如何驱动新的许可模式及价值分配，为行业提供关于未来通信标准知识产权商业化路径的关键洞察。
items.ai_summary_en: At IPBC Europe 2026, experts from Ericsson, InterDigital, Nokia, and Qualcomm examined how sixth-generation (6G) technology will reshape standard-essential patent (SEP) value creation. Panelists emphasized that the emergence of new services, devices, and vertical industries will significantly alter the current licensing landscape. The discussions focused on how technological evolution drives novel licensing models and value distribution mechanisms. These insights provide critical guidance for the industry regarding the commercialization pathways of intellectual property in future communication standards. Stakeholders are advised to consider these shifts as they prepare for the complex interplay between technical innovation and legal frameworks governing FRAND commitments in the next generation of connectivity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注6G SEP许可范式变化，提前布局相关专利组合以增强谈判筹码。鉴于新垂直行业涌入，建议评估跨界许可风险，建立灵活的FRAND应对策略，避免在下一代技术标准中陷入被动局面。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving paradigm of 6G SEP licensing to anticipate changes in negotiation dynamics. Proactively building robust patent portfolios is essential to strengthen bargaining positions in future licensing discussions. Given the influx of new vertical industries into the telecommunications ecosystem, companies must assess cross-industry licensing risks carefully. Establishing flexible FRAND response strategies will help mitigate potential disputes and avoid passive positioning in next-generation technical standards. This proactive approach ensures better alignment with emerging commercial realities and legal expectations surrounding standard-essential patents.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G新技术与垂直应用将重塑SEP价值创造逻辑", "主流通信巨头在IPBC Europe探讨未来许可趋势", "新服务模式可能引发SEP许可规则的重大调整"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New 6G technologies and vertical applications are expected to fundamentally reshape the logic of SEP value creation.", "Major communication giants discussed future licensing trends and strategic implications during IPBC Europe sessions.", "Emerging service models may trigger significant adjustments to existing SEP licensing rules and frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前布局6G专利以增强未来许可谈判筹码", "评估跨界许可风险并建立灵活FRAND应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Build early 6G patent portfolios to enhance leverage in future licensing negotiations.", "Practice note: Evaluate cross-industry licensing risks and develop flexible FRAND compliance strategies.", "What to monitor: Shifts in SEP licensing paradigms driven by new verticals and service models."]
core_points_en: New 6G technologies and vertical applications are expected to fundamentally reshape the logic of SEP value creation.
Major communication giants discussed future licensing trends and strategic implications during IPBC Europe sessions.
Emerging service models may trigger significant adjustments to existing SEP licensing rules and frameworks.

items.title: European patent offices raise official fees
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局及英国知识产权局上调官方费用
items.title_en: EPO and UKIPO Implement Official Fee Increases, Ending Long-Standing Price Freezes
items.summary: Two of Europe’s most important IP offices have implemented fee increases this month, with some long-standing UK charges, dating back to the late 1990s, finally seeing a rise.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲两个最重要的知识产权机构本月实施了费用上调。其中，英国部分长期未变的收费项目（可追溯至20世纪90年代末）终于迎来上涨。此举标志着欧洲主要法域专利维持与申请成本的结构性调整。
items.ai_summary_en: Two of Europe’s most significant intellectual property offices, the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), have officially implemented increases to their official fees this month. Notably, certain UK charges that had remained unchanged since the late 1990s are seeing their first adjustment in over two decades. These fee revisions reflect the growing operational costs required to maintain the intellectual property system. Consequently, applicants and rights holders will face higher expenditures for patent applications and maintenance within the European region. Market participants operating in or targeting Europe should closely monitor these changes, as they directly impact budget planning and portfolio management strategies across both jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注具体费率表更新及对存量专利年费预算的影响。建议持续追踪各局官方公告，以获取详细的费用生效日期及过渡期安排，确保合规缴费。
items.ai_insight_en: The simultaneous fee hikes by major European offices signal a structural shift in IP administration costs. Rights holders should immediately review existing patent portfolios to assess cost-benefit ratios, particularly for non-core or low-value assets where abandonment may optimize budgets. For new filings, teams must recalculate return on investment projections to account for sustained higher fees. Integrating these increased costs into long-term strategic planning is essential to manage financial exposure effectively while maintaining robust protection across key European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲两大重要知识产权局已于本月正式实施官方费用上调政策", "英国部分自20世纪90年代末以来长期保持不变的收费标准此次迎来上涨"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO and UKIPO have simultaneously implemented a new round of official fee increases for patent procedures.", "Specific UK patent fee items have ended a freeze lasting more than twenty years with formal price adjustments.", "These adjustments will directly increase corporate costs for patent applications and maintenance in Europe."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Immediate screening of European patent portfolios is needed to identify and drop low-value patents to offset rising costs.", "Practice note: Re-evaluate new application budgets and incorporate official fee growth into long-term intellectual property strategic planning.", "What to monitor: Future announcements from EPO and UKIPO regarding potential further adjustments or transitional provisions for pending cases."]

De manière classique, après dépôt d'une requête PPH, le traitement d'une demande de brevet déposée à l'INPI et revendiquant la priorité d'une de
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局（INPI）宣布与美国专利商标局（USPTO）建立专利审查高速路（PPH）机制。申请人若其申请已获一方办公室认定为可授权，可请求另一方加速审查，不受申请顺序限制。该程序在法国免费，旨在缩短审查周期，美国平均九个月内发出审查意见，要求权利要求范围相同或更窄。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) has announced a Patent Prosecution Highway (PPH) agreement with the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This mechanism allows applicants to request accelerated examination in either jurisdiction once the corresponding application has been examined by the other office, regardless of filing chronology or priority claims. Specifically, if the USPTO deems certain claims patentable, an INPI application with similar or narrower claims may qualify for fast-track processing. Conversely, INPI can accelerate examination for USPTO applications claiming priority from an INPI filing deemed patentable. The PPH request is free of charge in France, while the USPTO typically issues a patentability opinion within nine months under this program.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在法美同时布局专利时，应充分利用此免费加速通道。建议优先在美或法获取有利审查结果，随即启动 PPH 请求，以低成本快速获得双方法域专利权，提升海外维权与市场准入效率。
items.ai_insight_en: This agreement provides a cost-effective pathway for rights holders managing parallel patent portfolios in France and the United States. By leveraging positive examination outcomes from one office, applicants can potentially shorten the pendency period in the other jurisdiction without incurring additional official fees for the acceleration request. This alignment supports more predictable prosecution timelines for transatlantic intellectual property strategies.

Claims in the second-filed application must be sufficiently similar or narrower than those accepted as patentable by the first examining office.
PPH requests at INPI are free of charge, aiming to reduce procedural delays compared to standard examination timelines.

items.title: Samsung brands $1 billion InterDigital patent deal ‘outlandish’, ‘unsustainable’
items.title_zh: 三星斥 InterDigital 十亿美元专利交易荒谬且不可持续
items.title_en: Samsung Labels $1 Billion InterDigital SEP Deal Outlandish Amid Rising Royalty Costs
items.summary: The South Korean phone maker issued a rare public statement about the SEP licensing environment, as its financial reports reveal hundreds of millions of dollars in royalty payments each year
items.ai_summary_zh: 韩国手机制造商三星发布罕见公开声明，抨击与 InterDigital 达成的十亿美元专利许可协议“荒谬”且“不可持续”。此举背景是三星财报显示其每年需支付数亿美元特许权使用费。该事件凸显了当前标准必要专利（SEP）许可环境中，实施者与权利人之间关于 FRAND 费率及累积许可成本的激烈博弈，可能引发后续法律或谈判行动。
items.ai_summary_en: Samsung has issued a rare public statement criticizing the standard essential patent licensing environment, specifically branding its recent one billion dollar agreement with InterDigital as outlandish and unsustainable. This move follows revelations in Samsung’s financial reports that it pays hundreds of millions of dollars annually in royalty fees. The dispute highlights the intense friction between implementers and rights holders regarding fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory rates and cumulative licensing costs. As major technology firms face escalating financial pressures from patent portfolios, this high-profile conflict underscores the growing challenges in enforcing FRAND principles globally. It signals potential for further legal actions or renegotiations, reflecting broader market tensions over the valuation and aggregation of SEP royalties in the telecommunications sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国手机厂商在欧洲常面临类似高额 SEP 索偿。应建立专利费累积预警机制，积极参与全球费率诉讼以确立合理基准，避免被动接受不合理条款，同时加强自身专利储备以增加谈判筹码。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe and the US should closely monitor how high-value SEP disputes influence judicial interpretations of FRAND commitments. Implementers facing cumulative royalty demands must establish robust internal mechanisms to track and forecast total licensing liabilities, preventing cost overruns. Active participation in global rate-setting litigation is crucial to establishing favorable precedents and avoiding passive acceptance of potentially unreasonable terms. Strengthening proprietary patent portfolios remains a key strategy to enhance bargaining power during negotiations. Rights holders should also anticipate increased scrutiny on aggregate royalty burdens, which may shape future licensing strategies and regulatory approaches across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["三星公开谴责十亿美元级 SEP 许可交易缺乏合理性", "财报揭示巨头每年巨额专利费支出，凸显成本压力", "全球 SEP 许可环境紧张，FRAND 原则执行面临新挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Samsung publicly condemned the one billion dollar SEP licensing transaction with InterDigital as lacking commercial reasonableness and sustainability.", "Financial disclosures reveal massive annual royalty expenditures, highlighting significant cost pressures faced by major device manufacturers.", "Global SEP licensing tensions are rising, presenting new challenges to the consistent application and enforcement of FRAND principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利费累积预警，防范多重收费导致成本失控", "主动参与全球费率诉讼，争取有利 FRAND 裁决基准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implementers must develop early warning systems for cumulative patent fees to prevent uncontrolled cost escalation from multiple licensors.", "Practice note: Proactively engage in global rate-setting litigation to secure favorable FRAND benchmarks rather than accepting unilateral demands.", "What to monitor: Watch for shifts in judicial attitudes toward aggregate royalty caps and the impact of public corporate statements on negotiation dynamics."]
core_points_en: Samsung publicly condemned the one billion dollar SEP licensing transaction with InterDigital as lacking commercial reasonableness and sustainability.
Financial disclosures reveal massive annual royalty expenditures, highlighting significant cost pressures faced by major device manufacturers.
Global SEP licensing tensions are rising, presenting new challenges to the consistent application and enforcement of FRAND principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implementers must develop early warning systems for cumulative patent fees to prevent uncontrolled cost escalation from multiple licensors.
Practice note: Proactively engage in global rate-setting litigation to secure favorable FRAND benchmarks rather than accepting unilateral demands.
What to monitor: Watch for shifts in judicial attitudes toward aggregate royalty caps and the impact of public corporate statements on negotiation dynamics.

items.title_zh: 法定指南：One IPO数字服务使用方向
items.title_en: UKIPO Statutory Directions on Document Delivery via One IPO Digital Services
items.summary: Directions under section 124A of the Patents Act 1977 for the form and manner in which documents can be delivered using the One IPO Digital Services.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布依据《1977年专利法》第124A条制定的法定指南。该指南规定了通过“One IPO”数字服务交付专利相关文件的具体形式和方式，旨在规范数字化提交流程。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office has issued statutory directions pursuant to section 124A of the Patents Act 1977. These directions formally establish the prescribed form and manner for delivering documents through the One IPO Digital Services platform. This regulatory update provides the necessary legal framework governing electronic submissions, ensuring that all digital interactions with the Office comply with current statutory requirements. The guidance clarifies acceptable formats and procedural standards for patent-related filings, thereby enhancing operational clarity for users relying on digital channels for official communications and document transmission within the UK patent system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此为英国专利程序数字化转型的合规性文件。实务中需关注One IPO平台对文件格式、传输协议的具体技术标准，确保专利申请及后续程序文件的提交符合法定形式要求，避免因格式瑕疵导致程序延误。
items.ai_insight_en: This statutory instrument formalizes the technical and procedural standards for digital patent filings in the UK. For in-house counsel and IP practitioners, adherence to these specified forms is critical to ensure valid service of documents and avoid procedural defects. The directions reinforce the shift towards mandatory digital engagement, requiring teams to verify their submission workflows against the new standards. Monitoring compliance with these specific delivery protocols is essential to maintain the integrity of patent prosecution timelines and ensure seamless interaction with the UKIPO's digital infrastructure.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["指南依据《1977年专利法》第124A条制定，具有法定效力", "明确规定了使用One IPO数字服务交付文件的形式与方式要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Statutory directions issued under section 124A of the Patents Act 1977 govern document delivery procedures.", "The guidance specifies the required form and manner for submitting documents via One IPO Digital Services.", "These rules provide a clear legal framework for electronic filings and official communications with the UKIPO."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需核实One IPO平台支持的具体文件格式及大小限制", "关注数字化提交与传统纸质提交在法律效力上的等同性确认", "监测UKIPO后续是否针对特定类型专利文件发布补充技术指引"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all patent document submissions via One IPO adhere to the newly prescribed forms to prevent rejection or delays.", "Practice note: Update internal filing checklists and automated systems to reflect the specific technical requirements outlined in the section 124A directions.", "What to monitor: Future updates from the UKIPO regarding additional digital services or modifications to the accepted document formats under this statutory framework."]
core_points_en: Statutory directions issued under section 124A of the Patents Act 1977 govern document delivery procedures.
The guidance specifies the required form and manner for submitting documents via One IPO Digital Services.
These rules provide a clear legal framework for electronic filings and official communications with the UKIPO.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all patent document submissions via One IPO adhere to the newly prescribed forms to prevent rejection or delays.
Practice note: Update internal filing checklists and automated systems to reflect the specific technical requirements outlined in the section 124A directions.
What to monitor: Future updates from the UKIPO regarding additional digital services or modifications to the accepted document formats under this statutory framework.

items.title: Guidance: Apply for a patent, search and examination, and add inventors
items.title_zh: 指南：专利申请、检索审查及增加发明人
items.title_en: UKIPO Guidance: Patent Application, Search, Examination, and Adding Inventors
items.summary: Apply for a patent, search and examination. Add inventors to your patent.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布官方指南，涵盖在英国申请专利的流程、检索与审查阶段的操作规范，以及如何在已提交的专利申请中增加发明人的具体程序要求。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has released updated official guidance detailing the complete procedure for securing patent protection in the United Kingdom. This comprehensive document outlines the standard administrative steps required from the initial filing of a patent application through to the initiation of search and substantive examination processes. Furthermore, it provides specific operational norms for legally adding or correcting inventor details on existing applications. The guidance aims to offer applicants clear direction on navigating these essential bureaucratic requirements, ensuring that all procedural aspects are handled correctly to maintain the validity and enforceability of their intellectual property rights within the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 作为程序性指引，该文件明确了UKIPO对申请流程及著录项目变更（如增加发明人）的行政要求。实务中需关注此类程序细节以确保合规，避免因形式缺陷影响申请进程。
items.ai_insight_en: Entities seeking patent protection in the UK must strictly adhere to UKIPO procedural regulations, particularly regarding inventor attribution, which is often overlooked but carries significant legal weight. Familiarity with this updated guidance is essential to prevent formal defects that could compromise rights stability or cause unnecessary examination delays. Ensuring strict compliance with these administrative standards helps safeguard intellectual property assets and maintains the robustness of patent portfolios against potential validity challenges arising from procedural non-compliance during the prosecution phase.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["指南涵盖英国专利申请的基本流程及相关操作规范", "包含专利检索与实质审查阶段的具体指导信息", "说明了在专利申请过程中增加发明人的程序要求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Clarifies the standard workflow and examination mechanisms governing patent applications filed with the UK Intellectual Property Office.", "Details specific operational requirements and procedural steps during the critical search and substantive examination phases.", "Establishes formal procedures for adding or modifying inventor information after the initial patent application has been submitted."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确英国专利申请及审查阶段的标准化操作流程", "规范专利申请中增加发明人的行政程序与合规要求", "持续关注UKIPO关于申请程序细节的最新官方指引更新"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to UKIPO formal compliance requirements is critical to avoid procedural defects that may jeopardize patent validity or delay grant.", "Practice note: Promptly address any necessary changes to inventor details in accordance with the new guidance to ensure accurate record-keeping and legal standing.", "What to monitor: Ongoing updates to UKIPO administrative procedures, specifically concerning inventorship corrections and substantive examination timelines."]
core_points_en: Clarifies the standard workflow and examination mechanisms governing patent applications filed with the UK Intellectual Property Office.
Details specific operational requirements and procedural steps during the critical search and substantive examination phases.
Establishes formal procedures for adding or modifying inventor information after the initial patent application has been submitted.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to UKIPO formal compliance requirements is critical to avoid procedural defects that may jeopardize patent validity or delay grant.
Practice note: Promptly address any necessary changes to inventor details in accordance with the new guidance to ensure accurate record-keeping and legal standing.
What to monitor: Ongoing updates to UKIPO administrative procedures, specifically concerning inventorship corrections and substantive examination timelines.

items.title: Form: Request for an uncertified copy of a patent
items.title_zh: 表格：申请专利未认证副本
items.title_en: UKIPO Form for Requesting Uncertified Patent Copies or Comptroller Certificates
items.summary: Use this form to request a certificate of the Comptroller or an uncertified copy from a file or the register.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布专用表格，供公众向主计长申请专利证书或从档案及注册簿中获取未经认证的专利副本。该文件明确了行政申请流程，旨在规范专利文献的复制与分发服务，确保申请人及公众能合法、便捷地获取官方专利记录，属于基础行政程序指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published a specific form for requesting uncertified copies of patent documents from the register or file. This administrative tool also allows users to request a certificate issued by the Comptroller. The guidance outlines the procedural mechanism for accessing these official records, ensuring that applicants and the public can obtain necessary documentation through the designated channel.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This update clarifies the administrative pathway for retrieving uncertified patent copies and Comptroller certificates in the UK. For rights holders and legal practitioners, understanding the correct forms for document retrieval is essential for maintaining accurate portfolio records and preparing factual evidence. It ensures compliance with UKIPO procedural requirements when accessing official patent data.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["提供申请主计长证书或未认证副本的专用官方表格", "明确可从专利档案或注册簿中提取相关文件副本", "规范英国专利文献获取的行政程序与服务标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A dedicated form is available for requesting uncertified copies of patents from the UKIPO register or file.", "Users may also utilize this form to apply for an official certificate issued by the Comptroller of Patents.", "The publication standardizes the administrative process for obtaining specific patent-related documents and certifications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["掌握 UKIPO 表格体系可提升专利证据调取效率", "获取官方副本是应对英国专利纠纷的基础准备工作"]

core_points_en: A dedicated form is available for requesting uncertified copies of patents from the UKIPO register or file.
Users may also utilize this form to apply for an official certificate issued by the Comptroller of Patents.
The publication standardizes the administrative process for obtaining specific patent-related documents and certifications.

Practice note: Ensure use of the specific UKIPO form when requesting document copies or certificates to meet administrative standards.

items.title: New digital patents services have launched
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局推出新型数字专利服务
items.title_en: UKIPO Launches New Digital Services for UK Patent Applications and Management
items.summary: Today the IPO has launched a suite of new digital services to apply for, manage or renew UK patents.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）今日正式推出一套全新的数字服务系统。该系统旨在支持申请人及权利人在线完成英国专利的申请、管理及续展等全流程业务，以提升行政效率与服务便捷性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has officially launched a new suite of digital services designed to streamline the administration of UK patents. These online tools enable applicants and rights holders to apply for new patents, manage existing portfolio items, and process renewals digitally. This launch marks a shift toward fully digital interaction for core patent lifecycle events at the UKIPO. The announcement confirms the immediate availability of these platforms for public use, aiming to modernize the interface between the office and users. No detailed technical specifications or transitional arrangements were provided in this initial brief notice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于原文仅包含简短公告，缺乏关于系统具体功能、过渡期安排或操作指南的细节，目前仅能确认服务已上线。实务中需关注UKIPO后续是否发布针对新旧系统切换的操作指引或常见问题解答。
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a procedural shift for practitioners managing UK patent portfolios. As the UKIPO moves toward digital-first services, teams should verify that their internal workflows align with the new system requirements. Given the limited detail in the initial announcement, users should approach the transition cautiously. It is essential to confirm whether legacy submission methods remain temporarily available or if the digital channel is now mandatory for specific actions. Early adoption may reveal usability issues or documentation gaps that could impact filing accuracy during the initial rollout phase.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO已正式发布并上线一套新的数字服务套件", "新服务功能涵盖英国专利的申请、日常管理及权利续展"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO has launched a new suite of digital services for UK patent administration.", "The platform supports applying for, managing, and renewing UK patents online."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确认新数字服务平台已正式投入运行", "需观察后续是否有详细用户指南或系统维护通知发布"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must adapt to new digital interfaces for routine UK patent maintenance and filing tasks.", "Practice note: Verify current acceptance criteria for patent applications and renewals to ensure compliance with the new system.", "What to monitor: Watch for subsequent UKIPO guidance on transitional arrangements, technical FAQs, and potential service disruptions."]

The platform supports applying for, managing, and renewing UK patents online.

items.title: CJEU on continuity, heritage, and deceptive trademarks – Fauré Le Page Paris 1717
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定商标标注虚构年份可能构成欺骗
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Deception Threshold for Heritage Marks: Fauré Le Page Paris 1717
items.summary: To which extent can a sign invoke heritage, age, and prestige before it crosses into deception? In the luxury sector, a trademark’s reference to a long history can signal continuity, know-how, and quality, making the line between genuine legacy and misleading branding especially thin. On 26 March 2026, the CJEU drew that boundary by stating that the difference lay in whether a business had been acquired as a whole or whether only the trademark had changed hands (C‑412/24).
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 Fauré Le Page 案中明确，若企业仅受让商标而非整体业务，却在商标中加入暗示悠久历史的年份（如 1717），可能因误导消费者关于商品连续性、技艺及声望而构成欺骗。法院强调，欺骗须涉及商品特征，但在奢侈品领域，声誉形象属于关键特征。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C‑412/24, the Court of Justice of the European Union addressed whether a trademark incorporating a historical date constitutes deception under Article 3(1)(g) of Directive 2008/95/EC. The case concerned 'Fauré Le Page Paris 1717', filed by an entity that acquired only the trademark rights after the original business ceased operations in 1992. The CJEU held that using a date implying centuries of continuity can mislead consumers regarding the goods' characteristics, specifically heritage and know-how, when no actual business transfer occurred. The Court emphasized that while deception must relate to product characteristics, reputation and prestige are intrinsic features in the luxury sector, making such misleading indications sufficient to establish a risk of deception.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧注册含历史元素的奢侈品商标时，需确保商业实体与历史传承的真实性关联。若仅收购商标而无业务延续，应避免使用暗示悠久历史的年份或描述，以防被认定为欺骗性商标导致无效或被禁。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the boundary between legitimate brand heritage marketing and deceptive practices in the EU. Rights holders acquiring legacy marks must ensure that any reference to historical dates or longevity accurately reflects the continuity of the business entity and its know-how. Misrepresenting the origin or history of production capabilities, particularly in sectors where reputation drives consumer choice, poses significant validity risks. Practitioners should carefully assess the factual basis of historical claims embedded in new filings or rebranding efforts following asset-only acquisitions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅受让商标未承接整体业务时，标注历史年份易致欺骗", "奢侈品商标的声望形象被视为商品本身的重要特征", "虚构年份暗示技艺传承若无事实依据则违反商标指令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A trademark including a historical date may be deceptive if it falsely implies continuity of know-how from an original undertaking to the current owner.", "Deception under EU law requires misrepresentation of goods' characteristics; however, prestige and heritage are key characteristics in the luxury market.", "Acquiring only trademark rights without the underlying historic business does not justify claims of long-standing tradition or inherited craftsmanship."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["收购旧品牌商标时需核实是否包含整体业务资产", "避免在无实际传承时使用历史年份营销以防法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands acquiring legacy trademarks must verify if historical references in new marks align with actual business continuity to avoid invalidity for deception.", "Practice note: When filing marks with historical elements post-acquisition, distinguish between mere trademark ownership and the transfer of the underlying undertaking's goodwill…", "What to monitor: How national courts apply this CJEU standard to other luxury sectors where brand prestige and perceived heritage significantly influence consumer purchasing decis…"]
core_points_en: A trademark including a historical date may be deceptive if it falsely implies continuity of know-how from an original undertaking to the current owner.
Deception under EU law requires misrepresentation of goods' characteristics; however, prestige and heritage are key characteristics in the luxury market.
Acquiring only trademark rights without the underlying historic business does not justify claims of long-standing tradition or inherited craftsmanship.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands acquiring legacy trademarks must verify if historical references in new marks align with actual business continuity to avoid invalidity for deception.
Practice note: When filing marks with historical elements post-acquisition, distinguish between mere trademark ownership and the transfer of the underlying undertaking's goodwill…
What to monitor: How national courts apply this CJEU standard to other luxury sectors where brand prestige and perceived heritage significantly influence consumer purchasing decis…

items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in March 2026
items.title_zh: 2026年3月商标新闻综述：欧盟执法与全球动态
items.title_en: Global Trademark Update: EU Anti-Counterfeit Operations and Key Procedural Changes in March 2026
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.Argentina, U.S. ink trade-related pact with IP reform commitmentsOn 5 February 2026, Argentina and the United States signed the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade and Investment, which includes commitments by Argentina to modernise its intellectual property (IP) framework.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总2026年3月全球知识产权动态。欧洲方面，欧盟刑警组织在EUIPO支持下开展“SHIELD VI”行动，查获价值3200万欧元的假冒药品；欧盟理事会通过实施欧盟-南方共同市场协议中农产品双边保障条款的法规。国际方面涵盖阿根廷、巴林、巴西、智利、香港及印度的商标或工业产权程序更新。
items.ai_summary_en: This update compiles significant global trademark developments from early 2026, with a focus on European enforcement and international procedural shifts. A major highlight is Operation SHIELD VI, an Europol-led initiative supported by the EUIPO, which seized counterfeit medicines and doping substances valued at EUR 32 million across thirty European countries. The summary also covers Argentina’s commitment to modernize its IP framework under a new trade agreement with the United States. Additionally, it details regulatory updates in Brazil, Chile, India, and Hong Kong regarding examination procedures, classification standards, and administrative relocations. These changes reflect ongoing efforts to streamline industrial property systems and enhance cross-border intellectual property protection mechanisms for rights holders operating in diverse jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧洲层面持续强化跨境知识产权执法协作，特别是针对假冒药品的打击力度。同时，欧盟贸易政策与知识产权保护的联动性增强，企业需关注欧盟-南方共同市场协议带来的合规变化。全球范围内，主要司法辖区正加速商标审查程序的数字化与标准化，申请人应留意各国分类及优先权程序的最新调整。
items.ai_insight_en: The substantial seizure of counterfeit pharmaceuticals underscores the intensifying focus on public health within IP enforcement strategies. Rights holders in the healthcare sector should align their anti-counterfeiting measures with these coordinated law enforcement efforts. Furthermore, the procedural harmonization in key emerging markets like Brazil and Chile suggests a trend toward greater administrative efficiency. Companies must monitor these jurisdictional updates to ensure compliance with new filing requirements and classification standards, thereby maintaining robust portfolio management and minimizing procedural risks during prosecution and enforcement phases.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟刑警组织联合EUIPO开展行动，在欧洲30国查获价值3200万欧元的假冒药品及兴奋剂", "欧盟理事会采纳法规，以实施欧盟-南方共同市场伙伴关系协议下的农产品双边保障条款", "多国更新商标实务：智利强制使用尼斯分类第13版，巴西扩大优先审查资格，香港修订电子 f…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Europol and EUIPO collaborated on Operation SHIELD VI, seizing EUR 32 million in counterfeit goods across thirty European nations.", "Argentina committed to modernizing its intellectual property framework through a reciprocal trade and investment agreement with the United States.", "Brazil, Chile, India, and Hong Kong implemented new regulations affecting trademark examination, classification, and administrative procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟机构间执法合作深化，假冒药品仍是重点打击领域，合规风险需持续关注", "欧盟贸易协定中的知识产权条款落地，可能影响相关农产品市场的品牌保护策略", "全球主要局方推进程序现代化，包括分类标准更新及电子申请流程优化，需及时调整申请策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Healthcare brands should leverage coordinated EU enforcement actions to strengthen anti-counterfeiting strategies and protect public safety.", "Practice note: Monitor procedural updates in Brazil, Chile, and India to ensure timely compliance with new examination and classification requirements.", "What to monitor: Track the implementation of Argentina's IP framework modernization following the new US trade agreement commitments."]
core_points_en: Europol and EUIPO collaborated on Operation SHIELD VI, seizing EUR 32 million in counterfeit goods across thirty European nations.
Argentina committed to modernizing its intellectual property framework through a reciprocal trade and investment agreement with the United States.
Brazil, Chile, India, and Hong Kong implemented new regulations affecting trademark examination, classification, and administrative procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Healthcare brands should leverage coordinated EU enforcement actions to strengthen anti-counterfeiting strategies and protect public safety.
Practice note: Monitor procedural updates in Brazil, Chile, and India to ensure timely compliance with new examination and classification requirements.
What to monitor: Track the implementation of Argentina's IP framework modernization following the new US trade agreement commitments.

items.title: Driving the digital shift: highlights from the eSACEPO meeting
items.title_zh: 推动数字化转型：eSACEPO会议亮点
items.title_en: EPO eSACEPO Meeting Highlights: Digital Shift, Paperless Process and DOCX Filing
items.summary: On the 24th of March 2026, the EPO hosted a SACEPO Working party on e-Patent Process. The meeting focused on the digitalisation initiatives at the EPO, including the paperless project, MyEPO, DOCX filing system, the legal interact
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月24日，欧洲专利局（EPO）召开eSACEPO会议，重点讨论数字化举措。会议确认2027年将实现默认无纸化专利授予流程，电子提交成为标准。DOCX格式提交将于2026年4月1日生效但非强制。MyEPO平台功能增强，法律互动平台（LIP）引入AI并扩展至各国局。会议强调在搜索、分类等环节应用AI时坚持“人类主导”原则。
items.ai_summary_en: The EPO’s eSACEPO Working Party reviewed digitalisation initiatives, confirming the 2027 transition to a default paperless patent granting process. Electronic filing and notification will become standard, with paper options limited to specific cases. The meeting addressed legal frameworks for digital services, including provisions for last-day filings during outages. Updates on MyEPO highlighted new features like colour drawings and self-service tools. End-to-end DOCX filing enters into force on 1 April 2026 as a non-compulsory option with PDF backup safeguards. The Legal Interactive Platform continues to integrate AI improvements, supporting the shift toward efficient, user-friendly digital workflows while maintaining human oversight in critical processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。

items.title: Guidelines 2026 enter into force, streamlining procedural guidance
items.title_zh: 2026版EPO审查指南生效并简化程序指引
items.title_en: EPO 2026 Guidelines Enter into Force, Consolidating Procedural Guidance
items.summary: The 2026 EPC, PCT-EPO and Unitary Patent Guidelines enter into force on 1 April 2026, consolidating guidance and replacing the European Patent and Euro-PCT Guides while remaining open for user consultation until 3 April 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局2026版EPC、PCT-EPO及统一专利指南于2026年4月1日生效，取代原有的欧洲专利指南和Euro-PCT指南。新指南整合了此前分散的程序指引，旨在现代化和简化法律出版物。公众咨询持续至4月3日，反馈意见将用于制定2027版指南。旧指南将于5月31日从网站撤下。
items.ai_summary_en: The updated EPC, PCT-EPO, and Unitary Patent Guidelines entered into force on 1 April 2026, superseding the April 2025 editions. As part of a streamlining initiative, the European Patent Guide and Euro-PCT Guide are discontinued, with their content integrated into the main Guidelines and the EPO website. These resources remain accessible until 31 May 2026 before final withdrawal. The new editions are available in all three official languages, featuring clean and modified versions for easy tracking of changes. A public consultation on the 2026 Guidelines runs until 3 April 2026, with feedback scheduled for discussion by the SACEPO Working Party in May to inform the 2027 revision cycle.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次更新核心在于程序指引的结构化整合，申请人需适应从独立指南向综合指南的转变。实务中应重点关注官网提供的修订对比文件，以识别具体审查标准的变化。公众咨询窗口期短暂，从业者若有关键程序性建议需及时提交，这将影响后续年度指南的修订方向。
items.ai_insight_en: This consolidation simplifies the reference landscape for practitioners by merging standalone guides into the primary Guidelines. Users should leverage the new modification-tracking features to identify procedural updates efficiently. With the consultation window closing shortly, immediate feedback is critical for influencing next year's revisions. Teams must update internal knowledge bases to reflect the discontinuation of the legacy Guides by May 2026.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版EPC、PCT-EPO及统一专利指南于2026年4月1日正式生效，取代2025年版", "欧洲专利指南和Euro-PCT指南被废止，内容整合进新指南及官网，5月31日后撤下", "针对2026版指南的公众咨询开放至4月3日，反馈将在5月SACEPO工作组讨论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Updated EPC, PCT-EPO, and Unitary Patent Guidelines entered into force on 1 April 2026.", "European Patent Guide and Euro-PCT Guide are discontinued, with content integrated into main Guidelines.", "Public consultation on the 2026 Guidelines remains open until 3 April 2026."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["指南结构整合导致检索习惯变化，需熟悉新版在线浏览及修订显示功能", "公众咨询截止在即，反馈意见将直接关联2027版指南的预备工作", "旧指南过渡期至5月底，期间需注意引用来源的时效性与准确性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Centralized guidance reduces navigation complexity but requires updating internal compliance references to align with the new structure.", "Practice note: Utilize the 'Show modifications' feature in online HTML versions to quickly identify specific procedural changes since the 2025 edition.", "What to monitor: Final withdrawal of the European Patent Guide and Euro-PCT Guide from the EPO website on 31 May 2026."]
core_points_en: Updated EPC, PCT-EPO, and Unitary Patent Guidelines entered into force on 1 April 2026.
European Patent Guide and Euro-PCT Guide are discontinued, with content integrated into main Guidelines.
Public consultation on the 2026 Guidelines remains open until 3 April 2026.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Centralized guidance reduces navigation complexity but requires updating internal compliance references to align with the new structure.
Practice note: Utilize the 'Show modifications' feature in online HTML versions to quickly identify specific procedural changes since the 2025 edition.
What to monitor: Final withdrawal of the European Patent Guide and Euro-PCT Guide from the EPO website on 31 May 2026.

items.title: Filing in DOCX now available for all users - greater flexibility and smoother workflows
items.title_zh: EPO 向所有用户开放 DOCX 格式提交以提升灵活性
items.title_en: EPO Opens DOCX Filing to All Users for Enhanced Flexibility and Workflow Efficiency
items.summary: The new filing format builds on the EPO’s digital transformation, offering a more consistent and future-ready way to submit patent applications and other documents.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布在完成试点后，正式向所有用户开放使用 DOCX 格式提交欧洲专利申请及后续文件。此举旨在深化数字化转型，允许绘图嵌入文档或单独上传，减少格式转换错误与人工校对时间。该更新已纳入局长决定，支持与 MyEPO 等现代工具无缝对接，实现端到端数字化处理。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has officially rolled out support for filing European patent applications and subsequent documents in DOCX format to all users. Following a successful pilot phase, this initiative aligns with the EPO’s Strategic Plan 2028 to foster a fully digital granting process. The new format allows applicants to integrate drawings directly into DOCX files or submit them separately as PDFs. This update aims to streamline end-to-end digital workflows, reduce formatting errors, and improve data quality during search, examination, and publication stages. The Decision of the President concerning electronic filing has been amended to reflect these changes, offering greater consistency and time savings for rights holders and representatives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development supports portfolio management efficiency by minimizing administrative burdens associated with document conversion and formatting compliance. For European and US practitioners, adopting native DOCX workflows can enhance data integrity throughout the prosecution lifecycle. The ability to maintain stable paragraph references and reduce layout shifts facilitates smoother amendments and responses to examination reports. Rights holders should evaluate their internal document preparation systems to leverage these digital advantages, ensuring compatibility with future EPO tools and services while maintaining high-quality submissions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 全面推广 DOCX 格式，支持专利申请及后续文件提交", "绘图可嵌入 DOCX 或单独以 PDF 提交，提升操作灵活性", "减少格式错误与重复转换，显著优化审查与修改流程效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["DOCX filing is now available for all users, supporting the EPO's goal of a fully digital patent granting environment.", "Applicants may embed drawings directly within DOCX documents or file them separately as PDFs based on preference.", "The format reduces manual checks and formatting corrections, improving text stability and character handling."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即调整申请文件制作流程，优先采用原生 DOCX 格式", "利用格式优势减少补正通知，加快欧洲专利授权进程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adopting DOCX filing can reduce time spent on manual formatting checks and minimize risks of corrupted characters or layout errors.", "Practice note: Teams should review internal drafting protocols to allow direct integration of drawings into DOCX or manage separate PDF submissions effectively.", "What to monitor: Updates to the EPO Notice and feature guides regarding specific technical requirements for DOCX submissions and future tool integrations."]
core_points_en: DOCX filing is now available for all users, supporting the EPO's goal of a fully digital patent granting environment.
Applicants may embed drawings directly within DOCX documents or file them separately as PDFs based on preference.
The format reduces manual checks and formatting corrections, improving text stability and character handling.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adopting DOCX filing can reduce time spent on manual formatting checks and minimize risks of corrupted characters or layout errors.
Practice note: Teams should review internal drafting protocols to allow direct integration of drawings into DOCX or manage separate PDF submissions effectively.
What to monitor: Updates to the EPO Notice and feature guides regarding specific technical requirements for DOCX submissions and future tool integrations.

items.title: The Unitary Patent Guidelines April 2026 update
items.title_zh: 2026年4月统一专利指南更新生效
items.title_en: EPO Updates Unitary Patent Guidelines: Key Procedural Clarity Changes Effective April 2026
items.summary: Updated 2026 Unitary Patent Guidelines take effect 1 April, enhancing clarity, procedures, and legal detail across the UPC system.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）运行近三年后，统一专利（UP）指南迎来修订，新版于2026年4月1日生效。此次更新旨在提升UPC系统的清晰度、程序规范及法律细节。欧洲专利局（EPO）已发布标注修改痕迹的2026版指南，对比2025版进行了广泛调整。随着对UP系统及UPC信心的增强，新规在适当位置增加了更多程序与法律依据的细节，以优化用户体验并明确操作标准。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has published updated Unitary Patent Guidelines, which entered into force on 1 April 2026. Following nearly three years of Unified Patent Court operations, these amendments refine procedural clarity and legal detail across the system. A marked-up version comparing the 2026 guidelines against the 2025 version is available to help users track specific changes. The updates reflect growing confidence in the Unitary Patent system and aim to provide greater transparency regarding procedural requirements and legal bases for applicants and litigants.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing European portfolios, the refined guidelines offer improved predictability in Unitary Patent administration and UPC interactions. The availability of a tracked-changes document allows counsel to efficiently identify procedural adjustments without reviewing the entire text. This update supports better compliance with evolving administrative standards as the Unitary Patent system matures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年4月1日起，新版统一专利指南正式生效实施", "全面修订指南内容，重点增强程序清晰度与法律依据细节", "欧洲专利局提供新旧版本对比文档，便于用户追踪具体变更"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Updated Unitary Patent Guidelines took effect on 1 April 2026, refining procedures and legal details within the UPC system.", "The EPO provides a marked-up comparison document highlighting changes between the 2025 and 2026 guideline versions.", "Amendments focus on enhancing procedural clarity and providing more detailed legal basis information for system users."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["研读EPO对比文档，精准掌握UPC程序合规新要求", "优化欧洲专利组合管理，规避因程序疏漏导致的维权风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhanced procedural clarity reduces uncertainty in Unitary Patent maintenance and UPC-related filings.", "Practice note: Review the EPO's marked-up comparison document to identify specific amendments affecting current applications or litigation.", "What to monitor: Further refinements to guidelines as the UPC continues to develop its case law and administrative practices."]
core_points_en: Updated Unitary Patent Guidelines took effect on 1 April 2026, refining procedures and legal details within the UPC system.
The EPO provides a marked-up comparison document highlighting changes between the 2025 and 2026 guideline versions.
Amendments focus on enhancing procedural clarity and providing more detailed legal basis information for system users.

Practice note: Review the EPO's marked-up comparison document to identify specific amendments affecting current applications or litigation.
What to monitor: Further refinements to guidelines as the UPC continues to develop its case law and administrative practices.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds PI against Sinocare and Menarini
items.title_zh: 上诉庭维持对Sinocare和Menarini的临时禁令
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Upholds Preliminary Injunction Against Sinocare and Menarini
items.summary: The local division The Hague has become attractive for PI applications — especially in the medical devices sector. In Abbott’s dispute with Chinese competitor Sinocare and the latter’s distribution partner Menarini, the court thus appears to have delivered what companies and their lawyers expect. Yesterday, the UPC Court of Appeal upheld a PI from the […] The post Court of Appeal upholds PI against Sinocare and Menarini appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭维持了针对中国竞争对手三诺生物（Sinocare）及其分销合作伙伴美纳里尼（Menarini）的临时禁令。该裁决源于雅培（Abbott）与上述两方的纠纷。海牙地方分庭在医疗器械领域的临时禁令申请中日益受到青睐，此次上诉庭的裁决符合企业及律师的预期。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has recently issued a ruling upholding the preliminary injunction granted by the Local Division in The Hague against Chinese manufacturer Sinocare and its distribution partner, Menarini. This case involves a patent infringement dispute in the medical devices sector, initiated by Abbott. The decision further consolidates the attractiveness of the The Hague division for securing preliminary relief in medical device patent matters. It demonstrates that the UPC system is effectively providing efficient cross-border enforcement protection for rightsholders as anticipated, confirming the court's capability to deliver swift judicial remedies in high-stakes intellectual property conflicts across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC上诉庭对海牙地方分庭临时禁令决定的审查标准。医疗器械领域的权利人可能更倾向于选择海牙分庭提起紧急救济程序。后续应观察该判例对跨国分销商连带责任认定的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the medical device sector should closely monitor litigation trends at the UPC Local Division in The Hague, which has become a focal point for preliminary injunction applications. Rightsholders are increasingly leveraging this venue for efficient cross-border enforcement. Conversely, potential defendants must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses prior to market entry to mitigate infringement risks. Establishing robust legal response protocols is essential to address sudden injunctions promptly, thereby preventing significant disruptions to supply chains and ensuring continued market access across participating member states without prolonged operational halts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭维持针对Sinocare及Menarini的临时禁令", "案件当事人为雅培、三诺生物及美纳里尼", "海牙地方分庭在医疗器械领域PI申请中具吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Court of Appeal confirms the validity and continued effect of the preliminary injunction issued against Chinese enterprises.", "The Hague Local Division has emerged as a preferred jurisdiction for obtaining preliminary injunctions in the medical device sector.", "The judgment indicates the UPC mechanism is effectively meeting corporate demands for rapid cross-border rights enforcement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["海牙分庭在医疗器械临时禁令案件中吸引力上升", "上诉庭维持原判显示对一审紧急救济裁定的支持倾向", "需持续监测分销商在专利侵权禁令中的责任承担范围"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Medical device companies must assess litigation exposure in The Hague due to its growing preference for granting preliminary injunctions.", "Practice note: Implement proactive freedom-to-operate strategies and rapid response frameworks to manage sudden UPC enforcement actions effectively.", "What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments regarding preliminary relief standards and the strategic selection of local divisions by plaintiffs."]
core_points_en: UPC Court of Appeal confirms the validity and continued effect of the preliminary injunction issued against Chinese enterprises.
The Hague Local Division has emerged as a preferred jurisdiction for obtaining preliminary injunctions in the medical device sector.
The judgment indicates the UPC mechanism is effectively meeting corporate demands for rapid cross-border rights enforcement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Medical device companies must assess litigation exposure in The Hague due to its growing preference for granting preliminary injunctions.
Practice note: Implement proactive freedom-to-operate strategies and rapid response frameworks to manage sudden UPC enforcement actions effectively.
What to monitor: Track UPC case law developments regarding preliminary relief standards and the strategic selection of local divisions by plaintiffs.

items.title: Chinese car makers take Avanci licence for connected cars
items.title_zh: 七家中国车企签署Avanci网联汽车专利许可协议
items.title_en: Chinese car makers take Avanci licence for connected cars
items.summary: Seven Chinese car manufacturers have signed agreements with Avanci for its 4G and 5G licensing programmes.
items.ai_summary_zh: Avanci宣布其4G和5G车辆许可计划已达成十项许可协议。其中七家中国汽车制造商签署了相关协议，但新闻稿未披露具体企业名称。此前许多欧洲车企已获取此类许可，Avanci暗示未来数月将有更多交易达成。
items.ai_insight_zh: 此动态显示Avanci专利池在网联汽车领域的许可扩张至中国主要制造商。鉴于原文未披露具体费率或被许可人名称，需持续监控后续公开信息以评估对行业许可基准及潜在FRAND争议的影响。

items.title: Rapport annuel 2025
items.title_zh: 法国国家工业产权局 2025 年度报告
items.title_en: INPI 2025 Annual Report: Strategic Roadmap Launch and Key Filing Data
items.summary: Rapport annuel 2025 sgurtler@inpi.fr 31 mars 2026 L’année 2025 a été marquée par le lancement d’une nouvelle feuille de route stratégique pour l’INPI. Élément structurant pour l’Institut, le COP 2025-2029 fixe des ambitions fortes et s'inscrit dans la volonté d’apporter toujours plus de services répondant aux besoins des entrepreneurs et des innovateurs, pour contribuer à la croissance de la France. Découvrez dans ce rapport le bilan des premières actions engagées par l’Institut, tant en matière
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）发布 2025 年度报告，标志着其 2025-2029 年战略规划合同（COP）的启动。报告总结了机构在企业手续与知识产权保护方面的首批行动成果，披露了单一窗口及专利、商标等申请的关键数据。报告强调通过支持创新者需求、提升无形资产价值来推动法国经济增长，展现了机构的韧性与服务能力。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) released its 2025 Annual Report, marking the commencement of its new strategic roadmap for 2025-2029. The report outlines the objectives of the Performance Contract (COP), emphasizing enhanced services for entrepreneurs and innovators to support French economic growth. It provides a comprehensive overview of INPI's activities, including key statistics from the Single Window platform and intellectual property filings such as patents, trademarks, and designs. The document highlights the institute's resilience and its commitment to supporting businesses through every stage of development, focusing on the valuation of intangible assets and innovation support both domestically and internationally.

items.title: Statutory guidance: Forms directions under the Patents Act
items.title_zh: 法定指南：专利法案下的表格指令
items.title_en: UKIPO Updates Statutory Guidance on Patent Forms to Align with One IPO Digital Service
items.summary: Changes to the patents forms to align with the One IPO digital service.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布关于《专利法案》下表格指令的法定指南。此次更新旨在调整专利申请表单，以与“One IPO”数字化服务保持一致。该变动属于程序性行政更新，确保申请人使用符合新数字系统要求的标准化表格进行专利相关事务办理。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has issued updated statutory guidance concerning directions for forms under the Patents Act. This regulatory update is designed to modify existing official patent application forms and related procedural documents. The primary objective is to ensure full compatibility with the UKIPO’s new 'One IPO' digital service platform. By aligning statutory form requirements with this integrated digital infrastructure, the Office aims to streamline administrative processes and enhance user experience for applicants and representatives. These changes reflect a broader modernization effort within the UKIPO to digitize patent prosecution workflows, ensuring that all statutory filings adhere to the technical and procedural standards required by the new centralized digital environment.
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a critical transition in UK patent administration, moving towards a unified digital ecosystem. For in-house counsel and external practitioners, it is essential to review current filing templates and automated systems to ensure they accommodate the revised form structures. Failure to adapt may result in procedural delays or formal objections during prosecution. Stakeholders should verify that their internal docketing software and third-party service providers have implemented these changes. Monitoring further technical specifications from the UKIPO will be necessary to maintain compliance and leverage the efficiency gains promised by the One IPO initiative.

items.title: Statutory guidance: Forms directions under the Trade Marks Act
items.title_zh: 法定指引：商标法案下的表格指示
items.title_en: UKIPO Updates Trade Marks Forms to Reflect New Fee Structure Effective April 2026
items.summary: Changes to the fee-bearing trade marks forms to show the fees payable from 1 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布关于《商标法案》下表格指示的法定指引。主要变更涉及需缴费的商标表格，旨在显示自2026年4月1日起应付的费用标准。该指引为申请人提供最新的表格填写与费用缴纳规范。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has issued statutory guidance regarding directions under the Trade Marks Act, specifically focusing on updates to fee-bearing trade mark forms. This administrative update is designed to ensure that all official application forms accurately display the new fee schedule applicable from 1 April 2026. The changes are part of a routine procedural adjustment to align documentation with upcoming cost structures. By updating these forms in advance, the UKIPO aims to prevent discrepancies between the fees listed on applications and the actual amounts payable at the time of filing. This measure ensures transparency for applicants and facilitates smoother processing of trade mark registrations by eliminating potential errors related to outdated fee information on submitted documents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此为程序性更新提示。需关注2026年4月1日生效的新费用标准对商标申请成本的影响。建议持续监控UKIPO后续发布的具体费率表及表格版本更新，以确保合规申报。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders managing trade mark portfolios in the United Kingdom should anticipate increased official costs starting in April 2026. It is advisable to review and adjust budget forecasts for upcoming filings to accommodate these higher fees. Furthermore, legal teams and applicants must ensure they download and use the latest versions of official forms before the first quarter of 2026. Using outdated forms with incorrect fee calculations may lead to formal objections, delays in examination, or even refusal of applications due to insufficient payment. Proactive verification of form versions and fee accuracy is essential to maintain efficient prosecution timelines and avoid unnecessary administrative hurdles during the registration process.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["发布关于《商标法案》下表格指示的法定指引", "变更需缴费商标表格以显示2026年4月1日起应付费用"]

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 24, 2026, Order On Suspensive Effect, UPC_CoA_44/2026
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉法院关于中止执行效力的裁定
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Suspensive Effect Is Exceptional, Requiring Strict Proof and Concrete Evidence
items.summary: The Court of Appeal confirmed that suspensive effect is an exception to the general rule.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉法院在ALPINA诉CUP&CINO案中确认，中止执行仅为例外情形。上诉人需证明其维持现状的利益优于被上诉人的执行利益。因上诉人未针对 merits 进行论证，且仅基于推测性平行诉讼结果申请，法院驳回其中止执行申请，强调损害主张须具体确凿。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal confirmed that granting suspensive effect remains an exception to the general rule. In a case involving coffee system patents, the Court emphasized that appellants must demonstrate their interest in maintaining the status quo significantly outweighs the respondent’s interest in enforcing the first-instance decision. The ruling rejected arguments based on speculative future events, such as uncertain outcomes in parallel proceedings, and dismissed vague claims of financial or reputational harm. Furthermore, the Court highlighted that potential multiple patent infringements weigh heavily against granting suspensive effect, as doing so could unjustifiably reward continued infringement. Applicants must provide specific, quantified evidence of harm rather than relying on general assertions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC上诉阶段中止执行的严格标准。实务中，当事人需提供具体、确凿的证据证明不可弥补的损害，避免依赖推测性理由。模糊的财务负担主张难以获得支持，需注重证据的具体化与实质化。
items.ai_insight_en: Securing suspensive effect at the UPC Court of Appeal presents significant challenges following an adverse first-instance judgment. Parties must prepare concrete, quantified evidence regarding modification costs, production stoppages, and specific operational impacts, avoiding generic assertions of harm. In scenarios involving multiple potentially infringing patents, applicants should anticipate that courts may view suspension as rewarding infringement. Consequently, businesses should proactively develop alternative compliance strategies and immediate cessation plans to mitigate risks if suspensive relief is denied.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["中止执行属例外情形，需证明上诉人利益优于被上诉人执行利益", "基于平行诉讼等不确定未来事件的中止申请理由不被法院支持", "vague 的财务或声誉损害主张不足以对抗一审判决的执行利益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Suspensive effect is exceptional; appellants must prove their interest in maintaining the status quo outweighs the respondent's enforcement interest.", "Speculative future events, like parallel litigation outcomes, and vague damage claims are insufficient grounds for suspending enforcement.", "Potential multiple patent infringements strongly argue against suspensive effect, preventing unjust rewards for continued violation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中止执行申请需提交具体事实与证据，避免空泛主张", "平行诉讼的不确定性不构成中止执行的充分理由", "需关注UPC对“例外情形”及“具体损害”的认定尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Securing suspensive effect requires concrete, quantified evidence of harm, as vague claims and speculative arguments are routinely rejected by the Court.", "Practice note: In cases involving multiple patents, assume suspension will be denied to avoid 'rewarding' infringement; prepare immediate sales cessation protocols.", "What to monitor: How the Court assesses 'manifest error' and procedural rights violations when balancing appellant interests against enforcement needs."]
core_points_en: Suspensive effect is exceptional; appellants must prove their interest in maintaining the status quo outweighs the respondent's enforcement interest.
Speculative future events, like parallel litigation outcomes, and vague damage claims are insufficient grounds for suspending enforcement.
Potential multiple patent infringements strongly argue against suspensive effect, preventing unjust rewards for continued violation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Securing suspensive effect requires concrete, quantified evidence of harm, as vague claims and speculative arguments are routinely rejected by the Court.
Practice note: In cases involving multiple patents, assume suspension will be denied to avoid 'rewarding' infringement; prepare immediate sales cessation protocols.
What to monitor: How the Court assesses 'manifest error' and procedural rights violations when balancing appellant interests against enforcement needs.

items.title: LD Paris, March 23, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_1963/2025
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎地院裁定：灵活认定被告间商业联系以确立管辖
items.title_en: UPC Paris: No Direct Link Required Between Anchor and Co-Defendants for Joint Jurisdiction
items.summary: The commercial link is assessed flexibly to avoid multiplying parallel proceedings and the risk of contradictory decisions. This applies in particular in cases where all defendants...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎地方法院在Valeo诉Bosch案中发布程序令。法院裁定，《统一专利法院协议》第33(1)(b)条不要求锚点被告与其他被告存在直接商业联系，仅需所有被告间存在灵活认定的商业联系。同一涉嫌侵权指侵犯同一专利权，而非产品同一性。因初步异议被驳回，驳回起诉申请已无实际意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Paris issued a procedural order clarifying jurisdictional requirements under Article 33(1)(b) of the UPC Agreement. The court ruled that a direct commercial link between the anchor defendant and each co-defendant is unnecessary; rather, a general commercial connection among all defendants suffices. This flexible interpretation aims to prevent parallel proceedings and contradictory judgments, particularly within corporate groups sharing R&D, manufacturing, or distribution activities. Additionally, the court defined 'same alleged infringement' as referring to the violation of identical patent claims, regardless of product trade names or forms. Consequently, preliminary objections were dismissed, and the failure to amend the statement of claim did not result in dismissal of the infringement action.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定明确了UPC对集团型被告管辖权的宽松解释路径，降低了原告因管辖连接点不足而被驳回的风险。实务中需关注法院如何具体界定“灵活的商业联系”，特别是在研发、制造与销售分离的跨国供应链场景下，这对确定诉讼策略及被告选择具有参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts multinational corporations operating in Europe, particularly those with complex group structures. By lowering the threshold for establishing joint jurisdiction, the UPC facilitates the consolidation of patent disputes involving multiple entities within the same supply chain or corporate group. Litigants should anticipate that separate legal entities may be joined in a single proceeding if their activities are commercially related and target the same patent. Strategic planning must account for this risk, ensuring consistent defense strategies across all potentially implicated entities to mitigate the danger of fragmented litigation and inconsistent outcomes across different divisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPCA第33(1)(b)条仅要求所有被告间存在商业联系，无需锚点被告与各方直接关联", "“同一涉嫌侵权”指侵犯同一专利权利要求，不要求被控侵权产品具有相同名称或形式", "法院驳回初步异议后，原告未修改诉状不构成驳回侵权诉讼的理由，程序继续进行"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 33(1)(b) requires only a general commercial link among all defendants, not a direct relationship with the anchor defendant.", "'Same alleged infringement' concerns the identity of infringed patent rights, not the exact identity of allegedly infringing products.", "Dismissing a preliminary objection renders motions to dismiss the main action moot, with no penalty for unamended claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确认UPC倾向于通过灵活解释商业联系来避免平行诉讼和矛盾判决", "明确同一专利下的不同产品变体可构成“同一涉嫌侵权”，简化了共同被告认定", "需持续观察后续案件中法院对“商业联系”具体证据标准的细化要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Multinational groups face higher risks of consolidated litigation where commercially linked entities are sued together under one patent.", "Practice note: Defense strategies must be unified across all group entities, as product variations do not necessarily preclude joint infringement findings.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this flexible 'commercial link' standard to determine venue and joinder in future cases."]
core_points_en: Article 33(1)(b) requires only a general commercial link among all defendants, not a direct relationship with the anchor defendant.
'Same alleged infringement' concerns the identity of infringed patent rights, not the exact identity of allegedly infringing products.
Dismissing a preliminary objection renders motions to dismiss the main action moot, with no penalty for unamended claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Multinational groups face higher risks of consolidated litigation where commercially linked entities are sued together under one patent.
Practice note: Defense strategies must be unified across all group entities, as product variations do not necessarily preclude joint infringement findings.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this flexible 'commercial link' standard to determine venue and joinder in future cases.
