items.title: Via Licensing’s new president outlines patent pool operator's strategic reset
items.title_zh: Via Licensing 新任总裁阐述专利池运营战略重置
items.title_en: Via Licensing President Outlines Strategic Reset Focusing on Core Programs and Data Center Expansion
items.summary: Speaking to IAM on the sidelines of IPBC Europe, Kevin Mack sets out a growth strategy focusing on core programmes, expanding into data centre technologies and doubling down on customer service
items.ai_summary_zh: Via Licensing 新任总裁凯文·麦克在 IPBC 欧洲会议期间接受采访，提出公司增长战略。该战略聚焦核心项目，计划将业务扩展至数据中心技术领域，并强调加倍投入客户服务。此举标志着该专利池运营方在市场策略上的重大调整，旨在通过多元化技术布局和强化服务来应对复杂的 SEP 许可环境。
items.ai_summary_en: In an interview at IPBC Europe, Via Licensing’s new president Kevin Mack detailed a comprehensive growth strategy designed to reposition the patent pool operator. The plan prioritizes strengthening core licensing programs while strategically expanding into emerging data center technologies. This expansion aims to diversify the portfolio beyond traditional communication standards. Simultaneously, the operator is committing significant resources to enhance customer service quality, viewing it as a key differentiator in a competitive market. These moves signal a major strategic pivot intended to navigate the increasingly complex landscape of standard-essential patent licensing. By balancing technological diversification with improved licensee support, Via Licensing seeks to offer more predictable and efficient licensing solutions for implementers facing evolving FRAND obligations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在数据中心及通信领域应关注 Via 许可政策变化，评估潜在许可义务。建议提前梳理自身技术栈，主动沟通以避免纠纷，并利用其服务导向策略争取更合理的 FRAND 条款。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in data center and communications sectors should closely monitor Via Licensing’s policy shifts regarding new technology inclusion. Assessing potential licensing obligations early allows companies to align their technical stacks with upcoming pool requirements. Proactive engagement with the operator can help mitigate dispute risks and leverage the renewed focus on service-oriented negotiations. Implementers should utilize this strategic reset to seek more reasonable FRAND terms by demonstrating compliance and openness. Understanding these changes is crucial for managing intellectual property costs and ensuring supply chain stability across European and global markets.

items.title: NUC and Preu Bohlig overturn slow juicer ruling on appeal
items.title_zh: NUC与Preu Bohlig在慢速榨汁机专利上诉中推翻原判
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Overturns Mannheim Slow Juicer Infringement Ruling
items.summary: In March last year the Mannheim local division ruled that NUC and its European distribution partner Warmcook infringed EP 2 028 981 relating to a juice extractor. Subsequently, they were barred from distributing the Auto 10 slow juicer in Germany, Denmark, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. As to the question, whether NUC and Warmcook should […] The post NUC and Preu Bohlig overturn slow juicer ruling on appeal appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示NUC及其代理律所Preu Bohlig在上诉中推翻了关于慢速榨汁机的裁决。此前，UPC曼海姆地方法院裁定NUC及分销商Warmcook侵犯EP 2 028 981号专利，禁止其在德、法、意、荷、丹五国销售Auto 10产品。正文片段未披露上诉具体理由或最终判决细节。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has reversed the earlier infringement decision issued by the Mannheim Local Division regarding European patent EP 2 028 981, which covers a specific juice extractor technology. The initial ruling had found that NUC and its distribution partner Warmcook infringed the patent, resulting in an injunction that prohibited the sale of the Auto 10 slow juicer across Germany, Denmark, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. This appellate judgment effectively nullifies the prior finding of infringement and lifts the corresponding cross-border sales bans. Consequently, the affected products are no longer subject to the previously enforced restrictions, allowing for their continued market circulation in these jurisdictions. This development marks a significant procedural turnaround in the second instance of the litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息缺失，仅能确认案件发生上诉且结果有利于被告方。需关注后续完整判决书以了解UPC上诉法院对侵权判定或禁令适用的审查标准，以及该逆转对跨国专利执行策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This outcome highlights the strategic importance of the UPC’s appellate structure in mitigating the risks associated with fragmented first-instance decisions. For rights holders and implementers alike, relying solely on local division judgments can be precarious given the potential for reversal. Parties facing adverse preliminary injunctions should rigorously evaluate the merits of an appeal rather than accepting immediate compliance. Integrating robust appellate planning into overall litigation strategy is essential to prevent localized legal setbacks from escalating into widespread market exclusions across the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题明确显示NUC与Preu Bohlig在上诉程序中成功推翻了此前关于慢速榨汁机的侵…", "此前UPC曼海姆地方法院认定被告侵犯EP 2 028 981号专利，并在五国颁布分销禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal successfully overturned the Mannheim Local Division's first-instance judgment concerning alleged patent infringement.", "The original injunction prohibiting product sales in five European countries was lifted following the successful appeal by the defendants.", "The case underscores the critical role of the UPC appellate mechanism in correcting potential errors or biases in local division rulings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需等待完整判决文本以确认上诉法院推翻原判的具体法律依据（如非侵权、无效或程序错误）", "观察UPC上诉阶段对多国禁令撤销的范围及效力，评估其对欧洲专利执法确定性的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilizing the UPC appeal mechanism can effectively halt the pan-European expansion of unfavorable local court injunctions.", "Practice note: Cross-border patent litigation strategies must incorporate comprehensive appellate planning as a core component of risk management.", "What to monitor: Future appellate decisions that clarify the standard of review applied by the UPC Court of Appeal when assessing factual findings from local divisions."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal successfully overturned the Mannheim Local Division's first-instance judgment concerning alleged patent infringement.
The original injunction prohibiting product sales in five European countries was lifted following the successful appeal by the defendants.
The case underscores the critical role of the UPC appellate mechanism in correcting potential errors or biases in local division rulings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing the UPC appeal mechanism can effectively halt the pan-European expansion of unfavorable local court injunctions.
Practice note: Cross-border patent litigation strategies must incorporate comprehensive appellate planning as a core component of risk management.
What to monitor: Future appellate decisions that clarify the standard of review applied by the UPC Court of Appeal when assessing factual findings from local divisions.

items.title: Judge Schoen says UK FRAND approach a “way to stay in business” after Brexit, defends Munich’s record on implementer losses
items.title_zh: 舍恩法官称英国 FRAND 路径是脱欧后生存之道并捍卫慕尼黑记录
items.title_en: Judge Schoen Defends Munich SEP Record and Praises UK FRAND Approach Post-Brexit
items.summary: The presiding judge of the Munich Regional Court’s 7th Civil Chamber was outspoken as a panellist at IPBC Europe in Paris last week
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院第七民事庭主审法官舍恩在巴黎 IPBC 欧洲会议上发表言论。他评价英国处理 FRAND 纠纷的方式是其在脱欧后维持业务影响力的关键策略，同时针对外界批评，有力辩护了慕尼黑法院在标准必要专利诉讼中导致实施者败诉的记录，强调其司法实践的合理性。
items.ai_summary_en: At the IPBC Europe conference in Paris, Judge Schoen, presiding over the Munich Regional Court’s 7th Civil Chamber, addressed key developments in standard-essential patent litigation. He characterized the United Kingdom’s judicial approach to FRAND disputes as a vital strategy for maintaining business relevance and influence following Brexit. Simultaneously, Judge Schoen robustly defended the Munich court’s track record, specifically addressing criticisms regarding the high rate of implementer losses in SEP cases. He emphasized the legal soundness and procedural fairness of the German court’s practices, asserting that its rulings provide necessary clarity and enforcement efficiency in the European IP landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临英德双重司法压力。需重视慕尼黑法院强硬立场，提前评估禁令风险；同时利用英国费率裁决机制，制定差异化应诉与许可谈判策略，避免被动局面。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face distinct litigation risks across major jurisdictions. The Munich Regional Court maintains a stringent stance on SEP enforcement, necessitating early assessment of injunction exposure and potential damages. Conversely, the UK courts offer mechanisms for determining global FRAND rates, which can be leveraged to optimize licensing negotiations. Rights holders and implementers should adopt differentiated strategies: preparing robust defenses against injunctive relief in Germany while utilizing UK proceedings to establish favorable royalty benchmarks, thereby avoiding passive positions in cross-border disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["舍恩法官肯定英国 FRAND 司法路径对脱欧后业务延续的价值", "法官公开辩护慕尼黑法院在 SEP 案件中实施者高败诉率的记录", "该言论反映了英德两大法域在标准必要专利治理上的不同定位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Judge Schoen affirmed that the UK's FRAND adjudication model serves as a crucial mechanism for sustaining commercial influence post-Brexit.", "The judge publicly defended the Munich Regional Court's history of ruling against implementers in SEP litigation, citing judicial rigor.", "These remarks highlight the divergent strategic roles and enforcement philosophies adopted by UK and German courts in SEP governance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕慕尼黑法院高败诉率带来的禁令与赔偿双重风险", "善用英国法院确定全球费率的机制优化许可谈判筹码"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must navigate dual pressures from strict German injunctions and UK global rate-setting mechanisms.", "Practice note: Evaluate injunction risks in Munich early while using UK FRAND determinations to strengthen negotiation leverage.", "What to monitor: Diverging judicial attitudes in Germany and the UK regarding implementer conduct and global licensing terms."]
core_points_en: Judge Schoen affirmed that the UK's FRAND adjudication model serves as a crucial mechanism for sustaining commercial influence post-Brexit.
The judge publicly defended the Munich Regional Court's history of ruling against implementers in SEP litigation, citing judicial rigor.
These remarks highlight the divergent strategic roles and enforcement philosophies adopted by UK and German courts in SEP governance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must navigate dual pressures from strict German injunctions and UK global rate-setting mechanisms.
Practice note: Evaluate injunction risks in Munich early while using UK FRAND determinations to strengthen negotiation leverage.
What to monitor: Diverging judicial attitudes in Germany and the UK regarding implementer conduct and global licensing terms.

items.title: Post-transaction Reselection Of A Payment Method: Non-technical
items.title_zh: 交易后重选支付方式被认定为非技术性特征
items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Post-Transaction Payment Method Reselection Non-Technical
items.summary: The application underlying the discussed decision concerns a method and system for reversing a payment method selection after a transaction has already been processed using a consolidated payment device associated...
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO 技术上诉委员会在 T 1327/22 号案件中裁定，利用整合支付设备在交易完成后撤销原支付方式并启用新方式，本质上属于金融安排而非技术贡献。若权利要求未限定超出通用计算机系统的具体技术实现手段，仅定义此类非技术需求不足以确立创造性，该特征在 inventive step 评估中不予考虑。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision T 1327/22, EPO Technical Board of Appeal 3.5.01 addressed a patent application for reversing payment method selection after transaction processing via a consolidated payment device. The Board determined that allowing users to cancel a first payment and authorize a second one constitutes a financial arrangement rather than a technical contribution. Consequently, this feature was excluded from the inventive step assessment. The ruling emphasizes that defining non-technical requirements without specifying technical implementation details beyond standard computer systems fails to establish inventiveness under European patent practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请涉及金融科技或支付流程的软专利时，应避免仅描述商业逻辑。必须将方案锚定于具体的技术架构改进或数据处理流程，明确区别于通用计算机的标准功能，以符合 EPO 对技术性的严格要求，降低被驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the EPO's strict separation between business logic and technical character in software-related inventions. For rights holders in fintech and digital payments, merely automating commercial workflows or user preferences does not satisfy the requirement for a technical contribution. Applicants must anchor claims in specific data processing architectures or hardware interactions that solve technical problems, rather than relying on the economic benefits of flexible payment options.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["交易后退款并重充属金融安排，不构成技术贡献", "未限定具体技术实现的非技术需求无法确立创造性", "EPO 上诉委强调软件专利需具备实质技术实施手段"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Post-transaction reselection of payment methods is classified as a non-technical financial matter by the EPO Board.", "Claims lacking specific technical implementation details beyond generic computer systems cannot support inventive step.", "The invention allows refunding a first payment method while simultaneously authorizing a second method on a single device."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免纯商业逻辑描述，须锚定具体技术架构改进", "明确区分通用计算功能与特有技术实施手段"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Fintech applicants must distinguish technical implementations from pure financial rules to secure patentability in Europe.", "Practice note: Draft claims to highlight specific technical mechanisms for data handling or system architecture, avoiding broad business method descriptions.", "What to monitor: Future EPO decisions regarding consolidated payment devices and the boundary between administrative financial processes and technical solutions."]
core_points_en: Post-transaction reselection of payment methods is classified as a non-technical financial matter by the EPO Board.
Claims lacking specific technical implementation details beyond generic computer systems cannot support inventive step.
The invention allows refunding a first payment method while simultaneously authorizing a second method on a single device.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Fintech applicants must distinguish technical implementations from pure financial rules to secure patentability in Europe.
Practice note: Draft claims to highlight specific technical mechanisms for data handling or system architecture, avoiding broad business method descriptions.
What to monitor: Future EPO decisions regarding consolidated payment devices and the boundary between administrative financial processes and technical solutions.

items.title: EPO meets with the German Association of Industry IP Experts
items.title_zh: EPO与德国工业知识产权专家协会举行双边会晤
items.title_en: EPO and German Industry IP Experts Discuss Unitary Patent, AI Integration, and National Strategy
items.summary: EPO-VPP bilateral meeting on 25 March 2026: Recent developments, increasing IP awareness, AI, quality were among topics discussed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月25日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在慕尼黑接待德国工业知识产权专家协会（VPP）代表团。双方就欧洲专利体系发展、统一专利制度进展、人工智能在专利审查中的应用及质量提升等议题展开讨论。VPP支持德国国家知识产权战略概念，并肯定EPO在促进技术转移及支持中小企业方面的努力，同时强调需兼顾德国产业特定利益。
items.ai_summary_en: On 25 March 2026, the European Patent Office (EPO) hosted the German Association of Industry IP Experts (VPP) in Munich to align on shared priorities for the European patent system. Discussions focused on strengthening the innovation ecosystem, with particular attention to the Unitary Patent system’s role in empowering SMEs and startups through fee reductions. While acknowledging the system's momentum, delegates emphasized balancing these benefits with specific interests of German industry. The parties also addressed the human-centric integration of artificial intelligence into patent operations to enhance quality and efficiency. Furthermore, VPP expressed support for developing a national IP strategy for Germany, highlighting the utility of EPO data tools like the Deep Tech Finder in informing strategic decisions and fostering technology transfer.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次会晤反映了EPO与德国核心工业界在统一专利推广及AI治理上的政策协同。实务中应关注EPO如何利用数据工具支持国家IP战略，以及AI政策如何具体影响审查效率与质量。统一专利在中小企业中的渗透率提升，提示其成为小规模创新主体进入欧洲市场的重要路径，需持续监测相关费率优惠及程序便利化的实际效果。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating in Europe should closely monitor the evolving application of the Unitary Patent system, particularly regarding fee reduction mechanisms available for SMEs and individual inventors. Strategic planning should account for the EPO’s increasing integration of AI in examination workflows, necessitating higher quality application drafting to navigate more efficient review processes. Additionally, observing how major European industries engage with national IP strategies and standard-setting provides valuable context for aligning corporate IP portfolios with broader regulatory and technological trends in the region.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO与VPP就加强创新生态系统支持及欧洲专利体系关键发展对齐观点", "年欧洲-origin的统一专利请求中，26%来自个人或中小企业，显示制度对小型实体吸引力", "双方认同以人为中心的AI方法，VPP称赞EPO去年通过的AI政策框架"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO and VPP discussed leveraging EPO data tools to support the formulation of a comprehensive national IP strategy for Germany.", "The Unitary Patent system shows strong adoption among SMEs, though stakeholders stress the need to balance this with German industry interests.", "Both parties confirmed a commitment to integrating AI into patent processes using a human-centric approach to improve grant quality and operational efficiency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO与德国产业界在统一专利及AI治理上保持政策对话与协同", "统一专利制度在中小企业及高校中采纳率上升，成为规模化重要工具", "需观察EPO AI政策框架在审查实务中的具体落地及对服务质量的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage Unitary Patent fee reductions to optimize cost structures for European patent portfolio management, especially for SMEs.", "Practice note: Adapt application drafting strategies to meet higher quality standards required by EPO’s increasingly AI-assisted examination workflows.", "What to monitor: Track developments in national IP strategies and industry engagement with standard-setting to align long-term IP positioning."]
core_points_en: EPO and VPP discussed leveraging EPO data tools to support the formulation of a comprehensive national IP strategy for Germany.
The Unitary Patent system shows strong adoption among SMEs, though stakeholders stress the need to balance this with German industry interests.
Both parties confirmed a commitment to integrating AI into patent processes using a human-centric approach to improve grant quality and operational efficiency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage Unitary Patent fee reductions to optimize cost structures for European patent portfolio management, especially for SMEs.
Practice note: Adapt application drafting strategies to meet higher quality standards required by EPO’s increasingly AI-assisted examination workflows.
What to monitor: Track developments in national IP strategies and industry engagement with standard-setting to align long-term IP positioning.

items.title: Successful conclusion of second convergence of practice cycle
items.title_zh: EPO 成功完成第二轮专利审查实践趋同周期
items.title_en: EPO Adopts Common Practices on Broad Claims and Double Patenting
items.summary: The EPO has completed the second Convergence of Practice cycle with new common practices on Broad Claims and Double Patenting, strengthening alignment and legal certainty across the patent network.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布完成第二轮“实践趋同”计划，正式采纳关于“宽泛权利要求”和“重复专利”的共同实践准则。此举旨在统一欧洲专利网络内的审查标准，减少各国差异，提升法律确定性。新规则明确了过度宽泛权利要求的处理框架及重复专利异议的信息披露要求，为申请人提供更可预测的审查环境。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has concluded its second Convergence of Practice cycle, adopting new common practices regarding broad claims and double patenting. This initiative, a cornerstone of the EPO's Strategic Plan 2028, aims to harmonize examination standards across the European Patent Network, thereby enhancing legal certainty for users. Working Group 11 established a framework for identifying overly broad claims during the search phase, while Working Group 12 clarified the information requirements for double patenting objections. These measures seek to reduce procedural divergences among national offices and improve synergy between first and second filing procedures. The third work cycle is now underway, focusing on divisional applications and internet disclosures.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请需关注审查标准趋同，避免因权利要求过宽或重复布局被驳回。建议提前研判新指南，优化撰写策略以适配统一审查口径，降低合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The adoption of these common practices signals a move toward greater procedural uniformity within the European Patent Network. For rights holders and applicants, this reduces the risk of unexpected divergences in examination outcomes across different contracting states. The clarified approach to broad claims and double patenting objections provides a more transparent basis for responding to office actions. As the EPO continues to drive alignment through its Strategic Plan 2028, monitoring the implementation of these practices by national offices will be essential for maintaining consistent portfolio management strategies across Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 采纳宽泛权利要求与重复专利两项共同实践准则", "新框架旨在统一审查标准，增强欧洲专利网络法律确定性", "第三轮工作已启动，聚焦分案申请与互联网披露议题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New common practices on broad claims define frameworks for handling overly broad claims during the search phase.", "Double patenting guidelines clarify required examiner information for objections to ensure predictable outcomes.", "The third convergence cycle has commenced, addressing divisional applications and internet disclosure practices."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注宽泛权利要求新界定，优化专利撰写以避免审查障碍", "利用趋同红利预判审查走向，提升在欧洲各国的获权效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhanced legal certainty and reduced procedural divergences benefit applicants seeking protection across multiple EPC states.", "Practice note: Review claim drafting strategies against the new framework for overly broad claims to align with updated search phase expectations.", "What to monitor: Track national office updates on the Convergence Dashboard regarding the implementation of double patenting objection standards."]
core_points_en: New common practices on broad claims define frameworks for handling overly broad claims during the search phase.
Double patenting guidelines clarify required examiner information for objections to ensure predictable outcomes.
The third convergence cycle has commenced, addressing divisional applications and internet disclosure practices.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enhanced legal certainty and reduced procedural divergences benefit applicants seeking protection across multiple EPC states.
Practice note: Review claim drafting strategies against the new framework for overly broad claims to align with updated search phase expectations.
What to monitor: Track national office updates on the Convergence Dashboard regarding the implementation of double patenting objection standards.

items.title: Results of EQE paper F 2026 available
items.title_zh: 2026 年欧洲专利代理人资格考试 F 卷成绩公布
items.title_en: EPO Publishes 2026 EQE Paper F Results Amidst Modular Reform Transition
items.summary: Less than two weeks after the European Qualifying Examination (EQE) 2026 session concluded on 12 March 2026, the results of paper F have been distributed to around 650 candidates.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局公布 2026 年 EQE 基础卷（F 卷）成绩，约 650 名考生参试，通过率约 84%。作为考试模块化改革的关键一步，今年首次引入 M1/M2 卷并保留旧制试卷，标志着过渡期顺利完成。全数字化考试提升了效率，新架构旨在增强专利体系的包容性与可持续性，考生可按阶段评估能力并进阶。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has distributed results for the 2026 European Qualifying Examination (EQE) Paper F to approximately 650 candidates. The foundation paper, which assesses procedural patent law and claim analysis, achieved a pass rate of around 84%, with over 530 candidates succeeding. This release highlights the efficiency of the fully digital examination system introduced in 2021. The 2026 session marked a significant milestone as the final year legacy papers were offered alongside new modular components M1 and M2. This transitional phase supports the EPO's progressive structural reform aimed at enhancing quality, accessibility, and sustainability within the European patent profession.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局需关注代理人资质新规，模块化考试可能改变服务供给节奏。建议提前规划专利代理合作，利用早期模块评估潜在合作伙伴能力，确保在欧知识产权事务由具备最新资质的专业人士处理，以应对更严格的执业标准。
items.ai_insight_en: The progression toward a fully modular EQE structure alters the timeline for qualifying as a European patent attorney. For companies and firms relying on external counsel or building internal teams, understanding these phased competency assessments is crucial for talent acquisition and service provider evaluation. The early benchmarking capability allows for more structured career development paths for trainees, potentially impacting the availability of junior versus senior representatives in the market during the transition period.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, March 20, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1849/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院：诉讼语言变更裁定的多因素平衡框架
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Defendant’s Position Prevails in Language Change Balancing Test
items.summary: The President applied the Court of Appeal’s multi-factor framework, considering case-related circumstances (predominant language in the technology field...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫地方法院在HyGear诉Topsoe案中，应用上诉法院的多因素框架裁定诉讼语言变更。法院权衡技术与当事人相关情形，确立若双方利益相当，因原告享有择院灵活性，被告立场具有决定性。加速程序中，非德语被告的翻译负担成为改用英语的重要考量。慕尼黑工业大学仅凭一般原则提出的异议被驳回。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Düsseldorf applied the Court of Appeal’s multi-factor framework to rule on a language change request. The President determined that where balancing relevant circumstances yields comparable interests, the defendant’s position is decisive under Article 49(5) UPCA and Rule 323 RoP. Factors included the predominant technology language, exhibit languages, and internal working languages. In accelerated proceedings, the burden of translation costs and delays for a non-German-speaking defendant carried significant weight, supporting a switch from German to English. The claimant’s choice of patent language was not deemed inherently unfair, but specific negative impacts on the defendant justified the change. This decision clarifies how procedural fairness is weighed when parties face unequal linguistic burdens during urgent litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC诉讼语言变更的审查标准，强调被告在加速程序中的语言便利性权重。实务中需注意，若双方情况均衡，法院倾向于保护处于被动地位的被告。建议在答辩状提交前提出语言变更请求，并具体论证翻译负担对防御能力的实际影响，而非仅援引一般原则。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides clear guidance on managing linguistic disadvantages in UPC litigation. Parties facing non-native proceedings should actively argue that translation burdens impair their defense capabilities, particularly in accelerated cases. It is crucial to file language change requests before lodging the Statement of Defence, aligning with consistent Court of Appeal recommendations. While claimants retain forum and timing flexibility, defendants can leverage strict deadlines and cost implications to seek equitable procedural adjustments. Monitoring this approach helps ensure fair participation regardless of the initial language chosen by the patent holder or claimant.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院应用多因素框架，若利益平衡后情形相当，因原告有择院优势，被告立场具决定性", "加速程序中，非德语被告面临的翻译成本与延误负担，是支持将语言变更为英语的重要因素", "仅依据申请人有权选择德语及单一被告非德国注册的事实，不足以证明对己方地位有具体负面影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Language changes require balancing case and party circumstances; if interests are equal, the defendant’s position prevails.", "In accelerated proceedings, translation costs and delays for non-native speakers are critical factors favoring language changes.", "Claimants choosing the patent language do not automatically prevent changes if specific unfairness to the defendant is demonstrated."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确被告在利益平衡时的优先地位，因其受严格期限约束且无择院权", "加速程序中，翻译负担对非母语被告的影响被赋予更高权重，利于申请改用英语", "需持续观察法院对“具体负面影响”的证明标准要求，避免仅依赖抽象权利主张"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage this precedent to argue for language changes in accelerated proceedings, emphasizing translation costs and delays.", "Practice note: File language change applications strictly before submitting the Statement of Defence to maximize procedural rights.", "What to monitor: How courts balance claimant flexibility against defendant burdens in future multi-factor language disputes."]
core_points_en: Language changes require balancing case and party circumstances; if interests are equal, the defendant’s position prevails.
In accelerated proceedings, translation costs and delays for non-native speakers are critical factors favoring language changes.
Claimants choosing the patent language do not automatically prevent changes if specific unfairness to the defendant is demonstrated.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage this precedent to argue for language changes in accelerated proceedings, emphasizing translation costs and delays.
Practice note: File language change applications strictly before submitting the Statement of Defence to maximize procedural rights.
What to monitor: How courts balance claimant flexibility against defendant burdens in future multi-factor language disputes.

items.title: CD Munich, March 24, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_2296/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑裁定：初步异议范围穷尽且不含诉讼资格抗辩
items.title_en: UPC Munich: Rule 19.1 Preliminary Objections Exhaustive; Standing and Res Judicata Are Merits Issues
items.summary: The court confirmed, in line with the Court of Appeal’s decisions in Aylo v. DISH/SLING (UPC_CoA_188/2024) and Roku/Sun (UPC_CoA_288/2025)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院慕尼黑中央分部作出程序令，确认规则 19.1 所列初步异议清单具有穷尽性，排除诉讼资格缺失或既判力等实体抗辩。法院指出此类问题需经事实评估，不属“明显”不可受理情形，应合并审理以提升程序效率，避免单独听证。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Central Division in Munich issued a procedural order confirming that the list of preliminary objections under Rule 19.1 RoP is exhaustive, limited strictly to jurisdiction, divisional competence, and language. The court rejected the defendant’s attempts to raise lack of standing and res judicata as preliminary objections, classifying them as substantive defences requiring merits assessment. Consequently, anticipatory orders under Rules 361 and 362 RoP were deemed inapplicable as these issues were not manifestly inadmissible. Prioritizing procedural economy, the court denied a separate hearing on admissibility, opting instead for a single combined hearing with the merits.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 应诉时，不宜将诉讼资格等实体问题作为初步异议提出，以免被驳回并浪费程序机会。应聚焦管辖权等法定异议点，并准备在实质审理阶段充分举证经济利益关联，以应对资格挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the strict boundary between procedural admissibility and substantive validity at the UPC. Parties must ensure that preliminary objections align precisely with Rule 19.1 grounds to avoid dismissal. Arguments regarding standing based on corporate structures or prior national judgments should be prepared as part of the main defence strategy on the merits, rather than relying on early procedural dismissal mechanisms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["规则 19.1 初步异议仅限管辖权、分庭能力及语言，不得扩展至实体抗辩", "诉讼资格与既判力涉及事实审查，不符合规则 361/362 条“明显”不可受理标准", "基于程序经济原则，法院拒绝就可受理性举行单独听证，主张合并审理"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 19.1 RoP preliminary objections are exhaustive and exclude substantive defences like lack of standing or res judicata.", "Lack of standing and res judicata require factual and legal assessment, preventing their treatment as manifestly inadmissible under Rules 361 and 362 RoP.", "Procedural efficiency dictates a single hearing combining admissibility and merits rather than separate preliminary hearings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["初步异议策略需严格限定于管辖权等法定事由，避免混同实体抗辩", "针对诉讼资格质疑，应提前梳理子公司股权链条及经济利益证据以备实质审理"]

Lack of standing and res judicata require factual and legal assessment, preventing their treatment as manifestly inadmissible under Rules 361 and 362 RoP.
Procedural efficiency dictates a single hearing combining admissibility and merits rather than separate preliminary hearings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and defendants must distinguish between procedural jurisdiction challenges and substantive standing arguments to avoid wasting preliminary objecti…
Practice note: Prepare evidence regarding economic interest and corporate links for the merits phase, as standing disputes will not be resolved via anticipatory orders unless mani…
What to monitor: Upcoming Court of Appeal rulings on res judicata, which may further refine how prior national decisions impact UPC nullity actions.

items.title: UPC: Litigation Insurance Relevant to Security for Costs – Syntorr v Arthrex | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院：诉讼保险应纳入费用担保考量
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Litigation Insurance Must Be Considered for Security for Costs
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal holds that litigation insurance must be considered when deciding whether to order security for costs, overturning the Munich Local Division.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院在 Syntorr 诉 Arthrex 案中推翻慕尼黑地方分院裁定，确立诉讼保险政策是决定是否命令提供费用担保时必须考虑的因素。法院指出，虽然规则未直接将保险列为担保形式，但在评估原告财务状况及被告成本回收风险时，必须将保险作为自由裁量的重要依据，修正了此前仅接受存款或银行担保的狭隘观点。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal overturned a Munich Local Division order requiring Syntorr to provide €2,000,000 in security for costs. The appellate court held that litigation insurance policies must be considered when exercising discretion under Article 69(4) UPCA and Rule 158.1 RoP. While the lower division ruled that insurance does not constitute valid security, the Court of Appeal clarified that such policies are relevant factors in assessing whether a claimant's financial position creates a legitimate concern regarding the recoverability of legal costs. The review standard is limited to errors in the exercise of judicial discretion.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉诉时应积极利用诉讼保险降低被责令高额现金担保的风险。需提前规划保险方案，将其作为应对对方费用担保申请的核心抗辩理由，优化资金流动性并提升诉讼防御能力。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the scope of judicial discretion regarding security for costs at the UPC. Rights holders and defendants must now account for litigation insurance coverage when assessing the risk of cost orders. It signals that financial assessments will look beyond liquid assets to include contractual risk transfer mechanisms, potentially lowering barriers for well-insured claimants while maintaining protection for defendants against unrecoverable costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉院认定诉讼保险是评估费用担保必要性的关键因素", "推翻慕尼黑分院拒绝将保险纳入考量的原审裁定", "明确法院在行使自由裁量权时需综合评估财务风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal established that litigation insurance is a mandatory factor when determining if security for costs should be granted.", "Rule 158.1 RoP allows courts discretion to order security via deposit or bank guarantee, but does not exclude insurance from financial assessment.", "Appellate review focuses on whether the lower court misused or exceeded its discretion in evaluating the claimant's ability to cover costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用诉讼保险抗辩高额费用担保请求，缓解资金压力", "提前布局保险策略，增强在 UPC 诉讼中的财务防御力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Well-insured claimants may face reduced requirements for cash deposits or bank guarantees when opposing security for costs applications.", "Practice note: Legal teams should proactively disclose and analyze litigation insurance terms during preliminary cost security disputes to influence judicial discretion.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes adequate insurance coverage and how courts weigh policy limits against potential cost awards."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal established that litigation insurance is a mandatory factor when determining if security for costs should be granted.
Rule 158.1 RoP allows courts discretion to order security via deposit or bank guarantee, but does not exclude insurance from financial assessment.
Appellate review focuses on whether the lower court misused or exceeded its discretion in evaluating the claimant's ability to cover costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Well-insured claimants may face reduced requirements for cash deposits or bank guarantees when opposing security for costs applications.
Practice note: Legal teams should proactively disclose and analyze litigation insurance terms during preliminary cost security disputes to influence judicial discretion.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes adequate insurance coverage and how courts weigh policy limits against potential cost awards.

items.title: UPC Damages Claims: National Law Applies to Pre‑UPC Losses – Fives v REEL | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：UPC启动前损失适用国内法且驳回索赔
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg: National Law Governs Pre-UPC Losses; €6.5m Claim Rejected for Insufficient Proof
items.summary: The Hamburg Local Division confirms that national law governs loss‑of‑profit claims for events completed before the UPC began, and rejects a €6.5m damages claim for lack of proof.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院汉堡地方分庭确认，对于UPC启动前已完成事件造成的利润损失索赔，适用国内法。在Fives诉REEL案中，法院驳回了650万欧元的损害赔偿请求，理由是缺乏证据证明该折扣直接由侵权导致。此前德国杜塞尔多夫法院已认定侵权成立，UPC上诉法院亦确认其有权审理此类后续损害赔偿量化诉讼。
items.ai_summary_en: The Hamburg Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) ruled that national law governs claims for loss of profit arising from events completed before the UPC entered into force. In Fives v REEL, the court addressed damages for infringing offers made in late 2016 regarding a Bahrain aluminium project tender. Although the Court of Appeal confirmed the UPC’s competence to quantify damages following prior national infringement findings, the Local Division rejected the claimant’s €6.5 million damages request. The claimant argued that a price reduction in its successful 2017 bid reflected lost profits caused by the defendant’s earlier infringing offer. However, the court found insufficient evidence establishing a causal link between the defendant’s conduct and the alleged financial loss, leading to the dismissal of the claim under applicable national legal standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在处理跨时期侵权案件时的法律适用界限：启动前行为引发的损失需回归国内法体系举证。实务中需特别注意，即便侵权已在国家法院确立，在UPC阶段主张历史损失仍面临严格的因果关系证明标准，单纯的价格调整未必能被认定为可获赔的利润损失。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights critical procedural and evidentiary considerations for rights holders seeking damages at the UPC for historical infringements. While the UPC can assume jurisdiction over damage quantification post-national judgment, it strictly applies national substantive law to pre-UPC events. Parties must ensure robust evidence chains linking infringing conduct directly to alleged losses. Mere assertions of market impact are insufficient without concrete proof of causation. Litigants should anticipate rigorous scrutiny of economic arguments and prepare detailed forensic analyses early in proceedings to satisfy the high burden of proof required for substantial damages awards under varying national regimes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["汉堡地方分庭确认UPC启动前的利润损失索赔适用国内法而非UPCA统一规则", "法院驳回650万欧元赔偿请求，因原告未能证明降价与侵权行为间的因果关系", "UPC上诉法院此前裁定，UPC有权在国家法院认定侵权后单独审理损害赔偿量化"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC retains competence to separately adjudicate and quantify damages after national courts have established patent infringement liability.", "For infringing acts occurring before the UPC became operational, the calculation of damages is governed by the relevant member state's national law.", "The claim failed because the plaintiff could not prove the necessary causal link between the infringing offer and the specific financial losses claimed."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC启动前的侵权行为所致损失，其赔偿计算与举证需遵循相关成员国国内法", "在UPC程序中主张基于投标降价的利润损失，需提供严密的因果关系证据链", "需持续观察UPC如何处理国家法院既判力与UPC损害赔偿量化程序之间的衔接细节"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Damages for pre-UPC infringements are subject to national law, requiring tailored legal strategies for cross-border portfolios.", "Practice note: Secure comprehensive evidence early to prove direct causation between infringement and specific financial losses.", "What to monitor: How different national laws interpret causation and loss calculation in UPC proceedings involving historical acts."]
core_points_en: The UPC retains competence to separately adjudicate and quantify damages after national courts have established patent infringement liability.
For infringing acts occurring before the UPC became operational, the calculation of damages is governed by the relevant member state's national law.
The claim failed because the plaintiff could not prove the necessary causal link between the infringing offer and the specific financial losses claimed.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Damages for pre-UPC infringements are subject to national law, requiring tailored legal strategies for cross-border portfolios.
Practice note: Secure comprehensive evidence early to prove direct causation between infringement and specific financial losses.
What to monitor: How different national laws interpret causation and loss calculation in UPC proceedings involving historical acts.

items.title: LD Hamburg, March 24, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_1049/2025
items.title_zh: 汉堡地方法院程序令：适用诉讼规则版本以起诉日为准
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg: Applicable Rules of Procedure Determined by Filing Date, Not Subsequent Steps
items.summary: The applicable version of the RoP is determined by the filing date of the action, not by the date of subsequent procedural steps (Art. 5 of the Administrative Committee’s decision of 4 November 2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院汉堡地方法院在BTL诉Lexter案中发布程序令。法院依据行政委员会决定，确认适用《程序规则》的版本取决于起诉日期而非后续程序步骤日期。因原告在书面程序结束前撤诉，法院裁定依据2025版规则退还60%诉讼费。该案明确了新旧规则过渡期的适用标准及退费条件。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Hamburg clarified that the applicable version of the Rules of Procedure is determined strictly by the filing date of the action, rather than the timing of subsequent procedural steps. This interpretation aligns with Article 5 of the Administrative Committee’s decision dated 4 November 2025. Consequently, actions filed before 31 December 2025 remain governed by the prior 2025 version of the Rules, including the original Table of Court Fees and Guidelines for recoverable costs. The revised rules apply only to actions initiated after this cutoff date. In this specific infringement case, the claimant withdrew the action before the closure of the written procedure. The Court granted a 60% reimbursement of court fees pursuant to Rule 370.9(b)(i) of the 2025 Rules. This ruling confirms the validity of the transitional provisions and provides clear guidance on cost recovery mechanisms for cases lodged during the early operational phase of the Court.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC诉讼中程序规则版本适用的时间基准，即“起诉日”原则。对于在规则过渡期内的案件，当事人需重点关注起诉时间以确定适用的诉讼费标准及成本回收指南。此外，早期撤诉可获显著费用减免，提示当事人在评估诉讼策略时应综合考量程序节点对成本的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes a critical precedent regarding the temporal application of procedural rules at the Unified Patent Court. Litigants must precisely identify the filing date to determine whether the older or newer fee structures and cost guidelines apply. For cases initiated under the previous regime, strategic withdrawal before the conclusion of the written procedure offers a significant opportunity to recover sixty percent of court fees. Parties should carefully evaluate the merits of their claims early in the process. If prospects appear uncertain, timely withdrawal can mitigate financial exposure under the legacy fee schedule. Conversely, new filings after the cutoff date face updated cost implications. Understanding these transitional mechanics is essential for effective budget management and risk assessment in UPC litigation strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["适用《程序规则》版本由起诉日决定，非后续程序步骤日期", "年12月31日后起诉的案件才适用修订后的诉讼费表及成本指南", "书面程序结束前终止诉讼的，依据规则第370.9条退还60%诉讼费"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The applicable Rules of Procedure version depends on the action's filing date, not later procedural dates.", "Actions filed before 31 December 2025 remain subject to the prior 2025 Rules regarding fees and costs.", "A 60% court fee reimbursement is granted if an action terminates before the written procedure closes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确程序规则版本适用以起诉日为界，影响过渡期案件的成本预期", "书面程序结束前撤诉可获六成退费，凸显早期结案的经济激励", "需持续观察行政委员会关于诉讼费及可回收成本指南的后续修订动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accurate determination of the filing date is essential to predict applicable fee structures and cost recovery options.", "Practice note: Evaluate case strength early; withdrawing before the written procedure closes maximizes fee reimbursement under prior rules.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Article 5 regarding transitional periods and potential adjustments to cost guidelines."]
core_points_en: The applicable Rules of Procedure version depends on the action's filing date, not later procedural dates.
Actions filed before 31 December 2025 remain subject to the prior 2025 Rules regarding fees and costs.
A 60% court fee reimbursement is granted if an action terminates before the written procedure closes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accurate determination of the filing date is essential to predict applicable fee structures and cost recovery options.
Practice note: Evaluate case strength early; withdrawing before the written procedure closes maximizes fee reimbursement under prior rules.
What to monitor: Future applications of Article 5 regarding transitional periods and potential adjustments to cost guidelines.

items.title: Wanneer wordt een bewegingsmerk geweigerd wegens technische functionaliteit?
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：纯技术功能动态商标将被驳回
items.title_en: EU General Court Refuses Motion Mark for Hinged Window Due to Technical Functionality
items.summary: In January 2026, the General Court of the EU refused the application for a motion mark of a hinged window. The General Court considers that the movement consists only of a technical functional process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 1 月，欧盟普通法院维持 EUIPO 决定，驳回德国 Kct 公司关于折叠窗开合动画的动态商标注册申请。法院认定该运动序列仅由技术功能过程组成，缺乏显著性且属于《欧盟商标条例》禁止注册的技术解决方案范畴，旨在防止通过商标权无限期垄断技术特征。
items.ai_summary_en: In January 2026, the General Court of the European Union upheld the refusal of a motion mark application filed by Kct GmbH & Co. The mark consisted of an animation depicting the opening and closing mechanism of a hinged window. The Court determined that the movement represented solely a technical functional process necessary to achieve a specific result. Consequently, the application was rejected under Article 7(1)(e)(ii) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation, which prohibits registration of signs consisting exclusively of characteristics required to obtain a technical result. This decision reinforces the principle that trademark law cannot be used to secure a perpetual monopoly on technical solutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the boundary between protectable brand identity and unprotectable technical function for dynamic trademarks. Rights holders must ensure that motion marks include distinctive, non-functional visual elements beyond the mere depiction of a product's operational mechanics. Failure to distinguish aesthetic choices from technical necessities will likely lead to absolute grounds for refusal based on technical functionality.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["动态商标若仅展示产品技术运作过程，将被视为缺乏显著性", "欧盟法律禁止注册仅为获取技术结果所必需的形状或特征", "法院强调需区分功能性要素与装饰性要素以判定可注册性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court confirmed that motion marks depicting purely technical processes are excluded from registration under EU trademark law.", "Article 7(1)(e)(ii) prevents trademark protection for features essential to achieving a technical result, safeguarding against monopolies on technical solutions.", "Registration is only possible if the sign contains non-functional elements, such as decorative or fanciful features, that play a significant role."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免申请仅描述产品技术运作流程的纯功能性动态商标", "在设计中增加非功能性装饰元素以提升商标显著特征"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Applicants seeking motion mark protection must integrate unique, non-functional visual elements to avoid rejection based on technical functionality.", "Practice note: Conduct a thorough assessment to separate essential technical features from decorative aspects when drafting motion mark specifications and representations.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU decisions regarding the threshold for 'non-functional' elements in dynamic and multimedia trademark applications."]
core_points_en: The General Court confirmed that motion marks depicting purely technical processes are excluded from registration under EU trademark law.
Article 7(1)(e)(ii) prevents trademark protection for features essential to achieving a technical result, safeguarding against monopolies on technical solutions.
Registration is only possible if the sign contains non-functional elements, such as decorative or fanciful features, that play a significant role.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Applicants seeking motion mark protection must integrate unique, non-functional visual elements to avoid rejection based on technical functionality.
Practice note: Conduct a thorough assessment to separate essential technical features from decorative aspects when drafting motion mark specifications and representations.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU decisions regarding the threshold for 'non-functional' elements in dynamic and multimedia trademark applications.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal Issues Final Decision on Validity and Infringement | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院就专利有效性及侵权作出最终裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Overturns Revocation, Finds Patent Valid and Infringed Without Remittal
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal overturns a first‑instance revocation, finds the patent valid and infringed, and issues final remedies without remittal.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院推翻曼海姆地方法院撤销专利的决定，认定涉案专利有效且被侵权，并直接作出最终救济裁决而未发回重审。该案涉及Rematec与Europe Forestry关于研磨机专利EP 2 548 648的争议。一审曾基于现有技术D3认定缺乏新颖性而无效，但上诉法院依据UPCA第75(1)条行使权力，对未决问题作出终局判断。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal has overturned the Mannheim Local Division’s decision to revoke European Patent EP 2 548 648 and dismiss infringement claims. In a final judgment issued on 17 February 2026, the appellate court found the patent valid and infringed by Europe Forestry B.V., ordering remedies without remitting the case to the first instance. This approach aligns with Article 75(1) of the Unified Patent Court Agreement, which empowers the Court of Appeal to issue final decisions even when addressing issues not fully resolved below. The core dispute centered on the novelty of claim features defining essentially radial feed-in and discharge openings in a woodchip grinding mill. The Court of Appeal accepted that this specific arrangement provided a technical advantage over top-fed designs, thereby supporting the patentee’s validity arguments and establishing infringement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显UPC上诉法院在特定情形下直接作出最终裁决的程序倾向，避免案件发回重审带来的不确定性。对于涉及复杂技术特征新颖性争议的当事人，需关注上诉阶段对事实认定的重新评估权限及终局性裁决的适用条件，这对诉讼策略与风险管控具有参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the strategic importance of the UPC appeal stage, where parties can potentially reverse unfavorable first-instance outcomes on both validity and infringement. Litigants should prepare robust technical arguments regarding novel features early in proceedings, as the Court of Appeal may resolve these issues definitively. The trend toward final appellate judgments without remittal emphasizes the need for comprehensive evidence and legal reasoning at all levels. Parties facing adverse initial rulings must carefully assess the viability of appealing on substantive technical grounds, particularly where distinct mechanical arrangements are central to the patent’s inventive step.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院推翻一审撤销决定，认定专利有效且侵权，直接裁定救济措施", "法院依据UPCA第75(1)条，即使需审理一审未决事项，仍作出最终裁决而不发回", "争议焦点为进料与出料口径向布置特征，一审曾据此认定缺乏新颖性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal reversed the first-instance revocation, ruling the patent valid and infringed without sending the case back for retrial.", "Article 75(1) UPCA was applied to allow the appellate court to decide on validity and infringement directly, issuing final remedies immediately.", "Novelty hinged on the radial orientation of feed-in and discharge openings in the lower grinding region, distinguishing it from prior art."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院倾向于依据规则直接作出终局裁决，减少程序拖延", "技术特征的新颖性认定在二审中可能被重新评估并推翻一审结论", "需持续观察UPC在处理未决事实问题时的司法实践与裁量标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The appellate stage offers a critical opportunity to overturn adverse first-instance decisions on validity and infringement.", "Practice note: Ensure technical features, such as specific mechanical arrangements, are thoroughly analyzed for novelty to support appellate arguments.", "What to monitor: The increasing tendency of the UPC Court of Appeal to issue final judgments on merits without remitting cases to local divisions."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal reversed the first-instance revocation, ruling the patent valid and infringed without sending the case back for retrial.
Article 75(1) UPCA was applied to allow the appellate court to decide on validity and infringement directly, issuing final remedies immediately.
Novelty hinged on the radial orientation of feed-in and discharge openings in the lower grinding region, distinguishing it from prior art.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The appellate stage offers a critical opportunity to overturn adverse first-instance decisions on validity and infringement.
Practice note: Ensure technical features, such as specific mechanical arrangements, are thoroughly analyzed for novelty to support appellate arguments.
What to monitor: The increasing tendency of the UPC Court of Appeal to issue final judgments on merits without remitting cases to local divisions.

items.title: IP FAQ: Which trademark symbol should I use?
items.title_zh: 知识产权问答：应使用哪种商标符号？
items.title_en: Trademark Symbols Guide: Understanding TM, SM, and R Usage in Europe
items.summary: What the "TM" and circled "R" symbols mean, and why businesses need them.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析TM、SM及®符号的法律含义与适用场景。TM通常用于未注册商标以主张权利，®则确认已在国家或区域机构（如EUIPO）完成正式注册。正确使用符号有助于展示保护状态，错误使用可能导致虚假广告指控或削弱法律保护。
items.ai_summary_en: This article clarifies the legal meanings and appropriate usage scenarios for trademark symbols. The superscript TM symbol indicates a claim to rights for an unregistered mark used in commerce, applicable to both goods and services in many jurisdictions. In contrast, the circled R symbol confirms that a trademark has received official registration from a competent authority, such as the EUIPO or USPTO. Misusing these symbols can lead to significant legal risks, including allegations of false advertising or fraud. The text highlights the distinction between limited common law protection available to unregistered marks and the stronger, geographically broader rights conferred by formal registration. It emphasizes that businesses operating in Europe must strictly adhere to local regulations regarding symbol usage to ensure brand integrity and avoid compliance issues.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需严格区分未注册与已注册状态的符号标识，避免合规风险。虽然主要解释通用规则，但明确引用EUIPO作为注册权威机构，提示企业在欧盟市场运营时应依据注册状态准确标注，以维护品牌资产的法律确定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating within the European market must rigorously distinguish between registered and unregistered trademark statuses when selecting branding symbols. Using the circled R symbol before securing formal registration with the EUIPO constitutes a violation of local laws and may be interpreted as misleading commercial practice. To mitigate these risks, organizations should implement robust internal compliance mechanisms that dynamically track trademark application progress. This ensures that symbol usage accurately reflects the current legal status of each brand element. Such diligence prevents potential enforcement challenges and protects the brand's defensive capabilities against infringement claims in European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["TM符号通常关联未在领土内注册的商标，表明用户试图主张该品牌元素的知识产权权利", "®符号确认商标已在商标局正式注册，相比未注册商标拥有更强的法律权利和更广的地理范围", "错误使用商标符号可能带来严重后果，包括削弱法律保护或面临虚假陈述、欺诈等指控风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The TM symbol asserts rights for unregistered trademarks used in commerce for goods or services.", "The registered R symbol confirms official trademark status with enhanced legal protection and scope.", "Incorrect symbol usage may trigger legal liabilities, including claims of deceptive advertising or fraud."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业应建立商标符号使用内部审查机制，确保®仅用于已获EUIPO或成员国核准的注册类别", "注意TM与®混用的法律风险，未注册阶段滥用®可能构成虚假宣传，影响后续维权效力", "关注不同法域对服务标记（SM）的接受度差异，欧盟地区通常通用TM涵盖商品与服务"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using the registered R symbol prior to EUIPO approval to prevent violations of unfair competition laws.", "Practice note: Establish internal review protocols to align trademark symbol usage with the real-time status of registration applications.", "What to monitor: Track changes in national enforcement practices regarding improper trademark symbol usage across EU member states."]
core_points_en: The TM symbol asserts rights for unregistered trademarks used in commerce for goods or services.
The registered R symbol confirms official trademark status with enhanced legal protection and scope.
Incorrect symbol usage may trigger legal liabilities, including claims of deceptive advertising or fraud.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using the registered R symbol prior to EUIPO approval to prevent violations of unfair competition laws.
Practice note: Establish internal review protocols to align trademark symbol usage with the real-time status of registration applications.
What to monitor: Track changes in national enforcement practices regarding improper trademark symbol usage across EU member states.

items.title: MAN v Rolls-Royce – How not to advertise your mark
items.title_zh: MAN诉劳斯莱斯：如何避免错误的商标广告宣传
items.title_en: MAN v EUIPO: How Advertising Can Undermine Trademark Distinctiveness
items.summary: John Wanamaker famously said Half the money I spend on advertising is wasted; the trouble is I don’t know which half. I cannot help you with this either, but based on the General Court’s recent decision in MAN Truck & Bus v EUIPO (Case T-108/25), it is not wise to spend money on advertising explaining the descriptive character of your own trademark.  Background MAN Truck & Bus SE (“MAN”)
items.ai_summary_zh: 基于欧盟普通法院在MAN Truck & Bus诉EUIPO案（T-108/25）中的最新判决，文章指出花费资金通过广告解释自身商标的描述性特征是不明智的。该案背景涉及MAN Truck & Bus SE，强调了在商标维护中广告策略与法律认定之间的潜在冲突。
items.ai_summary_en: Based on the General Court’s recent judgment in MAN Truck & Bus v EUIPO (Case T-108/25), this analysis highlights the significant legal risks associated with advertising campaigns that explicitly explain the descriptive nature of a trademark. The case demonstrates that when rights holders actively emphasize the descriptive meaning or functional characteristics of their marks in promotional materials, such content may serve as critical evidence in subsequent validity or cancellation proceedings. Consequently, this self-admission can lead to the legal negation of distinctiveness, potentially resulting in the revocation of the trademark registration. The decision serves as a cautionary example for brand owners, illustrating how marketing strategies must be carefully aligned with intellectual property maintenance goals to avoid inadvertently weakening the legal foundation of their registered rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注法院对商标广告内容与显著性认定之间关系的司法态度。企业在进行品牌宣传时，应避免通过解释性广告削弱商标的固有显著性，以免在后续确权或维权程序中产生不利事实认定。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating within the European Union, it is crucial to ensure that all external promotional materials undergo rigorous legal review before publication. Marketing teams should avoid copy that over-explains trademark meanings or emphasizes product functions, as such language can be exploited by competitors as evidence in invalidation actions. Establishing a robust coordination mechanism between legal counsel and marketing departments is essential. This alignment ensures that brand communication supports rather than undermines trademark distinctiveness, thereby protecting the integrity of the company's IP portfolio against potential challenges based on descriptiveness or lack of inherent character.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院在MAN诉EUIPO案（T-108/25）中作出最新判决", "通过广告解释商标的描述性特征被视为不明智的商业与法律策略", "案件当事方为MAN Truck & Bus SE与欧盟知识产权局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court ruled that advertising content constitutes key evidence for determining whether a trademark is descriptive.", "A company's own explanation of its mark's meaning during promotion can lead to the legal denial of its distinctiveness.", "Marketing strategies must remain strictly consistent with intellectual property objectives to ensure effective rights maintenance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法院对解释性广告削弱商标显著性的认定倾向", "商标持有人在市场推广中需平衡宣传效果与法律风险", "后续类似案件中广告证据对商标描述性认定的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid self-proving descriptiveness in ad copy to prevent creating legal vulnerabilities for the trademark.", "Practice note: Implement mandatory legal review mechanisms for all marketing materials to safeguard the trademark's rights basis.", "What to monitor: Ensure market behavior does not contradict the distinctiveness claims required for maintaining valid trademark protection."]
core_points_en: The General Court ruled that advertising content constitutes key evidence for determining whether a trademark is descriptive.
A company's own explanation of its mark's meaning during promotion can lead to the legal denial of its distinctiveness.
Marketing strategies must remain strictly consistent with intellectual property objectives to ensure effective rights maintenance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid self-proving descriptiveness in ad copy to prevent creating legal vulnerabilities for the trademark.
Practice note: Implement mandatory legal review mechanisms for all marketing materials to safeguard the trademark's rights basis.
What to monitor: Ensure market behavior does not contradict the distinctiveness claims required for maintaining valid trademark protection.

items.title: Accord PPH avec le Brésil
items.title_zh: 法国工业产权局与巴西达成专利审查高速路协议
items.title_en: INPI France and Brazil Launch Patent Prosecution Highway Agreement for Accelerated Examination
items.summary: Accord PPH avec le Brésil llegrand@inpi.fr 27 mars 2026 Les conditions pour utiliser le PPH Les déposants peuvent demander l'accélération du traitement de leur demande auprès de l'un des deux offices à partir du moment où elle aura été examinée par l'autre office, indépendamment de l'ordre chronologique des dépôts et des revendications de priorité. De manière classique, après dépôt d'une requête PPH, le traitement d'une demande de brevet déposée à l'INPI France et revendiquant la priorité d’une
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局（INPI）公布与巴西的专利审查高速路（PPH）协议细节。申请人可基于一方审查结果请求另一方加速处理，不受申请顺序限制。巴西方面对PPH请求实施数量上限管理，包括国别总额、PCT通道限额及IPC分类限额，并要求提供法国授权证明作为加速依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) has announced a Patent Prosecution Highway (PPH) agreement with the Brazilian National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI). This bilateral arrangement allows applicants to request accelerated examination in either jurisdiction based on work products from the other office, regardless of the chronological order of filings or priority claims. If a French application claims priority from a Brazilian application deemed patentable by Brazil, or vice versa, the second office may expedite processing provided the claims are sufficiently similar. However, the Brazilian office imposes strict quantitative limits on incoming PPH requests, including caps on total applications, PCT-based entries, and specific International Patent Classification sections. Applicants must also provide proof of patent grant in France when seeking acceleration at the Brazilian office.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该协议为法巴双边专利申请提供加速通道，但巴西侧设有严格的配额管理与形式要件。实务中需重点关注巴西方面的数量限制规则及授权证明要求，合理规划提交节奏以符合合规性约束。
items.ai_insight_en: This PPH framework offers significant efficiency gains for rights holders seeking protection in both European and Latin American markets. By leveraging positive examination outcomes from one office, applicants can reduce pendency times in the other. However, the operational utility is constrained by Brazil’s rigid quota system. Practitioners should prioritize early filing strategies to secure slots before annual or weekly limits are exhausted. Ensuring precise claim correspondence between jurisdictions is critical for eligibility. Companies should monitor quota availability closely and align their prosecution timelines to maximize the benefits of this accelerated pathway while navigating the administrative restrictions imposed by the Brazilian office.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PPH允许基于一方审查的可专利性结论，向另一方请求加速，权利要求需相同或更窄", "巴西限制PPH请求总量为800件/国，其中PCT通道限100件，每IPC大类限150件", "巴西要求每个申请人每周仅能提交1件PPH请求，且需提供法国专利授权证明"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The agreement enables bidirectional accelerated examination between France and Brazil, independent of filing sequence or priority claim order.", "Brazil limits total incoming PPH requests to 800 annually, with a specific cap of 100 applications via the Patent Cooperation Treaty pathway.", "Requests are further restricted by IPC section quotas and weekly submission limits per applicant, requiring French grant proof for entry."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["巴西对PPH请求实施多维度的数量限制，包括国别总量、PCT专项及IPC分类配额", "从巴西向法国发起PPH需满足常规可专利性对应要求，反之则需额外提供授权证明", "需持续监控巴西工业产权局关于PPH配额使用情况及具体操作指南的后续更新"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the bidirectional mechanism to optimize patent prosecution speed and resource allocation across French and Brazilian portfolios.", "Practice note: Ensure strict claim alignment between offices and submit PPH requests early to avoid missing out due to Brazil's strict numerical quotas.", "What to monitor: Track remaining PPH capacity under the Brazil agreement, particularly regarding IPC section limits and weekly submission windows."]
core_points_en: The agreement enables bidirectional accelerated examination between France and Brazil, independent of filing sequence or priority claim order.
Brazil limits total incoming PPH requests to 800 annually, with a specific cap of 100 applications via the Patent Cooperation Treaty pathway.
Requests are further restricted by IPC section quotas and weekly submission limits per applicant, requiring French grant proof for entry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the bidirectional mechanism to optimize patent prosecution speed and resource allocation across French and Brazilian portfolios.
Practice note: Ensure strict claim alignment between offices and submit PPH requests early to avoid missing out due to Brazil's strict numerical quotas.
What to monitor: Track remaining PPH capacity under the Brazil agreement, particularly regarding IPC section limits and weekly submission windows.

items.title: Transsion fires back at Ericsson at UPC; IPO filing offers recent SEP deal value insight
items.title_zh: 传音在 UPC 反击爱立信；IPO 文件揭示 SEP 交易价值
items.title_en: Transsion Counters Ericsson at UPC as IPO Filings Reveal Key SEP Deal Valuation Data
items.summary: Previously unknown details about Transsion’s patent agreements emerge as smartphone maker retaliates for the first time against Ericsson’s global enforcement campaign
items.ai_summary_zh: 智能手机制造商传音首次针对爱立信的全球执法行动发起反击，在统一专利法院（UPC）提起诉讼。同时，传音的 IPO 备案文件披露了其专利协议的未知细节，为近期标准必要专利（SEP）的许可费率与交易价值提供了关键市场洞察，反映了双方在 FRAND 原则下的博弈新态势。
items.ai_summary_en: Smartphone manufacturer Transsion has launched its first direct counter-action against Ericsson’s global enforcement campaign by filing a lawsuit at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). This strategic move marks a significant escalation in their ongoing dispute over standard-essential patents (SEPs). Concurrently, previously undisclosed details regarding Transsion’s existing patent licensing agreements have emerged through its initial public offering (IPO) filings. These documents provide critical market insights into recent SEP transaction values and licensing rates. The disclosure highlights the evolving dynamics between implementers and rights holders under FRAND principles, offering a rare glimpse into the financial realities of modern SEP negotiations and the increasing willingness of manufacturers to utilize European judicial mechanisms for active defense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国手机厂商应重视 IPO 披露中的许可数据风险，提前规划 SEP 谈判策略。面对爱立信等权利人的全球诉讼，需善用 UPC 机制进行积极抗辩，并利用公开交易数据争取更合理的 FRAND 费率，避免被动接受高价许可。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely analyze IPO disclosures for potential risks related to licensed IP data exposure. In response to global litigation campaigns by major rights holders like Ericsson, companies are advised to actively utilize UPC procedures for robust defense strategies. Furthermore, leveraging publicly available transaction data can strengthen negotiating positions to secure more reasonable FRAND rates, thereby avoiding passive acceptance of inflated licensing demands. This approach emphasizes proactive legal engagement and data-driven negotiation tactics in complex SEP disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["传音首次在 UPC 对爱立信发起专利侵权反诉", "IPO 文件意外披露了传音过往 SEP 许可协议细节", "新数据为评估当前 SEP 许可费率提供重要参考基准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Transsion initiates its first patent infringement counterclaim against Ericsson within the Unified Patent Court framework.", "IPO documentation unexpectedly reveals specific details of Transsion’s historical SEP licensing agreements and terms.", "New data points serve as an important reference benchmark for evaluating current SEP licensing rates and deal values."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 IPO 披露数据优化 SEP 许可谈判筹码与策略", "积极运用 UPC 程序应对跨国专利巨头的执法行动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage IPO disclosure data to optimize SEP licensing negotiation leverage and strategic positioning.", "Practice note: Actively employ UPC procedural mechanisms to counter enforcement actions by multinational patent holders.", "What to monitor: Emerging trends in FRAND rate determinations derived from public financial filings and court rulings."]
core_points_en: Transsion initiates its first patent infringement counterclaim against Ericsson within the Unified Patent Court framework.
IPO documentation unexpectedly reveals specific details of Transsion’s historical SEP licensing agreements and terms.
New data points serve as an important reference benchmark for evaluating current SEP licensing rates and deal values.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage IPO disclosure data to optimize SEP licensing negotiation leverage and strategic positioning.
Practice note: Actively employ UPC procedural mechanisms to counter enforcement actions by multinational patent holders.
What to monitor: Emerging trends in FRAND rate determinations derived from public financial filings and court rulings.

items.title: George Orwell als merk? EUIPO zegt nee
items.title_zh: 乔治·奥威尔能否注册商标？欧盟知识产权局说不
items.title_en: EUIPO Rejects George Orwell Trademark: Famous Author Names Lack Distinctiveness for Media Services
items.summary: De Uitgebreide Raad van Beroep van het Europese Merkenbureau (IEUIPO) heeft recent een langverwachte uitspraak gedaan in een zaak over de vraag of de naam van een beroemde...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局扩大上诉委员会裁定，拒绝“GEORGE ORWELL”在数字媒体、娱乐及教育服务等类别的欧盟商标注册申请。裁决指出，鉴于作者知名度及文化影响，相关公众将该名称视为对商品内容的直接描述，缺乏显著性。版权状态及申请人身份不影响该基于公众感知的判断。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO Board of Appeal has refused registration of the mark GEORGE ORWELL for digital media, printed publications, and educational services. The Board determined that the relevant public perceives the name as a direct reference to the author’s content rather than an indicator of commercial origin. Consequently, the mark lacks distinctiveness under EU trademark law. This decision clarifies that while famous names are not automatically barred from registration, they face refusal if viewed descriptively regarding subject matter. Crucially, the ruling emphasizes that copyright status or the applicant’s identity does not influence this assessment. The outcome depends solely on public perception, establishing clear boundaries for registering cultural figures’ names in related sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了文化名人在媒体及教育服务领域申请商标的显著性门槛。实务中需重点评估姓名在目标市场的认知度及其与指定服务的关联性强弱。仅凭姓名本身难以在描述性强的类别获准注册，需结合具体市场证据进行个案抗辩。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights significant risks when using names of culturally significant Western figures as trademarks, particularly in publishing and media sectors. Brands must conduct rigorous distinctiveness searches and risk assessments to ensure marks serve as source identifiers rather than descriptive content labels. Relying on famous names without established secondary meaning often leads to refusal based on descriptiveness. Companies should prioritize developing unique brand identities that avoid immediate association with specific authors or cultural icons. Proactive evaluation of public perception is essential to prevent registration failures and optimize intellectual property investment strategies within the European Union market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局扩大上诉委员会维持对“GEORGE ORWELL”商标在广泛商品和服务上…", "裁决确立主题分析标准：若公众将名人姓名视为对商品内容或主题的指示，则缺乏显著性", "作者的版权状态、申请人身份或其与遗产的关系，均非判定商标显著性的决定性因素"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Board found GEORGE ORWELL descriptive for media and education services, lacking inherent distinctiveness.", "Refusal relies on public perception; names seen as content indicators cannot function as source identifiers.", "Applicant identity and copyright status are irrelevant to the objective assessment of trademark distinctiveness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["名人姓名在媒体/教育类服务上易被认定为描述性标识，注册难度显著增加", "显著性判断核心在于相关公众感知，而非申请人的权利来源或版权状态", "需持续监测EUIPO对文化人物姓名在不同商品类别上的显著性认定尺度变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid high-profile Western celebrity names in media sectors to prevent descriptive refusals.", "Practice note: Strengthen distinctiveness searches and compliance risk assessments for cultural goods trademarks.", "What to monitor: How EUIPO applies subject-matter analysis to other cultural figures and literary references."]
core_points_en: The Board found GEORGE ORWELL descriptive for media and education services, lacking inherent distinctiveness.
Refusal relies on public perception; names seen as content indicators cannot function as source identifiers.
Applicant identity and copyright status are irrelevant to the objective assessment of trademark distinctiveness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid high-profile Western celebrity names in media sectors to prevent descriptive refusals.
Practice note: Strengthen distinctiveness searches and compliance risk assessments for cultural goods trademarks.
What to monitor: How EUIPO applies subject-matter analysis to other cultural figures and literary references.

items.title: EPO hosts annual bilateral meeting with AIPLA
items.title_zh: EPO与AIPLA举行年度双边会议
items.title_en: EPO and AIPLA Discuss Unitary Patent Uptake, Quality Plan, and Pending Referral G1/25
items.summary: The EPO and AIPLA strengthen collaboration through exchanges on quality, digital transformation, the Unitary Patent, the UPC and developments in EPO practice
items.ai_summary_zh: 3月26日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在海牙总部接待美国知识产权法协会（AIPLA）代表团。双方就专利质量、数字化转型、统一专利（UP）及统一专利法院（UPC）进展展开对话。EPO强调通过“2026质量行动计划”提升审查一致性与法律确定性。数据显示，2025年美国申请人提交的UP请求增长28%，占非EPC申请人的16.6%。会议还讨论了待决转介G1/25及抗体发明审查实践。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) and the American Intellectual Property Law Association (AIPLA) held their annual bilateral meeting in The Hague on 26 March 2026. Discussions centered on the EPO’s Quality Action Plan 2026, digital transformation initiatives, and legal developments, including the pending referral G1/25 regarding description adaptation. The parties highlighted the growing strategic relevance of the Unitary Patent (UP) and Unified Patent Court (UPC). Data presented indicated that US applicants remain the largest group of non-EPC filers for UPs, accounting for 16.6% of requests following a 28% increase in uptake during 2025. The dialogue also covered recent changes in examination practice for antibody-related inventions and the continued strong use of the EPO validation system by US rights holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The significant rise in Unitary Patent filings by US applicants signals increasing confidence in the unitary system among transatlantic rights holders. For practitioners, the EPO’s emphasis on the Quality Action Plan 2026 and specific updates on antibody examination suggest a continued focus on legal certainty and technical precision. Monitoring the outcome of referral G1/25 is essential, as it may impact drafting standards for description adaptation across European portfolios.

core_points_en: US applicants filed 16.6% of Unitary Patent requests in 2025, marking a twenty-eight percent increase in uptake.
EPO outlined its Quality Action Plan 2026 to enhance examiner expertise, digital tools, and procedural consistency.
Discussions addressed pending referral G1/25 on description adaptation and updated examination practices for antibody inventions.
