items.title: 12 minutes with EPO President António Campinos
items.title_zh: EPO 主席坎皮诺斯谈欧洲竞争力与创新格局
items.title_en: EPO President Campinos: Patent Demand Hits Record, Single Market Fragmentation Persists
items.summary: European Patent Office (EPO) President António Campinos appeared on the Euronews programme “12 Minutes With” to discuss Europe’s competitiveness and innovation landscape. The interview was recorded shortly after the launch of the
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO 主席坎皮诺斯在访谈中指出，欧洲专利申请量首破 20 万，但在云计和 AI 领域面临挑战，需发力量子技术。他强调基础研究强劲但商业化转化不足，单一市场碎片化是主要障碍，并支持欧盟简化公司注册及统一规则以提升整体竞争力。
items.ai_summary_en: European Patent Office President António Campinos discussed Europe’s innovation landscape in an Euronews interview following the release of the EPO Technology Dashboard 2025. The data revealed that annual demand for European patents exceeded 200,000 for the first time. While acknowledging strong fundamental research and potential in quantum technology, Campinos highlighted significant challenges in cloud computing and artificial intelligence. He identified market fragmentation, including disparate national regulations and stock exchanges, as a major barrier to scaling innovative companies. The President supported the European Commission’s proposals to harmonize company registration rules, viewing this as essential for strengthening the single market and improving overall competitiveness.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The record patent filing volume underscores sustained R&D activity, yet the President’s emphasis on commercialization gaps highlights a structural disconnect between research output and market success. For rights holders and corporate counsel, the ongoing push for regulatory harmonization is strategically significant. Simplifying cross-border operations could reduce administrative friction for scaling startups and multinational entities, potentially altering the competitive dynamics for emerging technologies where Europe currently lags behind other global jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利申请年需求量首次突破二十万件大关", "云计与人工智能是短板，量子技术具竞争优势", "市场碎片化阻碍创新规模化，需 harmonise 规则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Annual demand for European patents surpassed 200,000 for the first time, according to the EPO Technology Dashboard 2025.", "Europe faces critical challenges in cloud computing and AI, though it retains competitive potential in quantum technology.", "Market fragmentation and complex regulatory differences remain primary obstacles to scaling innovative enterprises."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["聚焦量子技术等欧洲优势领域进行差异化布局", "关注欧盟统一规则红利，优化在欧商业化路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor regulatory harmonization efforts to anticipate reduced administrative barriers for scaling pan-European operations.", "Practice note: Consider strategic portfolio adjustments in emerging fields like quantum technology where Europe maintains a comparative advantage.", "What to monitor: Track the implementation of simplified company registration rules proposed by the European Commission to enhance single market cohesion."]

Europe faces critical challenges in cloud computing and AI, though it retains competitive potential in quantum technology.
Market fragmentation and complex regulatory differences remain primary obstacles to scaling innovative enterprises.

items.title: EPO President meets key EU partners in Brussels
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局局长在布鲁塞尔会见欧盟关键合作伙伴
items.title_en: EPO President Strengthens EU Cooperation and Shares 2025 Technology Dashboard Insights
items.summary: Examination KPIs show encouraging improvement
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局局长坎皮诺斯访问布鲁塞尔，旨在加强与欧盟关键伙伴的合作。期间分享了《2025年技术仪表盘》洞察，强调专利情报对创新政策的支持作用。此外，局长与欧洲创新理事会和中小企业执行机构（EISMEA）主任会晤，回顾双方自2022年起的合作，并讨论2026年联合工作计划，重点在于加强战略情报及新兴技术映射，以支持初创企业和中小企业。
items.ai_summary_en: EPO President António Campinos visited Brussels to reinforce collaboration with key European Union partners. The mission included high-level discussions on innovation policy and the presentation of the EPO Technology Dashboard 2025, which highlights global innovation hotspots. A central theme was the role of patent intelligence in enhancing Europe’s competitiveness, discussed during a Euronews panel with policymakers and industry leaders. Additionally, the EPO deepened its cooperation with the European Innovation Council and SMEs Executive Agency (EISMEA). Building on their 2022 partnership, the agencies reviewed how EPO examiner expertise supports the identification of high-potential startups and SMEs, aiming to translate scientific strengths into market success through data-driven insights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The EPO is increasingly positioning patent data as a strategic asset for EU industrial policy, moving beyond traditional grant functions. For rights holders and innovators, this signals that alignment with identified technological priorities may influence access to support mechanisms. Monitoring EPO intelligence outputs provides valuable context for R&D planning and understanding the regulatory and funding landscape for emerging technologies in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO局长访问布鲁塞尔，分享2025年技术仪表盘数据，探讨专利情报助力欧洲竞争力", "EPO与EISMEA深化合作，利用审查员专业知识协助识别具有高商业化潜力的初创企业", "双方制定2026年联合工作计划，将通过增强战略情报和新兴技术映射来支持中小企业"]

items.title: Top ten tips for the launch of the IPO patent service
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局专利新数字服务上线十大提示
items.title_en: UKIPO Launches New Digital Patent Service: Key Operational Tips for Users
items.summary: With the new IPO digital patents service due to launch soon, we want to make sure that your transition to the new live service goes as smoothly as possible. Here are our top ten tips for using the service.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局即将推出新的数字专利服务，并发布使用指南。现有用户过渡平滑，旧有网页提交及邮箱渠道暂时保留。指南强调管理员需设置中央联系邮箱，代理人申请时务必指定代表以确保案件归集。审查意见答复建议上传标记修订的合并文档，后续将更新默认代表设置功能。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office is launching a new digital patent service, aiming for a smooth transition for existing users while retaining legacy web filing and email channels temporarily. The guidance highlights critical administrative steps, such as setting central correspondence emails in profile settings to ensure proper routing. Attorneys must explicitly assign themselves as representatives during applications to guarantee case visibility within their organizational accounts. Additionally, applicants are advised to upload consolidated documents with tracked changes when responding to examination reports. Support resources, including demo videos and FAQs, are available, with future updates planned to default organization representation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该指南明确了英国专利数字化过渡期的操作规范，重点在于账户权限管理与文件提交格式。实务中需关注“指定代表”字段对案件可视性的影响，以及审查意见答复的新格式要求。后续应监测默认代表设置功能的上线时间，以优化事务所内部流程配置。
items.ai_insight_en: This update clarifies operational protocols for the UKIPO’s digital transition, emphasizing account hygiene and file formatting. Proper representative assignment is critical for portfolio visibility, while standardized response formats aim to streamline examiner review. Firms should align internal workflows with these requirements to prevent administrative errors or missed communications during the migration period.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新服务上线后，现有用户无感过渡，旧有网页提交及邮箱渠道将在短期内继续保留", "代理人申请专利时必须选择“指定代表”，否则案件不会显示在机构账户中", "答复审查意见时，建议将标记修订内容作为单一合并文档上传，以便审查员查看变更"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Attorneys must assign representative status during application to ensure cases appear in organizational accounts.", "Administrators should configure central email addresses in profile settings to secure correct correspondence routing.", "Responses to examination reports require uploading consolidated documents containing clear markup or tracked changes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确代理人需在申请阶段主动指定代表，以避免案件在机构账户中缺失", "审查意见答复需采用合并文档形式并包含修订标记，适应新的审查交互习惯", "需持续关注官方路线图，等待默认代表设置功能的更新以提升管理效率"]

items.title: A bump in the road? US DOJ subjects automotive licensing negotiation group to antitrust scrutiny
items.title_zh: 美司法部审查汽车SEP许可谈判组引发跨大西洋分歧
items.title_en: US DOJ Antitrust Scrutiny of Automotive Licensing Group Highlights Transatlantic SEP Divergence
items.summary: The US Department of Justice is investigating car manufacturers for participating in a licensee negotiation group (LNG), MLex has reported. The investig...
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国司法部正调查宝马、大众等车企参与的汽车标准必要专利许可谈判组，担忧其构成买方卡特尔。此举与欧盟委员会及德国联邦卡特尔局的宽容立场形成鲜明对比。欧盟草案指南拟为真正谈判组提供安全港，但美方敌意态度给该机制未来带来巨大不确定性，凸显跨大西洋反垄断监管分歧。
items.ai_summary_en: The US Department of Justice is investigating major car manufacturers for participating in an automotive licensing negotiation group (ALNG), raising concerns that the initiative constitutes an illegal buyer cartel under Section 1 of the Sherman Act. This enforcement action underscores a significant transatlantic regulatory divergence, contrasting sharply with the European Commission’s recent comfort letter and guidance from the German Bundeskartellamt, which viewed the ALNG favorably under EU competition law. The DOJ’s hostile stance creates substantial legal uncertainty for global standard essential patent (SEP) licensing strategies. If the automakers cannot alleviate US antitrust concerns, they face potential federal litigation. This conflict complicates compliance for companies operating across both jurisdictions, as global SEP licenses must now navigate opposing regulatory expectations regarding collective bargaining by implementers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 跨大西洋在SEP集体许可反垄断合规上存在显著分歧。企业需关注欧盟最终版技术转让指南对“安全港”的界定，以及美方调查是否升级为诉讼。尽管欧盟持开放态度，但全球许可架构仍受美国执法风险制约，需持续监测监管动态对谈判策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Technology licensors and implementers must navigate increasing antitrust divergence between US and EU approaches to collective licensing. While EU guidelines increasingly accommodate licensee negotiation groups to reduce transaction costs, US enforcement remains skeptical of such arrangements as potential buyer cartels. Companies engaging in cross-border SEP licensing should establish robust compliance firewalls to isolate US operations from collective bargaining activities permitted or encouraged in Europe. It is critical to document that joint negotiations aim to improve efficiency and reduce information asymmetry rather than suppress royalty rates. Legal teams should prepare for heightened scrutiny in the US market, ensuring that any collaborative efforts among implementers are carefully structured to avoid allegations of coordinated hold-out or price-fixing under Sherman Act standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["美司法部调查汽车SEP许可谈判组，质疑其违反谢尔曼法案构成买方卡特尔", "欧盟委员会与德国卡特尔局此前已对该谈判组发出无异议或指导意见，立场温和", "监管冲突导致谈判组前景不明，欧盟新指南拟区分真谈判组与卡特尔以增加确定性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The US DOJ has issued formal information requests to BMW, Volkswagen, Mercedes, and Thyssenkrupp regarding their participation in the ALNG.", "US authorities view the ALNG as a potential buyer cartel violating antitrust laws, while EU regulators have provided conditional approval via a comfort letter.", "This regulatory clash creates significant compliance risks and legal uncertainty for global SEP licensing negotiations involving automotive manufacturers."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["美欧在SEP被许可人集体谈判的反垄断认定上存在根本性监管分歧", "需密切关注欧盟2026年生效的技术转让指南中关于许可谈判组的最终规则", "观察美国司法部调查是否演变为联邦诉讼，及其对全球汽车SEP许可模式的冲击"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Multinational firms must manage conflicting antitrust risks when implementing collective SEP licensing strategies across US and EU markets.", "Practice note: Establish strict operational separation between US and EU licensing activities to prevent US authorities from characterizing EU-compliant collaborations as illegal…", "What to monitor: Further developments in the DOJ investigation against the ALNG members and the finalization of the EU’s revised Technology Transfer Guidelines expected in 2026."]
core_points_en: The US DOJ has issued formal information requests to BMW, Volkswagen, Mercedes, and Thyssenkrupp regarding their participation in the ALNG.
US authorities view the ALNG as a potential buyer cartel violating antitrust laws, while EU regulators have provided conditional approval via a comfort letter.
This regulatory clash creates significant compliance risks and legal uncertainty for global SEP licensing negotiations involving automotive manufacturers.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Multinational firms must manage conflicting antitrust risks when implementing collective SEP licensing strategies across US and EU markets.
Practice note: Establish strict operational separation between US and EU licensing activities to prevent US authorities from characterizing EU-compliant collaborations as illegal…
What to monitor: Further developments in the DOJ investigation against the ALNG members and the finalization of the EU’s revised Technology Transfer Guidelines expected in 2026.

items.title: Official Statistics: Monthly statistics: Patents, trade marks and designs: March 2026
items.title_zh: 官方统计：2026年3月专利、商标及外观设计月度数据
items.title_en: UKIPO Releases March 2026 Monthly Statistics on Patents, Trade Marks, and Designs
items.summary: These statistics include monthly data for patents, trade marks and designs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布2026年3月官方统计数据。该报告包含当月专利、商标和外观设计的申请与注册相关月度数据。原文仅提供标题及摘要说明，未展示具体数值或趋势分析。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has published its official monthly statistics for March 2026. This comprehensive report provides detailed data covering three primary intellectual property sectors: patents, trade marks, and registered designs. As a fundamental source of intelligence within the UK IP framework, these figures offer an objective, quantitative basis for understanding the current state of the British IP ecosystem. The data reflects the volume of applications and registrations, serving as a key indicator of market activity levels and emerging trends. Stakeholders, including rights holders, legal representatives, and market analysts, can utilize this information to gauge the intensity of intellectual property engagement in the UK market during this specific reporting period.
items.ai_insight_en: Regular tracking of UKIPO monthly data enables stakeholders to identify shifts in intellectual property filing trends and competitive dynamics within the UK market. By comparing current figures against historical benchmarks, organizations can assess the pacing and intensity of competitor activities. It is advisable to analyze data fluctuations alongside specific technical or commercial sectors to refine patent and trade mark portfolio strategies. This approach ensures that rights coverage in key markets remains timely and effective, supporting robust decision-making for both UK-specific and broader European intellectual property layouts.

items.title: Infringement of medical device claims: the standard use threshold (UPC_CFI_628/2024; UPC_CFI_125/2025)
items.title_zh: UPC判决：医疗器械专利侵权中的常规使用门槛
items.title_en: UPC Munich: Medical Device Infringement Requires Use Consistent with Professional Practice
items.summary: Court docket: LD Munich
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院慕尼黑地方分庭在Emboline诉AorticLab案中裁定，尽管被告设备技术上可能以符合专利权利要求的方式使用（如用钩子移除），但该非常规操作不符合医疗专业实践且会损坏设备，故不构成侵权。法院确立医疗器械侵权需结合医疗科学公认规则判断。此外，附条件反诉若未获裁决，费用由反诉原告承担。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Munich Local Division ruled in Emboline v AorticLab that technical capability for infringing use does not automatically constitute patent infringement for medical devices. The Court established that unconventional, claim-compliant usage is only relevant if it aligns with professional practice and recognized rules of medical science. Mere emergency procedures or operations risking device damage do not meet this threshold. This decision clarifies the 'standard use' requirement, emphasizing that infringement analysis must consider whether the alleged infringing method is a standard, accepted medical procedure rather than a technically possible but professionally irregular action.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC对医疗器械专利侵权的“专业实践”审查标准，即单纯的技术可能性不足以认定侵权，必须结合临床常规操作。这对界定医疗设备设计自由空间及侵权抗辩策略具有参考意义，需关注后续类似案件对“公认医学规则”的具体举证要求。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts the assessment of indirect and direct infringement for medical technologies at the UPC. Rights holders cannot rely solely on theoretical technical possibilities to establish infringement; they must demonstrate that the accused use reflects standard clinical protocols. For manufacturers, this provides a robust defense against claims based on rare or emergency-only scenarios that deviate from professional guidelines. Legal strategies should focus on defining the 'normal' use within the specific medical context, ensuring that product instructions and clinical evidence clearly distinguish between standard practices and exceptional, non-compliant interventions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["医疗器械非常规使用构成侵权的前提是必须符合医疗专业实践及公认医学规则", "仅技术可行但非专业、非预期且会损坏设备的紧急移除方式，不纳入侵权评估范围", "依附于侵权认定条件的反诉属于受限反诉，若法院未作裁决，反诉原告需承担相关费用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Technically feasible but medically non-compliant unconventional uses do not constitute patent infringement.", "Infringement requires proof that the usage aligns with current medical standards and professional practice.", "Counterclaims dependent on infringement findings incur costs if the primary claim fails."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC确立医疗器械侵权判定需通过“医疗专业实践”测试，排除纯理论或紧急违规操作", "反诉策略需注意程序风险，依附性反诉若因主诉不成立而未决，将导致费用负担", "需持续观察法院在不同医疗细分领域对“公认医学规则”的证据采信标准变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers can defend against infringement by proving accused uses violate professional medical standards.", "Practice note: Litigation strategies must include expert testimony on standard clinical practices versus theoretical device capabilities.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this 'professional practice' threshold to different types of medical devices."]
core_points_en: Technically feasible but medically non-compliant unconventional uses do not constitute patent infringement.
Infringement requires proof that the usage aligns with current medical standards and professional practice.
Counterclaims dependent on infringement findings incur costs if the primary claim fails.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers can defend against infringement by proving accused uses violate professional medical standards.
Practice note: Litigation strategies must include expert testimony on standard clinical practices versus theoretical device capabilities.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this 'professional practice' threshold to different types of medical devices.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Limits of International Jurisdiction – Keeex v Adobe | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院澄清国际管辖权界限：Keeex诉Adobe案
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Limits International Jurisdiction in Keeex v Adobe
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal rules that Article 7(2) Brussels Regulation jurisdiction cannot extend to alleged infringement outside UPCA territory, overturning the Paris Local Division.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定，依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第7(2)条的损害发生地管辖权，不能延伸至统一专利法院协定领土之外的侵权行为。该案中，巴黎地方分院此前认定对瑞士、英国等非UPC成员国的侵权拥有管辖权，但上诉法院推翻了这一决定，明确管辖权限于UPC领土范围内。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal ruled that Article 7(2) of the Brussels Regulation cannot extend jurisdiction to alleged infringements occurring outside UPCA territories. This decision overturns the Paris Local Division, which had previously accepted jurisdiction over acts in non-UPCA states like Switzerland and the UK. The case involved defendants domiciled primarily in the US and Ireland, with claims covering multiple European jurisdictions. The Court clarified that while internal jurisdiction exists for infringements within UPCA territory, the place of harm basis does not validate authority over territories not party to the Unified Patent Court Agreement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the territorial limits of UPC jurisdiction, preventing rights holders from using a single UPC action to cover non-member states. Litigants must now separate claims for UPCA and non-UPCA territories, likely increasing the need for parallel proceedings in national courts for countries like the UK and Switzerland.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院推翻巴黎地方分院裁决，否定对UPC领土外侵权的管辖权", "依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第7(2)条的管辖权基础不得扩展至UPC协定领土之外", "涉案被告主要位于美国及爱尔兰，原告曾主张涵盖瑞士、英国等多国侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 7(2) Brussels Regulation jurisdiction cannot extend to infringements outside UPCA territory.", "Paris Local Division ruling granting broader international jurisdiction was overturned on appeal.", "Jurisdiction remains limited to UPCA member states, excluding UK, Switzerland, and others."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确UPC管辖权仅限于协定成员国领土，无法通过损害地原则扩展至英国或瑞士", "针对跨国被告的侵权诉讼需区分UPC境内与境外部分，可能引发多国平行诉讼", "后续需观察该裁决对涉及混合领土侵权案件的起诉策略及管辖权异议成功率的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders cannot rely on UPC proceedings to enforce patents in non-UPCA territories such as the UK or Switzerland within the same case.", "Practice note: Defendants should raise preliminary objections to international jurisdiction early, as the Court of Appeal strictly confines Article 7(2) application to UPCA border…", "What to monitor: Future filings may split strategies between UPC actions for member states and separate national suits for excluded European jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: Article 7(2) Brussels Regulation jurisdiction cannot extend to infringements outside UPCA territory.
Paris Local Division ruling granting broader international jurisdiction was overturned on appeal.
Jurisdiction remains limited to UPCA member states, excluding UK, Switzerland, and others.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders cannot rely on UPC proceedings to enforce patents in non-UPCA territories such as the UK or Switzerland within the same case.
Practice note: Defendants should raise preliminary objections to international jurisdiction early, as the Court of Appeal strictly confines Article 7(2) application to UPCA border…
What to monitor: Future filings may split strategies between UPC actions for member states and separate national suits for excluded European jurisdictions.

items.title: [Blog] OEB : l’IA étendue à la rédaction de procès-verbaux pour le brevet unitaire
items.title_zh: 欧专局将AI辅助笔录扩展至统一专利口头程序
items.title_en: EPO Extends AI-Assisted Minutes to Unitary Patent Protection Division Oral Proceedings
items.summary: En 2025, l’Office européen des brevets (OEB) a conduit une phase pilote relative à l’utilisation...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年，欧洲专利局在完成AI撰写口头程序笔录的试点后，将该实践扩展至审查、异议及法律部门。近期，欧专局决定进一步将此AI辅助笔录应用扩展至统一专利保护分部（UPPD）的口头程序。最终文件仍由相关部门依据《欧洲专利公约》第124(3)条审核验证，随后发送给当事人，AI录音及转录数据将被删除。
items.ai_summary_en: Following a successful pilot phase in 2025, the European Patent Office has expanded the use of artificial intelligence for drafting minutes in oral proceedings. Initially applied to examination and opposition procedures, this practice now extends to the Unitary Patent Protection Division. The new protocol mirrors existing workflows: AI generates initial transcripts, which are subsequently reviewed and validated by the competent division under Rule 124(3) EPC. Once the final document is transmitted to the parties, all original audio recordings and AI-generated transcription data are permanently deleted. This move signifies a deeper integration of AI tools within the patent grant and protection framework, aiming to enhance procedural efficiency while maintaining strict oversight through mandatory human verification and robust data deletion protocols.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此更新表明欧专局正系统性地将AI工具整合进包括统一专利在内的核心审查与法律程序中。尽管强调人工审核与数据删除以保障合规，当事人需关注AI介入对程序记录准确性及数据隐私保护的潜在影响，并适应这一标准化的行政操作流程。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties participating in Unitary Patent proceedings should anticipate AI-assisted documentation as the new operational standard. While the EPO maintains a rigorous human review process to ensure accuracy, the reliance on automated transcription necessitates heightened precision during oral arguments to prevent misinterpretation. Stakeholders must also remain vigilant regarding data privacy and evidence preservation strategies, given the immediate deletion of source recordings after validation. Legal teams should adapt their preparation methods to align with these procedural efficiencies, ensuring that critical points are clearly articulated to withstand automated processing and subsequent human scrutiny.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧专局将AI辅助撰写口头程序笔录扩展至统一专利保护分部（UPPD）", "最终笔录须由主管部门依《欧洲专利公约》第124(3)条审核验证", "笔录发送当事人后，AI生成的录音及转录数据将被彻底删除"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO has fully adopted AI-assisted minute drafting across all oral proceedings following positive pilot results.", "The scope now includes oral hearings before the Unitary Patent Protection Division alongside examination and opposition cases.", "Final minutes require human validation per EPC rules, with all raw AI data and recordings deleted post-verification."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧专局在统一专利程序中深化AI应用，提升行政效率但维持人工最终审核权", "明确的数据删除机制旨在缓解当事人对AI处理敏感口头程序信息的隐私顾虑", "需持续观察AI辅助笔录在复杂技术争议或异议程序中的实际准确度与接受度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt oral advocacy strategies to ensure clarity and precision for AI-assisted transcription environments.", "Practice note: Verify final minutes carefully against personal notes, acknowledging that source recordings are deleted post-validation.", "What to monitor: Potential shifts in data retention policies or transparency requirements regarding AI tool usage in proceedings."]
core_points_en: The EPO has fully adopted AI-assisted minute drafting across all oral proceedings following positive pilot results.
The scope now includes oral hearings before the Unitary Patent Protection Division alongside examination and opposition cases.
Final minutes require human validation per EPC rules, with all raw AI data and recordings deleted post-verification.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt oral advocacy strategies to ensure clarity and precision for AI-assisted transcription environments.
Practice note: Verify final minutes carefully against personal notes, acknowledging that source recordings are deleted post-validation.
What to monitor: Potential shifts in data retention policies or transparency requirements regarding AI tool usage in proceedings.

items.title: Boots, Buckles And Trends: The CJEU Clarifies EU Design Protection In Deity Shoes
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院澄清Deity Shoes案外观设计保护标准
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies EU Design Law: No Creative Effort Required and Fashion Trends Do Not Limit Designer Freedom
items.summary: On 18 December 2025, the Court of Justice of the European Union ("CJEU") delivered its judgment in Case C323/24, Deity Shoes v Mundorama Confort and Stay Design, addressing a fundamental...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年12月18日，欧盟法院在Deity Shoes案中裁定，欧盟外观设计保护仅取决于新颖性和独特性，不要求真正的创造性努力或原创性。法院同时确认，时尚趋势不会限制设计师的自由度，从而降低独特性评估门槛。该案源于西班牙法院移送，涉及鞋类设计是否因基于目录组件而无效。
items.ai_summary_en: On 18 December 2025, the Court of Justice of the European Union delivered its judgment in Case C323/24, Deity Shoes v Mundorama Confort and Stay Design. The Court addressed whether EU design protection requires genuine creative effort or intellectual input. It ruled that protection depends solely on novelty and individual character, rejecting any requirement for originality akin to copyright law. Additionally, the Court held that fashion trends do not restrict a designer’s freedom when assessing individual character. This decision clarifies the objective nature of EU design rights and distinguishes them clearly from copyright standards. The case originated from a Spanish court’s reference regarding the validity of designs based on customized catalogue components, reinforcing that minor modifications can still yield protectable designs if they create a different overall impression.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该判决强化了欧盟外观设计保护的客观属性，厘清其与版权法界限。企业需注意，即便设计源自目录组件修改，只要产生不同整体印象即可受保护，无需证明高创造性。时尚行业应关注此标准对侵权判定及无效抗辩的影响，特别是关于趋势限制的论证空间被压缩。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the low threshold for obtaining EU design rights, confirming that protection is not contingent on high levels of creativity. Rights holders should focus on establishing unique combinations of existing elements to secure individual character. In invalidity proceedings, parties can argue that prevailing fashion trends do not constrain the designer's freedom, thereby strengthening the stability of their registered designs. Practitioners should ensure robust novelty searches prior to registration to preempt challenges based on catalogue sources. This approach supports a strategic portfolio management style that leverages the objective criteria of the Community Design Regulation effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟外观设计保护不需原创性或智力努力，仅需满足新颖性与独特性两条件", "时尚趋势不减少设计师自由度，不影响对设计独特性的客观评估标准", "即使基于现有元素组合，若整体印象不同，仍可获得外观设计法律保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU design protection relies exclusively on novelty and individual character, requiring no additional creative effort or intellectual input beyond these statutory criteria.", "Fashion trends do not limit the designer's degree of freedom, meaning industry trends must not influence the assessment of a design's individual character.", "Designs created by modifying or combining existing catalogue components remain eligible for protection if they produce a distinct overall impression."]

core_points_en: EU design protection relies exclusively on novelty and individual character, requiring no additional creative effort or intellectual input beyond these statutory criteria.
Fashion trends do not limit the designer's degree of freedom, meaning industry trends must not influence the assessment of a design's individual character.
Designs created by modifying or combining existing catalogue components remain eligible for protection if they produce a distinct overall impression.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the low creative threshold by focusing on unique combinations of existing elements to secure robust EU design rights.
Practice note: In invalidity litigation, assert that fashion trends do not restrict designer freedom to maintain the stability of design protections.
What to monitor: Monitor competitor strategies relying on catalogue-based designs and ensure thorough novelty searches to counter potential invalidity claims.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-412/24 Fauré Le Page
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：商标含虚构历史年份可能构成欺骗
items.title_en: CJEU: Trademark Number Evoking Fictitious Heritage May Deceive Consumers
items.summary: Luxury leather goods: the inclusion of a number in a trade mark, evoking a fictitious historical heritage, may be deceptive to consumers
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 Fauré Le Page 案中裁定，若商标包含暗示遥远成立年份的数字，从而虚构长期传统与技艺，即便该数字本身不直接描述商品特性，也可能因赋予商品虚假的质量保证与声望形象而被视为具有欺骗性。法院强调，奢侈品质量涵盖其吸引力与声望，成员国法院需结合整体语境评估公众认知。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-412/24, the Court of Justice ruled that a trademark containing a number suggesting a distant year of establishment may be deceptive under EU law if it evokes long-standing know-how and prestige that do not reflect reality. The case concerned 'Fauré Le Page Paris 1717', filed by a company established in 2009, while the historic firm ceased trading in 1992. The Court held that such marks mislead consumers regarding the quality and prestige of luxury goods, as the perceived guarantee of tradition is a characteristic of the goods. National courts must assess whether the relevant public perceives the number as indicating genuine historical continuity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧注册含历史年份或传统暗示的商标时，须确保事实依据真实，避免虚构传承故事。建议提前开展合规审查，防止因“声望误导”被宣告无效或面临侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies that brand narratives implying historical continuity can constitute deception if factually unsupported, particularly in the luxury sector where prestige influences consumer choice. Rights holders must ensure that any temporal references in trademarks align with verifiable corporate history to avoid invalidity risks based on misleading nature under EU trademark law.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["商标中暗示遥远成立年份的数字可被视为对商品质量与声望的误导", "奢侈品的质量属性包含其吸引力与声望形象，不仅限于物理特性", "是否构成欺骗需由成员国法院结合具体语境及相关公众感知综合判定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A trademark including a number evoking fictitious historical heritage may deceive consumers regarding quality and prestige.", "Luxury goods quality encompasses attractiveness and prestigious image, not merely physical characteristics or origin.", "Deceptiveness depends on whether the relevant public perceives the mark as implying non-existent long-standing know-how."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["虚构品牌历史年份易被认定为欺骗，需确保时间标识有实证支撑", "奢侈品出海应审慎使用传统、传承等叙事，防范声望误导法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands using historical dates must verify factual accuracy to prevent findings of deception regarding quality and prestige.", "Practice note: Assess trademark distinctiveness and potential deceptiveness by evaluating public perception of implied heritage claims.", "What to monitor: How national courts apply this standard to other sectors where brand history influences perceived product value."]
core_points_en: A trademark including a number evoking fictitious historical heritage may deceive consumers regarding quality and prestige.
Luxury goods quality encompasses attractiveness and prestigious image, not merely physical characteristics or origin.
Deceptiveness depends on whether the relevant public perceives the mark as implying non-existent long-standing know-how.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands using historical dates must verify factual accuracy to prevent findings of deception regarding quality and prestige.
Practice note: Assess trademark distinctiveness and potential deceptiveness by evaluating public perception of implied heritage claims.
What to monitor: How national courts apply this standard to other sectors where brand history influences perceived product value.

items.title: Marques patronymiques et risque de tromperie
items.title_zh: CJEU：姓名商标转让后的欺骗风险与失效条件
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Deception Risks and Revocation Conditions for Surnames After Trademark Assignment
items.summary: La CJUE précise les conditions pouvant conduire à la déchéance d’une marque ayant fait l’objet d’une cession.  Par un arrêt du 18 décembre 2025, la CJUE s’inscrit dans la saga opposant Jean-Charles Castelbajac à la société PMJC, cessionnaire de ses marques, et précise les conditions dans lesquelles une marque patronymique peut être frappée (suite) L’article Marques patronymiques et risque de tromperie est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年12月18日，欧盟法院（CJEU）在Jean-Charles Castelbajac诉PMJC案中作出裁决，澄清了姓名商标在转让后可能因导致公众产生误解而被宣告失效的条件。该案涉及设计师将其姓名品牌转让给公司后，关于商标权存续及是否构成欺骗性注册的争议。CJEU强调，若商标使用方式使公众对商品来源或作者身份产生错误认知，即便经过合法转让，该商标仍面临被剥夺权利的风险，确立了姓名商标商业利用中的诚信边界。
items.ai_summary_en: On December 18, 2025, the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) issued a judgment in the dispute between Jean-Charles Castelbajac and PMJC, clarifying the conditions under which a surname trademark may be revoked following its assignment. The case centers on whether the continued use of a designer’s name by an assignee company constitutes deceptive practice. The CJEU emphasized that even after a lawful transfer of rights, a trademark faces revocation if its use misleads the public regarding the commercial origin or the identity of the author. This ruling establishes critical boundaries for the good faith exploitation of surname trademarks, ensuring that consumer perception of source and authorship remains accurate despite changes in corporate ownership.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧进行品牌并购或授权时，需审慎评估姓名类商标的后续使用合规性。若保留创始人姓名作为品牌标识，应确保宣传不误导公众对实际控制人或设计来源的认知，避免因“欺骗性”风险导致商标无效。建议在设计许可协议时，明确约定品牌使用规范及侵权责任分担，加强尽职调查以规避此类法律隐患。
items.ai_insight_en: For entities engaging in brand acquisitions or licensing involving founder names, rigorous due diligence is essential. Acquirers must assess whether retaining the original surname as a brand identifier could mislead consumers about actual control or design origins post-transfer. Licensing agreements should explicitly define usage guidelines and liability allocation to mitigate risks of invalidation based on deception. Companies should monitor how assignees present the brand’s heritage to ensure transparency, avoiding any implication that the original creator remains involved when they do not, thereby safeguarding the trademark’s validity against claims of misleading the public.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU明确姓名商标转让后若致公众误解可被宣告失效", "Castelbajac案确立商标权行使不得误导消费者对来源的认知", "强调商业转让中需平衡个人姓名权与防止市场混淆原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU clarified that surname trademarks assigned to third parties may be revoked if their subsequent use deceives the public about product origin.", "The Castelbajac ruling confirms that trademark rights must not be exercised in a manner that misleads consumers regarding the true source or authorship of goods.", "The decision highlights the need to balance personal name rights with market clarity principles during commercial assignments to prevent consumer confusion."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["并购姓名品牌需评估后续使用是否引发来源误导风险", "完善许可协议条款以防范商标因欺骗性被宣告无效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate acquisition targets for potential deception risks if founder names are retained without clear disclosure of changed ownership.", "Practice note: Draft licensing and assignment clauses specifying strict usage norms to prevent consumer misunderstanding regarding source or authorship.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'deceptive use' in surname trademark cases to adjust branding strategies and compliance protocols accordingly."]
core_points_en: The CJEU clarified that surname trademarks assigned to third parties may be revoked if their subsequent use deceives the public about product origin.
The Castelbajac ruling confirms that trademark rights must not be exercised in a manner that misleads consumers regarding the true source or authorship of goods.
The decision highlights the need to balance personal name rights with market clarity principles during commercial assignments to prevent consumer confusion.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate acquisition targets for potential deception risks if founder names are retained without clear disclosure of changed ownership.
Practice note: Draft licensing and assignment clauses specifying strict usage norms to prevent consumer misunderstanding regarding source or authorship.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'deceptive use' in surname trademark cases to adjust branding strategies and compliance protocols accordingly.

items.title: WIPO Launches New Award for Cities of Innovation
items.title_zh: WIPO启动“创新城市”奖项计划
items.title_en: WIPO Launches New Award Program Recognizing Global Cities for Innovation Commitment
items.summary: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) today launched the “WIPO City of Innovation” award program, a new initiative that recognizes cities worldwide demonstrating exceptional commitment to fostering innovation and creativity among their residents.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）正式推出“WIPO创新城市”奖项计划。该 initiative 旨在表彰全球范围内在促进居民创新与创造力方面展现出卓越承诺的城市。此举意在通过认可地方层面的努力，推动全球创新生态系统的发展，鼓励城市利用知识产权框架激发社会活力，为居民创造更具创新性的生活环境。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially launched the WIPO City of Innovation award program. This new initiative aims to recognize cities worldwide that demonstrate exceptional commitment to fostering innovation and creativity among their residents. By acknowledging efforts at the local level, the program seeks to promote the development of global innovation ecosystems. It encourages municipalities to utilize intellectual property frameworks to stimulate social vitality and create more innovative living environments for their populations. The award serves as a benchmark for urban centers striving to enhance their creative capacities and support resident-driven innovation through effective policy and infrastructure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业应关注此奖项对城市创新环境的塑造作用。参与或对标此类标准有助于提升品牌国际形象，融入当地创新网络。建议企业在欧布局时，优先选择获此认证或具备高创新活力的城市，以获取更优质的政策资源、人才支持及合作机会，优化海外IP战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational corporations should monitor this award as it signals evolving standards for supportive innovation environments. Aligning with these criteria can enhance brand reputation and facilitate deeper integration into local innovation networks. For companies expanding operations in Europe, prioritizing locations with such certifications or high innovation vitality may provide access to superior policy resources, talent pools, and collaborative opportunities. This approach supports optimized overseas intellectual property strategies by situating operations within jurisdictions that actively foster creative ecosystems and offer robust institutional support for innovation-driven enterprises.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO正式启动“创新城市”奖项计划以表彰全球优秀城市", "重点评估城市在促进居民创新与创造力方面的承诺与成效", "旨在通过地方层面激励推动全球创新生态系统整体发展"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO officially launches the City of Innovation award to recognize outstanding municipal contributions globally.", "Evaluation focuses on city commitments and measurable outcomes in promoting resident innovation and creativity.", "The initiative aims to drive global ecosystem growth by incentivizing progress at the local administrative level."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注获奖城市创新环境，优化海外IP战略选址布局", "对标国际标准提升品牌形象，融入当地创新合作网络"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor award-winning cities to identify optimal locations for overseas IP strategy deployment and operational expansion.", "Practice note: Benchmark against international standards to elevate brand image and integrate effectively into local innovation cooperation networks.", "What to monitor: Track participating cities and evaluation criteria to assess local policy resources and talent support availability."]
core_points_en: WIPO officially launches the City of Innovation award to recognize outstanding municipal contributions globally.
Evaluation focuses on city commitments and measurable outcomes in promoting resident innovation and creativity.
The initiative aims to drive global ecosystem growth by incentivizing progress at the local administrative level.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor award-winning cities to identify optimal locations for overseas IP strategy deployment and operational expansion.

What to monitor: Track participating cities and evaluation criteria to assess local policy resources and talent support availability.

items.title: The 'Prompter' as Copyright Author? Lessons from Munich
items.title_zh: 提示者即作者？慕尼黑法院关于 AI 生成物版权的判决启示
items.title_en: Munich Court Denies Copyright for AI-Generated Logos: Prompter Not Author
items.summary: For the first time, a German court has addressed the "Prompter's" claim to authorship in the output. The District Court of Munich (AG München, Judgment of Feb 13, 2026 – 142 C 9786/25) offers a foundational blueprint for determining when the human-machine prompt crosses the threshold of copyright protection when it comes to the output. The Case
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院首次裁定，用户仅通过迭代提示词生成的 AI 图像不构成作品。法院认为，若提示词过于宽泛或仅做微调，实际创作决策由机器完成，无法体现人类个性。只有当提示词中的创意元素主导输出时，才受版权保护。
items.ai_summary_en: The District Court of Munich (AG München) dismissed a copyright infringement claim regarding three logos generated via text-to-image AI, ruling that the plaintiff's iterative prompting did not constitute authorship. The court held that copyright requires the output to reflect the author's personality through free creative decisions. It established that the AI must function as a mere tool rather than an independent creator. Protection arises only if creative elements in the prompt objectively define the output, dominating the machine's randomness. General instructions leaving design decisions to the algorithm fail the threshold for personal intellectual creation under German law.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧使用 AI 生成内容时，需保留详细创作日志，证明提示词对最终成果具有决定性控制。避免依赖简单指令，应深度介入参数调整与具体设计表达，以满足欧盟版权法对“人类智力创造”的严格要求。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical guidance on the human-AI interaction threshold for copyright subsistence in Germany. It clarifies that extensive but general prompting does not automatically equate to authorship if the machine retains significant discretionary power over the final expression. Rights holders and legal teams should note that the court applies the CJEU's 'sufficient objectivity' standard upstream to the prompt itself. This reinforces the need for demonstrable human control over specific design elements rather than relying on broad artistic directions or post-generation selection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI 须为工具而非独立创作者，人类需掌控核心设计决策", "宽泛指令与结果选择不足以构成个人智力创作", "提示词必须客观定义输出，排除机器随机性主导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright protection requires the work to reflect the author's personality via free creative decisions, not just triggering a process.", "The AI model must act as a mere tool; protection exists only if prompt creativity dominates the final output.", "Vague or open-ended instructions leave design decisions to the AI, failing the requirement for objectively defined intent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["保留全流程提示记录以证明人类创意主导地位", "避免宽泛指令，需深度介入具体设计表达细节"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Generative AI outputs may lack copyright protection if prompts are too vague, impacting enforcement strategies for digital assets.", "Practice note: Document specific, objective design instructions within prompts to demonstrate dominant human creative intent over machine randomness.", "What to monitor: Future EU jurisprudence applying the 'mere tool' versus 'independent instrument' distinction to other generative AI use cases."]
core_points_en: Copyright protection requires the work to reflect the author's personality via free creative decisions, not just triggering a process.
The AI model must act as a mere tool; protection exists only if prompt creativity dominates the final output.
Vague or open-ended instructions leave design decisions to the AI, failing the requirement for objectively defined intent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Generative AI outputs may lack copyright protection if prompts are too vague, impacting enforcement strategies for digital assets.
Practice note: Document specific, objective design instructions within prompts to demonstrate dominant human creative intent over machine randomness.
What to monitor: Future EU jurisprudence applying the 'mere tool' versus 'independent instrument' distinction to other generative AI use cases.

items.title: Düsseldorf LD, March 18, 2026, Decision On Infringement Action And Counterclaim For Revocation, UPC_CFI_135/2024, UPC_CFI_477/2024
items.title_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院就 Dolby 诉 Beko 案作出侵权与无效反诉判决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Dominant Position in FRAND Context and Implementer's Willingness
items.summary: The Düsseldorf Local Division held that a dominant position within the meaning of Article 102 TFEU may arise if smart TVs complying with a common standard...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Dolby 诉 Beko 案中裁定，若符合标准的智能电视无许可无法上市，专利权人可能拥有市场支配地位。法院强调，若实施者未在 FRAND 谈判初期表明许可意愿，则无需审查专利权人报价是否合规。该判决遵循华为诉中兴案框架，并与曼海姆分院立场保持一致。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that a dominant position under Article 102 TFEU may exist if standard-compliant smart TVs cannot be marketed without a license for the patent in suit, given consumer expectations for common codecs. The Court held that if an implementer fails to indicate willingness to conclude a license during initial FRAND negotiations, the patentee’s offer need not be examined for FRAND compliance. This decision aligns with the Mannheim Local Division’s approach in Panasonic v Oppo regarding the Huawei v ZTE framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口智能电视等标准产品时，须在收到侵权通知后立即明确表达许可意愿，否则将丧失 FRAND 抗辩机会。建议提前建立标准化应对流程，避免因程序瑕疵导致败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the threshold for establishing dominance in SEP cases involving consumer electronics and reinforces strict procedural requirements for implementers. Rights holders can rely on this to streamline enforcement against unresponsive parties, while implementers must ensure immediate, explicit responses to infringement notices to preserve FRAND defenses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标准合规电视若无许可无法销售，专利权人或具市场支配地位", "实施者未表许可意愿时，无需审查专利权人报价是否符合 FRAND", "判决遵循华为诉中兴框架，确认 UPC 内部裁判标准一致性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Dominant position may arise where consumers expect standard-compliant devices to decode all common video codecs covered by the standard.", "If the infringer fails to indicate willingness to license in response to notice, the analysis of the patentee's FRAND offer ends.", "The Düsseldorf Local Division aligns its FRAND framework application with the Mannheim Local Division's precedent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["收到侵权通知须立即书面表达许可意愿以保留抗辩权", "出口企业应预判标准必要专利风险并制定前置谈判策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implementers must respond promptly to infringement notices to avoid forfeiting FRAND defenses.", "Practice note: Patentees may cease detailed FRAND offer analysis if implementers show no initial willingness to negotiate.", "What to monitor: Consistency in FRAND application across UPC Local Divisions following this alignment with Mannheim precedents."]
core_points_en: Dominant position may arise where consumers expect standard-compliant devices to decode all common video codecs covered by the standard.
If the infringer fails to indicate willingness to license in response to notice, the analysis of the patentee's FRAND offer ends.
The Düsseldorf Local Division aligns its FRAND framework application with the Mannheim Local Division's precedent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implementers must respond promptly to infringement notices to avoid forfeiting FRAND defenses.
Practice note: Patentees may cease detailed FRAND offer analysis if implementers show no initial willingness to negotiate.
What to monitor: Consistency in FRAND application across UPC Local Divisions following this alignment with Mannheim precedents.

items.title: CD Milan, March 13, 2026, Order On Application For Security For Costs, UPC_CFI_927/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 米兰分部就诉讼费用担保申请作出裁定
items.title_en: UPC Milan Division Rules on Security for Costs: Claimant's Financials and SME Status
items.summary: Security for costs under Art. 69(4) UPCA / R. 158 RoP may be ordered where the claimant's own financial situation gives rise to a legitimate and real concern that a future costs award may not be recoverable or enforceable.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）中央分部米兰分院在撤销行动中裁定，若原告财务状况引发未来费用无法执行的合理担忧，可责令其提供费用担保。法院强调仅评估原告自身财力而非关联公司，且单纯主张中小企业身份若无数据佐证无效。此外，法院有权基于比例原则设定低于法定上限的担保金额。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Central Division in Milan issued an order regarding an application for security for costs under Article 69(4) of the UPC Agreement and Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure. The Court held that security may be required where the claimant’s financial situation creates a legitimate concern that future cost awards will not be recoverable. Crucially, the assessment focuses strictly on the claimant entity’s own financial position, excluding the strength of associated companies or the wider group unless specific guarantees exist. The ruling emphasizes that mere assertions of small or medium-sized enterprise status are insufficient to reduce security amounts without substantiating data such as headcount, turnover, and balance sheet totals. Furthermore, the Court clarified it is not bound to set security at the maximum recoverable limit, allowing for lower amounts based on proportionality and estimated first-instance costs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 提起诉讼时，应提前准备详尽的财务报表以证明偿付能力，避免被责令高额担保。切勿仅凭中小企业声明抗辩，需量化指标；同时注意集团财务隔离风险，必要时提供母公司担保以消除法院顾虑。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the Unified Patent Court should proactively prepare detailed, independent financial statements to demonstrate solvency and mitigate the risk of substantial security orders. Reliance on corporate group strength or generic SME classifications is ineffective without concrete quantitative evidence. Parties should consider providing parent company guarantees if their standalone balance sheets appear weak. This approach helps address judicial concerns regarding enforceability of potential adverse cost awards. Strategic preparation of financial documentation is essential to avoid procedural delays and excessive upfront financial burdens during patent litigation proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院仅审查原告自身财务状况，不自动延伸至关联集团", "未实质反驳对方证据将导致财务弱势被视为无争议事实", "单纯主张中小企业身份不足以减免担保，需提供具体数据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts assess only the claimant entity's financial standing, ignoring group strength absent explicit guarantees or special circumstances.", "Failure to substantively rebut evidence of financial weakness risks treating the defendant's claims as undisputed facts.", "Claiming SME status alone does not reduce security; specific metrics like turnover and headcount must be provided."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["赴欧诉讼需备妥独立财务报表，证明自身资金充裕", "集团架构不能天然豁免担保，需主动提供增信措施"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prepare independent financial reports to prove solvency and counter claims of unenforceable cost awards.", "Practice note: Avoid relying solely on SME status; provide quantified data on turnover, headcount, and balance sheets.", "What to monitor: Consider offering parent company guarantees to offset perceived financial weaknesses of the claimant entity."]
core_points_en: Courts assess only the claimant entity's financial standing, ignoring group strength absent explicit guarantees or special circumstances.
Failure to substantively rebut evidence of financial weakness risks treating the defendant's claims as undisputed facts.
Claiming SME status alone does not reduce security; specific metrics like turnover and headcount must be provided.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prepare independent financial reports to prove solvency and counter claims of unenforceable cost awards.
Practice note: Avoid relying solely on SME status; provide quantified data on turnover, headcount, and balance sheets.
What to monitor: Consider offering parent company guarantees to offset perceived financial weaknesses of the claimant entity.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, March 16, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_733/2024, UPC_CFI_255/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院就激光设备专利侵权案作出重要判决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Functional Claim Construction and Indirect Infringement Remedies in Laser Patent Case
items.summary: The court found that a "switchable device" is not limited to mechanical optics but includes electronic controls. "Coupling" does not require free-space propagation...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫分院在 TRUMPF 诉 IPG 案中作出判决。法院采用功能性权利要求解释认定侵权，驳回基于非现实起点的创造性抗辩。裁判明确间接侵权不适用召回等救济，确立了临时赔偿金涵盖取证费规则，并强调严格遵守程序期限及设定最低罚金以保障执行灵活性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Düsseldorf issued a significant judgment in the TRUMPF v. IPG laser patent dispute. The court adopted a functional approach to claim construction, finding infringement by interpreting 'switchable device' broadly to include electronic controls rather than limiting it to mechanical optics. It rejected inventive step defenses based on prior art from unrelated technical fields, requiring realistic starting points. Crucially, the ruling clarified that corrective measures such as recall and destruction apply only to directly infringing products, excluding those involved solely in indirect infringement. The decision also established that provisional damages may cover test purchase costs for evidence gathering. Furthermore, the court emphasized strict adherence to procedural deadlines for submissions and amendments, while setting minimum penalty payments to ensure flexible enforcement of injunctions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉需重视功能性解释风险，避免狭义抗辩失效。应严格遵循 UPC 程序期限，防止辅助请求被拒。同时注意间接侵权救济局限，合理规划产品合规与诉讼策略，利用判例中关于现有技术起点的规则进行有效抗辩。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the UPC’s willingness to adopt functional claim constructions, potentially broadening patent protection beyond literal mechanical definitions. Litigants must anticipate this risk when designing around patents or formulating non-infringement defenses. The distinction between direct and indirect infringement remedies is critical; companies relying on indirect infringement arguments cannot assume standard corrective measures like product recalls will be available against them. Strict compliance with procedural timelines is essential, as late submissions risk exclusion. Additionally, the precedent allowing provisional damages for evidence acquisition costs impacts litigation budgeting. Parties should leverage the requirement for realistic prior art starting points to strengthen invalidity challenges effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院采纳功能性解释，认定电子控制属于可切换装置范围", "间接侵权不适用召回销毁令，仅直接侵权产品适用此类救济", "确立临时赔偿涵盖取证成本，并设定最低罚金以确保执行弹性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Functional claim construction expands scope to electronic controls, rejecting narrow mechanical interpretations for switchable devices.", "Recall and destruction remedies are unavailable for indirect infringement, applying strictly to directly infringing subject matter.", "Provisional awards cover test purchase costs, with strict procedural deadlines enforced and minimum penalties set for flexibility."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕功能性解释扩大保护范围，提前布局规避设计方案", "严守 UPC 程序时限，避免因逾期提交导致关键抗辩理由失权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor functional interpretation risks to adjust freedom-to-operate analyses and avoid narrow defensive postures.", "Practice note: Adhere strictly to UPC procedural calendars to prevent exclusion of auxiliary requests and key evidentiary submissions.", "What to monitor: Observe how courts define 'realistic' prior art starting points for inventive step assessments in future cases."]
core_points_en: Functional claim construction expands scope to electronic controls, rejecting narrow mechanical interpretations for switchable devices.
Recall and destruction remedies are unavailable for indirect infringement, applying strictly to directly infringing subject matter.
Provisional awards cover test purchase costs, with strict procedural deadlines enforced and minimum penalties set for flexibility.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor functional interpretation risks to adjust freedom-to-operate analyses and avoid narrow defensive postures.
Practice note: Adhere strictly to UPC procedural calendars to prevent exclusion of auxiliary requests and key evidentiary submissions.
What to monitor: Observe how courts define 'realistic' prior art starting points for inventive step assessments in future cases.

items.title: CoA, March 16, 2026, Order Concerning A Preliminary Objection, UPC_CoA_904/2025, UPC_CoA_905/2025
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭澄清初步异议处理权限归属
items.title_en: UPC CoA: Panel May Defer Preliminary Objections to Main Proceedings, Not Just Judge-Rapporteur
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarifies that a decision under R. 20.2 RoP to deal with a preliminary objection in the main proceedings is not reserved to the judge-rapporteur alone.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Vivo与Sun Patent Trust案中裁定，根据《程序规则》第20.2条，将初步异议移交主诉讼程序审理的决定权并非独属于报告法官。合议庭亦有权决定将异议与实体 merits 一并审理。上诉庭确认一审法院拥有广泛的案件管理裁量权，巴黎地方分院将FRAND相关请求与侵权及禁令诉求合并审理并未越权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that under Rule 20.2 of the Rules of Procedure, the decision to defer a preliminary objection to the main proceedings is not reserved exclusively to the judge-rapporteur. The panel itself may determine that the objection should be addressed alongside the merits. This ruling confirms that the Court of First Instance possesses broad case-management discretion to decide whether to rule on preliminary objections promptly or defer them, depending on procedural appropriateness. Consequently, appellate review of such case-management decisions is limited. In this specific FRAND-related patent dispute, the Paris Local Division was entitled to treat the admissibility issue as sufficiently linked to the infringement action and injunction request, allowing it to be resolved within the main proceedings rather than as a standalone preliminary matter.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在处理涉及FRAND抗辩的初步异议时的程序灵活性，强化了合议庭在案件管理中的角色。当事人需注意，涉及SEP的管辖权或可受理性异议可能不会在早期阶段单独裁决，而是与实体侵权及禁令救济合并审理，这对诉讼节奏和策略规划具有程序性影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the significant case-management discretion afforded to UPC divisions, particularly regarding the timing of preliminary objection rulings. Parties facing SEP litigation must anticipate that tribunals may merge jurisdictional or validity challenges with substantive merits, especially where FRAND defenses are implicated. Litigants should prepare comprehensive strategies that address both procedural admissibility and substantive infringement simultaneously, rather than relying on early dismissal opportunities. Understanding how different local divisions exercise this discretion is critical for effective litigation planning and resource allocation in European patent disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭澄清R.20.2条下移交初步异议至主诉讼的决定权不限于报告法官，合议庭亦可决定", "确认一审法院拥有广泛案件管理裁量权，可根据具体案情决定即时裁决或延后处理初步异议", "巴黎地方分院将FRAND请求与侵权诉讼合并审理符合程序规定，上诉审查范围因此受限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The panel, not just the judge-rapporteur, holds authority to defer preliminary objections to the main proceedings under Rule 20.2 RoP.", "First instance courts retain broad discretion to manage procedure, choosing between immediate rulings or deferral based on case specifics.", "FRAND-related admissibility issues closely tied to infringement may be properly merged into main proceedings without exceeding judicial discretion."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭通过解释程序规则，扩大了合议庭在初步异议处理上的权限，不再局限于报告法官", "涉及FRAND条款的抗辩在UPC诉讼中更可能被纳入主诉讼程序一并审理，而非作为前置程序…", "需持续观察UPC各地方分院在行使案件管理裁量权时，对SEP案件中初步异议与实体审理分离…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prepare for merged hearings by developing robust arguments for both procedural admissibility and substantive merits simultaneously.", "Practice note: Monitor how specific UPC local divisions exercise their discretion to defer preliminary objections in SEP and FRAND contexts.", "What to monitor: Future appellate reviews limiting or defining the scope of first instance case-management powers regarding preliminary objections."]
core_points_en: The panel, not just the judge-rapporteur, holds authority to defer preliminary objections to the main proceedings under Rule 20.2 RoP.
First instance courts retain broad discretion to manage procedure, choosing between immediate rulings or deferral based on case specifics.
FRAND-related admissibility issues closely tied to infringement may be properly merged into main proceedings without exceeding judicial discretion.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prepare for merged hearings by developing robust arguments for both procedural admissibility and substantive merits simultaneously.
Practice note: Monitor how specific UPC local divisions exercise their discretion to defer preliminary objections in SEP and FRAND contexts.
What to monitor: Future appellate reviews limiting or defining the scope of first instance case-management powers regarding preliminary objections.

items.title: For Medical Devices, “Possible Use” Amounts To Infringement Only If The Use Is In Line With Medical Practice
items.title_zh: 医疗器械“可能使用”构成侵权须符合医疗实践
items.title_en: UPC Munich: Medical Device Infringement Requires Use Consistent With Medical Practice
items.summary: The UPC Munich Local Division (the LD Munich) has dismissed an infringement action relating to a medical device. It confirmed that a device can still infringe a patent, even if it is normally operated in a non-infringing way, as long as the patent-infringing use remains possible.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 慕尼黑分部驳回 Emboline 诉 AorticLab 专利侵权案。法院确认，即使设备通常以非侵权方式运行，只要侵权使用仍有可能，原则上可构成侵权。但针对医疗器械，法院引入关键限制：仅当该潜在用途符合专业实践及公认医学科学规则（即合乎医术）时，才认定为侵权。本案中被告设备若按专利方式移除将损坏组织，故不侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Munich Local Division dismissed an infringement action concerning an embolic protection device, establishing that potential infringing use must align with professional medical practices to constitute infringement. While confirming that a device can infringe if infringing use remains possible despite normal non-infringing operation, the court introduced a 'lege artis' qualification for medical devices. The court found the defendant's device lacked the claimed graspable structure suitable for hook engagement without damaging the filter mesh. Consequently, the alleged infringing removal method was deemed neither intended nor suitable under recognized rules of medical science.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械出口企业应重视 UPC 判例，产品设计不仅要规避技术特征，还需论证替代用途不符合医疗常规。建议在欧洲专利申请及维权中，将“符合医疗实践”作为侵权比对的重要抗辩或主张依据，提升应对精度。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment introduces a significant limitation on the scope of infringement for medical devices within the UPC system. Rights holders must now demonstrate that alleged infringing uses are not only technically possible but also compliant with standard medical procedures. For defendants, this provides a robust defense avenue by arguing that specific claim features are incompatible with safe, standard clinical practice, thereby negating infringement even if technical overlap exists.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 确立医疗器械侵权需同时满足“技术可能性”与“医疗合规性”双重标准", "若潜在侵权用途违背医学常规或造成组织损伤，则不构成专利侵权行为", "本案因被告设备无法在不损坏情况下通过钩状结构移除，被判定未落入保护范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Potential patent infringement for medical devices requires the use to be consistent with professional medical practices and recognized scientific rules.", "A device does not infringe if the alleged infringing feature is unsuitable for its intended purpose without causing damage to the device or tissue.", "The UPC Munich Local Division dismissed the claim as the defendant's device lacked a graspable structure engageable by a hook as required by the patent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口欧洲医械产品需评估潜在用途是否符合当地公认医疗操作规范", "利用“非合乎医术”抗辩策略，可有效阻断基于理论可能性的侵权指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent owners enforcing medical device patents in the UPC must assess whether alleged infringing uses align with standard clinical protocols and safety norms.", "Practice note: Defendants should evaluate whether the accused product's design makes the claimed infringing use medically impractical or unsafe, leveraging the 'lege artis' requir…", "What to monitor: Future UPC divisions may adopt similar 'medical practice' qualifications, potentially influencing claim construction and infringement assessments across member st…"]
core_points_en: Potential patent infringement for medical devices requires the use to be consistent with professional medical practices and recognized scientific rules.
A device does not infringe if the alleged infringing feature is unsuitable for its intended purpose without causing damage to the device or tissue.
The UPC Munich Local Division dismissed the claim as the defendant's device lacked a graspable structure engageable by a hook as required by the patent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent owners enforcing medical device patents in the UPC must assess whether alleged infringing uses align with standard clinical protocols and safety norms.
Practice note: Defendants should evaluate whether the accused product's design makes the claimed infringing use medically impractical or unsafe, leveraging the 'lege artis' requir…
What to monitor: Future UPC divisions may adopt similar 'medical practice' qualifications, potentially influencing claim construction and infringement assessments across member st…

items.title: UPC: Website Use Undermines Language Objections – KeyMed v PR Medical | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC：网站使用削弱语言异议效力
items.title_en: UPC Milan Division: Website Accessibility Undermines Language Objections in KeyMed v PR Medical
items.summary: The Milan Local Division rejects a language objection under Rule 14.2(b) RoP, holding that alleged cross‑border infringement via a website prevents SMEs relying on language protections.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院米兰地方法院在KeyMed诉PR Medical案中，驳回被告基于《程序规则》第14.2(b)条的语言异议。法院认定，被告通过网站进行的跨境侵权威胁，使其无法援引旨在保护中小企业的语言保护条款，确立了较高的异议门槛。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Milan Local Division has rejected a preliminary language objection raised under Rule 14.2(b) of the Rules of Procedure. The defendant, an Italian company, argued that proceedings should be conducted in Italian since it was domiciled in Italy and claimed infringement occurred solely there. However, the court found that the defendant’s website made its products accessible across multiple contracting member states, creating a threat of cross-border infringement. This potential for infringement in other jurisdictions meant that Article 33(1)(a) of the UPC Agreement allowed competence to lie with divisions other than the Italian one. Consequently, the strict language protection intended for small and medium-sized enterprises did not apply. This ruling establishes a high threshold for relying on local language protections when digital presence suggests broader market reach.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决大幅提高了UPC诉讼中语言异议的成立门槛。实务中需关注网站 accessibility 对管辖及程序语言的影响，传统基于被告所在地的语言保护策略在现代数字环境下可能失效，需重新评估程序抗辩路径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision significantly impacts entities operating within the UPC system, particularly those with an online commercial presence. Companies can no longer assume that being domiciled in a specific member state guarantees the right to litigate in the local official language if their website is accessible elsewhere. The mere possibility of cross-border infringement via digital channels is sufficient to defeat language objections under Rule 14.2(b). Practitioners should advise clients to anticipate English as the likely language of proceedings in many cross-border scenarios. Strategic planning must include readiness for multi-language litigation capabilities to avoid procedural disadvantages and ensure effective defense strategies from the outset.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["米兰地方法院驳回被告将诉讼语言由英语改为意大利语的初步异议申请", "法院认定被告网站构成的跨境侵权威胁，排除了仅适用被告所在地语言的规定", "该裁决表明，只要网站可跨国访问，中小企业很难满足语言保护规则的适用条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Website accessibility creates cross-border infringement threats, preventing reliance on single-jurisdiction language protections under Rule 14.2(b).", "Rule 14.2(b) aims to protect SMEs from unfamiliar languages but requires strict proof that infringement is confined to the defendant's domicile state.", "In modern digital contexts, defendants struggle to prove infringement is limited to their home country due to global website availability."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["网站跨境可访问性成为否定语言保护条款适用的关键事实依据", "中小企业在UPC诉讼中援引程序性语言保护规则的难度显著增加", "需持续观察后续案件是否沿袭此高标准，以及对该规则实际效用的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Global website accessibility may disqualify companies from invoking local language protections in UPC litigation.", "Practice note: Prepare for English-language proceedings by default when defending against cross-border infringement claims involving digital sales.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'threatened infringement' via websites and how courts assess the geographic scope of online activities."]
core_points_en: Website accessibility creates cross-border infringement threats, preventing reliance on single-jurisdiction language protections under Rule 14.2(b).
Rule 14.2(b) aims to protect SMEs from unfamiliar languages but requires strict proof that infringement is confined to the defendant's domicile state.
In modern digital contexts, defendants struggle to prove infringement is limited to their home country due to global website availability.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Global website accessibility may disqualify companies from invoking local language protections in UPC litigation.
Practice note: Prepare for English-language proceedings by default when defending against cross-border infringement claims involving digital sales.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'threatened infringement' via websites and how courts assess the geographic scope of online activities.

items.title: "Replay of the same movie”: DOJ flags familiar antitrust tactics in streaming patent wars
items.title_zh: 美司法部警示流媒体专利战中的反垄断策略
items.title_en: DOJ Warns Against Presuming Market Power in Streaming SEP Disputes
items.summary: Speaking at LeadershIP 2026, Antitrust Division official Dina Kallay drew parallels with smartphone wars and warned that courts should not presume market power because a patent is standard essential
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国司法部反垄断司官员在 LeadershIP 2026 会议上指出，流媒体领域的标准必要专利争端重现智能手机战争策略。其强调法院不应仅因专利属于标准必要而推定持有人拥有市场支配力，呼吁理性审视许可谈判中的竞争法边界，防止权利滥用。
items.ai_summary_en: During LeadershIP 2026, Antitrust Division official Dina Kallay highlighted striking similarities between current streaming patent conflicts and past smartphone litigation. She cautioned judicial bodies against automatically presuming market power solely based on a patent’s standard-essential status. The remarks underscore the need for courts to carefully evaluate competitive dynamics rather than relying on structural assumptions. This approach aims to prevent the misuse of intellectual property rights while ensuring fair licensing negotiations. By drawing these parallels, the Department of Justice signals a continued focus on balancing innovation incentives with antitrust principles, urging stakeholders to adhere to rigorous legal standards when resolving disputes involving standard-essential patents in emerging digital markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营需警惕美欧反垄断联动风险，避免在 SEP 许可中被认定为滥用市场地位。应完善 FRAND 合规流程，保留谈判证据，以应对潜在跨辖区调查。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in standard-essential patent licensing should anticipate increased regulatory scrutiny regarding antitrust compliance. The alignment between US and EU enforcement trends suggests that aggressive negotiation tactics may face cross-jurisdictional challenges. Rights holders and implementers alike must ensure their FRAND commitments are documented meticulously to demonstrate good faith. Maintaining detailed records of licensing discussions provides critical evidence against allegations of hold-up or hold-out strategies. Proactive compliance measures can mitigate risks associated with simultaneous investigations by multiple competition authorities, ensuring smoother resolution of complex IP disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["美司法部警示流媒体专利战重演手机行业策略", "反对仅因 SEP 属性直接推定市场支配地位", "呼吁法院在许可纠纷中审慎适用反垄断法规"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Streaming SEP disputes mirror historical smartphone patent wars, signaling recurring strategic patterns in licensing negotiations.", "Courts should not presume market dominance merely because a patent is designated as standard-essential technology.", "Judicial scrutiny must balance antitrust regulations with fair licensing practices to prevent potential rights abuse."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注美欧反垄断执法协同对 SEP 许可的影响", "强化 FRAND 谈判记录以防范跨境合规风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor evolving US-EU antitrust coordination impacting SEP licensing strategies and market conduct.", "Practice note: Strengthen FRAND negotiation documentation to substantiate good faith efforts and counter abuse claims.", "What to monitor: Cross-border regulatory actions targeting alleged anti-competitive behaviors in standard-essential patent cases."]
core_points_en: Streaming SEP disputes mirror historical smartphone patent wars, signaling recurring strategic patterns in licensing negotiations.
Courts should not presume market dominance merely because a patent is designated as standard-essential technology.
Judicial scrutiny must balance antitrust regulations with fair licensing practices to prevent potential rights abuse.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor evolving US-EU antitrust coordination impacting SEP licensing strategies and market conduct.
Practice note: Strengthen FRAND negotiation documentation to substantiate good faith efforts and counter abuse claims.
What to monitor: Cross-border regulatory actions targeting alleged anti-competitive behaviors in standard-essential patent cases.
