items.title: Failure to seek UPC confidentiality order strips EOflow’s trade secrets of protection
items.title_zh: 未申请UPC保密令致EOflow商业秘密丧失保护
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Failure to Seek Confidentiality Order Strips Trade Secrets of Protection
items.summary: Judges rule that information shared without obtaining Rule 262.2 RoP protection loses its confidential character in spat with rival insulin-delivery device maker Insulet
items.ai_summary_zh: 在EOflow与胰岛素输送设备制造商Insulet的纠纷中，法官裁定：若当事人未依据《程序规则》第262.2条申请保密保护，其在诉讼中共享的信息将失去机密性质。该裁决强调了在UPC程序中主动寻求保密令对维持商业秘密保护的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: Judges at the Unified Patent Court have ruled that information shared without obtaining protection under Rule 262.2 of the Rules of Procedure loses its confidential character. This decision arises from a dispute between EOflow and rival insulin-delivery device maker Insulet. The court emphasized that parties must proactively seek procedural safeguards to maintain secrecy. By failing to apply for a confidentiality order, EOflow exposed sensitive data, thereby forfeiting trade secret protection. This judgment establishes strict procedural compliance standards, highlighting that mere assertion of confidentiality is insufficient. Parties must actively utilize available legal mechanisms to secure their proprietary information during litigation, or risk irreversible disclosure and loss of legal remedies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显UPC程序规则对实体权利的影响。实务中需密切关注法院对程序性失误（如未及时申请保密令）导致实体权利（商业秘密）丧失的认定标准，以及后续类似案件中当事人对敏感信息披露的程序合规操作。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights critical procedural risks in UPC litigation. Parties must conduct upfront assessments to identify sensitive technical data requiring protection. It is essential to immediately apply for confidentiality orders under Rule 262.2 before exchanging any such information. Procedural compliance is as vital as substantive legal arguments; neglecting these steps can lead to irreversible commercial damage through public disclosure. Legal teams should integrate strict confidentiality protocols into their litigation strategy from the outset to prevent inadvertent waiver of trade secret rights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法官裁定未依UPC程序规则262.2条申请保护的信息丧失机密性", "该案涉及EOflow与竞争对手Insulet之间的胰岛素设备纠纷", "未能寻求保密令直接导致EOflow主张的商业秘密失去法律保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC judges ruled that sharing information without securing Rule 262.2 protection causes it to lose its confidential legal status.", "EOflow lost trade secret protection in its dispute with Insulet due to procedural negligence in failing to request a confidentiality order.", "The judgment underscores the mandatory obligation for parties to actively use procedural rules to safeguard sensitive information."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC程序合规直接影响商业秘密等实体权利的存续状态", "需观察法院对 Rule 262.2 适用条件及后果的进一步解释", "关注后续案件中当事人如何平衡证据披露与保密申请"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants must prioritize proactive procedural measures to preserve trade secret confidentiality during UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: Secure Rule 262.2 confidentiality orders immediately before sharing any sensitive technical or business information.", "What to monitor: Strict adherence to procedural compliance is required to avoid waiving protection and exposing core data to public view."]
core_points_en: UPC judges ruled that sharing information without securing Rule 262.2 protection causes it to lose its confidential legal status.
EOflow lost trade secret protection in its dispute with Insulet due to procedural negligence in failing to request a confidentiality order.
The judgment underscores the mandatory obligation for parties to actively use procedural rules to safeguard sensitive information.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants must prioritize proactive procedural measures to preserve trade secret confidentiality during UPC proceedings.
Practice note: Secure Rule 262.2 confidentiality orders immediately before sharing any sensitive technical or business information.
What to monitor: Strict adherence to procedural compliance is required to avoid waiving protection and exposing core data to public view.

items.title: BREAKING: Geely sued by IP Bridge in Brazil as Avanci licensors seek next Chinese licensee
items.title_zh: 突发：吉利在巴西遭 IP Bridge 起诉，Avanci 寻求下一家中国被许可人
items.title_en: IP Bridge Sues Geely in Brazil as Avanci Licensors Target Next Chinese Licensee
items.summary: At least four Chinese carmakers have taken a pool licence to date, and several bilateral deals have also been signed
items.ai_summary_zh: 日本 IP Bridge 代表 Avanci 专利池许可方在巴西起诉吉利汽车，指控其未获得标准必要专利许可。此前已有至少四家中国车企签署池许可或多边协议。此案标志着专利主张实体将诉讼战场扩展至南美，旨在迫使剩余中国主流车企接受全球许可条款，加剧了跨国知识产权博弈。
items.ai_summary_en: IP Bridge, representing Avanci patent pool licensors, has initiated legal proceedings against Geely in Brazil. The complaint alleges that the automotive manufacturer failed to secure licenses for standard-essential patents. This action follows agreements reached with at least four other Chinese carmakers through pool licensing or bilateral deals. The case signals a strategic expansion of patent enforcement into South America by non-practicing entities. The objective is to compel remaining major Chinese manufacturers to accept global licensing terms. This development intensifies cross-border intellectual property disputes and highlights the increasing pressure on automotive companies operating internationally to resolve SEP licensing obligations comprehensively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出海需警惕非欧盟法域的 SEP 诉讼风险，避免单一市场应对。建议提前评估全球专利组合，主动参与 FRAND 谈判或构建防御性专利联盟，防止被各个击破。
items.ai_insight_en: Automotive companies expanding globally must assess standard-essential patent risks beyond traditional European and US markets. Relying solely on local defenses is insufficient against coordinated multi-jurisdictional enforcement strategies. Rights holders are increasingly targeting emerging markets to create leverage for global settlements. Companies should proactively evaluate their FRAND compliance status and consider defensive patent alliances. Early engagement in negotiations prevents fragmented litigation exposure. A unified global IP response mechanism is essential to mitigate the risk of injunctions or excessive damages across different legal systems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IP Bridge 在巴西发起诉讼，意在施压吉利达成全球专利许可协议", "Avanci 专利池已获多家中国车企授权，但未覆盖所有主要制造商", "诉讼地选择巴西显示权利人多辖区围堵策略，增加企业应诉成本"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["IP Bridge filed suit in Brazil to pressure Geely into concluding a comprehensive global patent licensing agreement.", "While several Chinese automakers joined the Avanci pool, key manufacturers remain unlicensed, prompting further enforcement actions.", "Selecting Brazil as the litigation venue demonstrates a multi-jurisdictional strategy designed to increase defendants' compliance costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕权利人多法域连环诉讼，建立全球统一的专利应对机制", "主动评估 FRAND 合规性，避免因消极应对导致高额赔偿或禁售"]

While several Chinese automakers joined the Avanci pool, key manufacturers remain unlicensed, prompting further enforcement actions.

items.title: Information
items.title_zh: 2025 年司法统计：高活跃度伴随审理周期缩短
items.title_en: CJEU 2025 Statistics: Record Case Closures and Reduced Proceedings Duration
items.summary: Judicial statistics 2025: a very high level of activity accompanied by a reduction in the length of proceedings
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院与普通法院发布 2025 年司法统计数据。尽管部分初步裁决管辖权转移，案件总量仍创新高。两院结案数达 1898 件，积压案件降至 18 年来最低。平均审理周期显著缩短，其中普通法院仅需 16 个月。意大利和波兰提交的初步裁决请求数量显著，显示成员国司法互动频繁。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union released its 2025 judicial statistics, revealing a significant increase in judicial activity alongside improved procedural efficiency. The two courts closed a record 1,898 cases, while the backlog of pending cases dropped to its lowest level in 18 years. Average proceedings duration decreased for both institutions, with the General Court reducing its average time to 16 months. Italy and Poland submitted a notable volume of requests for preliminary rulings, reflecting sustained engagement from these Member States despite the transfer of certain jurisdictional responsibilities.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The reduction in average proceedings duration and the decline in case backlogs indicate enhanced procedural efficiency within the EU judicial system. For rights holders and litigants, this trend suggests potentially faster resolution times for intellectual property disputes and preliminary ruling references. The high volume of referrals from specific Member States highlights active national judicial engagement with EU law, which may influence the development of harmonized legal standards relevant to cross-border commercial operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["两院结案总数创历史新高，积压案件量降至十八年最低点", "案件平均审理周期缩短，普通法院效率提升至十六个月", "意大利与波兰成为初步裁决请求的主要来源成员国"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Justice and General Court closed 1,898 cases in 2025, marking the highest annual closure rate in the institution's history.", "Pending cases declined to 2,489, representing the lowest backlog level recorded in eighteen years across both EU courts.", "Average proceedings duration shortened significantly, with the General Court achieving an average of 16 months compared to 18.5 months previously."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["审理周期缩短利于企业快速解决争端并降低时间成本", "关注意波两国高频诉讼动态以提前布局合规与风控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Shorter average proceedings durations may reduce the temporal uncertainty associated with ongoing litigation and preliminary ruling references.", "Practice note: Monitor the evolving case management practices of the General Court, which achieved record closures while lowering its pending caseload.", "What to monitor: Track the nature and volume of preliminary ruling requests originating from Italy and Poland to anticipate emerging interpretative trends."]
core_points_en: The Court of Justice and General Court closed 1,898 cases in 2025, marking the highest annual closure rate in the institution's history.
Pending cases declined to 2,489, representing the lowest backlog level recorded in eighteen years across both EU courts.
Average proceedings duration shortened significantly, with the General Court achieving an average of 16 months compared to 18.5 months previously.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Shorter average proceedings durations may reduce the temporal uncertainty associated with ongoing litigation and preliminary ruling references.
Practice note: Monitor the evolving case management practices of the General Court, which achieved record closures while lowering its pending caseload.
What to monitor: Track the nature and volume of preliminary ruling requests originating from Italy and Poland to anticipate emerging interpretative trends.

items.title: Dolby wins four-country Opus injunction as UPC hands down third FRAND ruling
items.title_zh: 杜比获四国禁令：UPC 作出第三项 FRAND 裁决
items.title_en: UPC Grants Dolby Four-Country Opus Injunction in Third FRAND Ruling
items.summary: Beko/Arçelik failed to comply with Huawei-ZTE steps, local division finds
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）地方分院作出第三项关于标准必要专利的 FRAND 裁决。法院认定被告 Beko/Arçelik 未遵守华为 - 中兴框架下的谈判步骤，构成过错。据此，原告杜比实验室成功获得针对 Opus 编码技术在四个成员国的禁令救济。此案进一步明确了 UPC 在 FRAND 抗辩审查中的严格立场及禁令颁发标准。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Local Division has issued its third ruling concerning Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) licensing obligations. The court determined that the defendant, Beko/Arçelik, failed to adhere to the procedural steps established by the Huawei v ZTE framework during negotiations. Consequently, the defendant was found liable for fault in the licensing process. Based on this finding, the plaintiff, Dolby Laboratories, successfully secured an injunction against the infringement of its standard essential patents covering Opus audio coding technology. This relief applies across four UPC member states. The decision underscores the UPC's rigorous approach to evaluating FRAND defenses and establishes clear precedents for granting injunctive relief when implementers do not engage in good-faith negotiations according to established legal standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 诉讼时，须严格遵循华为 - 中兴步骤，避免因程序瑕疵遭禁令。建议提前评估专利许可策略，积极参与善意谈判，并密切关注 UPC 判例趋势以规避跨国禁售风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing SEP litigation in Europe must strictly comply with the Huawei-ZTE negotiation framework to mitigate the risk of injunctive relief. Procedural compliance is critical, as courts are increasingly willing to grant bans for even minor deviations from expected conduct. Rights holders should proactively assess their licensing strategies and engage in documented, good-faith negotiations early in any dispute. Implementers must remain vigilant regarding UPC case law trends, particularly concerning the threshold for proving willingness to license. Failure to align with these procedural expectations can lead to significant market disruption through multi-jurisdictional sales bans.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 裁定被告未遵循华为 - 中兴谈判步骤导致败诉", "杜比成功获颁覆盖四国的标准必要专利禁令", "此案为 UPC 成立以来作出的第三项 FRAND 相关裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Local Division ruled that the defendant failed to follow Huawei-ZTE negotiation steps, resulting in a finding of fault.", "Dolby Laboratories obtained a cross-border injunction covering four member states for its Opus standard essential patents.", "This judgment represents the third FRAND-related decision issued by the UPC since its establishment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严格遵循华为 - 中兴步骤是避免禁令的关键防线", "需高度警惕 UPC 跨成员国禁令带来的市场阻断风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to Huawei-ZTE steps is the primary defense against injunctions in SEP disputes.", "Practice note: Parties must document all negotiation interactions meticulously to demonstrate good-faith efforts.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on FRAND defenses and the scope of cross-border injunctive relief."]
core_points_en: The UPC Local Division ruled that the defendant failed to follow Huawei-ZTE negotiation steps, resulting in a finding of fault.
Dolby Laboratories obtained a cross-border injunction covering four member states for its Opus standard essential patents.
This judgment represents the third FRAND-related decision issued by the UPC since its establishment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to Huawei-ZTE steps is the primary defense against injunctions in SEP disputes.
Practice note: Parties must document all negotiation interactions meticulously to demonstrate good-faith efforts.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on FRAND defenses and the scope of cross-border injunctive relief.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 10, 2026, Order, Retroactive Time Extension Under R. 9.3(a) RoP – UPC_CoA_37/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定：依程序规则9.3(a)可 retroactive 延长提交期限
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Permits Retroactive Extension of Written Submission Deadlines Under Rule 9.3(a)
items.summary: R. 320 RoP applies only where missing a time limit causes a party to lose a substantive right or means of redress, such as the right to initiate appeal proceedings or to challenge default judgments.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在2026年3月10日的命令中澄清，程序规则第9.3(a)条适用于正在进行程序中的书面陈述期限延长，即使请求在截止后提出也允许追溯延期。第320条仅适用于错过期限导致丧失实质性权利的情形。法院确认一审汉堡地方法院行使裁量权无误，驳回了被告基于其他案例的抗辩，强调期限约束力仍由法院裁量掌控。
items.ai_summary_en: In a March 10, 2026 order (UPC_CoA_37/2026), the UPC Court of Appeal clarified that Rule 9.3(a) of the Rules of Procedure governs deadline extensions for written submissions in ongoing proceedings. The Court confirmed that such extensions may be granted retroactively, even if the request is filed after the original deadline has expired. This ruling distinguishes Rule 9.3(a) from Rule 320, which applies strictly where missing a time limit results in the loss of substantive rights or means of redress, such as the right to appeal. The decision reinforces procedural consistency by rejecting arguments that retroactive extensions circumvent strict deadline rules, thereby validating the Local Division’s exercise of procedural discretion in managing case timelines effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定统一了UPC内部对程序期限延长的解释，明确了第9.3(a)条与第320条的适用边界。实务中需注意，虽允许事后申请延期，但决定权完全在于法院裁量，并非当事人权利。不同地方法院此前存在的实践分歧在此案中得到一定程度的厘清，后续应关注法院对“明显错误”审查标准的持续应用。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity on procedural flexibility within the Unified Patent Court. Parties facing inadvertent missed deadlines for written pleadings can seek relief under Rule 9.3(a) without triggering the stricter forfeiture provisions of Rule 320. However, such relief remains subject to judicial discretion and requires demonstrating that the delay was not intentional or prejudicial to procedural fairness. Litigants should maintain robust internal docketing systems to minimize reliance on post-deadline remedies. Understanding this distinction between procedural management rules and substantive rights preservation is essential for effective litigation strategy and risk mitigation in UPC proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["程序规则9.3(a)条管辖进行中程序的书面陈述延期，允许在截止日后提出追溯延期请求", "程序规则320条仅适用于错过期限导致丧失上诉权等实质性救济手段的情形，不适用于普通文书…", "上诉法院确认一审地方法院将误交答辩状视为人为错误并批准延期，未构成明显裁量权滥用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 9.3(a) allows retroactive extension of deadlines for written submissions in pending proceedings, even when requested after expiry.", "Rule 320 applies exclusively to situations where missing a deadline causes loss of substantive rights or legal remedies.", "Granting retroactive extensions under Rule 9.3(a) constitutes valid procedural discretion and does not circumvent strict deadline rules."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确UPC程序规则中一般期限延长与丧失实体权利补救条款的适用区别", "确认法院拥有在截止日后接受延期申请的裁量权，强化了程序管理的灵活性", "需持续观察UPC各分院在行使程序裁量权时的一致性，特别是针对人为错误的认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage Rule 9.3(a) to seek retroactive relief for missed written submission deadlines, reducing procedural default risks.", "Practice note: Strengthen internal deadline monitoring protocols while familiarizing teams with UPC discretionary powers for urgent remedial actions.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 9.3(a) versus Rule 320 across different UPC divisions to track consistent procedural standards."]
core_points_en: Rule 9.3(a) allows retroactive extension of deadlines for written submissions in pending proceedings, even when requested after expiry.
Rule 320 applies exclusively to situations where missing a deadline causes loss of substantive rights or legal remedies.
Granting retroactive extensions under Rule 9.3(a) constitutes valid procedural discretion and does not circumvent strict deadline rules.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage Rule 9.3(a) to seek retroactive relief for missed written submission deadlines, reducing procedural default risks.
Practice note: Strengthen internal deadline monitoring protocols while familiarizing teams with UPC discretionary powers for urgent remedial actions.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 9.3(a) versus Rule 320 across different UPC divisions to track consistent procedural standards.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 6, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_789/2025 And UPC_CoA_813/2025 – Provisional Measures For Hair Care Appliance Attachment Patent
items.title_zh: UPC上诉院就护发附件专利临时措施作出裁定
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Claim Construction and Injunction Scope in Provisional Measures
items.summary: Claim construction must balance function and structure: interpreting structural claim features solely by their function is insufficient; the physical and spatial configuration taught by the patent must equally...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉院在护发设备附件专利案中裁定，权利要求解释需平衡功能与结构，不能仅依功能解读。法院确认一旦侵权成立，可颁发涵盖所有使用形式的广泛禁令，不限于既往行为。此外，审查历史在解释中权重有限，且上诉期间针对新产品的修改请求获准。部分程序因等待CJEU裁决而中止，其余部分继续进行。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued an order regarding provisional measures for a hair care appliance attachment patent. The court held that claim construction must balance function and structure, requiring geometric determination of spatial features like 'overlap' from viewpoints taught by the description, rather than solely functional interpretations. It rejected limiting injunctions to past infringing acts, confirming broad coverage under the UPCA once infringement is established. Additionally, the court assigned limited weight to prosecution history from the EPO or foreign offices, prioritizing the patent’s internal documents. Amended infringement claims covering new products were deemed admissible in appeal proceedings where they did not unfairly hinder the defense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在临时措施中对权利要求解释的严格标准，强调结构特征的几何确定性。同时确认了禁令的广泛适用性及上诉阶段修改请求的可接纳性，为后续UPC诉讼中的程序策略与证据提交提供了重要参考。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling reinforces the primacy of patent specification and drawings over external prosecution history in UPC proceedings. It confirms that successful rights holders can secure broad injunctive relief extending beyond specific past incidents. Practitioners should prepare robust geometric arguments for structural claim terms and anticipate admissibility of amended claims targeting newly launched products during appeals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["权利要求解释须结合说明书与附图的物理空间配置，仅依功能解读结构特征不足", "确立侵权后，禁令范围可覆盖所有专利使用形式，无需局限于已发生的具体侵权行为", "EPO或外国局审查历史权重有限，不得推翻基于专利文本本身的独立司法解释"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Claim construction requires balancing function with physical configuration taught by patent description and drawings.", "Injunctions may cover all forms of patent use, not limited to previously committed infringing acts.", "Prosecution history from EPO or foreign offices holds limited weight against clear patent text interpretation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC倾向于依据专利内部证据（权利要求、说明书、附图）进行独立解释，外部审查历史影响有限", "临时禁令范围具有扩张性，只要证明侵权风险，即可覆盖未发生但潜在的使用行为", "需关注涉及CJEU先行裁决时的部分中止程序机制，及其对紧急临时措施案件审理节奏的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Broad injunctions reduce the need for multiple enforcement actions against evolving product iterations once initial infringement is proven.", "Practice note: Prioritize description and drawings for claim construction arguments; do not rely heavily on EPO or US file histories to override clear text.", "What to monitor: Admissibility standards for amended infringement claims in appeal stages, particularly regarding newly marketed products."]
core_points_en: Claim construction requires balancing function with physical configuration taught by patent description and drawings.
Injunctions may cover all forms of patent use, not limited to previously committed infringing acts.
Prosecution history from EPO or foreign offices holds limited weight against clear patent text interpretation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Broad injunctions reduce the need for multiple enforcement actions against evolving product iterations once initial infringement is proven.
Practice note: Prioritize description and drawings for claim construction arguments; do not rely heavily on EPO or US file histories to override clear text.
What to monitor: Admissibility standards for amended infringement claims in appeal stages, particularly regarding newly marketed products.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, March 9, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_758/2024, UPC_CFI_259/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫：驳回模糊补充诉状请求的程序令解析
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Vague Request for Further Written Pleadings Under Rule 36
items.summary: The Court must take into account the reasons put forward by the requesting party as to why further pleadings are necessary.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫地方法院发布程序令，依据诉讼规则第 36 条驳回原告进一步书面陈述的请求。法院确立双重平衡测试：既需考量申请方理由，亦须评估对程序进度及延误风险的影响。裁定指出，仅泛称存在异议陈述而未指明具体新事实的请求过于模糊，且口头听证足以保障听审权，独任法官即可作出此类程序决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Düsseldorf issued a procedural order dismissing the claimant’s request for further written pleadings under Rule 36 of the Rules of Procedure. The Court established a two-pronged balancing test, requiring an assessment of both the requesting party’s justification and the potential impact on procedural efficiency and delay risks. The ruling emphasizes that vague assertions regarding objectionable statements, without identifying concrete new facts, are insufficient to justify additional written submissions. Furthermore, the Court clarified that denying such requests does not unduly restrict the right to be heard, as procedural objections remain available and the oral hearing serves as a meaningful safeguard for fairness. The decision was issued by the judge-rapporteur acting alone, confirming that full panel involvement is not required for these procedural matters.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中提出程序请求时，务必提供具体事实依据，避免空泛主张被驳。应重视口头听证环节的应对准备，将其作为弥补书面阶段限制、维护自身权益的核心防线，同时适应独任法官处理程序事项的高效节奏。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties appearing before the UPC must ensure that any request for additional written pleadings is substantiated with precise factual grounds rather than general objections. Courts will strictly apply the balancing test under Rule 36, prioritizing procedural efficiency over expansive written exchanges when specific new facts are absent. Consequently, litigants should prioritize rigorous preparation for the oral hearing, viewing it as the primary mechanism to address disputed points and preserve their right to be heard. Adapting to the efficient handling of procedural issues by single judges allows teams to focus resources on substantive oral advocacy, ensuring robust defense or enforcement strategies within the streamlined UPC framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["补充诉状请求须通过理由必要性与程序延误风险的双重平衡测试", "未指明具体新事实的模糊异议不足以正当化进一步书面陈述请求", "独任法官可签发程序令，口头听证被视为保障公平听审权的关键措施"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Requests for further pleadings require a dual balance between necessity reasons and procedural delay risks.", "Vague objections lacking specific new facts fail to justify additional written submissions under Rule 36.", "Judge-rapporteurs may issue procedural orders alone, with oral hearings safeguarding fair trial rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["程序请求必须具体化，泛泛而谈的异议无法获得法院支持", "充分利用口头听证机会，将其作为保障听审权的关键补救途径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all procedural requests cite specific new facts to avoid dismissal for vagueness under Rule 36.", "Practice note: Treat the oral hearing as the critical venue for addressing disputed arguments when written extensions are denied.", "What to monitor: Procedural orders issued by judge-rapporteurs acting alone, which do not require full panel deliberation."]
core_points_en: Requests for further pleadings require a dual balance between necessity reasons and procedural delay risks.
Vague objections lacking specific new facts fail to justify additional written submissions under Rule 36.
Judge-rapporteurs may issue procedural orders alone, with oral hearings safeguarding fair trial rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all procedural requests cite specific new facts to avoid dismissal for vagueness under Rule 36.
Practice note: Treat the oral hearing as the critical venue for addressing disputed arguments when written extensions are denied.
What to monitor: Procedural orders issued by judge-rapporteurs acting alone, which do not require full panel deliberation.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 6, 2026, Decision, UPC_COA_895/2025 & UPC_COA_896/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭裁定：撤诉许可及诉讼费退还新规适用
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Withdrawal Rules and Fee Reimbursement Under Amended RoP
items.summary: Under R. 265.1 RoP, a party may withdraw its action as long as no final decision has been rendered. The withdrawal shall not be permitted if the other party...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在 2026 年 3 月裁决中确认，若对方无异议且无合法利益，上诉人可依据程序规则第 265 条撤回上诉。同时，法院明确 2026 年生效的规则修订版将诉讼费退还比例从 60% 降至 50%，适用版本以申请退还日期为准。此外，若双方未请求，法院可在撤诉时不作出费用决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal confirmed that Rule 265.1 RoP permits withdrawal of appeals if the respondent consents and lacks a legitimate interest in continuation. The decision clarified that amended Rule 370.9 RoP, effective January 1, 2026, reduces fee reimbursement from 60% to 50%, with applicability determined by the date of the reimbursement application rather than the appeal filing date. Additionally, the Court declined to issue a cost decision upon withdrawal where neither party explicitly requested one, despite procedural provisions suggesting otherwise.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity on procedural mechanics for withdrawing actions at the appellate stage. Practitioners must carefully time reimbursement applications to navigate the reduced fee recovery rates introduced in 2026. Furthermore, the decision highlights that cost orders are not automatic upon withdrawal; parties must actively request them to secure formal determinations on costs, impacting overall litigation budgeting and final case resolution strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["对方同意且无合法利益阻碍时，允许依据规则 265 条撤回上诉", "诉讼费退还比例降至 50%，适用版本取决于退还申请提交日期", "若当事人未请求，上诉庭在撤诉程序中可不作具体费用裁定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 265.1 RoP allows withdrawal of appeals when the respondent consents and has no legitimate interest in the action being decided.", "Amended Rule 370.9 RoP reduces fee reimbursement to 50%, applying based on the reimbursement application date after December 31, 2025.", "The Court of Appeal refrained from issuing a cost decision upon withdrawal where neither party formally requested such a determination."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 诉讼费退还比例下调，企业需重新评估诉讼成本收益比", "撤诉策略应结合对方态度与程序节点，避免因规则误读受损"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Understanding the reduced fee reimbursement rate is essential for accurate litigation cost forecasting and settlement negotiations.", "Practice note: Parties should explicitly request cost decisions during withdrawal proceedings to ensure formal resolution of financial liabilities.", "What to monitor: Future jurisprudence regarding what constitutes a 'legitimate interest' for respondents opposing withdrawal under Rule 265.1."]
core_points_en: Rule 265.1 RoP allows withdrawal of appeals when the respondent consents and has no legitimate interest in the action being decided.
Amended Rule 370.9 RoP reduces fee reimbursement to 50%, applying based on the reimbursement application date after December 31, 2025.
The Court of Appeal refrained from issuing a cost decision upon withdrawal where neither party formally requested such a determination.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Understanding the reduced fee reimbursement rate is essential for accurate litigation cost forecasting and settlement negotiations.
Practice note: Parties should explicitly request cost decisions during withdrawal proceedings to ensure formal resolution of financial liabilities.
What to monitor: Future jurisprudence regarding what constitutes a 'legitimate interest' for respondents opposing withdrawal under Rule 265.1.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 11, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_934/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院裁定撤诉规则适用于临时措施申请
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Rule 265 Withdrawal Applies to Provisional Measures and Appeals
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarified that the term "action" (Klage) in Rule 265.1 RoP encompasses applications for provisional measures.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在 2026 年 3 月 11 日判决中澄清，程序规则第 265 条中的“诉讼”一词涵盖临时措施申请。法院确认该撤诉机制不仅适用于侵权或无效等主诉程序，同样延伸至临时救济程序及上诉阶段。若双方达成和解且无异议，法院可准许撤诉并免除单独的费用裁决，确立了更灵活的程序退出机制。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that 'action' under Rule 265.1 RoP encompasses applications for provisional measures, not just main infringement or revocation proceedings. The Court confirmed that applicants may withdraw such interim relief requests during pending appeals, provided the withdrawal occurs before a final appellate decision. In this case, involving Roche and Menarini regarding EP 1 962 668, the parties reached an out-of-court settlement. The Court permitted the withdrawal, noting no legitimate interests opposed it. Furthermore, the Court held that a separate cost decision is unnecessary under Rule 265.2(c) when parties unanimously agree to bear their own costs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利纠纷时，可利用此规则在上诉阶段灵活撤回临时措施申请以促成和解。建议密切关注 UPC 程序动态，在面临禁令风险时，将程序性撤诉作为谈判筹码，优化诉讼成本与商业策略的平衡。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision provides procedural clarity for rights holders and defendants engaging in UPC interim relief proceedings. It confirms that the withdrawal mechanism is available throughout the appellate phase for provisional measures, facilitating settlement negotiations even after an appeal has been lodged. The ruling on costs offers administrative efficiency, allowing parties to finalize disputes without additional judicial cost orders when mutual agreement on expense allocation exists. This flexibility supports strategic resolution of urgent patent disputes within the unified system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["程序规则第 265 条明确适用于临时措施申请，不限于狭义的主诉行动", "上诉阶段允许撤回临时措施申请，前提是终审判决作出前提出且无损对方利益", "双方一致同意各自承担费用时，法院无需再就费用问题作出单独裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 265.1 RoP's definition of 'action' extends to applications for provisional measures, allowing withdrawal in interim relief proceedings.", "Withdrawal of provisional measure applications is permissible at the appeal stage if declared before the final appellate decision is rendered.", "A separate cost decision is unnecessary under Rule 265.2(c) when parties unanimously agree that each side bears its own costs."]

items.title: LD Milan, March 6, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_141/2026
items.title_zh: UPC 米兰分院裁定：严格限制成员国官方语言作为诉讼语言的例外情形
items.title_en: UPC Milan Order: Strict Interpretation of Exceptions for Using National Official Languages
items.summary: The provision requires proceedings to be conducted in the official language of the Contracting Member State only if two specific conditions are both met.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 米兰地方法院在 KeyMed 诉 PR Medical 案中作出命令，严格解释程序规则第 14.2(b) 条。法院指出，仅当被告住所地在成员国且管辖权具有排他性时，方可使用该国官方语言。若侵权威胁波及多国，则排他性不成立，原告无需承担举证责任证明例外不适用，被告的语言能力可作为辅助考量。
items.ai_summary_en: In the case of KeyMed v. PR Medical, the UPC Local Division in Milan issued an order strictly interpreting Rule 14.2(b) of the Rules of Procedure. The Court clarified that proceedings may be conducted in the official language of a Contracting Member State only if two cumulative conditions are met: the defendant is domiciled in that state, and jurisdiction is exclusive under Article 33(1)(a) UPCA. If infringement threats extend to other jurisdictions, exclusivity is lost, preventing the application of the national language exception. The claimant bears the initial burden to demonstrate these conditions are not satisfied, with a standard calibrated to the preliminary stage. Furthermore, the defendant’s actual language proficiency and business practices serve as supplementary factors in assessing potential prejudice to the right of defense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉或起诉时，若业务覆盖多国，可利用“非排他管辖”策略争取英语审理，降低语言成本。同时需注意网站多语种发布可能成为管辖依据，应提前规划合规与证据布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties operating across multiple European markets should strategically assess jurisdictional links when selecting the language of proceedings. Demonstrating cross-border infringement activities can effectively prevent the mandatory use of a specific national language, potentially allowing for English proceedings and reducing translation costs. Conversely, rights holders must recognize that maintaining multilingual digital presences, such as websites or online catalogs, may establish jurisdictional bases in various states. This complicates efforts to confine litigation to a single local division using its native language. Legal teams should proactively manage international marketing materials and evidence preservation strategies to influence procedural outcomes regarding language and venue early in dispute resolution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["程序规则 14.2(b) 属例外条款，须满足被告住所地与管辖排他性双重条件", "在他国存在侵权威胁即打破管辖排他性，导致无法适用成员国官方语言", "原告仅需初步证明例外不适用，被告语言能力可佐证无辩护权受损"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 14.2(b) is an exceptional provision requiring strict interpretation, mandating both defendant domicile and exclusive jurisdiction simultaneously.", "Alleged infringement threats in other Contracting Member States defeat the exclusivity condition, blocking the use of national official languages.", "Claimants must preliminarily prove non-applicability of the exception, while defendant language skills help confirm no defense prejudice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用跨国侵权事实打破语言例外，争取英语审理以降低成本", "多语种官网可能确立管辖权，需审慎管理海外数字营销内容"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage multi-jurisdictional infringement facts to challenge exclusive jurisdiction, aiming for English proceedings to mitigate language costs.", "Practice note: Scrutinize overseas digital marketing content, as multilingual websites may establish broader jurisdictional grounds affecting language choices.", "What to monitor: How courts calibrate the burden of proof for language objections at preliminary stages and weigh defendant language competence."]
core_points_en: Rule 14.2(b) is an exceptional provision requiring strict interpretation, mandating both defendant domicile and exclusive jurisdiction simultaneously.
Alleged infringement threats in other Contracting Member States defeat the exclusivity condition, blocking the use of national official languages.
Claimants must preliminarily prove non-applicability of the exception, while defendant language skills help confirm no defense prejudice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage multi-jurisdictional infringement facts to challenge exclusive jurisdiction, aiming for English proceedings to mitigate language costs.
Practice note: Scrutinize overseas digital marketing content, as multilingual websites may establish broader jurisdictional grounds affecting language choices.
What to monitor: How courts calibrate the burden of proof for language objections at preliminary stages and weigh defendant language competence.

items.title: European Parliament Study Recommends Statutory Licensing as the Optimal Copyright Framework for AI Training
items.title_zh: 欧议会研究建议法定许可为 AI 训练最优版权框架
items.title_en: EU Parliament Study Identifies Statutory Licensing as Optimal Copyright Framework for AI Training
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲议会委托发布研究报告，评估不同版权框架对 AI 训练的影响。研究指出版权核心在于激励新作品创作，而非保护既有收入。对比四种政策选项后，结论认为“法定许可”既能保障创作者获酬以维持数据质量，又能最小化用户成本，是平衡各方利益的最优解，将影响欧盟后续立法。
items.ai_summary_en: A European Parliament-commissioned study evaluates copyright frameworks governing AI training data usage. The analysis emphasizes that copyright’s primary function is incentivizing future creation rather than protecting existing revenue streams. After assessing four distinct policy options, the report concludes that statutory licensing offers the optimal balance. This approach ensures creators receive compensation to maintain high-quality data supply while minimizing costs for users. The findings rely on economic modeling and are expected to significantly influence subsequent EU legislative developments regarding artificial intelligence and intellectual property rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 企业及出口商需关注欧盟可能推行的法定许可制度，提前规划数据合规成本。建议建立版权数据溯源机制，评估潜在许可费支出，并积极参与欧盟相关政策咨询，以应对未来更严格的版权监管环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should anticipate potential implementation of statutory licensing regimes within the EU, necessitating proactive budgeting for data training compliance costs. Establishing robust copyright data traceability mechanisms is critical for managing liability and verifying licensing obligations. Entities operating in this sector are advised to engage actively in ongoing policy consultations to shape regulatory outcomes. Monitoring these developments closely will help mitigate legal risks associated with stricter copyright enforcement environments and ensure alignment with evolving EU standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["研究主张版权旨在激励未来创作，无偿使用将导致数据质量下降", "评估四种方案后，认定法定许可是兼顾创作者与用户利益的最优解", "该结论基于经济模型，强调消费者福利应作为政策评估的核心基准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright aims to incentivize future creation; uncompensated use risks degrading training data quality.", "Statutory licensing balances creator remuneration with user cost efficiency, emerging as the optimal solution.", "Economic models prioritize consumer welfare as the central benchmark for evaluating copyright policy effectiveness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["预判欧盟将推行法定许可，企业需提前预算数据训练合规成本", "建议建立数据溯源体系并参与政策咨询，以规避未来法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate statutory licensing adoption and budget for data training compliance costs.", "Practice note: Implement copyright data traceability systems and participate in policy consultations.", "What to monitor: Evolving EU regulatory frameworks and stricter copyright enforcement mechanisms."]
core_points_en: Copyright aims to incentivize future creation; uncompensated use risks degrading training data quality.
Statutory licensing balances creator remuneration with user cost efficiency, emerging as the optimal solution.
Economic models prioritize consumer welfare as the central benchmark for evaluating copyright policy effectiveness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate statutory licensing adoption and budget for data training compliance costs.
Practice note: Implement copyright data traceability systems and participate in policy consultations.
What to monitor: Evolving EU regulatory frameworks and stricter copyright enforcement mechanisms.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-371/24 [Comdribus]
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定警方收集生物识别数据须严格必要且说明理由
items.title_en: CJEU Rules Police Biometric Data Collection Must Be Strictly Necessary and Justified
items.summary: A police authority may, in a criminal investigation, collect biometric data solely on the ground that that collection is strictly necessary
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在Comdribus案中裁定，警方在刑事调查中仅以“严格必要”为由方可收集生物识别数据。数据采集不得系统化进行，必须逐案提供清晰理由，否则因拒绝采集而施加的刑事处罚无效。该案源于法国巴黎一起示威案件，当事人因拒绝提供指纹和照片被定罪，后上诉至欧盟法院质疑法国立法与欧盟数据保护规则的一致性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled that police authorities may collect biometric data, such as fingerprints and photographs, during criminal investigations only when strictly necessary. Systematic collection without case-specific justification is prohibited under EU law. The Court clarified that biometric data constitutes sensitive personal data requiring enhanced protection. Consequently, criminal penalties for refusing consent to such data gathering are invalid if the authority fails to provide clear, individualized reasons for the measure. This judgment addresses a reference from the Paris Court of Appeal regarding French legislation, establishing that mere suspicion is insufficient to mandate systematic biometric processing without appropriate safeguards and specific justification for each instance.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces high thresholds for processing sensitive personal data in law enforcement contexts across the EU. It limits automated or blanket biometric screening practices, requiring documented, individualized necessity assessments. Rights holders and companies operating in regulated sectors should note the strengthened procedural safeguards for data subjects, which may influence how evidence obtained via police cooperation is evaluated in related civil or administrative proceedings involving data privacy compliance.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["生物识别数据属敏感个人数据，仅在严格必要且有适当保障措施时方可处理", "警方不得系统化收集嫌疑人生物识别数据，每项决定须包含清晰的个案理由说明", "若未提供清晰理由，对拒绝同意数据采集者施加的刑事处罚将被认定为无效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Biometric data collection by police requires strict necessity and cannot be applied systematically to all suspects.", "Authorities must provide clear, case-by-case reasons for gathering identification data to ensure procedural validity.", "Criminal penalties for refusing consent to biometric collection are invalid if proper justification is absent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["执法机关收集生物识别数据必须符合严格必要性原则，禁止无差别的系统化采集", "程序正当性要求警方在采集数据时必须提供清晰理由，以保障当事人的救济权", "需持续观察法国及其他成员国如何调整国内立法与执法实践以符合此判决要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Entities handling sensitive data must ensure internal protocols align with the CJEU’s strict necessity standard to avoid regulatory friction during law enforceme…", "Practice note: Legal teams should review contracts and compliance frameworks to confirm that any third-party data sharing with authorities includes mechanisms for verifying case-s…", "What to monitor: Observe how Member States, particularly France, amend national criminal procedure codes to implement this ruling and eliminate systematic biometric collection pra…"]
core_points_en: Biometric data collection by police requires strict necessity and cannot be applied systematically to all suspects.
Authorities must provide clear, case-by-case reasons for gathering identification data to ensure procedural validity.
Criminal penalties for refusing consent to biometric collection are invalid if proper justification is absent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Entities handling sensitive data must ensure internal protocols align with the CJEU’s strict necessity standard to avoid regulatory friction during law enforceme…
Practice note: Legal teams should review contracts and compliance frameworks to confirm that any third-party data sharing with authorities includes mechanisms for verifying case-s…
What to monitor: Observe how Member States, particularly France, amend national criminal procedure codes to implement this ruling and eliminate systematic biometric collection pra…

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-526/24 Brillen Rottler
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：若仅为索赔而行使数据访问权可被视为滥用
items.title_en: CJEU Rules GDPR Access Requests Made Solely for Compensation Claims May Be Abusive
items.summary: A request for access to one’s own personal data may be considered abusive and refused if it is made for the sole purpose of subsequently claiming compensation for an alleged infringement of the GDPR
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在Brillen Rottler案中裁定，若个人依据GDPR提出数据访问请求的唯一目的是随后主张侵权赔偿，该请求可被视为滥用并遭拒绝。案件源于奥地利居民向德国眼镜商索取数据被拒后索赔。法院指出，尽管形式合规，但若控制器能证明请求旨在人为制造索赔条件而非核实数据处理合法性，即属过度且滥用。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled in Case C-526/24 that a data subject’s request for access to personal data under Article 15 GDPR may be considered abusive and refused if made solely to subsequently claim compensation. The case involved an Austrian individual who subscribed to a German optician’s newsletter and immediately requested data access, later claiming damages for refusal. The Court clarified that even a first access request can be deemed excessive if the controller demonstrates the sole purpose was manufacturing conditions for compensation rather than verifying data processing legality.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides EU data controllers with a defense against strategic litigation where access rights are weaponized for compensation. It shifts focus to proving subjective intent, requiring robust documentation of user behavior patterns. Controllers should carefully assess requests showing signs of systematic abuse before refusal, ensuring evidence supports the claim of improper purpose to avoid liability for wrongful denial of access rights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["若数据访问请求 solely 旨在为GDPR索赔制造条件，可被认定为滥用并拒绝", "控制器需证明请求目的非为知晓处理情况或验证合法性，而是人为制造赔偿前提", "即使首次访问请求，在特定情形下也可因意图滥用而被视为GDPR意义上的“过度”"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["GDPR access requests made solely to claim compensation may be classified as abusive and refused by controllers.", "Controllers must demonstrate the request lacks genuine intent to verify processing, aiming instead to manufacture damage claims.", "Even initial access requests can be deemed excessive under GDPR if evidence confirms abusive strategic intent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确GDPR访问权不得作为人为制造索赔条件的工具，确立了反滥用原则", "企业抗辩关键在于举证请求人缺乏核实数据合法性的真实意图，仅追求赔偿", "需观察后续案件中法院对“系统性请求”及“主观恶意”的具体证据认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Offers a legal basis to refuse bad-faith GDPR access requests aimed at generating non-material damage claims rather than transparency.", "Practice note: Refusal requires strong evidence that the sole purpose was compensation; document systematic behaviors like mass subscriptions or identical legal templates.", "What to monitor: National court applications of this abuse standard, particularly regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving subjective intent in isolated versus systematic case…"]
core_points_en: GDPR access requests made solely to claim compensation may be classified as abusive and refused by controllers.
Controllers must demonstrate the request lacks genuine intent to verify processing, aiming instead to manufacture damage claims.
Even initial access requests can be deemed excessive under GDPR if evidence confirms abusive strategic intent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Offers a legal basis to refuse bad-faith GDPR access requests aimed at generating non-material damage claims rather than transparency.
Practice note: Refusal requires strong evidence that the sole purpose was compensation; document systematic behaviors like mass subscriptions or identical legal templates.
What to monitor: National court applications of this abuse standard, particularly regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving subjective intent in isolated versus systematic case…

items.title: UK Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence published
items.title_zh: 英国发布版权与人工智能报告
items.title_en: UK Government Publishes Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: The UK's Departments for Science, Innovation and Technology and Culture, Media and Sport have published their long-awaited Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence. The Government was required to publish the report under s.136 of the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 as part of a compromise in Parliamentary debates over transparency and the use of copyright by AI providers. While
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国科学创新技术部及文化媒体体育部发布了备受关注的《版权与人工智能报告》。根据《2025年数据（使用与访问）法案》第136条要求，政府必须发布此报告，这是议会就透明度及AI提供商使用版权问题进行辩论达成的妥协结果。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Departments for Science, Innovation and Technology and Culture, Media and Sport have published their long-awaited Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence. This publication fulfills a statutory obligation under section 136 of the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025. The report’s release follows a legislative compromise reached during Parliamentary debates concerning transparency requirements and the use of copyrighted materials by artificial intelligence providers. The document addresses the intersection of copyright law and AI development within the United Kingdom jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This report marks a significant policy milestone in the UK’s approach to AI and copyright. For rights holders and technology developers, it clarifies the government’s stance following recent legislative debates. While specific operational details require full text review, the publication confirms the UK’s continued focus on balancing innovation with creator protections. Stakeholders should assess how these findings align with existing licensing frameworks and future regulatory expectations in the region.

items.title: Policy paper: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
items.title_zh: 政策文件：版权与人工智能报告及影响评估
items.title_en: UKIPO Report and Impact Assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布关于在人工智能系统开发中使用版权作品的报告及影响评估。该文件由英国知识产权局（UKIPO）提供，旨在审视现行版权框架下AI训练数据的合法使用边界及其潜在影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published a comprehensive report and impact assessment regarding the use of copyright-protected works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. This document critically examines the applicability of the current copyright framework within the emerging era of generative AI. It evaluates the potential economic and legal implications of various policy options for key stakeholders, including creators, technology developers, and consumers. The analysis aims to provide robust factual evidence and decision-making references for the government. These insights will inform the formulation of subsequent regulatory rules, seeking to balance the promotion of technological innovation with the protection of intellectual property rights in the digital landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息缺失，无法判断具体政策倾向或立法建议。需关注后续完整报告是否提出新的版权例外条款或许可机制，这将直接影响AI企业在英合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders operating in the UK market should closely monitor developments regarding copyright exceptions, particularly changes to text and data mining regulations. It is advisable for organizations to proactively review the compliance status of their training datasets and establish early warning mechanisms for copyright risks. Engaging actively in public consultations allows entities to safeguard their legitimate rights and interests in both UK and broader European markets. Maintaining robust internal IP compliance systems is essential to mitigate potential liabilities arising from evolving statutory interpretations and policy shifts in this rapidly developing sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文仅显示发布了一份关于AI开发中使用版权作品的报告", "原文仅显示包含对该政策选项的影响评估内容"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Assessing the legal boundaries and economic impacts of using copyrighted materials for AI training purposes.", "Analyzing the advantages and disadvantages of different regulatory paths for the UK creative and tech industries.", "Laying the foundation for copyright policies that balance innovation incentives with the interests of rights holders."]

Analyzing the advantages and disadvantages of different regulatory paths for the UK creative and tech industries.
Laying the foundation for copyright policies that balance innovation incentives with the interests of rights holders.

items.title: Analytics show Huawei winning Wi-Fi 7 patent race
items.title_zh: 数据分析显示华为赢得 Wi-Fi 7 专利竞赛
items.title_en: Huawei Leads Wi-Fi 7 SEP Race as Sisvel Launches New Patent Pool
items.summary: As Wi-Fi 7 moves from specification to large-scale deployment across consumer, enterprise and industrial environments, the SEP landscape is likely to become more actively monetised and intensely scrutinised. As the ecosystem matures, portfolio transparency and stack positioning will increasingly shape licensing outcomes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着 Wi-Fi 7 标准部署，SEP 货币化与审查加剧。Sisvel 组建涵盖 Wi-Fi 6/7 的专利池，华为等为首批成员。报告显示涉 Wi-Fi 诉讼激增，慕尼黑法院近期基于 FRAND 原则颁发禁令并验证费率，凸显欧洲在标准必要专利执法中的关键地位及透明度的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: As Wi-Fi 7 transitions from specification to large-scale deployment, the standard essential patent landscape faces intensified monetization and scrutiny. Sisvel has launched a multimode patent pool covering Wi-Fi 6 and 7, with Huawei among the founding members, aiming to simplify global licensing. Concurrently, litigation involving Wi-Fi technologies has surged. Recent German court decisions have validated FRAND rates and granted injunctions, underscoring Europe’s pivotal role in SEP enforcement. This environment demands greater portfolio transparency and strategic stack positioning. Rights holders and implementers must navigate these evolving judicial precedents carefully, as compliance failures risk significant operational disruptions through injunctive relief.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严 SEP 执法环境，需提前评估 Wi-Fi 7 专利风险。建议加强自有专利储备以增强谈判筹码，密切关注慕尼黑等地司法动向，合理规划 FRAND 合规策略以避免禁令冲击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should proactively assess Wi-Fi 7 patent exposure given the rigorous SEP enforcement environment in Europe. Strengthening proprietary patent portfolios can enhance negotiation leverage during cross-licensing discussions. Companies must closely monitor judicial developments in Munich and other key jurisdictions to adjust export compliance strategies and litigation preparedness. Effective FRAND compliance planning is essential to mitigate the risk of injunctions that could disrupt supply chains and market access for connected devices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wi-Fi 7 技术升级推动 SEP 许可需求与诉讼风险显著增加", "Sisvel 成立新专利池整合多厂商资源简化全球许可流程", "德国法院判决确立 FRAND 费率有效性并支持禁令救济"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Wi-Fi 7 technical upgrades significantly increase SEP licensing demands and associated litigation risks for market participants.", "Sisvel established a new patent pool integrating multiple contributors to streamline and simplify global licensing processes.", "German courts confirmed FRAND rate validity and supported injunction remedies, highlighting strict European enforcement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用专利组合优势参与池许可或交叉授权降低成本", "紧盯欧洲判例动态调整出口产品合规与应诉预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage strong patent portfolios to participate in pool licensing or negotiate cross-licenses, reducing overall costs.", "Practice note: Monitor European case law dynamics to adjust product compliance and litigation response plans for exported goods.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in Munich regarding FRAND rate determinations and the issuance of injunctive relief."]
core_points_en: Wi-Fi 7 technical upgrades significantly increase SEP licensing demands and associated litigation risks for market participants.
Sisvel established a new patent pool integrating multiple contributors to streamline and simplify global licensing processes.
German courts confirmed FRAND rate validity and supported injunction remedies, highlighting strict European enforcement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage strong patent portfolios to participate in pool licensing or negotiate cross-licenses, reducing overall costs.
Practice note: Monitor European case law dynamics to adjust product compliance and litigation response plans for exported goods.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in Munich regarding FRAND rate determinations and the issuance of injunctive relief.

items.title: UK makes U-turn as government drops AI copyright exception
items.title_zh: 英国政府撤回人工智能版权例外提案
items.title_en: UK Government Abandons AI Copyright Exception Plan After Strong Opposition
items.summary: The government has abandoned its proposal on AI and copyright following a record consultation response. But with legislation off the table for now, what happens next?
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府在收到创纪录的公众咨询回应后，已正式放弃此前提出的关于人工智能与版权的立法提案。目前相关立法计划已被搁置，原文未进一步说明后续替代方案或具体政策走向，仅指出立法暂时不在议程上。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has officially withdrawn its legislative proposal to establish a copyright exception for artificial intelligence training, following an unprecedented volume of responses during the public consultation. This significant policy reversal ensures that the existing strict copyright framework remains fully applicable to AI data scraping and model training activities. Consequently, there will be no immediate legislative relaxation of restrictions on using copyrighted works for AI development. The decision highlights the intense ongoing conflict between creative industries and technology giants regarding intellectual property rights. With statutory changes off the table, the future regulatory landscape remains uncertain, leaving stakeholders to navigate current laws without the anticipated safe harbor provisions previously under consideration by policymakers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认英国政府因公众咨询反馈而暂停AI版权例外立法。需持续监测英国知识产权局或政府后续是否发布新的指导方针、行为准则或替代性政策框架，以明确AI训练数据的合规边界。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations operating in or targeting the UK market must immediately cease relying on expectations of a broad copyright exception for AI training data. It is critical to reassess compliance costs and strategies given the confirmed continuation of strict copyright enforcement. Entities should prioritize robust copyright clearance processes and actively explore legitimate licensing channels to mitigate infringement risks. Furthermore, stakeholders must closely monitor potential non-legislative guidance or voluntary codes of practice that may emerge as alternative regulatory mechanisms. Adapting to this stable but restrictive legal environment is essential to avoid litigation and ensure sustainable AI development practices within the jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府已放弃此前提出的人工智能与版权相关立法提案", "该决定是在收到创纪录数量的公众咨询回应后作出的", "目前相关立法工作已暂时搁置，后续步骤尚不明确"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK government formally revoked the AI copyright exception legislative plan due to strong opposition from various sectors.", "The current copyright protection framework remains unchanged, requiring AI developers to continue seeking authorization licenses.", "With legislation suspended, the government has not yet clarified alternative regulatory schemes or implementation timelines."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国AI版权例外立法已暂停，当前无新法律生效", "需观察政府后续是否推出非立法性质的行业准则或指南", "关注英国知识产权局是否会就现有版权法在AI领域的适用发布解释"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Organizations must restructure data compliance strategies and abandon expectations of favorable legislative changes.", "Practice note: Proactively establish copyright licensing mechanisms to mitigate litigation risks arising from policy reversals.", "What to monitor: Future non-legislative guidelines or voluntary frameworks introduced by the UK government."]
core_points_en: The UK government formally revoked the AI copyright exception legislative plan due to strong opposition from various sectors.
The current copyright protection framework remains unchanged, requiring AI developers to continue seeking authorization licenses.
With legislation suspended, the government has not yet clarified alternative regulatory schemes or implementation timelines.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Organizations must restructure data compliance strategies and abandon expectations of favorable legislative changes.
Practice note: Proactively establish copyright licensing mechanisms to mitigate litigation risks arising from policy reversals.
What to monitor: Future non-legislative guidelines or voluntary frameworks introduced by the UK government.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, 24 February 2026, Decision Concerning Public Access To The Register, UPC_CoA_9/2026, UPC_CoA_10/2026
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭关于公众查阅登记簿的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Public Access Procedures for Case Files and Registers
items.summary: This ensures that the decision will be taken by the judge-rapporteur, who is familiar with the case file.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭于2026年2月24日就公众查阅卷宗作出裁决。明确向初审法院或上诉庭提交的查阅申请应分别由对应机构处理，由熟悉案卷的法官报告员决定。上诉阶段通常无需重新提交文件，但若当事人重新提交，公众可向任一法院申请。申请必须具体且说明理由，以便平衡利益。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal issued a decision clarifying public access procedures under Rule 262.1(b) of the Rules of Procedure. The ruling establishes that reasoned requests for access to written pleadings and evidence must be directed to the specific judicial instance handling the case, whether the Court of First Instance or the Court of Appeal. This ensures the judge-rapporteur, who is familiar with the file, adjudicates the request. The court emphasized that such requests must be specific and reasoned, stating a clear purpose to allow for balancing interests under Article 45 UPCA. Furthermore, while re-lodging first-instance documents on appeal is generally unnecessary as the appellate court consults the lower court’s file, doing so allows the public to request access from either instance, thereby increasing flexibility in obtaining case materials.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决明确了UPC程序中公众查阅文件的管辖分工与实质要求。实务中需注意申请的具体性与理由陈述，避免泛化请求。若当事人在上诉中重新提交文件，将增加公众获取途径。需持续观察法院对“具体性”与“利益平衡”的裁量尺度。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC should carefully manage document submission strategies to control information exposure. Unnecessary re-lodging of files during appeals creates additional administrative burdens and expands the avenues through which competitors or the public may access sensitive case materials. Conversely, parties seeking discovery should draft precise, well-reasoned access requests that clearly articulate the necessity of specific documents. Meeting these procedural standards is critical for successful navigation of the UPC’s transparency rules, balancing the right to public access with the protection of confidential business information and litigation strategy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["查阅申请应按审级分别向初审法院或上诉庭提出，由法官报告员在咨询当事人后决定", "上诉庭依规则查阅初审卷宗，通常无需重新提交文件；若重新提交，公众可选择向任一法院申请", "查阅书面诉状和证据的申请必须具体并说明目的，不得要求法院按相关性标准筛选文件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Access requests must be submitted to the specific court instance handling the case, ensuring the judge-rapporteur decides based on familiarity with the file.", "Requests for documents must be specific and reasoned, stating a clear purpose; broad requests requiring the court to filter by relevance are prohibited.", "Re-lodging first-instance documents on appeal is not required but enables public access requests to be made to either the Court of First Instance or the Court of Appeal."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确查阅申请的管辖归属，避免向错误审级提交导致程序延误", "申请必须具体化并阐明目的，以满足法官进行利益平衡审查的要求", "关注当事人重新提交文件对公众查阅路径的影响及法院后续实践"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strategic management of document filing prevents unnecessary expansion of public access points to sensitive litigation materials.", "Practice note: Draft specific, purpose-driven access requests to satisfy judicial scrutiny and effectively obtain opponent evidence.", "What to monitor: Procedural compliance regarding document specificity and the implications of re-lodging files across different UPC instances."]
core_points_en: Access requests must be submitted to the specific court instance handling the case, ensuring the judge-rapporteur decides based on familiarity with the file.
Requests for documents must be specific and reasoned, stating a clear purpose; broad requests requiring the court to filter by relevance are prohibited.
Re-lodging first-instance documents on appeal is not required but enables public access requests to be made to either the Court of First Instance or the Court of Appeal.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strategic management of document filing prevents unnecessary expansion of public access points to sensitive litigation materials.
Practice note: Draft specific, purpose-driven access requests to satisfy judicial scrutiny and effectively obtain opponent evidence.
What to monitor: Procedural compliance regarding document specificity and the implications of re-lodging files across different UPC instances.

items.title: Central Division (Munich), February 24, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_829/2024
items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑分部裁定组分范围不清致专利撤销
items.title_en: UPC Munich Revokes Patent for Added Matter in Composition Ranges and Lack of Inventive Step
items.summary: The Court construed the coposition as claimed in claim 1 of the Patent as comprising a range of marker molecule calculated in respect of the total sugar composition...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院中央分部（慕尼黑）在 UPM-Kymmene 诉 International N&H 案中，因权利要求中数值范围计算基础与原始申请不一致，认定构成新增事项而撤销欧洲专利。法院强调组合物权利要求需精确界定，且中间产物若无创造性工艺关联则不具备创造性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Central Division (Munich) revoked European Patent EP 2 611 800 in UPM-Kymmene Oyj v International N&H Denmark ApS. The Court found that Claim 1’s numerical ranges for marker molecules, calculated against total sugar composition, differed from the original application’s disclosure based on weight relative to total sugar concentration. This discrepancy introduced technically relevant information not derivable from the application as filed, constituting added matter under Article 65 UPCA and Article 138(1)(c) EPC. Additionally, the Court held that the claimed intermediate product lacked an inventive step under Article 56 EPC. Without an inextricable link to a specific inventive process, the composition was deemed obvious over known xylose-rich compositions. The decision underscores strict drafting requirements for numerical ranges in composition claims and the necessity of linking intermediates to inventive processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局组合物专利时，务必确保权利要求数值范围计算基准与说明书完全一致，避免新增事项风险。对于中间产物，需在申请文件中明确其与特定创新工艺的内在联系，以防被认定显而易见。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should ensure that numerical ranges in composition claims strictly adhere to the calculation bases defined in the specification to mitigate added matter risks during opposition or revocation proceedings. For intermediate products, applicants must explicitly demonstrate their role within an inventive manufacturing route rather than relying solely on structural novelty. Failure to establish this functional connection may render such claims obvious over prior art compositions. This case highlights the UPC’s rigorous approach to clarity and inventive step, requiring diligent claim construction and robust technical evidence linking intermediates to innovative processes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["组分权利要求数值范围计算基础必须与原始披露一致", "缺乏与创造性工艺紧密关联的中间产物不具创造性", "专利权人负有精确界定保护范围的严格起草责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Composition claim numerical ranges must align precisely with the calculation basis disclosed in the original application to avoid added matter.", "Intermediate products require an inextricable link to an inventive process; otherwise, they lack inventive step.", "Patentees bear strict responsibility for precise drafting of numerical ranges and component definitions in composition claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["组合物专利申请需严格核对数值范围计算基准一致性", "中间产物主张须绑定具体创造性工艺路径以防无效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure numerical range bases in composition claims match original disclosures to prevent invalidity.", "Practice note: Link intermediate product claims explicitly to inventive process steps to satisfy inventive step requirements.", "What to monitor: UPC interpretations of added matter regarding calculation bases for chemical composition parameters."]
core_points_en: Composition claim numerical ranges must align precisely with the calculation basis disclosed in the original application to avoid added matter.
Intermediate products require an inextricable link to an inventive process; otherwise, they lack inventive step.
Patentees bear strict responsibility for precise drafting of numerical ranges and component definitions in composition claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure numerical range bases in composition claims match original disclosures to prevent invalidity.
Practice note: Link intermediate product claims explicitly to inventive process steps to satisfy inventive step requirements.
What to monitor: UPC interpretations of added matter regarding calculation bases for chemical composition parameters.

items.title: LD Mannheim, 24 February 2026, Order Of Court Of First Instance, UPC_CFI_735/2024, UPC_CFI_224/2025
items.title_zh: UPC曼海姆法院就光学器件专利侵权及程序问题作出判决
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim: Functional Interpretation and Procedural Clarifications in Optical Device Patent Case
items.summary: The patent is related to an optical device having a specific arrangement of a single input fiber for lighting. The accused embodiment comprises multiple input fibres, but only one of them is used for coupling...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC曼海姆地方法院在TRUMPF诉IPG案中认定，尽管被控产品含多根光纤，但仅一根用于耦合，构成对“单输入光纤”专利特征的字面侵权。法院采用功能性解释界定直径与重叠要求。程序上，针对辅助请求的侵权主张视为澄清而非变更。法院部分支持购买被控产品的临时损害赔偿请求。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of First Instance in Mannheim ruled on patent infringement involving an optical device with a specific single input fiber arrangement. The court found literal infringement despite the accused embodiment containing multiple fibers, reasoning that only one was used for coupling while others served unrelated functions. Regarding technical features like 'diameter' and 'overlap,' the court applied a functional interpretation rather than a strict geometric one, equating 'equal diameter' to functional equivalence and 'overlying' to substantial coverage. Procedurally, the court clarified that adapting infringement pleadings based on auxiliary requests constitutes a clarification under Rule 30 of the Rules of Procedure, not a case amendment requiring leave under Rule 263. Additionally, the court partially granted provisional damages, awarding costs for purchasing the accused product as necessary for testing, while dismissing filing cost claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC在权利要求解释中倾向于功能性视角的裁判思路，尤其在处理非核心附加特征时。同时明确了程序规则中“澄清”与“变更”的界限，降低了原告调整诉讼策略的程序门槛。临时损害赔偿的裁定也为权利人前期取证成本回收提供了参考路径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the UPC's tendency toward functional claim construction, which may expand infringement risks for complex products even if they deviate from strict structural descriptions. Parties should anticipate that additional, non-infringing features might be disregarded if the core patented function is replicated. Furthermore, the procedural flexibility regarding auxiliary requests allows for strategic narrowing of claims without facing significant procedural hurdles. However, securing provisional damages requires demonstrating the necessity of specific costs, such as product purchases for testing. Litigants must carefully document these expenses early to support financial remedies and manage litigation budgets effectively within the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定被控产品中额外光纤不影响功能，构成对单输入光纤专利特征的字面侵权", "对光纤直径和重叠采用功能性解释，视“等径”为功能等效，“重叠”为实质覆盖", "依据RoP第30条，适应辅助请求的侵权主张属澄清，无需依第263条获得许可"]

items.title: CAYENNE c. LITTLE CAYENNE - Coup de frein sur l'étendue de la renommée
items.title_zh: CAYENNE诉LITTLE CAYENNE：限制商标知名度的延伸范围
items.title_en: Paris Court Limits Porsche Cayenne Reputation: No Automatic Protection for Clothing or Food
items.summary: La renommée d'une marque de véhicules ne peut être étendue à des produits dérivés tels que les vêtements, produits alimentaires ou encore aux services de restauration et hôteliers.
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎上诉法院确认，保时捷“CAYENNE”汽车商标的知名度不能延伸至服装、食品或餐饮酒店服务。法院维持法国工业产权局决定，认定在同类商品上存在混淆风险，但在非类似商品上，因缺乏关联证据，驳回基于知名度的异议主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The Paris Court of Appeal confirmed that the reputation of a vehicle trademark does not automatically extend to unrelated derivative categories such as clothing, food products, or hospitality services. In a dispute between Porsche’s CAYENNE mark and an application for LITTLE CAYENNE, the court upheld the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) Director General’s decision. The ruling clarifies that while confusion exists for similar goods like printed matter, the high profile of the vehicle brand cannot justify cross-class opposition based on reputation alone without evidence of specific market radiation into those unrelated sectors. This judgment reinforces the boundaries of well-known trademark protection in France.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了欧盟商标知名度保护的边界，即知名度不自动跨越至非类似商品类别。权利人需提供具体证据证明公众在非类似商品上建立联系，否则难以援引反淡化条款。这对奢侈品牌或高知名度品牌的跨类维权策略具有参考意义，需关注法院对“联系”认定的证据标准。
items.ai_insight_en: High visibility in one sector does not guarantee broad protection across all classes. Rights holders must provide concrete evidence demonstrating that their reputation specifically radiates into the disputed product categories to succeed in anti-dilution claims. Without proof of actual use or consumer association in those fields, oppositions based on reputation are likely to fail. Companies should proactively register defensive marks in potential derivative categories to prevent third-party registrations. Maintaining comprehensive records of commercial use in Europe is essential to substantiate reputation arguments during enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎上诉法院裁定汽车商标知名度不自然延伸至服装、食品及餐饮服务领域", "法院确认在印刷品等类似商品上，因标志近似且缺乏显著性抗辩理由，存在混淆风险", "判决维持法国工业产权局此前决定，驳回保时捷对非类似商品基于知名度的异议主张"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Vehicle brand reputation does not automatically extend to unrelated derivative categories like clothing or food products.", "Courts confirm confusion risks only apply to similar goods, rejecting cross-class claims based solely on general reputation.", "The term Cayenne is deemed distinctive for the contested classes rather than a weak geographical indicator."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["知名度保护非自动跨类，需证明公众在非类似商品上建立具体联系", "法院倾向于严格审查知名度延伸的证据，仅凭高知名度不足以阻断他人在远类注册", "后续需观察此类判决对欧盟层面商标异议实践中证据要求的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: High fame in core goods does not equal automatic full-class protection; specific reputation scope must be proven.", "Practice note: Exporters should secure defensive registrations in derivative categories early to prevent squatting.", "What to monitor: Evidence requirements for establishing reputation transfer across unrelated industrial property classes."]
core_points_en: Vehicle brand reputation does not automatically extend to unrelated derivative categories like clothing or food products.
Courts confirm confusion risks only apply to similar goods, rejecting cross-class claims based solely on general reputation.
The term Cayenne is deemed distinctive for the contested classes rather than a weak geographical indicator.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: High fame in core goods does not equal automatic full-class protection; specific reputation scope must be proven.
Practice note: Exporters should secure defensive registrations in derivative categories early to prevent squatting.
What to monitor: Evidence requirements for establishing reputation transfer across unrelated industrial property classes.

items.title: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange
items.title_zh: WIPO 启动人工智能基础设施交流平台
items.title_en: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange for Expert Dialogue
items.summary: WIPO today launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII), a new initiative for expert dialogue on IP and AI issues that will focus on technical and operational issues and complement ongoing discussions at other WIPO forums.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）正式推出“人工智能基础设施交流平台”（AIII）。该倡议旨在建立专家对话机制，聚焦知识产权与人工智能领域的技术及运营议题。作为现有 WIPO 论坛讨论的补充，AIII 将专注于解决实际操作层面的问题，促进全球范围内关于 AI 技术架构与 IP 规则衔接的专业交流，为后续政策制定提供技术支撑。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII). This new initiative establishes a dedicated platform for expert dialogue focusing specifically on technical and operational issues at the intersection of intellectual property and artificial intelligence. The AIII is designed to complement ongoing discussions within other WIPO forums by addressing practical infrastructure matters, thereby supporting broader policy conversations with specialized technical insights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，国际后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and technology companies, this development signals a continued institutional focus on the operational realities of AI deployment within the IP framework. While the immediate output is dialogue rather than binding regulation, the technical definitions and operational standards discussed may influence future global harmonization efforts. Stakeholders should track how these technical discussions evolve alongside broader policy debates to anticipate shifts in international IP norms regarding AI infrastructure.
