items.title: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.title_zh: 欧盟手工艺品和工业产品地理标志新体系申请人重要提醒
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches New EU System for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.summary: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2025年12月1日起，EUIPO接管手工艺品和工业产品地理标志（CIGIs）注册职权。至2026年12月2日，各国原有保护体系将完全被欧盟新体系取代。二十个成员国的申请人需经国家阶段审查后转交EUIPO，而丹麦、瑞典等七国申请人直接向EUIPO提交。截至2026年2月27日，已公布64份来自法国、葡萄牙等国的现有名称保护申请。
items.ai_summary_en: As of December 1, 2025, the EUIPO assumes responsibility for registering Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products (CIGIs). By December 2, 2026, existing national protection systems will be fully replaced by this new EU framework. Most applicants must undergo a two-stage process: initial examination by national authorities followed by Union-level review at the EUIPO. However, applicants from Denmark, Finland, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, and Sweden may file directly with the Office. The system also allows protection for existing CIGIs established before the launch date. To support this transition, the EUIPO introduced GIportal, an intuitive e-filing tool, alongside comprehensive guidance resources. Early implementation has seen significant interest, with dozens of applications already published for sectors such as ceramics, textiles, and minerals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 新体系实施初期显示出强劲申请兴趣，特别是针对既有名称的保护过渡。实务中需严格区分直接提交与间接提交的成员国路径差异，并关注EUIPO推出的GIportal电子申报工具的使用反馈及后续审查标准细化。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting European craft and industrial goods should assess eligibility for CIGI protection during this transitional period. Utilizing the unified EU registration system can streamline legal protection across borders, reducing the complexity and cost associated with maintaining separate national rights. Rights holders are encouraged to leverage the new GIportal platform for efficient filing. Strategic planning should account for the specific procedural requirements of each jurisdiction, particularly regarding opposition timelines and evidence standards. Proactive engagement with the available EUIPO resources ensures smoother navigation of the new regulatory landscape and strengthens brand security within the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年12月1日起EUIPO接管CIGIs注册，2026年12月2日前各国体系逐步退出", "二十国申请人经国家审查后转交EUIPO，丹麦等七国申请人直接向EUIPO提交申请", "截至2026年2月27日，EUIPO已公布64份涉及石材、纺织等行业的现有名称保护申请"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO officially manages CIGI registrations starting December 1, 2025, replacing all national systems by late 2026.", "Most member states require a dual-phase review involving national preliminary examination before forwarding applications to the EUIPO.", "Applicants from seven specific member states can bypass national stages and submit CIGI applications directly to the EUIPO."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需关注不同成员国申请路径差异（直接vs间接）对申请策略的影响", "观察GIportal电子工具在初期运行中的稳定性及用户反馈", "监测首批公布申请的审查结果，以预判EUIPO对石材、纺织等领域的审查尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate product eligibility for CIGI status to secure unified EU protection and reduce multi-jurisdictional enforcement costs.", "Practice note: Prepare robust evidence packages for national opposition phases in jurisdictions requiring preliminary state-level examination.", "What to monitor: Track the specific filing pathways for the seven direct-access member states to optimize registration strategies."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO officially manages CIGI registrations starting December 1, 2025, replacing all national systems by late 2026.
Most member states require a dual-phase review involving national preliminary examination before forwarding applications to the EUIPO.
Applicants from seven specific member states can bypass national stages and submit CIGI applications directly to the EUIPO.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate product eligibility for CIGI status to secure unified EU protection and reduce multi-jurisdictional enforcement costs.
Practice note: Prepare robust evidence packages for national opposition phases in jurisdictions requiring preliminary state-level examination.
What to monitor: Track the specific filing pathways for the seven direct-access member states to optimize registration strategies.

items.title: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.title_zh: 知识产权惠及各方：知识产权对中小企业的关键重要性
items.title_en: IP Rights as Critical Strategic Assets: Driving SME Success and Attracting EU Venture Capital
items.summary: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局文章强调知识产权对欧盟中小企业至关重要。引用EUIPO与EPO联合研究显示，拥有知识产权的企业人均收入更高，中小企业注册知识产权后人均收入通常高出44%。知识产权密集型行业吸引了欧盟绝大部分风险投资，且初创企业若有注册商标或专利，获早期风投可能性高十倍，退出成功率高200%。
items.ai_summary_en: Professor Eleonora Rosati highlights that intellectual property is fundamental to the commercial success of small and medium-sized enterprises in the European Union. Recent joint studies by the EUIPO and EPO reveal that SMEs holding IP rights generate significantly higher revenue per employee compared to those without protection, with registered rights correlating to a forty-four percent increase. Furthermore, IP-intensive industries attract the vast majority of private equity and venture capital funding, receiving over eighty-eight percent of total investments between 2021 and 2023. The analysis underscores that protecting intangible assets like trademarks and patents not only boosts valuation and wage premiums but also serves as a vital signal of innovativeness, directly influencing access to capital and overall market competitiveness for emerging businesses across the EU single market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文通过量化数据证实知识产权资产与中小企业财务表现及融资能力的强相关性。实务中应关注EUIPO与EPO持续发布的联合研究报告，这些数据常被用于政策制定及企业IP战略价值评估，反映欧盟层面强化中小企业IP意识的政策导向。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, intellectual property strategy should be integrated into core business planning rather than treated solely as legal compliance. Securing trademark and patent registrations early provides tangible data on innovation capacity, which is crucial for attracting venture capital and enhancing exit valuations during mergers or acquisitions. This approach helps prevent the loss of intangible assets while signaling robustness to investors. Market participants should view IP portfolios as essential tools for securing funding and demonstrating competitive advantage, ensuring that creative outputs are legally protected to maximize financial returns and sustain long-term growth within the dynamic European marketplace.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["拥有知识产权的中小企业人均收入比无保护 counterparts 高出44%", "至2023年间，欧盟88%的风投资金流向知识产权密集型初创企业", "拥有注册商标或专利的初创企业获得早期风投的可能性高出十倍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SMEs registering IP rights achieve forty-four percent higher revenue per employee than unprotected counterparts.", "Over eighty-eight percent of EU venture capital funding flows to startups operating within IP-intensive industries.", "Registering patents or trademarks increases early-stage venture capital acquisition likelihood by tenfold."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["量化数据显示IP注册与中小企业营收及融资成功率呈显著正相关", "EUIPO与EPO联合研究成为评估欧盟IP经济影响的重要参考依据", "需持续关注欧盟委员会单一市场报告中关于IP与创新指标的更新"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat IP registration as a primary mechanism for securing EU venture capital and improving exit valuations.", "Practice note: Leverage IP asset portfolios to demonstrably increase per-employee revenue and justify wage premiums.", "What to monitor: Track EUIPO and EPO data trends linking IP intensity to investment flows and competitive performance."]
core_points_en: SMEs registering IP rights achieve forty-four percent higher revenue per employee than unprotected counterparts.
Over eighty-eight percent of EU venture capital funding flows to startups operating within IP-intensive industries.
Registering patents or trademarks increases early-stage venture capital acquisition likelihood by tenfold.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat IP registration as a primary mechanism for securing EU venture capital and improving exit valuations.
Practice note: Leverage IP asset portfolios to demonstrably increase per-employee revenue and justify wage premiums.
What to monitor: Track EUIPO and EPO data trends linking IP intensity to investment flows and competitive performance.

items.title: Opinion of the Advocate General in Cases C-496/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Marketplace) and C-497/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Data)
items.title_zh: 总检察长建议驳回Meta关于Facebook数据调查的上诉
items.title_en: AG Rantos Recommends Dismissing Meta Appeals Against EU Commission Data Requests
items.summary: Advocate General Rantos proposes the dismissal of the appeals by Meta Platforms Ireland relating to an investigation into abuse of a dominant position concerning the use of Facebook Data and the Facebook Marketplace service
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院总检察长Rantos建议驳回Meta Platforms Ireland针对欧盟委员会的两项上诉。该案源于欧委会对Meta滥用市场支配地位（涉及Facebook数据及Marketplace）的调查，要求提供内部文件。普通法院此前认定欧委会的信息请求合法、必要且符合比例原则。总检察长认为欧委会拥有广泛调查权，无需在初期证明每份文件的相关性，普通法院判决无误。
items.ai_summary_en: Advocate General Rantos has issued an Opinion recommending that the Court of Justice dismiss Meta Platforms Ireland’s appeals against General Court judgments. These judgments upheld the European Commission’s decisions requesting internal documents during an abuse of dominance investigation concerning Facebook Data and Marketplace services. The AG found no legal error in the General Court’s assessment that the information requests were necessary, proportionate, and compliant with privacy rights. This Opinion reinforces the Commission’s broad investigative powers under EU competition procedural regulations, confirming that regulators may request extensive internal records without proving the relevance of each individual document, provided adequate safeguards for sensitive personal data are implemented.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确认了欧盟委员会在竞争法调查中的广泛取证权力，即便涉及敏感个人数据。企业需注意，在配合调查时，以隐私为由拒绝提供文件的抗辩空间有限，需严格遵循虚拟数据室等程序性保障措施。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants subject to EU competition investigations should prioritize robust internal data governance to ensure compliant responses to regulatory document requests. Establishing clear protocols for document classification and cross-border data transfer risks is essential to mitigate penalties for obstructing inquiries. While companies may leverage privacy protections as a procedural defense, they must balance this with cooperation obligations. Legal teams should carefully monitor the boundaries of procedural challenges to avoid escalating enforcement actions, ensuring that claims regarding data sensitivity are substantiated by concrete technical and organizational safeguards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总检察长建议驳回Meta上诉，维持普通法院支持欧委会调查决定的判决", "欧委会有权在竞争调查中要求提供内部文件，无需详尽论证每份文件的个体相关性", "普通法院认定信息请求理由充分、必要且成比例，符合隐私权及良好行政原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals, upholding the General Court’s validation of the Commission’s information requests.", "The Commission possesses broad investigative powers in antitrust probes and is not required to demonstrate the specific relevance of every requested document individually.", "Information requests must comply with privacy principles and good administration, which was satisfied here through established virtual data room safeguards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟机构在竞争调查中享有广泛的信息索取权，隐私抗辩门槛较高", "企业应关注虚拟数据室等程序性保障在敏感数据披露中的实际应用标准", "后续需观察欧盟法院最终判决是否完全采纳总检察长意见，确立先例"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen internal data governance frameworks to ensure compliant and efficient responses to EU regulatory document demands.", "Practice note: Monitor the procedural boundaries of competition investigations to utilize privacy principles for legitimate, targeted defenses.", "What to monitor: Develop internal document classification systems and assess cross-border data transfer risks to prevent obstruction allegations."]
core_points_en: The Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals, upholding the General Court’s validation of the Commission’s information requests.
The Commission possesses broad investigative powers in antitrust probes and is not required to demonstrate the specific relevance of every requested document individually.
Information requests must comply with privacy principles and good administration, which was satisfied here through established virtual data room safeguards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen internal data governance frameworks to ensure compliant and efficient responses to EU regulatory document demands.
Practice note: Monitor the procedural boundaries of competition investigations to utilize privacy principles for legitimate, targeted defenses.
What to monitor: Develop internal document classification systems and assess cross-border data transfer risks to prevent obstruction allegations.

items.title: Judgment of the Court of Justice in Case C-337/22 P EUIPO v Nowhere
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：过渡期后仅依英国法享有的在先权利不得用于支持欧盟商标异议
items.title_en: CJEU Rules UK Rights Cannot Support EU Trademark Oppositions Post-Transition
items.summary: Brexit and EU trade marks: Earlier rights based solely on UK law can no longer be relied upon in support of an opposition where EUIPO rules after the end of the transition period
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在EUIPO诉Nowhere案中撤销普通法院判决，维持EUIPO决定。法院裁定，若EUIPO在脱欧过渡期结束后作出裁决，仅依据英国法律享有的在先未注册商标权利，不再能作为反对欧盟商标注册的依据。因为在先权利必须在EUIPO作出决定时仍然有效，而英国权利在过渡期后在欧盟境内已失效。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union set aside the General Court’s judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere, ruling that earlier rights based solely on UK law cannot support an opposition to an EU trademark application if the EUIPO decision is rendered after the Brexit transition period. Although the EU application was filed before the transition ended, the Court held that unregistered signs must confer a right to prohibit use at the time of the EUIPO decision. Since UK rights no longer have effect in the EU post-transition, they fail this second requirement. The Court thereby upheld the EUIPO’s rejection of the opposition, clarifying that the temporal validity of earlier rights is assessed at the date of the administrative decision, not merely the filing date.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了脱欧后欧盟商标异议程序中“在先权利持续有效性”的时间判断标准。实务中需严格区分权利产生时间与权利存续时间，仅凭申请日前的英国使用事实不足以支撑过渡期后的欧盟异议，权利人需确保在EUIPO裁决时刻拥有欧盟境内有效的在先权利基础。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes that the substantive validity of earlier rights in EU trademark oppositions is determined at the time of the EUIPO decision. Rights holders cannot rely on national rights that have ceased to have effect in the EU territory due to Brexit, even if those rights existed when the contested application was filed. This reinforces the territorial nature of IP rights and the impact of geopolitical changes on pending proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院规定基于未注册标志的异议需满足两项要求：申请日前取得权利及EUIPO裁决时仍享…", "仅依英国法享有的在先权利在脱欧过渡期结束后，无法在欧盟境内继续产生禁止使用的法律效力", "即使欧盟商标申请提交于过渡期结束前，若EUIPO裁决时在先英国权利已失效，异议不能成立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.", "UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.", "Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确EUIPO裁决时点在判断在先权利有效性中的决定性作用，而非仅看申请日", "纯英国法下的未注册商标权利在过渡期后彻底失去阻碍欧盟商标注册的程序资格", "后续需关注此类判决对涉及跨境未注册商标权益冲突案件的既判力影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…", "Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.", "What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights must confer prohibition rights at the time of the EUIPO decision, not just at filing.
UK-only rights lost effect in the EU after the Brexit transition period expired.
Oppositions relying solely on pre-transition UK unregistered rights fail if decided post-transition.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owners of UK-only unregistered rights cannot block later EU trademark registrations through oppositions decided after the transition period, regardless of prior…
Practice note: In opposition proceedings, verify that cited earlier rights remain legally effective in the EU at the anticipated date of the EUIPO decision.
What to monitor: Watch for similar applications of this temporal validity rule in other contexts where national rights diverge from EU protection scopes.

items.title: What happens to global SEP strategy if Wilus wins US injunction?
items.title_zh: 若 Wilus 获美禁令，全球 SEP 战略将如何演变？
items.title_en: Global SEP Strategy Shifts If Wilus Secures US Injunction and Restores Enforcement Venue
items.summary: It could dramatically reshape global SEP litigation by restoring the US as a top enforcement venue
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨若 Wilus 在美国成功获得标准必要专利（SEP）禁令，将如何重塑全球诉讼格局。分析指出，此举可能恢复美国作为顶级执行地的地位，迫使全球权利人与实施者重新评估其许可谈判策略及管辖地选择，进而影响包括欧洲在内的全球 SEP 生态体系。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the potential transformation of global standard-essential patent litigation should Wilus successfully obtain an injunction in the United States. Such a ruling could significantly restore the status of US courts as premier venues for enforcing SEP rights, altering the current landscape dominated by other jurisdictions. The outcome would likely compel both rights holders and implementers worldwide to reassess their licensing negotiation strategies and jurisdictional choices. Consequently, this shift would impact the broader global SEP ecosystem, including European markets, by changing leverage dynamics in cross-border disputes. The article highlights how a favorable US decision could rebalance enforcement power, influencing where parties choose to litigate and settle complex SEP conflicts globally.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营需警惕美国判决的域外溢出效应。建议提前评估在美专利风险，优化全球许可布局，避免因地缘司法变动导致在欧洲市场的谈判筹码受损或面临双重诉讼压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating across jurisdictions should anticipate the extraterritorial ripple effects of significant US SEP rulings. A decisive US injunction could alter negotiation leverage in Europe, potentially exposing companies to dual litigation pressures or diminished bargaining power. It is prudent to evaluate patent exposure in the US early within the licensing cycle. Stakeholders should develop defensive strategies that account for divergent judicial approaches in key markets like the US and Europe. Monitoring these developments helps mitigate risks associated with geopolitical judicial shifts and ensures robust positioning in global FRAND negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wilus 若获美禁令将显著提升美国法院执行力", "全球 SEP 诉讼重心可能向美国市场发生转移", "权利人需据此调整跨国许可与诉讼组合策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A successful US injunction for Wilus would substantially enhance the enforceability and strategic importance of US courts in SEP disputes.", "The center of gravity for global SEP litigation may shift toward the US market, altering traditional venue preferences for major stakeholders.", "Rights holders must adapt their multinational licensing frameworks and litigation portfolios to align with evolving US judicial enforcement trends."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕美国禁令对全球许可谈判的连锁反应", "需构建兼顾欧美司法特点的防御性专利策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate how US injunctive relief may diminish negotiation leverage in European SEP discussions.", "Practice note: Develop integrated defense strategies addressing distinct judicial standards in both US and European forums.", "What to monitor: Track the progression of the Wilus case and its immediate impact on global forum selection tactics."]
core_points_en: A successful US injunction for Wilus would substantially enhance the enforceability and strategic importance of US courts in SEP disputes.
The center of gravity for global SEP litigation may shift toward the US market, altering traditional venue preferences for major stakeholders.
Rights holders must adapt their multinational licensing frameworks and litigation portfolios to align with evolving US judicial enforcement trends.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate how US injunctive relief may diminish negotiation leverage in European SEP discussions.
Practice note: Develop integrated defense strategies addressing distinct judicial standards in both US and European forums.
What to monitor: Track the progression of the Wilus case and its immediate impact on global forum selection tactics.

items.title: The never-ending story of Brexit – The Final Chapter
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院就EUIPO异议案中在先权利存续时间作出判决
items.title_en: CJEU Rules: Earlier Rights Must Exist Until EUIPO Decision in Opposition Proceedings
items.summary: The Court of Justice of the EU (“CJEU”) has rendered its long-awaited decision in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), disagreeing with the Advocate General’s (“AG”) opinion. In short, the CJEU held that, in opposition proceedings, the earlier right must continue to exist until the time the European Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) or its Board of Appeal (“BoA”) renders the decision.   Background In
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在EUIPO v Nowhere案（C-337/22）中作出判决，驳回了总检察官的意见。法院裁定，在商标异议程序中，作为异议基础的在先权利必须持续存在，直至欧盟知识产权局或其上诉委员会作出决定之时。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU has issued its final judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), departing from the Advocate General’s opinion. The Court clarified that, within opposition proceedings, the earlier right serving as the basis for opposition must remain valid and existing until the moment the European Union Intellectual Property Office or its Board of Appeal renders its decision. This ruling resolves significant uncertainty regarding the temporal validity of rights, particularly concerning United Kingdom trademarks following Brexit. By establishing that the critical assessment point is the date of the administrative decision rather than an earlier procedural stage, the CJEU provides a definitive standard for evaluating the continuity of earlier rights. This interpretation ensures legal certainty for all parties involved in cross-border intellectual property disputes within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了异议程序中在先权利有效性的截止时间点，对评估异议基础稳定性具有程序性意义。需关注该判决对后续类似异议案件证据提交及权利状态核查的具体影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders relying on third-country marks, such as those from the UK, to oppose EU applications must ensure these rights remain in force throughout the entire administrative process. Since the validity is assessed at the time of the final decision, any lapse or cancellation before that date will invalidate the opposition grounds. Practitioners should implement robust monitoring systems to track the status of cited earlier rights dynamically. It is advisable to prepare alternative legal strategies or supplementary evidence in case the primary earlier right expires during lengthy proceedings, thereby mitigating the risk of opposition failure due to loss of standing.

items.title: Presidium announces establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division
items.title_zh: UPC主席团宣布杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭
items.title_en: UPC Presidium Establishes Second Panel at Düsseldorf Local Division Effective March 2026
items.summary: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided the establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division (DE) as of 1 March 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）主席团于2026年1月20日作出决定，自2026年3月1日起在杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭。贝雷尼斯·托姆法官被任命为第二合议庭审判长，英戈·林肯法官加入该庭。第一合议庭由罗尼·托马斯法官任审判长，朱勒·舒马赫法官加入。该决定依据UPCA及程序规则作出，旨在优化案件审理能力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided to establish a second panel at the Düsseldorf Local Division, effective 1 March 2026. This decision, adopted on 20 January 2026 under Article 8 of the UPCA and Rule 341.4 of the Rules of Procedure, aims to enhance judicial capacity. Ms Bérénice Thom is appointed as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by legally qualified judge Mr Ingo Rinken. Panel 1 remains presided over by Mr Ronny Thomas, with Ms Jule Schumacher serving as the second legally qualified judge. The expansion reflects ongoing adjustments to manage caseloads in one of the court’s busiest divisions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This structural expansion addresses capacity needs in Düsseldorf, a key venue for European patent litigation. Rights holders should note that case allocation mechanisms may evolve to distribute work between the two panels. Monitoring the composition of these panels is essential for understanding potential judicial approaches in upcoming proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC主席团决定自2026年3月1日起在杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭", "贝雷尼斯·托姆法官任第二合议庭审判长，英戈·林肯法官为成员", "罗尼·托马斯法官继续担任第一合议庭审判长，朱勒·舒马赫法官为成员"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A second panel at the UPC Düsseldorf Local Division becomes operational on 1 March 2026.", "Ms Bérénice Thom serves as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by Mr Ingo Rinken.", "Mr Ronny Thomas continues as Presiding Judge of Panel 1, alongside Ms Jule Schumacher."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["杜塞尔多夫地方法院案件量增长推动司法资源扩容，反映其作为专利诉讼首选地的地位", "新合议庭的成立可能改变案件分配机制，影响具体案件的审理法官组合", "需观察后续是否还有其他地方法院跟进增设合议庭，以应对UPC诉讼量的整体变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Increased judicial capacity in Düsseldorf may influence forum selection decisions for parties seeking efficient resolution of patent disputes in Germany.", "Practice note: Litigators should track case assignment protocols to anticipate which panel will hear specific matters, as judicial composition can impact procedural management.", "What to monitor: Observe whether other high-volume Local Divisions implement similar panel expansions to handle growing UPC caseloads across the system."]
core_points_en: A second panel at the UPC Düsseldorf Local Division becomes operational on 1 March 2026.
Ms Bérénice Thom serves as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by Mr Ingo Rinken.
Mr Ronny Thomas continues as Presiding Judge of Panel 1, alongside Ms Jule Schumacher.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Increased judicial capacity in Düsseldorf may influence forum selection decisions for parties seeking efficient resolution of patent disputes in Germany.
Practice note: Litigators should track case assignment protocols to anticipate which panel will hear specific matters, as judicial composition can impact procedural management.
What to monitor: Observe whether other high-volume Local Divisions implement similar panel expansions to handle growing UPC caseloads across the system.

items.title: Article 33.1(b) UPCA: “same infringement” does not require identical accused products
items.title_zh: UPCA第33.1(b)条：“同一侵权”不要求被告产品完全相同
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Same Infringement Claim Does Not Require Identical Accused Products
items.summary: Valeo v Bosch – Local Division Paris, 17 February 2026 (UPC_CFI_1963/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎地方法院在Valeo诉Bosch案中裁定，UPCA第33.1(b)条规定的“同一涉嫌侵权”指侵犯同一专利，而非要求被控产品、分销渠道或具体行为完全一致。法院驳回博世关于管辖权的初步异议，强调该条款应灵活解释以避免多重诉讼，只要被告间存在商业关系且涉嫌侵犯同一专利即可合并审理。
items.ai_summary_en: In Valeo v Bosch, the Paris Local Division of the Unified Patent Court clarified Article 33.1(b) of the UPC Agreement regarding multi-defendant actions. The court rejected Bosch’s preliminary objection challenging internal competence, which argued that different entities were accused regarding distinct product variants and distribution channels. The judges held that 'same alleged infringement' refers to the violation of the same patent, not identical products or commercial conduct. This flexible interpretation aims to prevent multiple proceedings and irreconcilable decisions. The ruling confirms that a commercial relationship within a corporate group, combined with allegations of infringing the same patent, is sufficient to consolidate cases against multiple defendants, regardless of differences in specific product embodiments or roles in manufacturing and distribution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决降低了在UPC针对多个被告合并起诉的门槛，原告无需证明所有被告涉及完全相同的产品。企业需注意，即便供应链角色不同，只要涉及同一专利侵权，均可能在同一分院被诉。这有助于减少碎片化诉讼，但增加了被告应对联合诉讼的风险，需关注法院对“商业关系”认定的灵活性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation in Europe should recognize that the threshold for consolidating multi-defendant actions has been lowered. Even if different corporate entities handle distinct product variants or operate in separate regions, they may still be sued together for infringing the same patent. Companies must reassess the effectiveness of their current entity structures and risk isolation measures within Europe. It is crucial to evaluate potential liability across the entire corporate group rather than assuming geographic or product-based separation provides jurisdictional safety. Proactive coordination of defense strategies and compliance assessments across all relevant entities is essential to manage exposure effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定“同一涉嫌侵权”指侵犯同一专利，不要求被控产品或分销链完全相同", "不同被告涉及不同产品变体或制造分销角色，仍可满足合并审理的管辖条件", "隶属同一集团并参与相关商业活动足以建立所需的商业关系，支持合并管辖"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.", "Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.", "Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确UPC合并被告管辖标准，降低原告举证难度，利于集中维权", "被告不能仅以产品型号或销售渠道差异为由挑战法院管辖权", "需持续观察后续案件中对“商业关系”及“同一专利”认定的具体边界"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.", "Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.", "What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively."]
core_points_en: Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.
Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.
Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.
Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.
What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE insurance can eliminate the need for security for costs
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院：事后保险可免除诉讼费用担保
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE Insurance Can Eliminate Need for Security for Costs
items.summary: On 18 February 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal set aside an order for security for costs of €2 million, holding that the Munich Local Division (Munich LD)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月18日，统一专利法院上诉法院撤销慕尼黑地方法院要求原告提供200万欧元诉讼费用担保的命令。法院认定，下级法院未考量原告持有的事后保险（ATE），属行使裁量权错误。该保险由马耳他 insurer 承保，含反避损条款，足以保障被告权益，故无需额外担保。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal set aside a Munich Local Division order requiring a claimant to provide €2 million in security for costs. The appellate court held that the lower division erred by failing to consider the claimant’s existing After The Event (ATE) insurance when assessing the necessity of such security. The ATE policy, issued by an EU-based insurer with a €4 million limit and an anti-avoidance endorsement, was deemed sufficient to protect the defendant’s interests regarding potential costs orders. Consequently, the requirement for additional cash deposits or bank guarantees was removed. This decision clarifies that valid ATE insurance forms part of a claimant’s financial position and can effectively substitute traditional security measures under Rule 158.1 of the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决确立了ATE保险在UPC程序中作为诉讼费用担保替代方案的地位。当事人需确保保单包含不可撤销及直接受益条款，以证明其充分性。保险公司需符合欧盟监管标准，以消除偿付能力疑虑。这降低了原告的现金流压力，但提高了保险合规审查的重要性。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts litigation strategy before the Unified Patent Court by validating After The Event insurance as a viable alternative to substantial cash or bank guarantees. Rights holders can leverage compliant ATE policies to mitigate liquidity constraints while defending against security for costs applications. To succeed, applicants must ensure their policies include robust anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by reputable EU-regulated insurers. This approach allows market participants to optimize capital allocation during patent proceedings without compromising the opposing party's ability to recover awarded costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院认定慕尼黑地方法院未考量原告ATE保险，错误行使了关于诉讼费用担保的裁量权", "法院确认包含反避损条款的ATE保险构成原告财务状况一部分，足以消除对费用回收的合理担忧", "基于欧盟偿付能力II指令，法院驳回了对马耳他保险公司偿付能力及保单有效性的质疑"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant's financial position and must be considered when assessing security for costs.", "Policies with anti-avoidance endorsements from EU-compliant insurers can effectively replace traditional cash or bank guarantees.", "Insurer termination rights do not invalidate the policy as security if mechanisms like waiting periods are included."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["ATE保险若含反避损及直接执行条款，可被UPC视为充分的费用回收保障，无需另行提供现金…", "保险公司需受欧盟偿付能力II指令监管，以确保证券有效性及偿付能力，避免因地域因素被质疑", "后续需观察法院对保单具体条款（如终止权限制、索赔机制）的审查标准，以及是否扩展至其他非…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Using compliant ATE insurance reduces upfront cash requirements for security for costs, preserving working capital for companies engaged in UPC litigation.", "Practice note: Ensure ATE policies include anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by EU-regulated insurers to withstand judicial scrutiny under Rule 158.1 RoP.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions refining the criteria for acceptable ATE terms, particularly regarding insurer solvency and unilateral termination clauses."]
core_points_en: ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant's financial position and must be considered when assessing security for costs.
Policies with anti-avoidance endorsements from EU-compliant insurers can effectively replace traditional cash or bank guarantees.
Insurer termination rights do not invalidate the policy as security if mechanisms like waiting periods are included.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Using compliant ATE insurance reduces upfront cash requirements for security for costs, preserving working capital for companies engaged in UPC litigation.
Practice note: Ensure ATE policies include anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by EU-regulated insurers to withstand judicial scrutiny under Rule 158.1 RoP.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions refining the criteria for acceptable ATE terms, particularly regarding insurer solvency and unilateral termination clauses.

items.title: Exemptions and compensation schemes for commercial users: Private copying levies after the CJEU’s ruling in bluechip v ZPÜ (C-822/24)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院bluechip案：商业用户私人复制 levy 豁免与补偿
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Private Copying Levy Exemptions and Compensation Conditions for Commercial Users
items.summary: In bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) further determines the conditions under which EU Member States can implement the exception or limitation for reproduction of protected works for private uses under art.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在bluechip诉ZPÜ案中裁定，成员国仅当存储设备被自然人用于非直接或间接商业目的的私人复制时，方可依据《信息社会指令》征收公平补偿金。法院强调需平衡权利人利益与用户权益，明确了向商业终端用户销售设备时的举证责任及补偿金收取条件。
items.ai_summary_en: In Bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union clarified that Member States may only levy fair compensation under Article 5(2)(b) of the InfoSoc Directive when devices are used by natural persons for non-commercial private copying. The Court addressed whether a rebuttable presumption applies to devices sold to commercial end-users, assuming such devices facilitate private reproduction. It confirmed that while this presumption exists, manufacturers, importers, or traders can avoid liability if they provide evidence proving that the devices were not used for private copying on more than a small scale. This ruling defines the boundaries for collecting levies and establishes the specific burden of proof required from rights holders and device suppliers within the EU copyright framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案澄清了商业环境下私人复制补偿金的适用边界，强调了“非商业目的”的核心地位。企业需关注设备销售对象及使用场景的举证责任分配，以及各国据此调整国内补偿金征收机制的可能性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants selling storage media in the EU should review their historical levy payment records, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany where presumptions favor collection from commercial sales channels. Establishing robust mechanisms to document final user purposes is critical for challenging these presumptions effectively. Companies should assess past transactions to identify potential overpayments and evaluate eligibility for reimbursement claims. Proactive evidence retention regarding customer usage profiles will help mitigate financial exposure and prevent double costs arising from conflicting national interpretations of private copying exceptions and compensation schemes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院明确私人复制例外仅适用于自然人非商业目的的复制行为", "成员国可要求制造商支付补偿金，除非其证明设备仅用于小规模私人复制", "立法目的在于补偿未经授权使用造成的损害，需平衡权利人与用户利益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.", "A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.", "Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确私人复制补偿金征收前提是设备用于自然人非商业目的", "制造商需承担反驳推定的举证责任，证明设备未用于私人复制", "观察成员国如何根据此裁决调整针对商业用户的补偿金征收政策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.", "Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.", "What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities."]
core_points_en: Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.
A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.
Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.
Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.
What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities.

items.title: Via adds two major Chinese auto suppliers to Qi patent pool
items.title_zh: Via 无线充电专利池新增两家中国主要汽车供应商
items.title_en: Via Adds Major Chinese Auto Suppliers to Qi SEP Pool as Powermat Expands Independent Licensing
items.summary: The programme gets fresh licensee endorsement while non-pool licensor Powermat steps up own licensing efforts
items.ai_summary_zh: Via Licensing 管理的 Qi 标准必要专利池获得两家中国主要汽车供应商的许可背书，显示该专利池在汽车行业渗透力增强。与此同时，非专利池成员 Powermat 正加强其独立许可计划。这一动态反映了无线充电领域 SEP 许可格局的演变，可能影响进入欧洲市场的中国汽车供应链企业的合规策略与授权成本结构。
items.ai_summary_en: Via Licensing’s Qi standard-essential patent pool has secured license endorsements from two major Chinese automotive suppliers, signaling strengthened penetration within the global automotive supply chain. This development highlights the growing acceptance of pooled licensing models for wireless charging technologies among key industry participants. Concurrently, non-pool licensor Powermat is intensifying its independent licensing efforts, creating a dual-track enforcement environment. These parallel dynamics illustrate the evolving landscape of SEP licensing in the wireless charging sector. For market participants, particularly those supplying European automakers, this shift necessitates careful evaluation of compliance strategies and potential authorization cost structures. The coexistence of pool-based and bilateral licensing approaches suggests a complex negotiation environment where rights holders are actively pursuing diverse monetization paths across different jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及供应商应评估 Via 与 Powermat 双重许可压力，避免重复付费。建议提前开展 FTE 分析，利用 FRAND 原则谈判，并关注欧洲潜在反垄断监管动向以维护自身权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess exposure to dual licensing pressures from both Via and Powermat to mitigate risks of double-dipping or redundant royalty payments. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in product development cycles allows companies to leverage FRAND principles effectively during negotiations. Additionally, monitoring potential antitrust scrutiny by European regulators regarding cumulative royalty rates and licensing practices is crucial. Rights holders and implementers alike must navigate these overlapping claims carefully, utilizing EU competition law tools where appropriate to optimize licensing terms and ensure compliant market access without excessive financial burden.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Via 专利池获两家中国头部汽配商加入，扩大行业覆盖", "非池内权利人 Powermat 同步强化独立许可攻势", "汽车领域无线充电 SEP 许可竞争态势日趋复杂"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Via Licensing expands Qi patent pool coverage by adding two leading Chinese automotive component suppliers as new licensees.", "Non-pool licensor Powermat simultaneously strengthens its independent licensing program, increasing competitive pressure on implementers.", "The wireless charging SEP licensing landscape becomes increasingly complex with concurrent pool and bilateral enforcement strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需警惕多重许可主张，建立内部 SEP 风险评估机制", "善用 FRAND 抗辩与欧盟竞争法工具优化许可条款"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate exposure to dual licensing demands from Via and Powermat to prevent duplicate payments and manage budget forecasts.", "Practice note: Implement internal SEP risk assessment mechanisms and utilize FRAND defenses alongside EU competition law arguments to negotiate favorable terms.", "What to monitor: Track regulatory developments concerning cumulative royalties and antitrust investigations into wireless charging SEP licensing practices in Europe."]
core_points_en: Via Licensing expands Qi patent pool coverage by adding two leading Chinese automotive component suppliers as new licensees.
Non-pool licensor Powermat simultaneously strengthens its independent licensing program, increasing competitive pressure on implementers.
The wireless charging SEP licensing landscape becomes increasingly complex with concurrent pool and bilateral enforcement strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate exposure to dual licensing demands from Via and Powermat to prevent duplicate payments and manage budget forecasts.
Practice note: Implement internal SEP risk assessment mechanisms and utilize FRAND defenses alongside EU competition law arguments to negotiate favorable terms.
What to monitor: Track regulatory developments concerning cumulative royalties and antitrust investigations into wireless charging SEP licensing practices in Europe.

items.title: BREAKING: Ericsson sues Verifone at UPC in first IoT SEP enforcement actions
items.title_zh: 爱立信在 UPC 起诉 Verifone，启动首例物联网 SEP 执法
items.title_en: Ericsson Sues Verifone at UPC in First IoT SEP Enforcement Action
items.summary: SEP owner has asked Mannheim Local Division to determine whether its offer is FRAND
items.ai_summary_zh: 标准必要专利持有人爱立信已向统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院提起诉讼，指控 Verifone 侵权。此案标志着 UPC 受理的首批物联网领域 SEP 执法行动。核心诉求在于请求法院裁定爱立信提出的许可要约是否符合公平、合理及无歧视（FRAND）原则，这将测试新法院在处理复杂许可纠纷上的效能。
items.ai_summary_en: Standard essential patent holder Ericsson has initiated infringement proceedings against Verifone before the Unified Patent Court. Filed at the Mannheim Local Division, this case represents the first enforcement action concerning Internet of Things standard essential patents within the new court system. The core legal request asks the tribunal to determine whether Ericsson’s licensing offer complies with fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory principles. This litigation serves as a critical test for the UPC’s capacity to handle complex global licensing disputes. It highlights the court's emerging role as a central venue for resolving high-stakes intellectual property conflicts across Europe, setting important precedents for future technology sector litigation involving connectivity standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业需警惕 UPC 成为 SEP 诉讼新高地，其裁决具泛欧效力。建议提前评估专利风险，制定符合 FRAND 原则的谈判策略，避免在德国等地遭遇禁令威胁，并密切关注曼海姆分院的裁判风向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the connected device sector should recognize the UPC as an increasingly influential forum for SEP disputes, given its pan-European injunctive relief capabilities. Rights holders and implementers must ensure their licensing negotiations strictly adhere to FRAND obligations to mitigate litigation risks. Companies should proactively assess their patent exposure and refine negotiation protocols to withstand judicial scrutiny, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany known for rigorous enforcement. Monitoring early procedural developments and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division is essential for adapting corporate IP strategies to this new legal landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信发起 UPC 首例物联网 SEP 诉讼，被告为 Verifone", "案件由曼海姆地方分院审理，聚焦许可要约的 FRAND 合规性", "此举确立 UPC 作为欧洲 SEP 争议解决新核心平台的地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.", "The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.", "This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 首案确立新范式，中国出口企业面临泛欧禁令风险", "需优化 FRAND 谈判合规性，以应对德国分院的严格审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.", "Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms."]
core_points_en: Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.
The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.
This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.
Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms.

items.title: World Intellectual Property Report: Pace of Innovation Diffusion Reaches Historical Heights, But Access, Usage and Capability Gaps Remain
items.title_zh: WIPO 报告：技术扩散速度创历史新高，但应用差距仍存
items.title_en: WIPO Report: Innovation Diffusion Hits Record Pace, Yet Access and Capability Gaps Persist
items.summary: New technologies are spreading across borders at unprecedented pace and gaps are narrowing in how intensively countries are using the innovations, the latest World Intellectual Property Report (WIPR) 2026, Technology on the Move finds.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织发布《2026 年世界知识产权报告》，指出新技术跨境传播速度达到历史峰值，各国在创新使用强度上的差距正在缩小。然而，报告同时警示，不同国家在获取技术、实际 usage 及创新能力建设方面仍存在显著鸿沟，需政策干预以促进均衡。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization has released the 2026 World Intellectual Property Report, titled Technology on the Move. The report indicates that new technologies are spreading across international borders at an unprecedented historical pace. Concurrently, disparities regarding the intensity with which different nations utilize these innovations are narrowing significantly. However, the analysis also issues a cautionary note, highlighting that substantial gaps remain concerning technology access, practical application, and the development of innovation capabilities across various countries. These persistent structural divides suggest that policy interventions are necessary to promote more balanced global technological adoption and ensure equitable participation in the modern innovation ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应利用技术扩散加速窗口期，优化欧洲专利布局。同时需关注目标市场的能力建设缺口，通过技术合作或本地化运营弥补应用差距，避免单纯依赖出口模式。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should leverage the current window of accelerated technology diffusion to optimize their European patent portfolios strategically. It is critical to address capability gaps in target markets by fostering local technical collaborations rather than relying solely on export models. Companies must assess local application strengths to bridge usage divides effectively. This approach ensures robust IP protection while enhancing market penetration through localized operational strategies and cooperative frameworks that align with regional innovation ecosystems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新技术跨境扩散速度达到前所未有的历史高点", "各国间创新使用强度的差距呈现收窄趋势", "技术获取渠道与应用能力差距依然是主要挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Cross-border diffusion of new technologies has reached an unprecedented historical peak in speed and reach.", "Disparities in the intensity of innovation usage among different countries are showing a clear narrowing trend.", "Significant challenges persist regarding technology access channels and overall application capabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["把握技术快速扩散机遇，加速高价值专利海外布局", "针对应用能力短板，深化在欧洲的本地化技术合作"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accelerate overseas deployment of high-value patents to capitalize on rapid technology diffusion opportunities.", "Practice note: Deepen localized technical cooperation in Europe to address specific application capability shortcomings.", "What to monitor: Evolving policy interventions aimed at reducing global technology access and usage disparities."]
core_points_en: Cross-border diffusion of new technologies has reached an unprecedented historical peak in speed and reach.
Disparities in the intensity of innovation usage among different countries are showing a clear narrowing trend.
Significant challenges persist regarding technology access channels and overall application capabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accelerate overseas deployment of high-value patents to capitalize on rapid technology diffusion opportunities.
Practice note: Deepen localized technical cooperation in Europe to address specific application capability shortcomings.
What to monitor: Evolving policy interventions aimed at reducing global technology access and usage disparities.

items.title: Unitary effect requires designation of all participating Member States
items.title_zh: 单一效力要求指定所有参与成员国：Papst Licensing案判决
items.title_en: UPC Confirms Unitary Effect Requires Designation of All Participating Member States
items.summary: Papst Licensing v EPO – Court of Appeal, 9 February 2026, UPC_CoA_8/2026
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院驳回Papst Licensing针对EP 3 327 608单一效力注册被拒的上诉。因该专利源于2005年申请，当时马耳他未加入EPC，导致授权时未指定马耳他。法院依据第1257/2012号条例，确认若授权专利未覆盖所有参与成员国，则不得授予单一效力，不存在领土 carve-out 空间。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal dismissed Papst Licensing’s appeal, upholding the EPO’s refusal to register unitary effect for EP 3 327 608. The patent originated from a 2005 application filed before Malta joined the EPC, resulting in a granted text that excluded Malta. By the time of grant in 2025, Malta was a participating Member State. The Court ruled that Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 strictly requires the same claims for all participating states, prohibiting territorial carve-outs. Arguments based on proportionality or fundamental rights were rejected as contra legem interpretations. This decision clarifies that historical designation gaps prevent unitary effect, even if unintentional. While currently rare due to Malta’s early EPC accession, similar compliance risks may arise if Montenegro joins the EU and UPCA, given its later EPC entry in 2022.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案揭示了单一专利体系的结构性限制：仅影响马耳他加入EPC前提交的极老旧申请。实务中需关注黑山等后续加入EPC但暂未参与单一专利体系的国家，若其未来加入欧盟及UPCA，可能引发类似资格问题。目前风险局限于特定历史申请。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders managing legacy patent portfolios must verify whether early applications predate the EPC membership of current unitary participating states. If a participating state was not designated due to historical non-membership, the patent cannot obtain unitary effect. Stakeholders should assess such assets for conversion into traditional European patent bundles to ensure enforceability. Proactive audits of long-standing patent families are essential to identify territorial coverage gaps. For affected patents, alternative litigation strategies or licensing structures should be developed to mitigate validity risks associated with the lack of unitary status.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院确认，授予单一效力的前提是欧洲专利必须以相同权利要求覆盖所有参与成员国", "因马耳他在申请日尚未加入EPC，涉案专利未指定该国，故EPO拒绝注册单一效力获法院支持", "法院强调，比例原则或基本权利不能作为违背法律明文规定的理由，且拒绝向欧盟法院提请裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.", "Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.", "Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单一专利不允许领土排除，必须覆盖所有参与国，否则无法注册", "当前风险仅存在于马耳他加入EPC（2007年）前提交的专利申请家族", "需监测黑山等非欧盟EPC成员国未来加入欧盟后的制度衔接潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.", "Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.", "What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings."]
core_points_en: Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.
Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.
Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.
Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.
What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings.

items.title: Oath taking of newly appointed legally qualified judges
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院五名新任法律法官举行就职宣誓
items.title_en: Oath taking of newly appointed legally qualified judges
items.summary: Oath taking of 5 newly appointed legally qualified judges will take place at the Local division Munich (DE), Local division The Hague (NL), the Nordic-Baltic Regional division, Local division Düsseldorf (DE) and Central division Munich (DE)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）宣布五名新任法律合格法官将于各分部举行公开宣誓就职仪式。依据行政委员会2025年11月4日的决定，Ina Schnurr、Robert van Peursem等五人分别被任命至慕尼黑、海牙、北欧-波罗的海地区及杜塞尔多夫等地方法庭或中央法庭。根据《UPC规约》第6条，他们在正式履职前须在公开法庭宣誓，标志着UPC司法团队进一步扩充，旨在保障欧洲统一专利诉讼体系的顺利运行与案件审理能力。
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC司法团队的持续扩充表明其审理能力正在稳步提升，未来专利诉讼效率可能提高。中国出口企业应密切关注新任法官所在分部的管辖倾向，特别是慕尼黑和海牙等活跃法庭的司法实践。建议提前完善在欧洲的专利FTO分析，并针对UPC程序特点优化应诉策略，以应对潜在的高强度专利纠纷。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC五名新任法律法官将在德荷等地方法庭及中央法庭举行公开宣誓", "任命依据行政委员会2025年11月4日决定，涵盖慕尼黑、海牙等多个关键司法管辖区", "法官需依《UPC规约》第6条在公开法庭宣誓后方可正式履行司法职责"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC司法资源扩充预示诉讼效率提升，企业需警惕案件处理周期缩短", "关注新任法官所在地法庭的裁判倾向，针对性优化在欧洲的专利合规布局"]

items.title: Ericsson reveals market-by-market IoT approach, new healthcare/agriculture deals
items.title_zh: 爱立信披露分市场 IoT 策略及新医疗农业协议
items.title_en: Ericsson Unveils Market-Specific IoT Licensing Strategy and New Healthcare, Agriculture Deals
items.summary: New insights from Swedish company helps build picture of how leading licensors are tackling tricky and fragmented IoT markets
items.ai_summary_zh: 瑞典爱立信公司披露其针对碎片化物联网市场的逐国许可策略，并宣布在医疗与农业领域达成新交易。该动态揭示了主要 SEP 权利人如何应对复杂市场，为观察欧洲物联网领域的标准必要专利许可实践提供了关键视角。
items.ai_summary_en: Swedish technology giant Ericsson has disclosed its market-by-market approach to licensing Internet of Things (IoT) patents, alongside announcements of new agreements in the healthcare and agricultural sectors. This strategic shift provides critical insights into how leading standard-essential patent (SEP) holders are navigating the complex and highly fragmented IoT landscape. By tailoring licensing efforts to specific industry verticals and geographic markets, Ericsson demonstrates a methodical response to the challenges posed by diverse IoT implementations. These developments offer a significant perspective on current SEP licensing practices within Europe, highlighting how major licensors are adapting their commercial strategies to address the unique regulatory and technical requirements of distinct industrial sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业应关注爱立信分市场策略，评估出口欧洲的许可成本。建议提前布局医疗农业领域专利交叉许可，防范 SEP 诉讼风险，优化合规架构。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely analyze Ericsson’s segmented licensing model to anticipate potential fluctuations in European royalty rates and compliance costs. Companies operating in connected health or smart agriculture sectors must strengthen their patent reserves to better position themselves during future negotiations with SEP holders. Proactively assessing cross-licensing opportunities in these high-growth verticals can help mitigate litigation risks. Furthermore, firms should review their internal IP compliance architectures to ensure alignment with evolving industry-specific licensing norms, thereby optimizing cost structures and reducing exposure to enforcement actions in key export markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信采取按市场细分的物联网专利许可策略", "新增医疗健康与农业科技领域的专利授权合作", "揭示头部许可方应对碎片化市场的实际操作路径"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.", "The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.", "These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注分市场策略以预判欧洲许可成本波动", "加强医疗农业领域专利储备以应对许可谈判"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.", "Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.", "What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends."]
core_points_en: Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.
The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.
These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.
Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.
What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends.

items.title: WIPO Coordination Committee Nominates Singapore’s Daren Tang for Second Term as Director General
items.title_zh: WIPO 协调委员会提名邓鸿森连任总干事
items.title_en: WIPO Coordination Committee Nominates Daren Tang for Second Term as Director General
items.summary: The Coordination Committee of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has nominated Mr. Daren Tang from Singapore for a second six year term to the post of Director General.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）协调委员会正式提名来自新加坡的邓鸿森先生，续任下一届六年期的总干事职务。该提名标志着国际社会对其首个任期工作的认可，后续需经成员国大会正式任命。作为全球 IP 体系的核心领导者，其连任将确保 WIPO 在推动国际专利、商标及版权合作方面的政策连续性与稳定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Coordination Committee of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially nominated Mr. Daren Tang from Singapore for a second six-year term as Director General. This nomination reflects international recognition of his performance during the first term and serves as a precursor to formal appointment by the WIPO Member States Assembly. As the central leader of the global intellectual property system, Mr. Tang’s re-election is expected to ensure policy continuity and stability in promoting international cooperation regarding patents, trademarks, and copyrights. The process underscores the importance of stable leadership in maintaining the effectiveness and predictability of the multilateral IP framework for rights holders and applicants worldwide.
items.ai_insight_zh: 邓鸿森连任有利于维持多边 IP 规则稳定性，降低中国企业出海面临的制度不确定性。建议企业密切关注其在任期内对全球专利审查高速路及数字版权议题的推进，提前布局国际申请策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Leadership continuity at WIPO supports the stability of multilateral IP rules, reducing institutional uncertainty for market participants engaged in cross-border operations. Stable governance facilitates the continuation of existing international cooperation mechanisms, which is beneficial for strategic global IP portfolio management. Rights holders should closely monitor policy developments regarding the Patent Prosecution Highway and digital copyright issues during this term. Proactive adjustment of international filing strategies and attention to WIPO’s policy direction in digitalization and green technology sectors will help organizations navigate the evolving global IP landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO 协调委员会完成对现任总干事邓鸿森的第二个任期提名", "新任期时长为六年，旨在维持全球知识产权治理体系的稳定", "提名程序完成后，尚需等待成员国大会进行最终正式任命"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The WIPO Coordination Committee has completed the nomination of current Director General Daren Tang for a second term.", "The new six-year term aims to maintain stability within the global intellectual property governance system.", "Following the nomination procedure, final formal appointment remains subject to approval by the Member States Assembly."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["领导层稳定有助于延续现有国际合作机制，利好跨国 IP 布局", "企业应关注 WIPO 未来六年在全球数字化与绿色技术领域的政策导向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leadership stability supports the continuity of existing international cooperation mechanisms, benefiting multinational IP layout strategies.", "Practice note: Organizations should proactively adjust international application strategies in response to ongoing policy frameworks.", "What to monitor: Monitor WIPO's policy orientation in global digitalization and green technology sectors over the next six years."]
core_points_en: The WIPO Coordination Committee has completed the nomination of current Director General Daren Tang for a second term.
The new six-year term aims to maintain stability within the global intellectual property governance system.
Following the nomination procedure, final formal appointment remains subject to approval by the Member States Assembly.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leadership stability supports the continuity of existing international cooperation mechanisms, benefiting multinational IP layout strategies.
Practice note: Organizations should proactively adjust international application strategies in response to ongoing policy frameworks.
What to monitor: Monitor WIPO's policy orientation in global digitalization and green technology sectors over the next six years.

items.title: BREAKING: Wilus enforces Wi-Fi 6 injunction, asks Asus to remove products from Germany
items.title_zh: 突发：Wilus 执行 Wi-Fi 6 禁令，要求华硕撤出德国市场
items.title_en: Wilus Enforces Wi-Fi 6 Injunction Against Asus in Germany Following Munich Court Ruling
items.summary: Sisvel licensor moves to impose sales ban awarded by Munich Regional Court
items.ai_summary_zh: Sisvel 被许可方 Wilus 依据慕尼黑地区法院此前作出的判决，正式执行针对华硕（Asus）产品的销售禁令。该行动聚焦于 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利侵权纠纷，强制要求华硕立即从德国市场移除相关涉案产品。此举标志着德国法院在 SEP 执法层面的强硬立场，展示了专利权人利用当地司法程序迅速实现市场排除的商业策略，对涉及无线通信标准的终端制造商构成直接合规挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Wilus, a Sisvel licensor, has moved to enforce a sales ban against Asus products in Germany. The action follows an injunction awarded by the Munich Regional Court regarding alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents. This development highlights ongoing enforcement activities in the German jurisdiction concerning wireless communication technologies and the immediate commercial implications for device manufacturers facing such judicial orders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the continued significance of German courts in SEP disputes, particularly regarding Wi-Fi standards. Rights holders are actively utilizing local injunctive relief to secure market exclusivity or leverage licensing negotiations. For stakeholders, this reinforces the need to monitor enforcement trends in key European jurisdictions where patent litigation outcomes can rapidly impact product availability and supply chain stability.

items.title: L’OEB ajuste son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026
items.title_zh: 欧专局2026年4月起调整专利收费表
items.title_en: EPO Adjusts Fee Schedule Effective April 1, 2026, Raising Search and Designation Costs
items.summary: 1. L’Office européen des brevets (OEB) ajustera son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026. 2.1 Les hausses concernent notamment les taxes suivantes : La taxe de recherche européenne (de 1520 € à 1595 €) La taxe de désignation (de 685 € à 720 €) Les taxes annuelles La taxe d’examen (de (suite) L’article L’OEB ajuste son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026 est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布自2026年4月1日起调整专利收费表。主要涨幅包括：欧洲检索费由1520欧元增至1595欧元，指定费由685欧元增至720欧元。此外，年费及审查费也将相应上调。此举旨在反映运营成本变化，申请人需提前规划预算以应对即将到来的费用增长。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has announced adjustments to its official fee schedule, which will take effect on April 1, 2026. This update reflects necessary changes to cover operational costs and ensures the sustainable funding of the patent granting process. Key increases include the European search fee, rising from EUR 1,520 to EUR 1,595, and the designation fee, increasing from EUR 685 to EUR 720. Additionally, annual renewal fees and examination fees are subject to corresponding upward adjustments. These changes impact all applicants filing or maintaining patents before the EPO. Rights holders should review their current portfolios and financial planning to accommodate these higher costs, ensuring that budget allocations for upcoming fiscal periods account for the revised fee structure across various procedural stages.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重新评估在欧专利组合维持策略，针对非核心专利考虑提前放弃以控制成本。建议财务部门预留预算应对2026年的费用上涨，并优化申请流程以降低检索与审查阶段的支出压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should proactively reassess their European patent portfolios in light of the impending fee increases. Evaluating the commercial value of each asset allows for strategic decisions, such as abandoning non-core or low-value patents to control maintenance costs. Finance and IP teams must align budgets for 2026 to accommodate higher search, examination, and renewal fees. Furthermore, optimizing application strategies during the search and examination phases can help mitigate expenditure pressures. Early planning ensures that cost management measures are integrated into broader IP lifecycle strategies without disrupting critical protection objectives.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧专局将于2026年4月1日正式实施新的专利收费表", "欧洲检索费与指定费均出现明显上涨，具体金额已公布", "年费及审查费等其他常规费用也将同步进行价格调整"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO will implement a new fee schedule effective April 1, 2026, impacting various procedural costs for patent applicants and rights holders.", "Specific fee increases include the European search fee rising to EUR 1,595 and the designation fee increasing to EUR 720.", "Annual renewal fees and examination fees will also be adjusted upwards, requiring updated budget planning for ongoing patent maintenance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新评估在欧专利组合，剔除低价值专利以控制成本", "预留预算应对2026年费用上涨，优化申请流程降低支出"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess European patent portfolios to identify and abandon low-value assets, thereby controlling maintenance costs amidst rising fees.", "Practice note: Reserve adequate budget for 2026 to cover increased search, designation, examination, and annual renewal fees.", "What to monitor: Optimize application workflows to reduce expenditure pressures during search and examination stages under the new fee regime."]
core_points_en: The EPO will implement a new fee schedule effective April 1, 2026, impacting various procedural costs for patent applicants and rights holders.
Specific fee increases include the European search fee rising to EUR 1,595 and the designation fee increasing to EUR 720.
Annual renewal fees and examination fees will also be adjusted upwards, requiring updated budget planning for ongoing patent maintenance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess European patent portfolios to identify and abandon low-value assets, thereby controlling maintenance costs amidst rising fees.
Practice note: Reserve adequate budget for 2026 to cover increased search, designation, examination, and annual renewal fees.
What to monitor: Optimize application workflows to reduce expenditure pressures during search and examination stages under the new fee regime.

items.title: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.title_zh: EUIPO召开手工艺和工业地理标志咨询委员会首次全会
items.title_en: EUIPO Convenes First Plenary of Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.summary: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）召开了手工艺和工业地理标志（CIGIs）咨询委员会的首次全体会议，标志着欧盟保护手工艺和工业地理标志新框架实施的重要里程碑。会议选举了主席和副主席，讨论了委员会的任务和工作方法，并初步讨论了相关指南，以确保新法规在欧盟范围内的高效、一致应用。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office held the inaugural plenary meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications. This event marks a significant milestone in implementing the new EU framework for protecting these specific indications. The Board elected its leadership and discussed its mandate, working methods, and national implementation progress. Members also initiated discussions on guidelines to ensure consistent application of the Regulation across the Union. The Board will now operate fully to support EUIPO’s new competences, providing expert opinions on quality criteria, reputation, and genericness while facilitating cooperation among Member States and the Commission.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The Advisory Board’s activation provides a critical expert layer for assessing craft and industrial GI applications. Rights holders should note the Board’s role in evaluating quality criteria and reputation, which may influence examination outcomes. Monitoring forthcoming guidelines and Board opinions will be essential for understanding evidentiary standards and ensuring consistent procedural expectations across Member States during the transition to the new regulatory framework.

items.title: Mannheim on penalties and the limits of enforcing UPC decisions in non UPC territory
items.title_zh: 曼海姆分院论处罚及UPC判决在非UPC领土的执行限制
items.title_en: Mannheim Division Confirms Penalties and Limits UPC Enforcement in Non-Member States
items.summary: Unified Patent Court – Mannheim Local Division, 30 January 2026 (UPC_CFI_365/2023)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院曼海姆地方分院在2026年1月30日的裁定中，确认对未履行信息披露及销毁义务的行为处以巨额罚款。裁定明确区分了UPC的国际管辖权与域外执行力，指出在非UPC成员国境内，强制执行UPC命令（特别是针对外国市场行为的处罚）通常需经当地法院承认。但在UPC境内制造的侵权产品，即便涉及境外销售，仍可责令披露信息以计算境内损害赔偿。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Mannheim Local Division confirmed substantial penalty orders for continued non-compliance with information, recall, and destruction obligations during a second enforcement round. The judgment clarifies that the judge-rapporteur may independently issue such penalties under Rule 354.4 of the Rules of Procedure. Crucially, the Court distinguished between international jurisdiction on the merits and enforceability abroad. It held that coercive enforcement of UPC decisions in non-UPC territories requires prior recognition by local courts, meaning cross-border injunctions do not automatically trigger penalty exposure for foreign-market conduct. However, the Court affirmed that measures within UPC territory remain enforceable even if factually linked to overseas activities, including ordering disclosure of foreign sales data to quantify domestic infringement damages.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定厘清了UPC判决跨境执行的边界，表明其长臂管辖并非自动延伸至非成员国。企业需注意，虽境外行为难直接处罚，但境内关联活动仍可能触发信息披露义务。实务中应关注各国对UPC判决的承认机制，以及利用境内披露程序获取全球侵权证据的策略可行性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and defendants must recognize that while UPC penalties do not automatically extend to non-contracting states, the Court retains significant leverage over global supply chains. Foreign sales data can be compelled for damage quantification, creating indirect financial exposure. Parties should establish rapid response protocols for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can act swiftly without full panel review. Additionally, assessing the legal isolation of non-EU markets is critical. Companies should prepare robust data compliance frameworks to manage potential disclosure orders regarding international operations, ensuring that global commercial activities do not inadvertently exacerbate liability within the UPC system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法官报告员有权依据程序规则第354.4条签发处罚令，无需合议庭全体参与", "UPC判决在缔约国直接执行，但在非UPC国家强制执行需经当地法院承认", "针对境内制造但境外销售的行为，可责令披露信息以量化境内侵权损害"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The judge-rapporteur holds authority to independently issue penalty orders for procedural violations under the Rules of Procedure.", "Cross-border UPC injunctions lack automatic coercive force in non-member states, requiring prior local court recognition for enforcement.", "Courts may order disclosure of overseas sales data within UPC territory to accurately calculate damages for domestic infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC处罚令可由法官报告员单独签发，挑战前需先经合议庭复核", "跨境禁令的处罚效力受限于非UPC国家的司法承认，非自动生效", "需持续观察非UPC国家对UPC判决的承认实践及后续上诉法院态度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Cross-border injunctions require local recognition for enforcement outside UPC states, limiting immediate penalty risks in non-member jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can issue orders independently under current procedural rules.", "What to monitor: Potential orders disclosing foreign sales data to quantify domestic damages, necessitating prepared global supply chain data compliance strategies."]
core_points_en: The judge-rapporteur holds authority to independently issue penalty orders for procedural violations under the Rules of Procedure.
Cross-border UPC injunctions lack automatic coercive force in non-member states, requiring prior local court recognition for enforcement.
Courts may order disclosure of overseas sales data within UPC territory to accurately calculate damages for domestic infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Cross-border injunctions require local recognition for enforcement outside UPC states, limiting immediate penalty risks in non-member jurisdictions.
Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can issue orders independently under current procedural rules.
What to monitor: Potential orders disclosing foreign sales data to quantify domestic damages, necessitating prepared global supply chain data compliance strategies.
