items.title: Prior use infringement defence at the LD Munich: the UPC puts its German hat on (UPC_CFI_114/2024; UPC_CFI_448/2024)
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院：UPC对先用权抗辩适用德国法
items.title_en: UPC Munich Local Division Applies German Prior Use Defence in Heraeus v Vibrantz
items.summary: Court docket: LD
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC慕尼黑地方法院在Heraeus诉Vibrantz案中裁定，先用权抗辩非统一概念，需依《统一专利法院协定》第28条援引成员国国内法。法院适用德国法认定被告享有先用权，虽构成字面侵权但因抗辩成立而在德国不可执行。因原告未证明其他缔约国存在侵权行为，故未发现整体侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Munich Local Division ruled that the prior use defence under Article 28 UPCA is not a unitary concept but relies on national law. In Heraeus v Vibrantz, the court applied German law to uphold the defence, preventing enforcement despite finding literal infringement. The decision confirms that prior use rights are determined territorially by the specific Contracting Member State’s laws, requiring defendants to prove pre-priority date use or preparations within that jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确认UPC审理先用权抗辩时具有“属地性”，需依赖各国国内法界定权利范围。企业应关注在特定成员国保留优先日前使用证据的重要性。同时，UPC对直接侵权认定的宽泛倾向与抗辩的严格属地适用形成对比，需分别评估风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies that prior use defences at the UPC remain strictly territorial. Rights holders must anticipate country-specific evidentiary hurdles, while defendants should preserve detailed records of pre-priority activities in each relevant jurisdiction to support potential national defences.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC裁定先用权抗辩依协定第28条逐国适用国内法，非统一概念", "法院适用德国法，被告证明优先日前已在德制造销售，先用权抗辩成立", "因原告未举证法意等国侵权，且德国抗辩成立，故未发现可执行侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 28 UPCA functions as a referral rule, importing national prior use definitions rather than creating a unified European standard.", "The court applied German legal principles, requiring proof of use or necessary preparations in Germany before the patent priority date.", "Literal infringement was established, but enforcement was barred in Germany due to the successful prior use defence and lack of evidence elsewhere."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC将先用权视为通过国内法引入的属地抗辩，而非统一的欧洲专利概念", "被告无需在优先日前知晓具体技术参数，只要产品固有满足且实现技术效果即可", "原告需在所有主张侵权的缔约国分别提供实质性侵权证据，否则可能败诉"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prior use defences are assessed country-by-country; success in one state does not guarantee protection across all UPC territories.", "Practice note: Defendants must provide robust evidence of pre-priority date use or preparations specifically within the jurisdiction where the defence is invoked.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions may reveal divergent interpretations of prior use conditions when applying different national laws under Article 28 UPCA."]
core_points_en: Article 28 UPCA functions as a referral rule, importing national prior use definitions rather than creating a unified European standard.
The court applied German legal principles, requiring proof of use or necessary preparations in Germany before the patent priority date.
Literal infringement was established, but enforcement was barred in Germany due to the successful prior use defence and lack of evidence elsewhere.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prior use defences are assessed country-by-country; success in one state does not guarantee protection across all UPC territories.
Practice note: Defendants must provide robust evidence of pre-priority date use or preparations specifically within the jurisdiction where the defence is invoked.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions may reveal divergent interpretations of prior use conditions when applying different national laws under Article 28 UPCA.

items.title: Samsung and ZTE deliver final UK FRAND arguments as Chongqing decision looms
items.title_zh: 三星与中兴提交英国 FRAND 终辩，重庆判决在即
items.title_en: Samsung and ZTE Conclude UK FRAND Final Arguments as Chongqing Judgment Approaches
items.summary: The relative timings of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments could play an important role in how dispute is resolved, while UPC and German hearings are also pending
items.ai_summary_zh: 三星与中兴已在英国法院完成 FRAND 费率最终辩论，案件走向将受中英两国费率判决时序影响。与此同时，统一专利法院（UPC）及德国相关听证会亦 pending，多国司法程序交织可能重塑全球 SEP 许可格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Samsung and ZTE have completed their final oral arguments regarding FRAND royalty rates in the English High Court. The ultimate resolution of this dispute is heavily influenced by the relative timing of the upcoming judgments from both the English courts and the Chongqing Intermediate People's Court in China. Concurrently, parallel proceedings are advancing at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and within the German judicial system. These overlapping international legal processes create a complex multi-jurisdictional landscape. The interplay between these distinct forums suggests that the sequence of rate-setting decisions will significantly shape the global standard-essential patent licensing framework, potentially establishing critical precedents for future cross-border intellectual property disputes involving major technology firms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临多法域诉讼风险，需统筹中英德及 UPC 策略。建议密切跟踪重庆判决对全球费率锚定效应，提前布局跨国应诉与许可谈判方案。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and implementers must navigate an increasingly fragmented global SEP enforcement landscape where multiple jurisdictions compete for influence over global licensing terms. The strategic coordination of litigation efforts across the UK, China, Germany, and the UPC is essential to optimize outcomes. Stakeholders should closely monitor how the Chongqing court's decision interacts with Western rulings, as this may establish new benchmarks for global royalty anchors. Proactive management of parallel proceedings allows parties to leverage favorable timings and mitigate risks associated with conflicting injunctions or rate determinations across different legal systems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英中两国费率判决时序将决定纠纷解决路径", "UPC 与德国听证会同步进行增加变量", "三星与中兴已完成英国阶段最终口头辩论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The chronological sequence of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments will critically determine the strategic path for resolving the ongoing dispute.", "Simultaneous hearings at the Unified Patent Court and in Germany introduce additional procedural variables to the already complex litigation environment.", "Samsung and ZTE have successfully concluded the final stage of oral arguments within the United Kingdom jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注中英判决时序对全球许可定价影响", "统筹 UPC 与成员国诉讼以优化应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the interaction between English and Chinese rate-setting judgments to assess impacts on global licensing pricing strategies.", "Practice note: Coordinate litigation tactics across UPC, German, and national courts to optimize defense and negotiation positions.", "What to monitor: Track the Chongqing judgment's potential effect as a global rate anchor and its influence on subsequent international settlements."]
core_points_en: The chronological sequence of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments will critically determine the strategic path for resolving the ongoing dispute.
Simultaneous hearings at the Unified Patent Court and in Germany introduce additional procedural variables to the already complex litigation environment.
Samsung and ZTE have successfully concluded the final stage of oral arguments within the United Kingdom jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the interaction between English and Chinese rate-setting judgments to assess impacts on global licensing pricing strategies.
Practice note: Coordinate litigation tactics across UPC, German, and national courts to optimize defense and negotiation positions.
What to monitor: Track the Chongqing judgment's potential effect as a global rate anchor and its influence on subsequent international settlements.

items.title: EPO PACE reform: How to assess the impact on your patent strategy
items.title_zh: EPO PACE改革：如何评估对专利策略的影响
items.title_en: EPO PACE Reform: Impact of Limiting Accelerated Prosecution to Examination Phase
items.summary: As of 1 February 2026, new rules came into effect for patent applicants using the EPO PACE programme. Discover what has changed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2026年2月1日起，欧洲专利局（EPO）PACE计划新规生效。鉴于检索报告发布时间已缩短且可预测，EPO决定将PACE加速范围限制在审查阶段。申请人不再能通过PACE请求加速检索，官方表格EPO 1005仅用于请求加速审查。此举旨在提升服务质量与可预测性。
items.ai_summary_en: Effective 1 February 2026, the European Patent Office (EPO) has revised the rules governing the Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications (PACE). Citing significant improvements in processing efficiency, the EPO determined that search reports are now issued within short, predictable timeframes. Consequently, the PACE programme is restricted exclusively to the substantive examination phase. Applicants can no longer submit separate requests to accelerate the European search phase using form EPO 1005. This strategic adjustment aligns with the EPO’s Strategic Plan 2028, aiming to enhance service quality and predictability. Rights holders must adapt their filing strategies to focus acceleration efforts solely on examination, leveraging faster official actions to potentially secure grants more rapidly under the updated procedural framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此次调整反映EPO检索效率提升，程序重心转向审查加速。实务中需调整申请策略，不再依赖PACE加速检索环节。应关注新规下审查阶段加速的具体时效表现，以及EPO 2028战略计划后续服务优化措施对整体授权周期的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: With the EPO restricting PACE to the examination phase, rights holders should adjust their prosecution strategies to avoid unnecessary procedural steps during the search stage. Since search reports are now issued predictably, resources previously allocated to accelerating searches can be redirected toward managing the examination phase efficiently. Applicants should prioritize rapid responses to examination reports to fully leverage the accelerated timeline. Monitoring the effective date of 1 February 2026 is critical to ensure compliance with the new form requirements and prevent invalid submissions that could delay proceedings or incur unnecessary costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年2月1日起，EPO PACE计划新规生效，加速范围仅限审查阶段", "因检索时间已缩短，申请人不再可通过PACE请求加速欧洲专利检索程序", "官方表格EPO 1005更新，仅允许提交加速审查请求，不再支持加速检索"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["As of 1 February 2026, the PACE programme no longer supports separate requests to accelerate the European search phase for patent applications.", "Due to optimized search timelines, the EPO restricts accelerated prosecution strictly to the substantive examination phase of the application process.", "Updated official forms only permit requests for accelerated examination, aiming to shorten the overall timeline for granting European patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["PACE功能缩减为仅加速审查，检索阶段不再适用加速请求", "需适应新版EPO 1005表格功能变化，调整案件管理流程", "观察EPO 2028战略计划下审查阶段加速的实际处理时效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Discontinue separate PACE requests for the search phase to prevent resource waste and procedural confusion under the new EPO rules.", "Practice note: Utilize the accelerated examination mechanism effectively by preparing rapid responses to office actions to shorten the overall grant timeline.", "What to monitor: Ensure all PACE requests filed after 1 February 2026 strictly target the examination phase using the updated official forms."]
core_points_en: As of 1 February 2026, the PACE programme no longer supports separate requests to accelerate the European search phase for patent applications.
Due to optimized search timelines, the EPO restricts accelerated prosecution strictly to the substantive examination phase of the application process.
Updated official forms only permit requests for accelerated examination, aiming to shorten the overall timeline for granting European patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Discontinue separate PACE requests for the search phase to prevent resource waste and procedural confusion under the new EPO rules.
Practice note: Utilize the accelerated examination mechanism effectively by preparing rapid responses to office actions to shorten the overall grant timeline.
What to monitor: Ensure all PACE requests filed after 1 February 2026 strictly target the examination phase using the updated official forms.

items.title: DesignView becomes the world’s largest design image search engine
items.title_zh: DesignView成为全球最大外观设计图像搜索引擎
items.title_en: DesignView Becomes World's Largest Industrial Design Image Search Engine
items.summary: DesignView becomes the world’s largest design image search engine As of 9 February 2026, the DesignView search functionality for industrial design images has been implemented for all connected intellectual property (IP) offices, making it the largest search engine for design images in the world. Wit
items.ai_summary_zh: 截至2026年2月9日，随着所有连接的知识产权局完成实施，DesignView的工业设计图像搜索功能已全面上线。此举使其正式成为全球规模最大的外观设计图像搜索引擎，提升了全球外观设计数据的可检索性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office announced that as of February 9, 2026, the DesignView platform has fully implemented image search functionality for industrial designs across all connected intellectual property offices. This milestone establishes DesignView as the largest global search engine for design images. By integrating comprehensive data resources from participating jurisdictions, the platform significantly enhances the efficiency and scope of design searches. This development provides robust support for applicants and examiners worldwide, facilitating more thorough prior art checks and improving the overall quality of design registration processes through unified access to a vast, consolidated database of industrial design representations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注DesignView数据库覆盖范围的扩大对外观设计新颖性检索及侵权比对实务的影响。作为全球最大图像库，其数据完整性将直接影响在先设计抗辩的证据效力，建议持续监测其更新机制。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and legal practitioners should leverage the expanded DesignView capabilities to conduct comprehensive prior design searches. Utilizing this extensive global repository helps mitigate infringement risks and improves the likelihood of successful design registrations by ensuring thorough clearance checks. Teams are advised to update their existing search workflows to align with these new image-based search standards. Adapting to this larger, integrated database ensures better compliance for products entering international markets and supports higher quality in securing design rights across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["截至2026年2月9日，DesignView图像搜索功能已在所有连接局实施", "DesignView因此成为全球最大的工业设计图像搜索引擎"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["DesignView achieved full image search coverage across all connected IP offices on February 9, 2026.", "The platform is now recognized as the largest industrial design image search engine globally.", "The upgrade aims to optimize global design search efficiency and enhance data integration capabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["全球最大图像库地位确立，可能改变外观设计在先设计检索的行业标准", "需观察各连接局数据接入的质量与时效，以评估检索结果的可靠性", "后续应关注EUIPO是否针对图像搜索算法优化发布进一步的技术指南"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage enhanced image search to strengthen prior design clearance and reduce infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Adjust search workflows to utilize the world's largest design library for improved rights acquisition quality.", "What to monitor: Track updates in cross-border design data integration and evolving image search standards."]

The platform is now recognized as the largest industrial design image search engine globally.
The upgrade aims to optimize global design search efficiency and enhance data integration capabilities.

items.title: Standard essential patents (SEPs): a new board for the global geostrategic game
items.title_zh: 标准必要专利：全球地缘战略博弈的新棋盘
items.title_en: Standard Essential Patents: A New Board for the Global Geostrategic Game
items.summary: Standard essential patents (SEPs) are patents that protect technologies that are indispensable for implementing a technical standard.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文剖析标准必要专利（SEP）作为数字经济基石的地缘战略意义，指出其已从电信业扩展至汽车等互联领域。文章对比了美、亚、欧三地动态：美国成立工作组并推出加速计划以扶持创新；日本法院作出首例 SEP 禁令；欧盟则面临平衡 FRAND 承诺与防止市场滥用的监管挑战，凸显全球规则博弈加剧。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the geostrategic significance of Standard Essential Patents (SEPs) as foundational elements of the digital economy, highlighting their expansion beyond telecommunications into connected sectors such as automotive and industrial manufacturing. The article contrasts recent regulatory and judicial developments across three key jurisdictions. In the United States, authorities have established a dedicated working group and launched the SPARK pilot program to support innovators against perceived ecosystem hostility. Japanese courts have issued their first SEP injunctions, establishing strict negotiation frameworks. Meanwhile, the European Union faces complex regulatory challenges in balancing FRAND commitments with the prevention of market abuse. These diverging approaches underscore an intensifying global contest over intellectual property rules, supply chain control, and the future governance of interoperable technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及电子企业出海需警惕欧美日 SEP 规则分化。建议提前构建 FRAND 合规谈判流程，避免被认定为恶意拖延；同时关注美国加速审查红利，主动参与标准制定以获取战略优势。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating globally must navigate the increasing divergence in SEP enforcement regimes across Europe, the US, and Japan. Rights holders and implementers should proactively establish robust FRAND compliance frameworks to mitigate risks of being characterized as unwilling licensees or hold-out actors. Companies should strategically leverage US acceleration incentives by actively contributing to standard-setting organizations. Simultaneously, firms must prepare for stricter judicial scrutiny regarding negotiation conduct, particularly in jurisdictions like Japan where injunctions are becoming more accessible. Aligning internal data disclosure protocols and negotiation strategies with evolving local precedents is essential for maintaining operational continuity and competitive advantage in interconnected markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["SEP 成为连接汽车与工业的关键，引发全球监管与诉讼激增", "美国通过设立工作组及 SPARK 计划，旨在扭转对创新者 hostile 的生态", "日本法院确立两阶段谈判机制，因实施者不配合而发出销售禁令", "欧盟需在防止权利人劫持与实施者拖延之间寻找新的监管平衡点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SEPs have become critical connectors for automotive and industrial sectors, triggering a significant surge in global regulatory actions and litigation disputes.", "The USPTO created a new working group and the SPARK initiative to reverse an ecosystem perceived as hostile toward innovators through accelerated review incentives.", "Japanese courts established a two-stage negotiation mechanism, issuing sales bans against implementers deemed uncooperative during licensing discussions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕日本式禁令风险，完善诉讼应对与数据披露合规机制", "利用美国 SPARK 计划激励，加大在标准组织中的技术贡献力度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the risk of injunctions similar to Japanese precedents and strengthen litigation response capabilities alongside data disclosure compliance mechanisms.", "Practice note: Leverage US SPARK program incentives to increase technical contributions within standard-setting organizations for strategic positioning.", "What to monitor: Diverging SEP rules in Europe, the US, and Japan, focusing on FRAND negotiation standards and potential hold-out determinations."]
core_points_en: SEPs have become critical connectors for automotive and industrial sectors, triggering a significant surge in global regulatory actions and litigation disputes.
The USPTO created a new working group and the SPARK initiative to reverse an ecosystem perceived as hostile toward innovators through accelerated review incentives.
Japanese courts established a two-stage negotiation mechanism, issuing sales bans against implementers deemed uncooperative during licensing discussions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the risk of injunctions similar to Japanese precedents and strengthen litigation response capabilities alongside data disclosure compliance mechanisms.
Practice note: Leverage US SPARK program incentives to increase technical contributions within standard-setting organizations for strategic positioning.
What to monitor: Diverging SEP rules in Europe, the US, and Japan, focusing on FRAND negotiation standards and potential hold-out determinations.

items.title: UPC Munich Local Division rejects GXD-Bio’s claim against Myriad; EP 3 346 403 revoked
items.title_zh: UPC慕尼黑分院驳回GXD-Bio诉请并撤销涉案专利
items.title_en: UPC Munich Dismisses GXD-Bio Claim and Revokes Myriad Patent for Added Matter
items.summary: Summary/Background The Local Division (LD) Munich dismissed GDX-Bio’s infringement action against My
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）慕尼黑地方法院驳回了GXD-Bio对Myriad的侵权诉讼，并以增加新事项为由撤销欧洲专利EP 3 346 403。法院认定，原权利要求未披露靶基因位于FFPE乳腺癌组织样本中，且实验实施例7仅显示参考基因在一般FFPE样本中的稳定性，未明确归属于该特定发明。辅助请求因同样理由被拒。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Munich Local Division dismissed GXD-Bio’s infringement action against Myriad Genetics and revoked European Patent EP 3 346 403. The court found the patent invalid due to added matter, specifically regarding claims directed at quantifying gene expression in formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) breast cancer tissue using the OAZ1 reference gene. The division applied the established 'gold standard' for assessing added matter, determining that the original application did not directly and unambiguously disclose this specific tissue type as part of the invention. Auxiliary requests filed by GXD-Bio were similarly refused. The court reasoned that while experimental data showed general applicability to FFPE samples, it did not establish a specific link to breast cancer tissue required to support the amended claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了UPC在评估“增加新事项”时采用严格标准，强调特征必须“直接且明确”地属于发明。实务中需注意，仅披露实施例不足以支持将特定应用场景（如特定组织类型）加入权利要求，除非原申请明确将其界定为发明核心。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the strict application of the added matter prohibition under the UPC framework. Rights holders must ensure that specific technical features or application scenarios claimed in litigation are explicitly supported by the original disclosure, rather than inferred from general examples. When facing revocation actions, parties should proactively assess the sufficiency of disclosure for any proposed amendments. Relying on broad experimental data to narrow claims to specific embodiments carries significant risk if the original text does not clearly associate those embodiments with the core inventive concept.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC慕尼黑分院以增加新事项为由，撤销涉及FFPE乳腺癌组织基因表达量化的欧洲专利EP…", "法院认定原申请旨在寻找通用参考基因，未将靶基因限定于FFPE乳腺癌样本作为发明的一部分…", "GXD-Bio基于实验实施例7提出的修改请求被拒，因该实施例未提供特定FFPE组织中的…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Munich revoked EP 3 346 403 for added matter and dismissed the associated infringement claim against Myriad.", "The court applied the gold standard, requiring features to be directly and unambiguously disclosed in the original application.", "General experimental data on FFPE stability was insufficient to prove specific breast cancer tissue belonged to the invention."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC在新增事项审查中适用严格标准，要求特征必须直接且无歧义地归属于发明整体", "通用技术问题的背景披露可能限制后续将特定应用场景（如特定组织）纳入权利要求的范围", "需持续观察UPC对实验数据与权利要求支持之间关联性的具体认定逻辑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure specific claim limitations have explicit basis in the original specification to avoid added matter objections during UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: Evaluate the robustness of auxiliary requests against the gold standard before filing, focusing on direct textual support for narrowed scopes.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions interpret the threshold for linking general experimental examples to specific claimed embodiments in future cases."]
core_points_en: UPC Munich revoked EP 3 346 403 for added matter and dismissed the associated infringement claim against Myriad.
The court applied the gold standard, requiring features to be directly and unambiguously disclosed in the original application.
General experimental data on FFPE stability was insufficient to prove specific breast cancer tissue belonged to the invention.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure specific claim limitations have explicit basis in the original specification to avoid added matter objections during UPC proceedings.
Practice note: Evaluate the robustness of auxiliary requests against the gold standard before filing, focusing on direct textual support for narrowed scopes.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions interpret the threshold for linking general experimental examples to specific claimed embodiments in future cases.

items.title: InterDigital posts record smartphone revenue in 2025
items.title_zh: InterDigital 获德巴禁令后营收创新高
items.title_en: InterDigital Reports Record Smartphone Revenue Following German Injunction Against Disney
items.summary: The company sees video streaming licensing as a major growth driver, and CEO Liren Chen “could not be happier” after winning German and Brazilian injunctions against Disney
items.ai_summary_zh: InterDigital 宣布 2025 年智能手机许可收入创纪录，视频流媒体授权成为新增长引擎。CEO 陈立人表示，公司在德国和巴西成功获得针对迪士尼的禁令后倍感振奋。该动态凸显了标准必要专利持有人在欧洲主要法域通过诉讼获取禁令救济、进而推动全球许可谈判的战略有效性。
items.ai_summary_en: InterDigital has announced record smartphone licensing revenue for 2025, identifying video streaming licensing as a primary driver of future business growth. The company’s CEO expressed strong satisfaction after securing injunctions against Disney in both Germany and Brazil. This development underscores the strategic effectiveness of standard essential patent holders leveraging litigation to obtain injunctive relief in key European jurisdictions. By successfully enforcing these rights, rights holders are significantly strengthening their position in global licensing negotiations. The outcome highlights how judicial victories in major markets like Germany can directly influence commercial outcomes and accelerate settlement discussions with implementers across various technology sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 禁令风险，尤其在流媒体等新兴领域。建议提前评估产品侵权风险，制定 FRAND 抗辩策略，并关注德国法院最新裁判倾向，避免遭遇类似迪士尼的禁令打击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face increasing risks associated with SEP injunctions, particularly within emerging sectors such as video streaming. Companies should proactively assess potential infringement liabilities and develop robust FRAND defense strategies to mitigate exposure. It is critical to closely monitor recent judicial trends in German courts, which have demonstrated a willingness to grant injunctive relief that can disrupt supply chains. Understanding these dynamics helps implementers avoid severe legal setbacks similar to those experienced by Disney, ensuring better preparedness for complex global licensing disputes.

items.title: Avanci Video signs first licensee, announces royalty rates
items.title_zh: Avanci Video 签约首位被许可人并公布费率
items.title_en: Avanci Video Signs First Licensee and Announces Standard Essential Patent Royalty Rates
items.summary: Newly unveiled royalty discounts provide powerful incentives for streamers to sign up early
items.ai_summary_zh: Avanci Video 专利池正式签署首位被许可人，并同步公布其标准必要专利许可费率。该方案特别推出早期签约折扣机制，旨在通过经济激励促使流媒体服务商尽快达成许可协议。此举标志着视频编码技术 SEP 许可模式在欧洲市场的进一步落地与商业化推进。
items.ai_summary_en: The Avanci Video patent pool has officially signed its first licensee, marking a significant commercial milestone in the licensing of video coding standard essential patents. Concurrently, the organization announced its standardized royalty rates, establishing a transparent pricing benchmark for the industry. A key feature of this new framework is the introduction of early-signing discounts, designed to provide strong economic incentives for streaming service providers to conclude licensing agreements promptly. This development signifies the further implementation and commercial advancement of video SEP licensing models within the European market. By securing an initial agreement and clarifying cost structures, Avanci aims to streamline negotiations and encourage broader adoption among technology companies operating in the digital media sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国流媒体及终端企业应评估 Avanci 新费率对欧业务成本影响。建议密切关注早期优惠窗口期，结合 FRAND 原则审慎谈判，避免因延迟签约导致合规风险或成本上升，优化欧洲市场知识产权布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess the potential impact of Avanci’s new fee structure on operational costs for businesses exporting to or operating in Europe. Companies are encouraged to engage in prudent negotiations aligned with FRAND principles, ensuring compliance while optimizing their intellectual property strategies. Delaying participation may result in increased financial burdens and heightened regulatory risks. Therefore, evaluating the cost implications and leveraging available incentives can help rights holders and implementers achieve a more efficient and compliant IP landscape in the European market.

items.title: RELIEF - CJEU confirms EUIPO practice contrary to AG Cápeta opinion in APE TEES
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院确认EUIPO关于在先权利有效性的实践
items.title_en: CJEU Confirms EUIPO Practice: Earlier Rights Must Remain Valid Until Final Decision
items.summary: We reported on Advocate General (AG) Cápeta’s opinion in the APE TEES case (C-337/22 P, aka Nowhere, the name of the applicant before the GC) here expressing the hope – in fact expectation – that the CJEU would not follow that opinion. We are very relieved to report that this expectation has not been disappointed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在APE TEES案中判决，确认EUIPO实践：在商标异议或无效程序中，在先权利必须在EUIPO作出实体决定时依然有效。若在先权利在此期间失效（如因脱欧、未使用撤销等），则不再构成冲突。该判决推翻了总检察长意见，并明确适用于所有导致权利失效的情形，对欧盟成员国具有约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has ruled in APE TEES that an earlier trademark right must remain valid and enforceable until the EUIPO issues its final decision on opposition or invalidity. This judgment confirms established EUIPO practice, rejecting Advocate General Cápeta’s contrary opinion. The Court held that if an earlier right ceases to exist during proceedings, whether due to Brexit, non-use cancellation, surrender, or expiry, the conflict between rights disappears. Consequently, there is no legitimate interest for the former rights holder to block the later mark. This principle applies broadly to any loss of validity occurring between the application date and the final substantive decision.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides legal certainty by prioritizing commercial logic over formalism in EU trademark proceedings. It clarifies that the existence of a conflicting earlier right is a continuing requirement throughout the administrative process. Rights holders and opponents must now ensure their cited grounds remain active until the final board decision. For practitioners, this reinforces the need for dynamic portfolio management during litigation, as procedural delays could inadvertently extinguish the basis for an opposition if the earlier mark lapses or is cancelled before the final ruling.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院确认在先权利须在EUIPO作出实体决定时仍有效，否则不构成冲突", "判决适用范围广，涵盖因脱欧、未使用撤销、放弃或到期等原因导致的权利失效", "该判决对欧盟成员国知识产权局具有约束力，但英国知识产权局持相反立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable at the time of the final EUIPO decision.", "Loss of validity during proceedings eliminates the conflict, preventing refusal or invalidity of later marks.", "The ruling applies broadly to expiry, surrender, non-use cancellation, and post-Brexit status changes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法院确立以实体决定日为基准的权利有效性审查标准，强化了商业逻辑优于形式主义的导向", "实务中需密切关注异议或无效程序期间在先商标的法律状态，避免因权利中途失效而误判案件走向", "需持续观察英国知识产权局与欧盟实践的分歧对英欧跨境商标争议策略的具体影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Opponents relying on earlier rights must verify that those rights remain valid and enforceable throughout the entire duration of opposition or invalidity proceed…", "Practice note: The CJEU confirms that both the application date and the final decision date are critical; if the earlier right lapses in between, the opposition fails regardless o…", "What to monitor: Observe how this EU standard interacts with divergent national practices, such as the UKIPO approach, particularly in cross-border disputes involving pre-Brexit r…"]

Loss of validity during proceedings eliminates the conflict, preventing refusal or invalidity of later marks.
The ruling applies broadly to expiry, surrender, non-use cancellation, and post-Brexit status changes.

items.title: European SEP developments spur calls to add UK, Germany and UPC to US patent watch list
items.title_zh: 欧洲 SEP 动态引发将英德及 UPC 列入美国专利观察名单呼声
items.title_en: Stakeholders Urge USTR to Add UK, Germany, and UPC to Patent Watch List Amid SEP Disputes
items.summary: As standard essential patent policy debates escalate, some stakeholders advocate for adding European jurisdictions on the USTR watch list
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着标准必要专利（SEP）政策辩论升级，部分利益相关方呼吁美国贸易代表办公室（USTR）将英国、德国及统一专利法院（UPC）列入特别 301 报告观察名单。此举旨在施压欧洲司法辖区调整 FRAND 许可规则，反映跨大西洋在 SEP 执法与定价机制上的深层分歧，可能重塑全球专利许可格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Amid escalating debates over standard essential patent (SEP) policy, certain stakeholders are advocating for the inclusion of specific European jurisdictions on the United States Trade Representative's Special 301 Report watch list. This initiative specifically targets the United Kingdom, Germany, and the Unified Patent Court. The primary objective is to exert diplomatic pressure on these European judicial bodies to adjust their approaches to FRAND licensing rules. This development highlights deep-seated transatlantic divergences regarding SEP enforcement mechanisms and pricing determinations. Such geopolitical friction threatens to reshape the global landscape of patent licensing, creating a more complex regulatory environment for rights holders and implementers alike as they navigate conflicting legal standards across major markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更复杂的地缘政治化专利环境。需警惕美欧规则冲突导致的合规风险，建议提前评估英德诉讼策略，建立多元化 FRAND 抗辩预案，避免成为大国博弈牺牲品。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating across the Atlantic face an increasingly complex and geopolitically charged patent environment. The growing divergence between US and European regulatory stances on SEPs introduces substantial compliance risks that require proactive management. Companies should carefully evaluate litigation strategies within key jurisdictions like the UK and Germany, anticipating potential shifts in judicial interpretation. Establishing diversified FRAND defense frameworks is essential to mitigate exposure to cross-border enforcement actions. Stakeholders must remain agile, ensuring their licensing negotiations and legal postures are robust enough to withstand the pressures of this evolving international IP landscape without becoming collateral damage in broader trade disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利益方呼吁 USTR 将英、德及 UPC 纳入专利观察名单", "争议焦点在于欧洲 SEP 禁令救济与 FRAND 费率裁定机制", "此举标志欧美在标准必要专利政策协调上出现显著裂痕"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Stakeholders formally request the USTR to include the UK, Germany, and the UPC on the patent watch list.", "The central dispute focuses on European courts' handling of SEP injunctions and FRAND rate-setting methodologies.", "This move signals a significant rift in US-EU coordination regarding standard essential patent policies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注美欧政策分歧对在英德诉讼策略的潜在冲击", "构建应对地缘政治化专利纠纷的多元化合规预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how US-EU policy divergences may impact ongoing and future litigation strategies in the UK and Germany.", "Practice note: Develop comprehensive, multi-jurisdictional compliance plans to address the rising trend of geopolitically influenced patent disputes.", "What to monitor: Track updates from the USTR regarding the Special 301 Report and subsequent reactions from European judicial authorities."]

items.title: New implementation of the EPO PACE programme starting 1 February 2026
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局 PACE 加速审查计划新规将于 2026 年实施
items.title_en: EPO Revises PACE Programme: Major Structural Changes Effective 1 February 2026
items.summary: On 1 February 2026, the revision of the PACE programme (Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications) of the European Patent Office (EPO) entered into force. This update, announced in the Notice from the Official Journal of December 2025 (OJ EPO 2025, A69), introduces a major structural change in the acceleration of the European ... Read more La entrada New implementation of the EPO PACE programme starting 1 February 2026 se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局宣布对 PACE（欧洲专利申请加速审查计划）进行重大修订，新版本将于 2026 年 2 月 1 日正式生效。该更新已通过 2025 年 12 月官方期刊公告发布，旨在对欧洲专利申请的加速程序进行结构性调整，以优化审查流程效率。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has announced significant revisions to the Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications (PACE). These changes, detailed in the Notice published in the Official Journal of December 2025 (OJ EPO 2025, A69), introduce a major structural overhaul designed to optimize the efficiency of the accelerated examination process. The updated framework officially enters into force on 1 February 2026. This reform aims to streamline procedural steps and enhance the overall speed and predictability of patent prosecution for applicants seeking faster grant decisions. Rights holders should note that this update represents a fundamental shift in how acceleration requests are managed and processed within the EPO system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需提前评估现有加速请求策略，适应新程序结构以避免审查延误。建议密切关注 EPO 后续操作指南，调整高价值专利布局节奏，确保在过渡期内合规利用加速通道。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants currently utilizing or planning to use the PACE programme must reassess their existing acceleration strategies to align with the new structural requirements. Failure to adapt to these procedural changes may result in unintended delays or non-compliance during the transition period. It is crucial for IP teams to closely monitor subsequent operational guidelines issued by the EPO to ensure full understanding of the new mechanics. Strategic adjustment of high-value patent filing schedules is recommended to leverage the accelerated channel effectively while maintaining compliance with the updated regulatory framework.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需重新评估现有专利申请的加速请求策略", "应密切跟踪 EPO 发布的后续操作指南细节"]

items.title: Technologies quantiques – Projet « OQULUS » pour 2030
items.title_zh: 法国2030计划：光子量子计算OQULUS项目优先发展
items.title_en: France 2030 Report Prioritizes OQULUS Photonics Quantum Computer Development
items.summary: Le rapport annuel officiel de France 2030 sur la stratégie [1] dans le domaine quantique donne les priorités de développement pour l’avenir.  Parmi celles-ci, le projet « OQULUS » vise à développer un ordinateur quantique à base de photons selon plusieurs technologies. En particulier, il s’agira de mettre au point un prototype à (suite) L’article Technologies quantiques – Projet « OQULUS » pour 2030 est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国发布“2030计划”年度官方报告，明确量子领域未来发展优先级。其中，“OQULUS”项目被列为重点，旨在基于多种技术开发光子量子计算机。该项目核心目标是研制出原型机，以推动法国在量子计算领域的战略自主与技术领先，体现了国家层面对前沿硬科技知识产权储备的重视。
items.ai_summary_en: The official annual report for France’s 2030 investment plan outlines strategic priorities for the future of quantum technologies. A central focus is the OQULUS project, which aims to develop a photonic quantum computer utilizing multiple technological approaches. The primary objective is to create a functional prototype, reinforcing France’s strategic autonomy and technical leadership in this critical sector. This initiative highlights the national commitment to securing intellectual property reserves in advanced hard technologies. By prioritizing photonics-based solutions, the strategy seeks to establish a robust foundation for next-generation computing capabilities, ensuring that European stakeholders remain competitive in the global race for quantum supremacy and related industrial applications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注法国及欧盟在量子计算等前沿领域的政策动向与专利布局。建议提前进行FTO分析，规避潜在专利风险，并加强自主研发与核心专利储备，以应对未来可能的技术壁垒和市场竞争。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor policy shifts and patent landscapes in quantum computing, particularly regarding France's strategic initiatives. Conducting early Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses is essential to mitigate potential infringement risks associated with emerging photonic technologies. Companies are advised to strengthen independent research and development efforts while building core patent portfolios. Proactive IP management will help navigate future technical barriers and competitive pressures. Aligning R&D strategies with national priorities can also facilitate access to funding and collaborative opportunities within the EU quantum ecosystem.

items.title: EUIPO launches 2026 SME Fund - GJE
items.title_zh: EUIPO启动2026年中小企业基金
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches 2026 SME Fund Offering Over €7,000 for IP Services
items.summary: EU SMEs can access €7,000+ in IP funding through the EUIPO 2026 SME Fund for patents, trade marks, designs and related services.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月2日，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）启动2026版中小企业基金。该基金由欧盟委员会发起，旨在为符合条件的欧盟中小企业提供知识产权服务资金支持。 eligible项目涵盖专利、商标、外观设计及相关服务，单个企业可获取超过7000欧元的资助额度。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 2, 2026, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) officially launched the 2026 edition of its SME Fund. This initiative, backed by the European Commission, provides financial support to small and medium-sized enterprises operating within the European Union. Eligible companies can access funding exceeding €7,000 to cover costs associated with patents, trademarks, designs, and related intellectual property services. To qualify, applicants must strictly meet the EU definition of an SME, which requires having fewer than 250 employees and an annual turnover below €50 million or a balance sheet total under €43 million. The program aims to reduce the financial burden of securing IP rights, thereby encouraging innovation and protecting market assets for smaller businesses across the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该基金是欧盟支持中小企业知识产权布局的常规政策性工具。实务中需关注申请窗口期及具体报销流程。对于在欧有业务布局的中小企业，此政策可降低初期IP注册成本，建议持续监控EUIPO发布的详细申请指南与资格认定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should assess their eligibility for this funding scheme to optimize budget allocation for intellectual property protection. Rights holders are advised to prepare necessary compliance documentation early to ensure smooth processing. Integrating trademark and patent strategies during the application phase allows companies to maximize the available subsidies effectively. This approach not only lowers overall acquisition costs but also strengthens the legal framework surrounding key brand and technology assets. Proactive engagement with the fund enables businesses to enhance their competitive positioning while managing operational expenditures efficiently within the current regulatory environment.

core_points_en: The EUIPO has opened the application window for the 2026 SME Fund, providing significant financial assistance for various intellectual property registrations and services.
Eligibility is strictly limited to EU-based entities meeting specific SME criteria regarding employee count, annual turnover, and balance sheet totals.
Individual companies may receive over €7,000 in funding, covering expenses for patents, trademarks, designs, and other eligible IP-related professional services.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Eligible SMEs should apply promptly to offset costs for trademark and patent filings, reducing overall IP portfolio maintenance expenses.
Practice note: Coordinate trademark and patent applications simultaneously to fully utilize the funding cap and streamline administrative procedures.
What to monitor: Verify strict adherence to EU SME definitions regarding employee numbers and financial thresholds to prevent application rejection.

items.title: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder
items.title_zh: 2026年欧盟中小企业知识产权基金重启启动
items.title_en: EUIPO Relaunches SME Fund 2026 for IP Protection Cost Support
items.summary: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder - Individueller IP-Scan, Anmeldung von Patenten, Marken, Designs und Sorten sowie Stand-der-Technik-Recherchen: Kleine und mittelgroße Unternehmen erhalten EU-Förderung, um ihr geistiges Eigentum zu schützen – Laut KMU-Barometer verfügen nur 10 Prozent der kleinen Unternehmen über Schutzrechte – DPMA-Präsidentin: Nur wer geistiges Eigentum schützt, kann dessen volles wirtschaftliches Potenzial ausschöpfen - Das
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）于2026年2月2日重启“中小企业基金2026”，为欧盟中小企业提供知识产权保护资助。资助范围包括个性化IP扫描、专利、商标、设计及植物新品种申请费用，以及现有技术检索。德国专利商标局（DPMA）负责德国层面的协调。数据显示仅10%的欧盟中小企业拥有知识产权，该基金旨在降低保护成本，释放经济潜力。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has relaunched the SME Fund 2026, effective February 2, 2026. This program provides financial support to small and medium-sized enterprises for identifying and protecting intellectual property rights. Eligible activities include individual IP scans, prior art searches, and filing fees for patents, trademarks, designs, and plant variety rights. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) coordinates the initiative in Germany. EUIPO data indicates that only ten percent of small enterprises currently hold IP rights, despite ninety-three percent of SMEs using such rights reporting positive economic effects. The fund aims to bridge this gap by subsidizing costs associated with securing legal protection against imitation and enhancing investment attractiveness.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This funding cycle offers a strategic opportunity for SMEs to offset initial IP registration costs across Europe. Rights holders should note that reimbursement typically requires pre-approval via vouchers before incurring expenses. Given the high reported positive impact of IP ownership on business performance, leveraging this subsidy can improve portfolio density without straining operational budgets. Monitoring national coordination details is essential for compliant claims.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["资助覆盖专利与商标申请费，显著降低中小企业进入欧洲市场的IP合规成本", "实务操作须严格遵循“先获批vouchers、后消费服务、最后报销”的程序要求", "后续需观察2300万欧元总预算在德国及其他成员国的具体分配与消耗速度"]

core_points_en: EUIPO reopened the SME Fund 2026 on February 2, covering IP scans and filing costs.
Subsidies apply to patents, trademarks, designs, plant varieties, and prior art search expenses.
DPMA coordinates the program in Germany, addressing low IP adoption rates among local SMEs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Subsidies reduce financial barriers for SMEs seeking patent, trademark, and design protection in the EU market.
Practice note: Applicants must secure EUIPO vouchers before initiating services or filings to ensure eligibility for cost reimbursement.
What to monitor: Track DPMA-specific procedural guidelines and fund allocation limits to avoid missing application windows or budget caps.

items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in January 2025
items.title_zh: 2025年1月商标新闻综述：丹麦规费及国际动态
items.title_en: January 2025 Trademark Update: Nice Classification Changes and Denmark Fee Adjustments
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总2025年1月全球商标动态。重点包括：WIPO发布2026版尼斯分类并于2026年1月生效；WIPO宣布丹麦指定国际注册及续展的个人规费修订，分别于2026年1月和7月生效；格林纳达加入马德里议定书；中国提交商标法修正案草案；香港推出图像商标搜索服务。
items.ai_summary_en: This update compiles key global trademark developments from January 2025, with significant implications for European rights holders. The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) released the 2026 version of the Nice Classification, introducing substantial changes to common product categories effective 1 January 2026. Concurrently, revised individual fees for designating Denmark in Madrid System applications will enter into force in two stages during 2026. The summary also covers procedural updates from Argentina, China, Grenada, and New Zealand. Grenada’s accession to the Madrid Protocol expands the system’s reach to 132 countries. These developments provide essential intelligence for managing international trademark portfolios, ensuring compliance with updated classification standards, and optimizing budget planning for designated territories within the Madrid System.
items.ai_insight_zh: 丹麦规费调整直接影响通过马德里体系指定丹麦的商标申请与维持成本，需关注具体费率变化。尼斯分类更新要求申请人重新评估商品服务项目归类，以确保注册范围准确。其他非欧动态如中国修法、格林纳达入约等，主要影响全球布局策略，需持续跟踪后续实施细则。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should review existing trademark portfolios against the new 2026 Nice Classification standards to ensure accurate identification of goods and services. Misalignment may affect protection scope or require corrective filings. For strategies involving Denmark, applicants must account for the scheduled fee increases in 2026. Evaluating these cost implications allows for better budget forecasting and potential optimization of renewal timelines. Additionally, monitoring procedural shifts in other jurisdictions like Argentina and China helps maintain a cohesive global enforcement strategy. Proactive adaptation to these regulatory and financial changes ensures continued robust protection and efficient resource allocation across international markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO宣布修订丹麦指定国际注册及个人规费，新费率将于2026年1月1日及7月1日生效", "版尼斯分类由WIPO发布，已于2026年1月1日生效，涉及常见产品类别的重大变更", "格林纳达于2025年12月15日加入马德里议定书，成为第116个成员国，覆盖范围增至1…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The 2026 Nice Classification entered into force on 1 January 2026, introducing significant adjustments to descriptions of common goods and services.", "Denmark's individual fees under the Madrid System will be adjusted in two stages, effective 1 January 2026 and 1 July 2026 respectively.", "Grenada acceded to the Madrid Protocol, increasing the total number of member countries in the international registration system to 132."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["丹麦指定规费变更将影响马德里国际注册在丹麦的成本结构，需核实新旧费率差异", "尼斯分类2026版生效，需审查现有商标及新申请的商品服务项目是否符合新分类标准", "格林纳达加入马德里体系扩大了国际注册覆盖范围，为进入加勒比市场提供新路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify that current goods and service specifications comply with the 2026 Nice Classification to maintain adequate protection scope.", "Practice note: Assess the financial impact of Denmark's revised Madrid fees on upcoming renewals and new designations to optimize budget planning.", "What to monitor: Track further procedural amendments in Argentina, China, and other key jurisdictions to adapt international filing strategies accordingly."]
core_points_en: The 2026 Nice Classification entered into force on 1 January 2026, introducing significant adjustments to descriptions of common goods and services.
Denmark's individual fees under the Madrid System will be adjusted in two stages, effective 1 January 2026 and 1 July 2026 respectively.
Grenada acceded to the Madrid Protocol, increasing the total number of member countries in the international registration system to 132.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify that current goods and service specifications comply with the 2026 Nice Classification to maintain adequate protection scope.
Practice note: Assess the financial impact of Denmark's revised Madrid fees on upcoming renewals and new designations to optimize budget planning.
What to monitor: Track further procedural amendments in Argentina, China, and other key jurisdictions to adapt international filing strategies accordingly.
